scheme_name: AICTE SHORT TERM TRAINING PROGRAMME-SFURTI SCHEME
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) & Ministry of MSME
objective: Short Term Training Programme-SFURTI Program aims to provide financial assistance from AICTE to the institutions for conducting faculty and student trainings to enable faculty members and students in the field of technical education.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to technical institutions to conduct training programs that help faculty and students understand the SFURTI scheme (Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries).
benefits_list: Financial assistance for organizing Short Term Training Programs (STTP).
Training on developing detailed project reports (DPR) for traditional industry clusters.
Funding covers honorarium for experts, travel, and contingency expenses for the training.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved Technical Institutions.
University Departments.
Institutions with a track record of conducting training programs.
how_to_apply: Applications are invited online through the AICTE AQIS (Audit and Quality Improvement Scheme) Portal. The institute must log in with their AICTE credentials and submit the proposal under the AQIS tab.
required_documents: Detailed proposal of the training program.

objective: Micro & Small Enterprise in their Individual Capacity faces problems to procure and execute large order/ which inhibit and restrict their growth. NSIC accordingly/ adopts Consortia approach and forms consortia of units manufacturing the same products/ thereby easing out marketing problems of MSEs.
benefits_summary: Helps Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) bid for large tenders by forming consortia. It provides marketing support and exemptions in government tenders.
benefits_list: Formation of Consortia to bid for large orders.
Exemption from payment of Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) for NSIC registered units.
Issue of Tender Sets free of cost.
Price preference of up to 15% over non-MSE units in tenders.
eligibility_criteria: Micro & Small Enterprises (MSEs) registered with NSIC through Single Point Registration Scheme (SPRS).
Must have valid Udyam Registration.
how_to_apply: MSEs can apply by visiting the nearest NSIC Branch Office. An agreement is signed among the consortium members and NSIC.

scheme_name: Handloom Marketing Assistance Component of the National Handloom Development Programme
issuing_authority: Development Commissioner for Handlooms, Ministry of Textiles
objective: The scheme aims to develop and promote marketing channels for handloom products to bring greater visibility to the sector and ensure commensurate remuneration to weavers. It provides Marketing Incentive to eligible handloom agencies for creating conducive conditions for marketing handloom products.
benefits_summary: Offers financial support to handloom agencies for marketing activities, organizing expos, and a direct marketing incentive based on sales turnover.
benefits_list: Marketing Incentive (MI) of 10% on average sales turnover of the last 3 years.
Financial assistance for organizing National Handloom Expos and Special Handloom Expos.
Funding for setting up Urban Haats and marketing complexes.
Support for participation in international fairs.
eligibility_criteria: State Handloom Corporations.
Apex Co-operative Societies.
Primary Handloom Weavers Co-operative Societies.
Self Help Groups (SHGs) engaged in handloom.
how_to_apply: Eligible agencies must submit proposals to the State Director of Handlooms, who forwards recommended proposals to the Office of the Development Commissioner for Handlooms.
required_documents: Audited Balance Sheet and Profit & Loss Account for the last 3 years.
Sales Turnover Certificate certified by CA.
Utilization Certificate of previous grants (if any).

scheme_name: AFFDF-Financial Assistance For Treatment Of Serious Diseases To Non Pensioner Ex-Servicemen (All Ranks)/Widows
issuing_authority: Kendriya Sainik Board (KSB), Ministry of Defence
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to a non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen of all ranks and widows to meet medical expenses related to treatment of approved serious diseases like cancer/ renal failure/ knee replacement and heart surgery.
benefits_summary: Provides financial aid to non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen (ESM) and their widows for treating serious diseases listed by the KSB.
benefits_list: Up to 90% (for Other Ranks) or 75% (for Officers) of medical expenses.
Maximum cap of ₹1.25 Lakhs for general serious diseases (higher limits apply for Cancer/Dialysis).
Covers diseases like Cancer, Renal Failure, Heart Surgery, Joint Replacement, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a Non-Pensioner Ex-Serviceman or Widow.
Rank must be Havildar/equivalent or below (for some components, all ranks for others).
Must not have access to ECHS or other medical reimbursement schemes.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the Kendriya Sainik Board (KSB) website. Hard copies of the application must be submitted to the Zila Sainik Board (ZSB) / Rajya Sainik Board (RSB).
required_documents: Original Medical Bills and Discharge Summary from the hospital.
Certificate from the Doctor regarding the nature of the disease.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: A Fellowship Scheme by AICTE/ MoE. The Distinguished Chair Professor Fellowship intends to utilize the expertise of highly qualified and experienced superannuated professionals who have made an unparalleled/ exceptional professional contribution to society in their respective fields/disciplines.
benefits_summary: Enables institutions to invite retired experts (Distinguished Professors) to interact with faculty and students, providing an honorarium for their services.
benefits_list: Honorarium of ₹10,000 per day for the Distinguished Chair Professor.
Maximum of 5 days per single trip.
Opportunity for institutes to gain from the expertise of veteran professionals.
eligibility_criteria: Host: AICTE approved technical institutions.
Fellow: Highly qualified superannuated (retired) professional with significant contributions in their field.
Age limit for Fellow: Usually up to 75 years (extendable to 80).
how_to_apply: The host institution applies online through the AICTE portal with the consent of the proposed Distinguished Chair Professor.
required_documents: Biodata/CV of the Distinguished Chair Professor.

scheme_name: AICTE - Grant For Augmenting Infrastructure In North Eastern Region
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: The scheme "AICTE - Grant for Augmenting Infrastructure in North Eastern Region (GAINER)" was introduced by AICTE to enhance the functional efficiency of the technical institutes located in far-flung areas of North East India/ by addressing deficiencies in water/ electricity/ and internet.
benefits_summary: Provides funding to technical institutes in the North East Region (NER) to solve critical infrastructure issues like water scarcity, power shortages, and internet connectivity.
benefits_list: Solar Power/Alternative Energy Projects: Funding up to ₹20 Lakhs.
Water Supply/Harvesting Projects: Funding up to ₹15 Lakhs.
Internet Connectivity Projects: Funding up to ₹5 Lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved Government or Government-aided technical institutions located in the North East Region.
The institute must have been in existence for at least 5 years.
how_to_apply: Eligible institutes must apply online via the AICTE portal when the call for proposals is open.
required_documents: Detailed Project Report (DPR) with cost estimates.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: The scheme "AICTE - Research Promotion Scheme (RPS)" was introduced by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)/ Department of Higher Education (DoHE) in order to promote research in identified thrust areas of technical education. The maximum limit of funding is ₹ 25,00,000.
benefits_summary: Offers research grants to faculty members in technical institutes to conduct research in engineering and technology thrust areas.
benefits_list: Maximum funding of ₹25 Lakhs per project.
Duration of the project is usually 3 years.
Grant covers non-recurring expenses (equipment) and recurring expenses (consumables, travel, contingencies).
eligibility_criteria: Full-time regular faculty members of AICTE approved institutions.
Principal Investigator (PI) should have a Ph.D. or Master's degree with research experience.
Institute must be AICTE approved.
how_to_apply: Faculty members must submit a research proposal online through the AICTE AQIS login during the application window.
required_documents: CV of Principal Investigator and Co-PI.
Endorsement from the Head of the Institution.

scheme_name: AICTE - Scheme For Promoting Interests/ Creativity And Ethics Among Students
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE-SPICES scheme provides financial support to institutions for developing students' clubs for the well-rounded development of students by promoting their interests/ creativity/ and ethics. This club should serve as a model for other clubs in the institution and also those in other institutions.
benefits_summary: Provides a one-time grant to support student clubs that promote hobbies, creativity, and professional ethics.
benefits_list: One-time financial grant of ₹1,00,000 to the student club.
Supports activities like workshops, events, and competitions organized by the club.
Encourages student networking and soft skill development.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved institutions with at least 5 years of existence.
The student club must have a minimum of 50 student members.
A faculty coordinator must be assigned to the club.
how_to_apply: The institute applies online via the AICTE portal, submitting details of the club and the proposed activities.
required_documents: Proposal of activities for the club.
Declaration by the Head of the Institution.

scheme_name: AICTE - YOUTH UNDERTAKING VISIT FOR ACQUIRING KNOWLEDGE (YUVAK)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: The scheme is intended for providing Financial Grant for conducting a Study Tour of ATAL TUNNEL by Students / faculty of AICTE approved Institutions in the field of technical education.
benefits_summary: Funds a study tour for engineering students and faculty to visit the Atal Tunnel to learn about modern tunneling technology.
benefits_list: Financial grant to cover travel, boarding, and lodging.
Grant amount varies by region (approx. ₹1.70 Lakh to ₹2.00 Lakh per institute).
Covers a team of 10 students and 1 faculty member.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved Technical Institutions.
Ideally for students in Civil, Mechanical, or related Engineering disciplines.
Institutions often selected based on NIRF ranking or location (priority to certain regions).
how_to_apply: Institutes must apply online via the AICTE portal when the scheme notification is released.
required_documents: List of participating students and faculty.

issuing_authority: AICTE in collaboration with INFLIBNET (Ministry of Education)
objective: AICTE e-Shodh Sindhu Scheme (eSS) provides subscriptions of e-Resources like Web of Science/ Scopus/ASME/ ASCE and Bentham Science to institutions.
benefits_summary: Provides technical institutions with access to high-quality electronic resources and bibliographic databases for research.
benefits_list: Free or subsidized access to e-journals and databases like IEEE, ASME, ASCE, Scopus, and Web of Science.
Access to a vast collection of full-text articles for research purposes.
Enhances the research capability of faculty and students.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved Government/Government-aided technical institutions.
University Departments in technical fields.
Must have adequate computing and internet infrastructure.
how_to_apply: Institutes must register with the e-ShodhSindhu Consortium via the INFLIBNET portal or respond to the AICTE circular for subscription.
required_documents: Application Form signed by the Head of Institution.
Details of IP addresses (Static IP) for access activation.

scheme_name: AICTE – Mitacs Globalink Research Internship (GRI) Scheme
objective: AICTE/ on 23rd February 2018/ entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with Mathematics of Information Technology and Computer Systems (MITACS) Canada for AICTE – MITACS Globalink Research Internships (GRI) Program to foster cross-border partnerships.
benefits_summary: Provides a 12-week research internship in Canada for Indian students. It covers travel, living expenses, and offers hands-on research experience.
benefits_list: Access to industry and academic networks in Canada.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate (3rd or 4th year undergraduate student).
Must be enrolled in an AICTE approved institution.
Minimum CGPA of 8.0 or equivalent.
how_to_apply: Students must apply online through the Mitacs Globalink GRI portal during the open call period (usually Aug-Sept).

scheme_name: AICTE – Saksham Scholarship Scheme For Specially-Abled Student (Degree)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Saksham is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Education/ implemented by AICTE/ aimed at providing encouragement and support to specially-abled children to pursue technical education. This is an attempt to give every young specially-abled student the opportunity to study further.
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to differently-abled students pursuing technical degree courses to help them complete their education.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹50,000 per annum for every year of study.
Amount can be used for college fees, books, equipment, or medical needs.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (not less than 40%).
Required education level: Admitted to 1st year of Degree level course in an AICTE approved institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
required_documents: Bank Account Details linked with Aadhaar.

scheme_name: AICTE – Saksham Scholarship Scheme For Specially-Abled Student (Diploma)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Saksham is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Education/ implemented by AICTE/ aimed at providing encouragement and support to specially-abled children to pursue technical education. This is an attempt to give every young specially-abled student the opportunity to study further.
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to differently-abled students pursuing technical diploma courses.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹50,000 per annum for every year of study.
Amount can be used for college fees, books, equipment, or medical needs.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (not less than 40%).
Required education level: Admitted to 1st year of Diploma level course in an AICTE approved institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: AICTE- TECHNICAL UNIVERSITY JOINT TRAINING PROGRAMS FOR TEACHERS
issuing_authority: AICTE in collaboration with Technical Universities
objective: AICTE- Technical University Joint Training Programs for Teachers has been launched to train faculty members to prepare them for being successful in a knowledge society.
benefits_summary: Offers training programs for faculty members to upgrade their subject knowledge and teaching skills.
benefits_list: Training on latest technologies and pedagogies.
Financial assistance to the host university to conduct the training.
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate Faculty.
Must be a faculty member in an AICTE approved institution affiliated with the respective Technical University.
how_to_apply: Faculty members must register through their respective Technical University's notification or the AICTE ATAL portal if applicable.
required_documents: No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Institute.

scheme_name: AICTE- Technical University Joint Training Programs for Librarians
issuing_authority: AICTE in collaboration with Technical Universities
objective: AICTE- Technical University Joint Training Programs for Librarians/ aims to provide financial assistance in conducting training to librarians to prepare them for being successful and effective in a knowledge society.
benefits_summary: Provides professional development training for librarians working in technical institutions.
benefits_list: Training on library automation, digital resources, and knowledge management.
Financial support for organizing the training program.
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Graduate (Library Science).
Must be a Librarian or Library Staff in an AICTE approved institution.
how_to_apply: Librarians must apply via the notification issued by the partnering Technical University.
required_documents: NOC from the Head of Institution.

scheme_name: AICTE-IDEA (Idea Development/ Evaluation & Application) LAB SCHEME
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Through Idea Lab Scheme/ AICTE will be setting up Idea Labs in AICTE approved institutions to encourage students for application of science/ technology engineering and mathematics (STEM) fundamentals towards enhanced hands-on experience/ learning by doing and even product visualization.
benefits_summary: Funds the establishment of 'IDEA Labs' in colleges to promote hands-on learning, fabrication, and product development among students.
benefits_list: Financial Grant (up to ₹1 Crore total project cost, shared by AICTE and Industry/Institute).
Access to advanced machinery (3D printers, laser cutters, etc.) for students.
Training for faculty and student mentors.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved institutions with at least 10 years of existence.
Must have NBA accreditation for at least one course.
how_to_apply: Institutions must apply online via the AICTE portal by submitting a Detailed Project Report (DPR).
required_documents: Layout plan of the proposed lab.

issuing_authority: AICTE & Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE)
objective: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) and Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE) distinguished visiting Professor scheme envisages promotion of industry-institute interaction.
benefits_summary: Facilitates visits by experienced industry professionals to engineering colleges to deliver lectures and mentor students.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired or Working Professional (Industry Fellow).
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Must be an INAE Fellow or a distinguished expert in the field.
how_to_apply: The host institution must submit the proposal to INAE, including the bio-data and consent of the proposed professor.

scheme_name: AICTE-INAE Travel Grant Scheme For Engineering Students
issuing_authority: AICTE & Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE)
objective: A Travel Grant Scheme by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) and by the Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE)/ for the students from AICTE approved Engineering College/institutions. The scheme aims at enhancing the quality of engineering education in the country.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to engineering students to present research papers at international conferences abroad.
benefits_list: Travel expenses (Airfare) up to a maximum limit (usually ₹1 Lakh).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student (B.E./B.Tech or M.E./M.Tech).
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate.
Must have a paper accepted for presentation at an international conference.
how_to_apply: Students must send the application form along with required documents to the INAE office via post/email.
required_documents: Acceptance Letter of the Research Paper.
No Objection Certificate from the Institute.

issuing_authority: AICTE & Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE)
objective: AICTE-ISTE Induction/ Refresher Programme provide financial assistance to AICTE approved institutions/universities in conducting training for the teaching faculties working in technical institutions.
benefits_summary: Provides funding to institutions to conduct 1-2 week induction or refresher training programs for faculty.
benefits_list: Financial grant up to ₹3,00,000 to the host institute.
Covers honorarium for experts, travel, and course materials.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved institutions with ISTE Institutional Membership.
Required education level: Graduate (Faculty members).
how_to_apply: Institutions must apply online through the AICTE-ISTE portal submitting their proposal for the program.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE-LILAVATI AWARD intends to recognize efforts being made by AICTE-approved institutions to treat women with ‘equality and fairness’ in all spheres of their lives.
benefits_summary: Awards institutions that have done outstanding work in women empowerment across various sub-themes like health, education, and self-defense.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved technical institutions.
Must demonstrate significant contribution to women empowerment.
how_to_apply: Institutions must apply online via the AICTE Lilavati Award portal by uploading proof of their activities.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Margdarshan Initiative has been introduced by All India Council for Technical Education in 2018 for facilitating the technical institutes in order to improve the quality of technical education as a whole through accreditation by providing access to the facilities available in host institute.
benefits_summary: Funds 'Mentor' institutes to guide 'Mentee' institutes in improving their quality and achieving NBA accreditation.
benefits_list: Grant-in-aid up to ₹50 Lakhs to the Mentor Institute.
Funds cover fellowship for experts, travel, and conducting workshops.
Mentee institutes receive guidance on best practices and accreditation processes.
eligibility_criteria: Mentor Institute: Must be AICTE approved with NBA accreditation.
Mentee Institute: AICTE approved institutes seeking accreditation.
how_to_apply: Mentor institutes apply online via the AICTE portal, listing the mentee institutes they plan to guide.
required_documents: NBA Accreditation Proof of Mentor Institute.
Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Mentee Institutes.

scheme_name: AICTE-National Initiative for Training of Technical Teachers (NITTT)
objective: Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) and All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) jointly proposed an initiative called “National Initiative for Technical Teachers Training (NITTT)” to address the technical teachers of the country who plays an active role in the technical education.
benefits_summary: A mandatory training program for newly inducted faculty to ensure they acquire necessary pedagogical skills.
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate.
Must be an Inductee Teacher (newly joined faculty) in an AICTE approved institution.
how_to_apply: Teachers must register on the NITTT portal (nittt.ac.in) and complete the modules and mentorship.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE-Professional Development Scheme enables meritorious faculty to interact at International Level Conferences/ both within and outside India/ Seminars/ and Symposia.
benefits_summary: Provides travel grants to faculty members for presenting research papers at international conferences.
eligibility_criteria: Regular full-time faculty in AICTE approved institutions.
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate.
Paper accepted at a reputed international conference.
how_to_apply: Faculty must apply online via the AICTE AQIS portal well in advance of the conference.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: The All India Council for Technical Education in its 55th Meeting of the Council held on 14th August 2018 approved the Quality Improvement Programme (Foreign University) (QIP-FU). The QIP(FU) is an extension of the QIP program launched by the Government of India in the year 1970.
benefits_summary: Offers faculty members the opportunity to pursue a Ph.D. in specific foreign universities with which AICTE has an MoU.
benefits_list: Tuition fee waivers (subject to MoU terms).
Opportunity for global exposure and research.
eligibility_criteria: Regular faculty members of AICTE approved institutions.
Required education level: Post Graduate.
Must meet the admission criteria of the foreign university.
how_to_apply: Apply through the AICTE portal when the specific call for the foreign university program is announced.

scheme_name: AICTE-SHORT TERM TRAINING PROGRAMME (STTP) (UT-J&K/LADAKH AND NER)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE-Short Term Training Program (STTP) intends to conduct faculty training through financial assistance from AICTE to enable faculty members in the field of technical education.
benefits_summary: Provides funding to institutions in J&K, Ladakh, and North East Region to conduct training programs for faculty.
benefits_list: Financial grant to conduct the training program.
Covers honorarium for experts, travel, and contingency.
Helps faculty in remote regions stay updated with latest technologies.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved institutions located in UT of J&K, Ladakh, or North East Region.
Required education level: Graduate (Faculty participants).
how_to_apply: Institutes must apply online via the AICTE AQIS portal.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Student Development Cell (StDC) of All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) to provide encouragement and support to orphans/ wards of parents who died due to Covid-19/ wards of Armed Forces/ and Central Paramilitary Forces martyred in action (Shaheed).
benefits_summary: Provides financial scholarship to orphans, wards of Covid-19 victims, and wards of martyrs to pursue technical education.
benefits_list: Paid as a lump sum amount towards college fees, computer, books, etc.
Duration: Up to 4 years for Degree / 3 years for Diploma.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant must be an Orphan OR Ward of parents died due to Covid-19 OR Ward of Martyr.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Pursuing Degree/Diploma in AICTE approved institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE-Technical Book Writing & Translation scheme has been launched by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) to promote the use of 'Scheduled Regional Language' in Technical Education for creating knowledge base in local language.
benefits_summary: Encourages authors and translators to create technical textbooks in Indian regional languages.
benefits_list: Honorarium of ₹1,00,000 for writing an original book.
Honorarium of ₹50,000 for translating a book.
Recognition for contributing to regional language education.
eligibility_criteria: Faculty members, experts, or professionals in technical fields.
Proficiency in the specific regional language.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the AICTE portal when the call for proposals is active.

scheme_name: AICTE-Training programs for Physical Education Trainers/Sport-Games Teachers/Director of Sports
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Training programs for Physical Education Trainers/Sport-Games Teachers/Director of Sports/ aims to support institution in conducting trainings to develop the skill of the participants to involve the masses in traditional sports/games and fun games for physical activity through recreation.
benefits_summary: Funds training programs for sports directors and physical education teachers in technical institutes.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹5,00,000 to the host institute.
Training on modern sports management and traditional games.
Travel and boarding expenses for participants are covered.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved institutions.
Participants must be Physical Education Trainers or Sports Directors.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Institutions apply via the AICTE AQIS portal to host the training.

objective: The “ASEAN-India Research Training Fellowship (AI-RTF)” is a fellowship scheme initiated by the Department of Science & Technology/ Government of India under ASEAN-India Science & Technology Development Fund to support young talented researchers/scientists of ASEAN member countries.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships for ASEAN researchers to work at Indian research/academic institutions for up to 6 months.
benefits_list: One-time grant of ₹40,000 for research contingencies.
eligibility_criteria: National of any ASEAN Member State.
Required education level: Postgraduate (M.Tech/M.Sc) or Ph.D.
Age limit: Up to 40 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the ASEAN-India Science, Technology & Innovation Cooperation (AISTIC) portal.
required_documents: Acceptance Letter from Indian Host Institute.

issuing_authority: LIC of India / Ministry of Labour & Employment
objective: The Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana (AABY) is a social security scheme for rural landless households implemented by the Ministry of Labour & Employment/ Government of India. The scheme provides insurance coverage to the head of the household or an earning member of the family in the age group of 18 to 59.
benefits_summary: A social security insurance scheme for rural landless households providing death and disability coverage. (Note: In many states, this has converged with PMJJBY/PMSBY).
benefits_list: Scholarship benefit (Shiksha Sahayog) for children of beneficiaries (₹100/month per child).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 18 and 59 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000 (Historically for BPL/Marginally above BPL).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Rural Landless Household.
Must belong to one of the identified occupational groups (e.g., Fishermen, Handloom Weavers, Cobblers).
how_to_apply: Contact the Nodal Agency appointed by the State Government or the nearest LIC office.
required_documents: Proof of belonging to the specific occupational group/Landless certificate.

scheme_name: Abdul Kalam Technology Innovation National Fellowship
issuing_authority: Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE) & DST
objective: Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE) will award "Abdul Kalam Technology Innovation National Fellowship" to outstanding engineers to recognize/ encourage and support translational research by individuals to achieve excellence in engineering/ innovation and technology development.
benefits_summary: A prestigious fellowship for engineers working in public-funded institutions to encourage innovation and translational research.
benefits_list: Fellowship amount of ₹25,000 per month (in addition to regular salary).
Research Grant of ₹15.00 Lakh per annum.
Overhead of ₹1.00 Lakh per annum to the host institute.
Duration: Initially 3 years, extendable by up to 2 more years.
eligibility_criteria: Indian national working in public-funded institutions.
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D. in Engineering/Technology.
Minimum 5 years of service left before retirement.
Must have a track record of translational research.
how_to_apply: Nominations must be sent by Heads of Institutions, Presidents of National Science Academies, or JC Bose/SS Bhatnagar awardees to INAE via email/post. Self-nominations are not accepted.
required_documents: Brief description of the proposed research work.

scheme_name: Access To Knowledge For Technology Development And Dissemination (A2K+) - Studies
issuing_authority: Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR)
objective: Access to Knowledge for Technology Development and Dissemination (A2K+) is a scheme targeted towards developing mechanisms to disseminate science/ technology and innovation-related information to industries/ research and academic institutions.
benefits_summary: Provides grants to conduct studies, conferences, and events that help disseminate scientific and technological knowledge to industries and researchers.
benefits_list: Financial support for organizing national/international conferences.
Support for creating technology development mechanisms.
eligibility_criteria: Industry Associations/Chambers of Commerce.
Technical Consultancy Organizations.
Academic and Research Institutions.
Public funded bodies.
how_to_apply: Submit proposals in the prescribed format (hard copy and soft copy) to the Head (A2K+), DSIR, New Delhi.
required_documents: Annual Reports/Audited Accounts of the organization.

objective: Advance Authorisation scheme enables duty free import of inputs for export production/ including replenishment of inputs or duty remission Advance Authorisation covers manufacturer exporters or merchant exporters tied to supporting manufacturer(s).
benefits_summary: Allows exporters to import raw materials duty-free if they are used to manufacture products for export.
benefits_list: Exemption from Basic Customs Duty, Additional Customs Duty, Education Cess, Anti-dumping Duty, and IGST.
Inputs allowed based on Standard Input Output Norms (SION).
eligibility_criteria: Manufacturer Exporters or Merchant Exporters tied to supporting manufacturers.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Business Owner.
Must hold a valid Importer-Exporter Code (IEC).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the DGFT website (ANF 4A form).
required_documents: Bank Realization Certificate (BRC) for past exports (if applicable).
Export Promotion Copy of Shipping Bill.

issuing_authority: Department of Military Affairs, Ministry of Defence
objective: A welfare scheme by the Department of Military Affairs/ MoD under which rigorous military training will be imparted to the youth aged between 17.5 to 23 years and meeting other educational/ physical/ and medical criteria/ who are aspirational to be a part of the armed forces.
benefits_summary: A recruitment scheme for Indian youth to serve in the Armed Forces for a period of 4 years as 'Agniveers'.
benefits_list: Seva Nidhi Package: Approx. ₹11.71 Lakh (Tax-free) upon exit.
Opportunity for 25% of the batch to be enrolled in the regular cadre.
eligibility_criteria: Age: 17.5 to 21 years (upper limit revised from 23).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
Required education level: Class 10th/12th (depending on the trade).
how_to_apply: Apply online via the respective service portals: Join Indian Army, Join Indian Navy, or CASB (Air Force) during recruitment rallies.

objective: A welfare scheme by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare was launched in 2002. AC&ABC aims at agricultural development/ supplementing the efforts of public extension by providing extension and other services to farmers either on a payment basis or free of cost as per the business model.
benefits_summary: Provides training and financial subsidy to agriculture graduates to set up their own Agri-Clinics or Agri-Business Centres.
benefits_list: Loan assistance from banks with NABARD subsidy.
Subsidy: 36% (General) to 44% (SC/ST/Women/NE/Hill states) of the project cost.
Ceiling of project cost: ₹20 Lakh (Individual) / ₹1 Crore (Group).
eligibility_criteria: Graduates/Diploma holders in Agriculture or allied subjects.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply online for training on the AC&ABC website. After training, submit a project report to the bank for a loan.

scheme_name: Agricultural Extension (Sub-Mission on Agricultural Extension - ATMA)
objective: Extension Division endeavours towards successful implementation of Agricultural Extension activities. Through its various programmes/ schemes and activities/ Extension Division helps farmers to get information regarding scientific research and new knowledge in agricultural practices.
benefits_summary: Focuses on technology dissemination to farmers through training, demonstrations, and exposure visits.
benefits_list: Training and capacity building for farmers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Farmers' groups (FIGs), Self Help Groups (SHGs).
how_to_apply: Contact the nearest Block Technology Manager (BTM) at the Block level or the Project Director (ATMA) at the District level.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / NABARD
objective: The revitalized and farmers’ income enhancing centric sub-scheme Agricultural Marketing Infrastructure (AMI) of Integrated Scheme for Agricultural Marketing (ISAM) is meant for holistic development of agricultural value chain critically focusing each linkage of post-harvest value chain.
benefits_summary: Provides capital subsidy for creating agricultural marketing infrastructure like warehouses, cold storages, and grading units.
benefits_list: Subsidy of 25% (General) to 33.33% (SC/ST/Women/NE) on capital cost.
Promotes creation of scientific storage capacity.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers, Agri-preneurs, FPOs, Cooperatives, Individuals.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer/Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Apply for a term loan at a bank. The bank submits the subsidy claim to NABARD.

scheme_name: Agriculture And Processed Foods Export Promotion Scheme Of APEDA
issuing_authority: Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)
objective: The Financial Assistance Scheme (FAS) is an export promotion scheme run by APEDA. The scheme aims to facilitate the export of agri-products by providing assistance to exporters.
benefits_summary: Offers financial assistance to exporters for infrastructure development, quality certification, and market development.
benefits_list: Assistance for setting up packhouses, cold storages, etc.
Reimbursement of quality certification costs (HACCP, ISO, etc.).
Support for participating in international trade fairs.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Exporters with APEDA (RCMC holders).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Business Owner.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the APEDA Exporter Interface (FAS component).

objective: Sub-Mission on Agroforestry was launched in 2016-17 to promote plantation on farmlands along with agricultural crops. The scheme aims to increase farm income/ expand tree cover and promote better carbon sequestration for climate change adaptation and mitigation.
benefits_summary: Encourages farmers to plant trees on their farmland by providing subsidies for saplings and maintenance.
benefits_list: Support for block plantation and peripheral plantation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Must have land ownership or lease rights.
how_to_apply: Contact the State Agriculture Department or Forest Department. Applications are often processed through district-level offices.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE-Distinguished Professionals Scheme has been launched by the All India Council for Technical Education/ Ministry of Education to improve the quality of Technical Education in India by utilizing the expertise of Professionals/ Emeritus professors & Eminent Personalities to share their knowledge.
benefits_summary: Allows institutions to invite experts from industry and research to deliver lectures and mentor students.
benefits_list: Honorarium of ₹10,000 per session to the expert.
Maximum of ₹1.5 Lakhs per year per institute.
eligibility_criteria: Host: AICTE approved Institutions.
Expert: Distinguished professional with 10+ years of experience.
Applicant's age must be between 58 and 70 years (for the expert).
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: The institute applies online via the AICTE portal to host the professional.

objective: The Ministry of Textiles introduced the Amended Technology Upgradation Fund Scheme (ATUFS). This scheme aims to facilitate investment/ employment/ productivity/ quality and import and export substitution in the textile industry.
benefits_summary: Provides Capital Investment Subsidy (CIS) for benchmarking machinery in the textile sector.
eligibility_criteria: Textile units (MSMEs and Non-MSMEs).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Business Owner.
Machinery must be new and benchmarked.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the i-TUFS portal. A Unique ID (UID) is generated after machinery purchase/installation verification.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: Under the scheme/ assistance would be provided to the training institutions under the Ministry of MSME for creation/ strengthening and expansion of infrastructure/ including setting-up of new branches and centres.
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to training institutions (EDIs) to build infrastructure and conduct entrepreneurship training.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹2.5 Crore for infrastructure (building/equipment).
Assistance for conducting Entrepreneurship Development Programs (EDPs) and ESDPs.
eligibility_criteria: National Level Entrepreneurship Development Institutes (EDIs).
State Level EDIs recommended by State Govt.
Training Institutions of the Ministry of MSME.
how_to_apply: Institutions submit proposals to the Ministry of MSME through the designated nodal agency.

scheme_name: Assistance to Voluntary Organizations Working for Welfare of OBCs
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The "Assistance to Voluntary Organizations Working for Welfare of OBCs" scheme aims to involve the voluntary sector in improving the educational and socio-economic conditions of the target group i.e. OBCs to upgrade skills to enable them to start income-generating activities on their own.
benefits_summary: Provides grants-in-aid to NGOs for running vocational training and skill development centers for OBCs.
benefits_list: Grant-in-aid covers 90% of the approved project cost.
Funds for vocational training trades like carpentry, computer, craft, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Registered NGOs/Voluntary Organizations functioning for at least 2 years.
Must be registered on the NITI Aayog Darpan Portal.
Beneficiaries must belong to OBC category.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the e-Anudaan portal of the Ministry.

objective: The scheme “Atal Beemit Vyakti Kalyan Yojana (ABVKY)” was introduced by the Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) on a pilot basis for a period of 2 years w.e.f. 01.07.2018 for providing relief to the Insured Persons (IPs) who have become unemployed.
benefits_summary: Provides cash compensation to ESIC insured persons in case of unemployment.
benefits_list: Relief of 50% of average per day earning.
Payable for a maximum of 90 days of unemployment.
eligibility_criteria: Insured Person under ESIC Act.
Must have been in insurable employment for 2 years.
Must have contributed for at least 78 days in the preceding contribution period.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Private-employed (currently unemployed).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the ESIC portal using the AB-1 form. The claim is processed directly to the bank account.
required_documents: Proof of unemployment (Self-declaration usually suffices if online).

scheme_name: Atal Mission For Rejuvenation And Urban Transformation (AMRUT 2.0)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
objective: Providing basic services (e.g. water supply/ sewerage/ urban transport) to households and building amenities in cities which will improve the quality of life for all/ especially the poor and the disadvantaged is a national priority.
benefits_summary: Aims to provide universal coverage of water supply to all households in statutory towns and coverage of sewerage/septage management.
benefits_list: Development of parks and green spaces.
eligibility_criteria: Target: All statutory towns in India.
Implemented by State Governments and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
how_to_apply: Citizens cannot apply directly for the scheme but can apply for water/sewerage connections at their local Municipality/Corporation once infrastructure is built.
required_documents: For connection: Proof of ownership/occupancy, Aadhaar, Application form of local municipality.

issuing_authority: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)
objective: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) is an old age income security scheme for a savings account holder in the age group of 18-40 years/ who is not an income tax-payee. It helps to address longevity risks among workers in unorganized sector and encourages the workers to voluntarily save for retirement.
benefits_summary: A government-backed pension scheme for the unorganized sector providing a guaranteed minimum pension.
benefits_list: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1000, ₹2000, ₹3000, ₹4000, or ₹5000 after age 60.
Pension amount depends on the contribution.
Return of corpus to the nominee upon death of the subscriber.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 18 and 40 years.
Must have a Savings Bank Account.
Should not be an Income Tax Payer.
how_to_apply: Visit any Bank or Post Office to fill the APY registration form. Can also be done via Net Banking.

scheme_name: Award Of Scholarships To Young Artists In Different Cultural Fields
objective: The scheme “Award of Scholarships to Young Artists in Different Cultural Fields” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides scholarships to young artists for advanced training in Indian Classical Music, Dance, Theatre, Visual Arts, etc.
benefits_list: Covers training under a Guru or Institute.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 18 and 25 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student/Artist.
Must have undergone training for at least 5 years.
Income limit usually applies.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the CSMS (Cultural Schemes Monitoring System) portal of the Ministry of Culture.
required_documents: Certificate from Guru/Institute regarding 5 years training.

scheme_name: Awarding Teleeducation Scholarships To Afghan Students
objective: The "Awarding Teleeducation Scholarships To Afghan Students" scheme under e-VidyaBharati Network Project/ launched by the Ministry of External Affairs/ offers 1000 annual scholarships to Afghan students for UGC-approved online UG and PG programs from Indian universities.
benefits_summary: Offers full scholarships to Afghan students to pursue online Undergraduate and Postgraduate degrees from Indian universities.
benefits_list: Degree awarded by Indian University upon completion.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a national of Afghanistan.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Secondary (12th Pass) for UG, Graduate for PG.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the iLearn portal (ilearn.gov.in) under the e-VidyaBharati project.

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
objective: AB PM-JAY is a health insurance scheme for low-income families in rural and urban areas. The scheme aims to provide affordable healthcare facilities to the Poor. It is the largest health assurance scheme in the world which aims at providing a health cover of ₹5,00,000/- per family per year.
benefits_summary: Provides cashless health insurance cover for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
benefits_list: Health cover of ₹5 Lakh per family per year.
Cashless and paperless access to services.
Accepted at empaneled public and private hospitals.
eligibility_criteria: Families listed in SECC 2011 database (Deprivation criteria).
PM-JAY Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) cardholders.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000 (varies by specific state add-ons).
how_to_apply: Check eligibility on the PMJAY portal (mera.pmjay.gov.in). If eligible, visit a Common Service Centre (CSC) or Empaneled Hospital for Ayushman Card generation.

objective: The Research Fellowship in Sciences for meritorious students BSR scheme of the UGC is open to candidates who are selected for registering for the Ph.D. program of the university through a procedure outlined already in the UGC notification by a regular admission procedure.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships to meritorious students pursuing Ph.D. in Sciences in eligible universities.
eligibility_criteria: Registered for Ph.D. in Science subjects.
Must be in a UGC-BSR eligible Department/University (SAP/CPE).
Non-GATE/Non-NET candidates are also eligible if selected by the University.
how_to_apply: Universities select eligible candidates during Ph.D. admission and submit the list to UGC/Canara Bank for fund release.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (NSSH)
objective: Under the Ministry Of Micro/ Small & Medium Enterprises/ Bank Guarantee Charges Reimbursement Scheme is a sub scheme under National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) scheme.
benefits_summary: Provides reimbursement of bank guarantee charges to SC/ST Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) to reduce their cost of doing business.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of 80% of bank guarantee charges paid to the bank.
Maximum reimbursement of ₹1,00,000 (1 Lakh) per annum per MSE unit.
eligibility_criteria: Micro or Small Enterprise owned by SC/ST entrepreneurs.
Must hold a valid Udyam Registration.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) portal.
required_documents: Proof of payment of bank guarantee charges.

scheme_name: Bank Loan Processing Fee Reimbursement Scheme (NSSH)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (NSSH)
objective: The Bank Loan Processing Fee Reimbursement Scheme comes under the purview of the Ministry Of MSME (MSME) of the Government of India.
benefits_summary: Reimburses the loan processing fees charged by banks to SC/ST MSEs to facilitate easier access to credit.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of 80% of loan processing fees charged by the bank.
Maximum reimbursement cap of ₹1,00,000 per annum per MSE unit.
eligibility_criteria: Micro or Small Enterprise owned by SC/ST entrepreneurs.
Must have availed a business loan from a bank/financial institution.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) portal.
required_documents: Statement showing deduction of processing fee.

scheme_name: Bima Yojana - Direct Benefit to Artisan
objective: Launched on 15th August 2022/ the scheme "Bima Yojana - Direct Benefit to Artisan (BYDBTA)" is a Social Welfare Scheme by the Ministry of Textiles/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides life and accident insurance coverage to handicrafts artisans by reimbursing the premium for PMJJBY and PMSBY.
benefits_list: Accidental Insurance (PMSBY): ₹2 Lakhs for accidental death/disability.
Full premium amount is reimbursed/paid by the government.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 18 and 50 years (for PMJJBY) / 18-70 (for PMSBY).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed (Handicraft Artisan).
Must hold a Pehchan Card (Artisan ID).
how_to_apply: Eligible artisans can enroll through the field offices of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts) or during special camps.

scheme_name: Biotechnology Career Advancement And Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme For Women Scientists
objective: Biotechnology Career Advancement and Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme/ a special scheme of the Department of Biotechnology (DBT)/ Ministry of Science & Technology/ the government of India aims to enhance the participation of women scientists in India towards research in Biotechnology.
benefits_summary: Provides research grants to women scientists who are unemployed or want to return to mainstream science after a career break.
benefits_list: Funding for equipment, consumables, and travel.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed (or employed on temporary basis).
Required education level: Ph.D. / M.D. / M.Tech in Life Sciences or Biotechnology.
Age limit: Up to 55 years.
how_to_apply: Applications are invited online through the DBT eProMIS portal.

objective: The “Biotechnology Ignition Grant Scheme (BIG)” is a flagship scheme of BIRAC which recognizes and invests in innovative ideas in the biotech sector. BIG scheme supports individual entrepreneurs/ start-ups from ideation to Proof of Concept.
benefits_summary: Offers funding to biotech startups and entrepreneurs to establish Proof of Concept (PoC) for their innovative ideas.
eligibility_criteria: Individual Entrepreneur or a Startup (less than 5 years old).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Student/Researcher.
Must have an innovative idea in the biotechnology sector.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the BIRAC website during the call for proposals (usually twice a year).

objective: The Buti Foundation Award/ established in 2007 by the Buti Foundation/ New Delhi/ is a biennial award recognizing innovative and outstanding research in the field of Plasma Science & Technology. The award aims to honor a Scientist/Engineer under the age of 45 years for their significant contribution
benefits_summary: Recognizes outstanding research contributions in Plasma Science & Technology.
eligibility_criteria: Scientist/Engineer working in India.
Age: Under 45 years.
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Field of work: Plasma Science & Technology.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited from Heads of Institutions/Laboratories/Universities. Self-nominations are not accepted.

scheme_name: CBSE Merit Scholarship Scheme For Single Girl Child
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Education for meritorious Single Girl Students/ who are the only child of their parents; and have passed the CBSE Class 10th Examination with 60% or more marks and are continuing their further school education of Class 11th and 12th.
benefits_summary: Encourages meritorious single girl children to pursue their education by providing financial scholarships.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Must be the ONLY child of her parents.
Must have passed CBSE Class X with 60% or more marks.
Continuing education in a CBSE affiliated school.
Tuition fee in Class X should not exceed ₹1,500/month.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the CBSE Scholarship portal.
required_documents: Affidavit for Single Girl Child status.

issuing_authority: ICAR - Central Institute of Fisheries Education (CIFE)
objective: From the year 2010 onwards/ the division is offering Masters and PhD in two disciplines namely Fish Nutrition and Feed Technology and Fish physiology and Biochemistry with an objective of moulding professionals with fundamental and advanced knowledge and expertise.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowship support to students pursuing Masters and Doctoral programs at CIFE.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate (B.F.Sc) for Masters, Masters for Ph.D.
Must be admitted to CIFE through ICAR entrance exam.
how_to_apply: Automatically considered upon admission to CIFE programs, subject to ICAR/Institute norms.

scheme_name: Capacity Building For Service Providers - Entrepreneurship Programme
objective: The scheme "Entrepreneurship Programme" is a Suo-Moto Sub-Scheme of the Ministry of Tourism under the Umbrella Scheme of "Capacity Building for Service Providers". In this scheme financial assistance is provided to the institutes (given below) to conduct entrepreneurship development programs.
benefits_summary: Funds training institutes to conduct entrepreneurship development programs (EDP) for the tourism and hospitality sector.
benefits_list: Training to acquire skills to start tourism-related businesses.
Stipend to trainees during the course.
eligibility_criteria: Institutes: IHMs, FCIs, IITTM, etc.
Trainees: 8th/10th Pass (depending on course), Age 18+.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed aspiring entrepreneur.
how_to_apply: Institutes apply to Ministry of Tourism for funding. Individuals apply to the respective Institutes offering the course.

scheme_name: Capacity Building For Service Providers - Skill Testing & Certification
objective: The scheme "Skill Testing & Certification" is a Suo-Moto Sub-Category of Programmes under the Umbrella Scheme of "Capacity Building for Service Providers"/ Ministry of Tourism. In this scheme/ financial assistance is provided to conduct Skilling and Re-Skilling Programmes.
benefits_summary: Provides skill testing and certification for existing service providers in the hospitality sector who have learned skills on the job but lack formal certification.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of testing fees to the implementing agency.
eligibility_criteria: Existing service providers (waiters, cooks, drivers, etc.).
No formal qualification required, but must have work experience.
Required education level: Secondary (preferred).
how_to_apply: Apply through Institutes of Hotel Management (IHMs) or Food Craft Institutes (FCIs) conducting the testing.
required_documents: Employment proof or Self-declaration of experience.

scheme_name: Central Assistance For One Time Settlement Of Displaced Families From PoK And Chhamb
issuing_authority: Ministry of Home Affairs / J&K Government
objective: A relief and rehabilitation scheme by the Ministry of Home Affairs provides central assistance to the displaced families from PoK And Chhamb and settled in the state of Jammu & Kashmir. The amount of Central Assistance of Rs. 5/49/692/- per family will be distributed through Direct Benefit Transfer.
benefits_summary: Provides a one-time financial settlement to displaced families from Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK) and Chhamb settled in J&K.
benefits_list: One-time financial assistance of ₹5,49,692 per family.
Amount transferred directly to the bank account (DBT).
eligibility_criteria: Displaced families from PoK (1947) and Chhamb (1965/1971).
Must be settled in Jammu & Kashmir.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Apply to the Designated Authority (Relief & Rehabilitation Commissioner, J&K) with required proofs.
required_documents: Proof of displacement/Registration as Displaced Person.

scheme_name: Central Sector Scheme for Rehabilitation of Bonded Labourer
objective: The scheme addresses eradicating bonded labour in India. It introduces varying rehabilitation assistance amounts based on beneficiary categories/ such as adults/ children/ women/ and extreme cases/ with provisions for annuity schemes and Direct Benefit Transfer.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance and rehabilitation to released bonded labourers.
benefits_list: Rehabilitation package: ₹1 Lakh (Men), ₹2 Lakhs (Women/Children), ₹3 Lakhs (Extreme cases/Transgenders).
Non-cash assistance like land allotment, house, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a released bonded labourer holding a Release Certificate.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed/freed labourer.
how_to_apply: Through the District Magistrate (DM) or Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) upon release and certification.

scheme_name: Central Sector Scheme of National Fellowship for Scheduled Caste Students (NFSC)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment / NSFDC
objective: The scheme provides opportunities to Scheduled Castes students to pursue higher education leading to M.Phil/Ph.D degrees in Sciences/ Humanities and Social Science streams. The objective of the scheme is to provide fellowships in the form of financial assistance to SC students.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships to SC students to pursue M.Phil and Ph.D. degrees.
benefits_list: Fellowship amount similar to UGC JRF/SRF (approx ₹31,000 - ₹35,000 + HRA).
Contingency grant for Humanities and Sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
Must have passed the NET-JRF or UGC-CSIR NET examination.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D. in a recognized university.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or UGC portal based on the notification.

objective: CVC initiated an Internship Scheme in the year 2016. This Scheme seeks to engage Indian Nationals/ who are Graduates (awaiting final year results) /Post Graduate or Research Students enrolled in reputed Universities/Institutions in India or abroad.
benefits_summary: Offers an opportunity for students to understand the working of the CVC and contribute to vigilance-related policies.
benefits_list: Exposure to the functioning of the Central Vigilance Commission.
Opportunity to work on policy analysis and legal research.
No stipend is usually provided (Honorary Internship).
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate (Final year Law/IT/Management/etc.) or Post Graduate.
Strong academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the prescribed application form via email or post to the CVC office.
required_documents: Recommendation letter from the Head of the Institution.

scheme_name: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to OBC students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. These scholarships shall be available for studies in India only and will be disbursed via Direct Benefit Transfer.
benefits_summary: Provides scholarship for OBC students pursuing studies after Class 10 (Class 11, 12, Degree, Diploma, etc.).
benefits_list: Maintenance allowance (varies by course type, e.g., Hostellers/Day Scholars).
Study tour charges and thesis typing/printing charges.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Applicant must belong to the Other Backward Classes (OBC).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Passed Class 10.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the State Scholarship Portal or National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
required_documents: Mark sheet of the last qualifying exam.

scheme_name: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Pre-matric Scholarship to OBC Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Pre-matric Scholarship to Other Backward Classes for Studies in India is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. The objective is to award scholarships to school-going children of poorer OBC parents.
benefits_summary: Financial support for OBC students studying in Class 1 to 10 to reduce dropout rates.
benefits_list: Scholarship amount of ₹4,000 per annum (spread across academic and admission fees).
Ad-hoc grant of ₹500 per annum for day scholars and hostellers.
Helps parents complete the education of their wards.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Applicant must belong to the OBC category.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Studying in Class 1 to 10 in a Government or Recognized school.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the State Scholarship Portal or National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Centrally-Sponsored Scheme Of Pre-Matric Scholarship For SC Students (Class 9 & 10)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: A scholarship scheme by Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment for regular/ full-time students from Scheduled Castes studying in Class 9th and 10th in a Govt. School / a School recognized by Govt. / CBSE / State Board of Secondary Education.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to SC students in Class 9 and 10 to support their education and prevent dropouts.
benefits_list: Scholarship for Day Scholars: ₹3,500 per annum.
Additional allowance for students with disabilities.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
Studying in Class 9 or 10.
Not receiving any other centrally funded scholarship.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or State Portal.

scheme_name: Coaching Schemes For SC/ST/OBC & Minority Students (Free Coaching Scheme)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / Ministry of Minority Affairs
objective: Coaching schemes for students belonging to SC (Scheduled Caste)/ ST (Scheduled Tribe)/ OBC (Other Backward Classes) (non-creamy layer)/ and minority communities are implemented to provide them with equal opportunities and support in pursuing higher education.
benefits_summary: Provides free high-quality coaching for competitive exams (Civil Services, JEE, NEET, etc.) to disadvantaged students.
benefits_list: 100% Course fees paid directly to the coaching institute.
Monthly stipend of ₹4,000 for local students / ₹9,000 for outstation students.
Covers Group A, B, C exams, and entrance exams for professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Belong to SC/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) or Minority communities.
Annual family income limit applies (e.g., ₹8 Lakhs).
Required education level: Graduate/12th pass (depending on exam).
how_to_apply: Apply online at the coaching.dosje.gov.in portal.

objective: The “Coconut Palm Insurance Scheme (CPIS)” is being implemented by the Coconut Development Board/ Ministry Of Agriculture/ Government of India with the objective of insuring coconut palms against natural calamities/ climatic risks/ pests/ diseases/ and other perils.
benefits_summary: Provides insurance coverage for coconut palms against natural disasters and pests.
benefits_list: Insurance cover for palms in the age group of 4 to 60 years.
Compensation for loss of palms due to storms, floods, pests, etc.
50-75% subsidy on premium payment shared by CDB and State Govt.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Must have at least 5 healthy nut-bearing coconut palms.
Palms should be in the contiguous area.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Agriculture Department/Coconut Development Board with the prescribed application form.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme: Consolidation of Coffee (NER)
objective: The scheme "Consolidation of Coffee" aims to increase the productivity of the existing coffee holdings by gap filling or rejuvenation/ followed by improved cultivation methods such as application of compost/ bush management/ shade management/ pest and disease control/ etc.
benefits_summary: Supports coffee growers in the North Eastern Region (NER) to consolidate existing plantations and improve yield.
benefits_list: Subsidy for gap filling and rejuvenation of coffee plants.
Technical assistance on bush and shade management.
Financial support for inputs like compost.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Must have coffee holdings in the North Eastern Region.
Must have valid land possession certificate.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the local Junior Liaison Officer (JLO) or Senior Liaison Officer (SLO) of the Coffee Board.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme: Expansion of Coffee (NER)
objective: The scheme "Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Expansion of Coffee" by the Coffee Board/ Department of Commerce/ Ministry of Commerce and Industry/ aims to facilitate afforestation in the Jhum lands and help settle the tribals on a permanent footing.
benefits_summary: Encourages expansion of coffee cultivation in North East India to prevent Jhum (shifting) cultivation.
benefits_list: Subsidy for new planting of coffee.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Tribal growers in NER willing to shift from Jhum to permanent coffee cultivation.
Land suitable for coffee.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Coffee Board's regional offices in NER.

objective: The scheme "Quality Upgradation / Certification: Drying Yards" aims to facilitate the improvement of the quality of coffee produced by growers by encouraging them to establish infrastructure for quality enhancement and obtain certification for their coffees/ enabling them to earn better returns.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to construct concrete drying yards to improve coffee quality.
benefits_list: Subsidy for construction of drying yards.
Helps in hygienic drying of coffee beans.
Increases the market value of the coffee.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Coffee grower in NER with a minimum producing area.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the local Coffee Board office.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme: Supply of Baby Pulpers (NER)
objective: The scheme "Supply of Baby Pulpers" aims to facilitate the improvement of the quality of coffee produced by growers by encouraging them to establish infrastructure for quality enhancement and obtain certification for their coffees/ enabling them to earn better returns.
benefits_summary: Subsidizes the cost of Baby Pulpers (machinery) for small growers to process coffee cherries.
benefits_list: Subsidy on the purchase of Baby Pulpers.
Improves quality of the coffee bean.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Small coffee grower in NER.
how_to_apply: Apply to the local Coffee Board Liaison Officer.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme: Eco-Certification / Organic Certification (NER)
objective: The scheme aims to encourage coffee growers to improve quality for higher returns and improve access to high-value specialty markets through the production of eco-certified coffees. It also encourages small growers to form groups/collectives to take up eco-certification.
benefits_summary: Supports growers in obtaining Organic or Eco-certification to fetch premium prices in the market.
benefits_list: Assistance for internal control systems for grower groups.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Growers or Groups of growers in NER.
Must adhere to organic farming standards.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal/application to the Coffee Board.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme: Support for Group Nurseries (NER)
objective: The scheme "Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Support for Group Nurseries" by the Coffee Board/ Department of Commerce/ Ministry of Commerce and Industry/ aims to provide growers with high-quality coffee seedlings to ensure the proper establishment of plantations.
benefits_summary: Provides financial aid to Self Help Groups (SHGs) or grower collectives to set up coffee nurseries.
benefits_list: Subsidy for infrastructure like shade nets and irrigation for nurseries.
Support for input costs (seeds, bags, soil).
Ensures availability of quality planting material locally.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer (Group/SHG).
Must have suitable land and water source for nursery.
how_to_apply: The Group/SHG leader must apply to the Coffee Board office.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Water Augmentation
objective: The scheme "Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Water Augmentation" aims to improve the productivity and quality of coffee by facilitating the creation of infrastructure for water augmentation for irrigation and wet processing of coffee.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance for creating water resources like check dams and tanks to ensure irrigation for coffee plantations in the North East.
benefits_list: Subsidy for construction of water harvesting structures (tanks, check dams).
Support for installing sprinkler irrigation systems.
Ensures water availability during dry spells.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Coffee growers in NER with land ownership/possession rights.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the local Senior Liaison Officer (SLO) or Junior Liaison Officer (JLO) of the Coffee Board.
required_documents: Technical estimate of the water structure.

objective: The scheme "Coir Udyami Yojana" by the Coir Board/ Ministry of MSME/ Government of India/ aims to modernize the coir industry and generate rural employment. It offers credit-linked financial assistance to establish coir units by eligible applicants.
benefits_summary: A credit-linked subsidy scheme for setting up coir manufacturing units.
benefits_list: Maximum project cost admissible is ₹10 Lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Individuals, SHGs, NGOs, Co-operative Societies.
Age: 18 years and above.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Coir Board website or visit the nearest Coir Board Field Office/DIC.

scheme_name: Coir Vikas Yojana- Coir Industry Technology Upgradation Scheme (CITUS)
objective: The scheme “Coir Industry Technology Upgradation Scheme (CITUS)” is a component of “Coir Vikas Yojana” and the scheme was launched by Ministry of Micro/ Small & Medium Enterprises/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance for modernizing existing coir units and setting up new state-of-the-art units.
benefits_list: 25% subsidy on the cost of plant and machinery.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Business Owner.
Existing or new coir units registered with Coir Board.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the CITUS portal on the Coir Board website.

scheme_name: Coir Vikas Yojana- Export Market Promotion Scheme
objective: Coir Board is implementing the Central Sector Scheme “Export Market Promotion Scheme” with a view to improve the export performance of Indian Coir Sector through various export market promotion activities.
benefits_summary: Assists exporters in promoting Indian coir products in international markets.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of space rent for participating in international fairs.
Assistance for publicity and market study tours.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed (Exporter).
Must be a registered exporter with Coir Board (RCMC holder).
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to Coir Board prior to the event and claim reimbursement after participation.

scheme_name: Coir Vikas Yojana-Skill Upgradation and Mahila Coir Yojana
objective: The scheme “Skill Upgradation and Mahila Coir Yojana” is a component of “Coir Vikas Yojana” and the scheme was launched by the Ministry of Micro/ Small & Medium Enterprises/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: A women-oriented scheme that provides training in coir spinning and weaving, along with a stipend and subsidized equipment.
benefits_list: Stipend of ₹3,000 per month during training.
Free motorized ratts/spinning equipment provided after successful training (75% subsidy).
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed.
Age: 18 to 55 years.
how_to_apply: Contact the nearest Coir Board training center or field office.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Collaborative Research Scheme (CRS) has been introduced by the All India Council for Technical Education to support innovation & research across institutes with collaboration among TEQIP Faculty/ Regular faculty of Project institutions (focus and non-focus) & Premier institutions of the country.
benefits_summary: Provides research grants to faculty members of TEQIP institutes to collaborate with premier institutions (IITs/NITs).
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Faculty of TEQIP funded institutions.
Must have a collaborator from a premier institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the AICTE portal when the call for proposals is open.

objective: The Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Scheme was launched to create a world-class infrastructure that will cater to the business needs of local artisans & SMEs in order to boost production and export.
benefits_summary: Develops Mega Handicraft Clusters with world-class infrastructure to boost production and exports.
benefits_list: Hard interventions (Common Facility Centers, Raw material banks).
eligibility_criteria: Clusters with a large concentration of artisans (Mega Clusters).
Implementing Agencies (SPVs, State Govts, NGOs).
Artisans within the cluster.
how_to_apply: Implementing agencies submit Detailed Project Reports (DPR) to the Ministry of Textiles.

scheme_name: Construction of Hostels for OBC Boys and Girls
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The Scheme aims at providing hostel facilities to students belonging to socially and educationally backward classes/ especially from rural areas/ to enable them to pursue secondary and higher education.
benefits_summary: Provides funding to State Governments and Universities to construct hostels for OBC students.
benefits_list: Central assistance for construction costs (60% to 90% depending on region).
Creation of residential facilities for OBC students.
eligibility_criteria: State Governments/UT Administrations.
Central/State Universities.
Beneficiaries: OBC Students.
how_to_apply: Institutions/States must send proposals to the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
required_documents: Plan layout approved by competent authority.

scheme_name: Credit Based Schemes For SC - Term Loan (TL)
issuing_authority: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
objective: A loan scheme by Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for Entrepreneur of Scheduled Caste Category.
benefits_summary: Provides term loans at concessional interest rates to SC entrepreneurs for income-generating activities.
benefits_list: Loan up to 95% of the project cost.
Maximum loan limit varies (up to ₹30 Lakhs usually).
