eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) community.
how_to_apply: Apply through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) or designated banks.

scheme_name: Credit Enhancement Guarantee Scheme For The Scheduled Castes (CEGSSC)
issuing_authority: IFCI Ltd / Ministry of Social Justice
objective: a loan scheme by the Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for Entrepreneurs of Scheduled Caste Category.
benefits_summary: Provides credit guarantees to banks to encourage them to lend to SC entrepreneurs without collateral.
benefits_list: Guarantee cover for loans from ₹15 Lakhs to ₹5 Crore.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Registered Companies/Partnerships/Societies belonging to SCs (at least 51% shareholding).
No default history.
how_to_apply: Approach Member Lending Institutions (Banks) for the loan under CEGSSC. The bank applies to IFCI for the guarantee.

scheme_name: Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for Middle Income Group (PMAY-U)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
objective: Under Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme/ beneficiaries of Middle-Income Group (MIG) can seek housing loans from Banks/ Housing Finance Companies and other such institutions for acquisition/ construction of house (including re-purchase).
benefits_summary: Provides interest subsidy on housing loans for Middle Income Groups (MIG) to buy or build a house. (Note: MIG component ended in March 2021; currently active for EWS/LIG).
benefits_list: Subsidy calculated on loan amount up to ₹9 Lakh or ₹12 Lakh.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income: ₹6-12 Lakh (MIG-I) or ₹12-18 Lakh (MIG-II).
Must not own a pucca house in India.
Aadhaar is mandatory.
how_to_apply: Apply for a housing loan at a Primary Lending Institution (Bank/HFC) and request subsidy under PMAY-CLSS.
required_documents: Affidavit of not owning a pucca house.

scheme_name: Cultural Function And Production Grant Scheme
objective: The scheme “Cultural Function and Production Grant Scheme” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance for organizing seminars, conferences, festivals, and exhibitions related to Indian culture.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹5 Lakhs (can be increased to ₹20 Lakhs in exceptional cases).
Covers expenses for honorarium, travel, boarding, lodging, and staging.
Supports production of new cultural plays/performances.
eligibility_criteria: Registered NGOs/Societies/Trusts working for at least 3 years in art & culture.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Ministry of Culture's scheme portal.

issuing_authority: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: DPIIT has introduced an internship scheme for students pursuing Under Graduate/Post Graduate degrees or Research Scholars enrolled in recognized universities/Institutions within India or abroad. The applicants can choose the period of their choice ranging from one/two/three months.
benefits_summary: Offers students exposure to government functioning and policy formulation in industry and trade.
benefits_list: Practical experience in government policy work.
Certificate of Internship upon successful completion.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Pursuing Graduate (Engineering/Law/Economics/Management) or Postgraduate.
Strong academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DPIIT website when the application window opens.

issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: The umbrella Central Sector Scheme of this Ministry called the "Scheme to Promote Voluntary Action for Persons with Disabilities" was revised w.e.f. 01.04.2003 and was renamed as the "Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)"
benefits_summary: Provides grants to NGOs to run rehabilitation centers, special schools, and vocational training for PwDs.
benefits_list: Grant-in-aid (up to 90% of project cost) for recurring and non-recurring expenses.
Supports projects like Special Schools, Half-Way Homes, and Community Based Rehabilitation.
Funding for staff honorarium and rent.
eligibility_criteria: Registered NGOs functioning for at least 2 years.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (Beneficiaries).
how_to_apply: NGOs must apply online via the e-Anudaan portal.

scheme_name: Deen Dayal SPARSH Yojana (Scholarship for Philately)
objective: Deen Dayal SPARSH Yojana awards annual scholarships to students who have good academic records and are also pursuing 'Philately' as a hobby. The objective is to “Promote Philately among children at a young age in a sustainable manner that can reinforce and supplement the academic curriculum.
benefits_summary: A scholarship scheme to promote the hobby of stamp collecting (Philately) among school children.
benefits_list: Scholarship amount: ₹6,000 per annum (₹500 per month).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Studying in Class 6 to 9.
Must have a good academic record (min 60% marks).
Must have a Philately Deposit Account.
how_to_apply: Apply through the school or local Head Post Office. A selection quiz/project work is usually conducted.

scheme_name: Deen Dayal Upadhyay Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
objective: DDU-GKY focuses on the rural poor youth and its emphasis on sustainable employment through the prominence and incentives given to post-placement tracking/ retention/ and career progression. DDU-GKY is designed to provide high-quality skill training opportunities to the rural poor.
benefits_summary: A placement-linked skill development scheme for rural poor youth.
benefits_list: Free skill training in various trades.
Free uniform, course material, and tablet.
Reimbursement of travel and boarding costs (for residential courses).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 15 and 35 years (up to 45 for women/SC/ST/PwD).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
Must belong to a rural poor family (BPL/MGNREGA worker household/RSBY card holder).
how_to_apply: Register via the Kaushal Panjee portal or visit the nearest DDU-GKY training center.
required_documents: BPL Card / MGNREGA Card / RSBY Card.

scheme_name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
objective: DAY-NRLM is the flagship program of MoRD for promoting poverty reduction through building strong institutions for the poor/ particularly women/ and enabling these institutions to access a range of financial services and livelihoods. States are enabled to formulate their poverty reduction plans.
benefits_summary: Mobilizes rural poor women into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and provides financial and livelihood support.
benefits_list: Community Investment Fund (CIF) up to ₹2.5 Lakh per SHG.
Interest subvention on bank loans (loans available at 7% or 4%).
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Must belong to a rural poor household (SECC data).
Must join a Self Help Group (SHG).
how_to_apply: Contact the local Gram Panchayat or NRLM Block Office to join an SHG.

scheme_name: Design Scheme Under MSME Innovative Scheme
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: Design scheme will help MSMEs to avail advice on all aspects of design. It helps MSMEs realize and achieve their design-related objectives. This specialist advice will be provided by experienced designers for new product development as well as enhancing existing product portfolio.
benefits_summary: Provides grants to MSMEs to hire expert designers for new product development or improvement.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹40 Lakhs (75% of project cost) for design projects.
Student design projects funded up to ₹1.5 Lakhs.
Improves product aesthetics, ergonomics, and value.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Business Owner.
Registered MSMEs with Udyam Registration.
Must have a valid design project proposal.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the MSME Innovative Scheme portal.

scheme_name: Development Support for Coffee: Farmers Producer Organizations (FPOs)
objective: To provide broad-based supportive ecosystem to facilitate development of vibrant/ socially and economically sustainable coffee communities.
benefits_summary: Encourages coffee growers to form FPOs to gain collective bargaining power and better market access.
benefits_list: Financial support for FPO registration and infrastructure.
Support for creating processing and marketing facilities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Group of coffee growers forming an FPO.
how_to_apply: Apply to the Coffee Board with the FPO registration details and project plan.

scheme_name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Eco-certification
objective: The scheme encourages coffee growers to access high-value speciality markets through the production of eco-certified coffees to realize higher returns by adhering to a set of standards that help in the communication of the various conditions surrounding the production of coffee.
benefits_summary: Subsidizes the cost of obtaining eco-certification (e.g., Rainforest Alliance, UTZ) for coffee growers in traditional areas.
benefits_list: Support for maintaining internal control systems.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Growers in traditional coffee areas (Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu).
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Coffee Board Liaison Officer.

scheme_name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Replantation
objective: The objective of the scheme "Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Replantation" is to improve the production and productivity of coffee plantations by encouraging replantation of old/senile and unproductive plantations with high-yielding/ disease-tolerant coffee varieties.
benefits_summary: Provides subsidy for uprooting old, unproductive coffee plants and replanting them with high-yielding varieties.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Must own coffee plantation with old/senile plants.
how_to_apply: Apply to the Senior/Junior Liaison Officer of the Coffee Board.

scheme_name: Development Support for Coffee: Support to Small Growers' Collectives/Marketing
objective: The objective of the scheme is to extend support to small and tiny growers/ incentivizing them to form collectives/ Self Help Groups/ Cooperatives/ thus encouraging them to take up marketing of coffee produced on a community-based approach through suitable financial incentives.
benefits_summary: Incentivizes small growers to market their coffee collectively to reduce costs and increase bargaining power.
benefits_list: Financial incentives (e.g., per kg premium) for coffee marketed through collectives.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Small growers organized into SHGs or Collectives.
how_to_apply: The Collective/SHG submits the claim to the Coffee Board.

scheme_name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Water Augmentation
objective: The scheme "Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Water Augmentation" by the Coffee Board/ Department of Commerce/ aims to improve the production and productivity of coffee plantations by encouraging water augmentation in traditional areas.
benefits_summary: Subsidy for creating irrigation infrastructure in traditional coffee growing areas (South India).
benefits_list: Subsidy for water tanks and check dams.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Coffee growers in Karnataka, Kerala, or Tamil Nadu.
how_to_apply: Apply to the local Coffee Board office.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
objective: An opportunity for a student to secure practical work experience under the guidance of a qualified and experienced Supervisor/Mentor in Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) in the form of Digital India Internship Scheme.
benefits_summary: Provides students with an opportunity to work on Digital India projects and gain government experience.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: B.E./B.Tech (2nd/3rd year) or M.E./M.Tech/M.Sc (1st year).
Minimum 60% marks in the last degree/semester.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the MeitY internship portal (usually opens twice a year).

scheme_name: Disha - Early Intervention and School Readiness Scheme
issuing_authority: The National Trust (Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment)
objective: This scheme aims at setting up Disha Centres for early intervention for Person with Disability (PwD) in 0-10 years of age covered under the National Trust Act/ through therapies/ trainings and providing support to family members.
benefits_summary: Provides early intervention support, therapies, and school readiness training for children with developmental disabilities.
benefits_list: Day care facilities for at least 4 hours a day.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 0 and 10 years.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Intellectual Disability, Multiple Disabilities).
Must be registered with the National Trust.
how_to_apply: Enroll at a registered 'Disha' centre run by an NGO (Registered Organization of National Trust).
required_documents: BPL card (if applicable for fee waiver).

issuing_authority: Department of Personnel and Training (DoPT), Government of India
objective: The scheme "Domestic Funding of Foreign Training (DFFT)" is a scheme introduced by the Department of Personnel and Training/ Government of India in the year 2001-02 with an objective to upgrade the knowledge & skills of Government officers.
benefits_summary: The Domestic Funding of Foreign Training (DFFT) scheme aims to upgrade the knowledge and skills of Government officers by providing financial support for their specialized foreign training programs.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for attending foreign training programs.
Upgradation of knowledge and skills of government officers.
Exposure to international best practices and advanced methodologies.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Government-employed.
how_to_apply: Ministries/Departments identify suitable officers and relevant foreign training programs. Proposals for training are prepared by the respective Ministries/Departments and forwarded to the Department of Personnel & Training (DoPT) for approval and funding. The application process is governed by specific guidelines and proformas issued by DoPT.
required_documents: Nomination letter from the concerned Ministry/Department.
Detailed proposal of the foreign training program (course content, duration, institution details).
Justification for the proposed training and its relevance to the officer's duties.
Cost estimates for the training (tuition fees, travel, accommodation, etc.).
Officer's service profile and experience details.
Any other documents specified in the latest DoPT guidelines/proformas.

scheme_name: Dr Ambedkar Central Sector Scheme of Interest Subsidy on Educational Loans for Overseas Studies for OBCs and EBCs
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: The scheme provides interest subsidy to the students belonging to the OBCs and EBCs on the interest payable for the period of the moratorium for the Education Loans for overseas studies to pursue approved courses of studies abroad at Masters/ M.Phil. and PhD level.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides full interest subsidy on educational loans for overseas Masters, M.Phil., and PhD studies for eligible Other Backward Classes (OBC) and Economically Backward Classes (EBC) students during the moratorium period, which includes the study period and one year post-completion or six months after securing a job, whichever is earlier.
benefits_list: Full interest subsidy on educational loans for overseas studies during the moratorium period.
Moratorium period covers the course duration plus one year, or six months after securing employment, whichever is earlier.
Applicable for students pursuing Masters, M.Phil., and PhD level courses abroad.
Benefits students belonging to OBC and EBC categories with a parental annual income ceiling of Rs. 8.00 Lakh.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: OBC/SC/ST.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Students apply for an education loan from any scheduled bank. The bank then identifies eligible students based on the scheme's criteria (OBC/EBC, income, course level abroad). The interest subsidy claim is processed by the lending bank and submitted to Canara Bank, the Nodal Bank for the scheme, which then disburses the subsidy amount to the lending banks.
required_documents: Admission letter from the overseas institution for an approved Masters/M.Phil./PhD course
Educational loan sanction letter from a scheduled bank
Academic mark sheets and degree certificates
Self-declaration/undertaking regarding non-receipt of other similar subsidies

scheme_name: Dr. Ambedakar Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarships for the Economically Backward Class (EBC) Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to the Economically Backward Class (EBC) students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. These scholarships shall be available for studies in India only.
benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial assistance to Economically Backward Class (EBC) students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education within India, enabling them to complete their studies.
benefits_list: Maintenance allowance (varying rates for hostellers and day scholars)
Thesis typing/printing charges for research scholars
Book allowance for students of correspondence courses
Additional allowances for students with disabilities
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹250,000.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: OBC/SC/ST.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Secondary.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) by registering, filling out the application form with personal and academic details, uploading the required documents, and finally submitting the application. The application will then undergo verification by the educational institution and state/central authorities.
required_documents: Income Certificate issued by a competent authority (proving EBC status)
Bank Passbook (account should be linked with Aadhaar)
Previous academic mark sheets or certificates
Current year admission proof or fee receipt

scheme_name: Dr. Ambedkar Foundation Essay Competition Scheme for Class 10th-12th & Colleges/Universities/Institutions Students in 22 Indian Languages and English
issuing_authority: Dr. Ambedkar Foundation, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: The scheme aims to encourage school/ college & university students of 10th and 12th standards to write on the contribution of Babasaheb/ particularly on his thoughts on social justice/ empowerment of the depressed section/ removal of caste-based discriminations/ national integration/ etc.
benefits_summary: The scheme offers significant cash prizes and certificates to school and college students who write essays on the life and contributions of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, particularly focusing on his thoughts on social justice, empowerment, and national integration.
benefits_list: Cash prize of Rs. 1,00,000 for 1st place in the College/University category
Cash prize of Rs. 60,000 for 2nd place in the College/University category
Cash prize of Rs. 40,000 for 3rd place in the College/University category
Cash prize of Rs. 50,000 for 1st place in the School (10th-12th) category
Cash prize of Rs. 30,000 for 2nd place in the School (10th-12th) category
Cash prize of Rs. 20,000 for 3rd place in the School (10th-12th) category
Five consolation prizes of Rs. 10,000 each for both College/University and School categories
Certificates of Appreciation to all prize winners
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
how_to_apply: Students must write an essay on specified themes related to Dr. B.R. Ambedkar in one of the 22 Indian languages or English. The essays should adhere to word limits and submission guidelines announced in the official notification. Applications are typically submitted online through the designated portal on the Dr. Ambedkar Foundation website or through respective educational institutions as per the competition's specific instructions.
required_documents: Original essay written on the given theme
Proof of enrollment in a recognized educational institution (School/College ID Card or certificate)
Student's personal details (name, address, contact information)
Declaration of originality of the essay

scheme_name: Dr. D.S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellowships In Sciences
objective: Dr. D.S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellowships in Sciences was introduced by the UGC. In addition to the existing RA/PDF positions provided by various funding agencies/ a need was felt for a PDF scheme running in a flexible mode/ with fast track/ online handling/ and decision-making.
benefits_summary: The Dr. D.S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellowships in Sciences scheme provides financial assistance to highly motivated Ph.D. scholars in Sciences for advanced research, including a monthly fellowship, an annual contingency grant, and House Rent Allowance (HRA), for a maximum duration of three years.
benefits_list: Fellowship: Rs. 46,500/- p.m. for the 1st year
Fellowship: Rs. 48,500/- p.m. for the 2nd and 3rd years
House Rent Allowance (HRA): As per UGC norms for the category of city/town
Medical facilities: As per the rules of the host institution
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Applications are typically invited online through a dedicated portal. The process generally involves: registration on the online portal, filling in personal and academic details, uploading necessary documents, submission of a detailed research proposal, obtaining a consent letter from the prospective research supervisor, and a consent letter from the Head of the Host Institution, followed by final submission of the online application form.
required_documents: Mark sheets/Certificates of all qualifying examinations
Consent Letter from the prospective Research Supervisor
Consent Letter from the Head of the Host Institution
Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC - Non-Creamy Layer, if applicable)

scheme_name: Dr. D.S. Kothari Research Grant For Newly Recruited Faculty Members
objective: The scheme "Dr. D.S. Kothari Research Grant for Newly Recruited Faculty Members" is a research grant scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC)/ Department of Higher Education (DoHE). The purpose of the research grant is to provide opportunities to regularly appointed faculty members.
benefits_summary: The Dr. D.S. Kothari Research Grant provides financial assistance of INR 10 Lakh for a period of two years to newly recruited regular faculty members in Indian universities and colleges, enabling them to undertake high-quality research projects.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of INR 10 Lakh for a period of two years to support research.
Grant covers research expenses such as equipment, consumables, travel, books, journals, and research assistance.
Opportunity to initiate and pursue independent research projects for newly recruited faculty.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Government-employed.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Applicants need to visit the official UGC website (ugc.ac.in) and navigate to the relevant scheme section. They must register/login to the online application portal, fill out the detailed application form, upload their research proposal along with all required documents, and ensure the application is endorsed by the Head of their Institution. The complete application must be submitted online before the specified deadline.
required_documents: Appointment letter as a regular faculty member.
Letter of undertaking/endorsement from the Head of the Institution.
Ethical clearance certificate (if applicable for research involving human/animal subjects).

scheme_name: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan UGC Post-Doctoral Fellowship
objective: The scheme "Dr. S. Radhakrishnan UGC Post-Doctoral Fellowship" is a fellowship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC)/ Department of Higher Education (DoHE). Post-doctoral research is an important enabling step in grooming young researchers for launching an academic/research career.
benefits_summary: The Dr. S. Radhakrishnan UGC Post-Doctoral Fellowship scheme provides financial assistance to unemployed Ph.D. holders to conduct advanced research and establish themselves as independent researchers or academicians. It aims to groom young researchers for an academic or research career.
benefits_list: Fellowship amount of Rs. 50,000/- per month for 2 years (non-extendable)
Contingency grant of Rs. 50,000/- per annum
Escorts/Reader assistance of Rs. 2,000/- per month for physically disabled candidates
Maternity/Paternity leave as per Government of India norms
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates typically apply online through the official UGC or National Testing Agency (NTA) portal when applications are invited. The process involves registering on the portal, filling out the detailed application form with personal, academic, and research proposal information, uploading scanned copies of all required documents, and submitting the application.
required_documents: Consent letter from the host institution/supervisor

scheme_name: E-YUVA Scheme: BIRAC’s E-YUVA Fellows (for under graduate students)
issuing_authority: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) under the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India
objective: BIRAC’s E-YUVA Fellows shall apply as a team and pursue their research work at their College/University labs and will also be allowed access to facilities at EYC and EYC Knowledge Partners as and when required.
benefits_summary: The E-YUVA Scheme provides undergraduate students with financial assistance, mentorship, and access to research facilities to encourage innovative biotechnology-related research and entrepreneurship at an early stage.
benefits_list: Opportunity to pursue innovative research work in college/university labs
Mentorship and guidance from experienced faculty and experts
Access to specialized facilities at E-YUVA Centers (EYCs) and their Knowledge Partners
Exposure to the biotechnology research and innovation ecosystem
Support for developing an entrepreneurial mindset
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Applications are typically invited through specific calls for proposals announced on the BIRAC website. Interested teams of undergraduate students, accompanied by a faculty mentor, must submit a detailed research proposal through the BIRAC online application portal. The application process generally involves registration, submission of proposal details, and uploading all required supporting documents.
required_documents: Detailed research proposal outlining the project objectives, methodology, and expected outcomes
Curriculum Vitae (CV) of all student team members and the supervising faculty mentor
Academic transcripts or records of the undergraduate students
Letter of support or No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the head of the host institution/university
Proof of identity for all applicants
Relevant ethical clearances, if applicable to the research project

scheme_name: E-YUVA Scheme: BIRAC’s Innovation Fellows (for post graduates and above)
issuing_authority: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), Department of Biotechnology, Government of India
objective: BIRAC’s Innovation fellows shall apply as an individual and are required to work full time at EYC. BIRAC’s Innovation Fellows are expected to initiate biotech startups. Hence/ only proposals with potential to commercialize innovative products and technologies will be considered for support.
benefits_summary: The E-YUVA Scheme by BIRAC supports post-graduates and above with innovative ideas in biotechnology to become 'BIRAC Innovation Fellows'. It provides financial assistance, mentorship, and incubation support at e-YUVA Centres (EYCs) to help them develop innovative products/technologies with commercial potential and establish biotech startups.
benefits_list: Seed funding/grant for proof-of-concept and prototype development.
Access to state-of-the-art incubation facilities and infrastructure at designated e-YUVA Centres (EYCs).
Mentorship from industry experts, scientists, and entrepreneurs.
Technical and business guidance for product development, intellectual property, and commercialization.
Opportunity to establish a biotech startup based on the developed innovation.
Networking opportunities with investors, industry leaders, and scientific community.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 18 and 35 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: 1. Identify an e-YUVA Centre (EYC) recognized by BIRAC where you wish to incubate your idea.2. Prepare a detailed proposal outlining your innovative idea, its commercial potential, technical feasibility, and a clear execution plan.3. Submit the application online through the BIRAC portal or the specific EYC's application portal when the call for proposals is open.4. Applications are evaluated by experts, followed by presentations and interviews for shortlisted candidates.5. Successful candidates are selected as BIRAC Innovation Fellows and provided support at the chosen EYC.
required_documents: Detailed project proposal/business plan outlining the innovation and its commercialization strategy.
Comprehensive Curriculum Vitae (CV) of the applicant.
Proof of academic qualifications (Master's degree or higher in relevant life sciences/biotechnology disciplines).
Any letters of support or recommendation (if applicable).
Declaration forms and other documents as required by BIRAC or the specific e-YUVA Centre.

issuing_authority: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) under Department of Biotechnology, Government of India
objective: E-YUVA Centers are housed within the University/Institute set-up. It acts as an anchor and extends requisite support and mentoring to students. Each EYC has a BIRAC-supported pre-incubation space which offers infrastructure and equipment for basic research and experimentation by selected students.
benefits_summary: The E-YUVA scheme establishes pre-incubation centers in universities/institutes to provide infrastructure, equipment, mentoring, and financial support to students for applied research and innovative projects in biotechnology.
benefits_list: Access to BIRAC-supported pre-incubation space with infrastructure and equipment for basic research and experimentation.
Mentoring and guidance from experienced professionals and faculty.
Financial support in the form of a monthly stipend for selected student innovators.
Opportunity to translate innovative ideas into applied research and viable projects with societal or industry relevance.
Promotion of an entrepreneurial mindset and culture of innovation among students.
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Students interested in the scheme must apply to an E-YUVA Center (EYC) located within their university or institute. The application process typically involves submitting a research proposal or innovative idea, followed by a selection process conducted by the specific EYC. Institutions interested in hosting an EYC can apply through BIRAC's call for proposals.
required_documents: Project proposal outlining the innovative idea or research plan
Any other specific documents requested by the respective E-YUVA Center during their application call

issuing_authority: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: A scheme named Education Loan Scheme by National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) under the M/o Social Justice and Empowerment provides loans to students from Scheduled Castes who are pursuing full-time professional or technical courses in recognized institutions.
benefits_summary: The Education Loan Scheme by NSFDC provides financial assistance in the form of term loans to eligible Scheduled Caste students for pursuing full-time professional or technical courses in recognized institutions, both in India and abroad, with subsidized interest rates.
benefits_list: Loan amount up to Rs. 15.00 lakhs for studies in India and up to Rs. 30.00 lakhs for studies abroad.
Interest rate of 4% per annum for male beneficiaries and 3.5% per annum for female beneficiaries, with a rebate for timely repayment.
Repayment period of up to 10 years, commencing after a moratorium period (course period + 1 year or 6 months after getting a job, whichever is earlier).
Coverage for tuition fees, examination fees, library/laboratory fees, purchase of books, equipment, and other reasonable educational expenses.
Available for students belonging to Scheduled Castes with an annual family income not exceeding Rs. 3.00 lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
how_to_apply: Eligible Scheduled Caste students must apply through the designated State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) appointed by NSFDC in their respective state/union territory. Applicants should contact their local SCA to obtain the application form, fill it out completely, and submit it along with all necessary documents for verification and processing. The SCA will scrutinize the application and recommend eligible cases to NSFDC for sanction.
required_documents: Proof of Admission to the professional/technical course (Admission letter from the institution)
Educational qualification certificates (mark sheets and passing certificates of previous examinations)
Identity Proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card, Passport)
Bank account details of the applicant
Surety/Guarantor documents (if required by the SCA)
Affidavit stating that no similar loan has been availed from other financial institutions

issuing_authority: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India
objective: The scheme implemented by the National Scheduled Castes Finance & Development Corporation/ Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment/ Govt. of India. Under this Scheme/ loan is provided for pursuing full-time Professional/Technical courses in recognized institutions as one time assistance.
benefits_summary: The Education Loan Scheme by NSFDC provides financial assistance to eligible Scheduled Caste students to pursue full-time Professional and Technical courses in India and abroad, covering various academic and living expenses at low-interest rates.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for Scheduled Caste students pursuing full-time Professional/Technical courses.
Covers expenses such as tuition fees, examination fees, library/laboratory fees, purchase of books, equipment, instruments, and uniforms.
Includes caution deposit, building fund, and refundable deposits (up to 10% of tuition fees).
For studies abroad, it covers travel expenses and purchase of a computer at a reasonable cost.
Maximum loan limit of Rs. 15.00 Lakhs for studies in India.
Maximum loan limit of Rs. 30.00 Lakhs for studies abroad.
Low annual interest rate: 3% for male beneficiaries and 2% for female beneficiaries.
Repayment period of up to 5 years after the completion of the course, with an additional 6 months of moratorium period.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹300,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: 1. Contact the State Channelizing Agency (SCA) of NSFDC in your respective State/UT.2. Obtain the application form and detailed scheme guidelines from the SCA.3. Fill out the application form accurately and attach all necessary documents.4. Submit the completed application form along with the required documents to the SCA.5. The SCA will process the application and forward it to NSFDC for sanction and disbursement.
required_documents: Academic mark sheets and certificates of qualifying examinations
Proof of admission to the Professional/Technical course (admission letter)
Detailed fee structure from the educational institution
Identity proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport)
Address proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Utility Bill)
For studies abroad: Passport, Visa, and acceptance letter from the foreign institution
Any other documents or security as required by the State Channelizing Agency (SCA)

objective: A fellowship scheme by University Grants Commission to provide an opportunity to superannuated teachers to pursue active research in their respective field of specialization in Indian Universities/Colleges/ Institutions.
benefits_summary: The Emeritus Fellowship provides superannuated teachers an opportunity to actively pursue research in their specialized field at Indian Universities, Colleges, or Institutions, offering financial support and a research tenure.
benefits_list: Contingency Grant of Rs. 50,000/- per annum
Fellowship tenure for a period of two years
Opportunity to continue active research after superannuation
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Applications are typically invited through an official notification or advertisement on the UGC website. Eligible superannuated teachers must submit their application, along with a detailed research proposal and all required documents, through their host institution. Applications are then screened and selected by an Expert Committee constituted by UGC.
required_documents: Endorsement/No Objection Certificate from the host institution

issuing_authority: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO), Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India
objective: The scheme ensures retirement security for employees covered under the EPF Act/ offering monthly pensions/ family pensions/ and withdrawal benefits. It targets workers in organised sectors/ providing financial support post-retirement/ during disability/ or to dependents after the member’s death.
benefits_summary: The Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) offers retirement security by providing monthly pensions to eligible employees post-superannuation or due to permanent disablement. It also extends financial support to families in the form of widow/widower, children, and orphan pensions upon the member's demise, along with withdrawal benefits for short service members.
benefits_list: Monthly pension for life after attaining 58 years of age (superannuation pension).
Widow/Widower pension for life or until remarriage.
Children pension for up to two children until 25 years of age.
Orphan pension if both parents are deceased.
Nominee pension for designated nominees in specific cases.
Withdrawal benefit for members with less than 10 years of eligible service who do not opt for a Scheme Certificate.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Private-employed.
how_to_apply: Employees covered under the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act are automatically enrolled in EPS through their employer's contributions. There is no separate application for enrollment. To claim pension benefits, members or their nominees must submit the appropriate application form (e.g., Form 10D for monthly pension, Form 10C for withdrawal benefit/scheme certificate) to the EPFO, either online through the 'Unified Member Portal' or offline through their employer, along with the necessary supporting documents.
required_documents: Guardianship Certificate (if applicable for minor beneficiaries)

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India
objective: The O/o DC-MSME under "Development of MSMEs" vertical has launched the "Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programme (ESDP)". The Programme is being organized regularly to nurture the talent of youth by enlightening them on various aspects of industrial/business activity required for setting up
benefits_summary: The Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programme (ESDP) aims to cultivate entrepreneurial skills among youth, educate them on various aspects of industrial and business activities, and provide guidance for setting up their own enterprises, thereby promoting self-employment.
benefits_list: Skill development training in various trades.
Guidance on identifying business opportunities and market surveys.
Information on government policies and schemes for MSMEs.
Facilitation of interaction with financial institutions and support agencies.
Development of an entrepreneurial mindset and managerial capabilities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Required education level: Secondary.
how_to_apply: Interested individuals can apply for the program through the respective MSME-Development Institutes (MSME-DIs) or partnering training organizations when specific program batches are announced. Application forms are usually available online on the MSME portal or at the institutes, requiring submission of necessary documents and adherence to eligibility criteria.
required_documents: Identity Proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card)
Address Proof (e.g., Utility Bill, Aadhaar Card)

scheme_name: Establishment of Entrepreneur for Breed Development in Small Ruminant Sector (Sheep and Goat Farming)
issuing_authority: Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India
objective: The scheme for the "Establishment of Entrepreneurs for Breed Development in the Small Ruminant Sector (Sheep and Goat Farming)" falls under the Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry. The Entrepreneurs / Eligible Entities can establish sheep and goat breeding unit.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides a 50% capital subsidy to entrepreneurs for establishing sheep and goat breeding units, aiming to promote breed improvement, increase production of mutton, chevon, and milk, and generate rural employment opportunities.
benefits_list: 50% capital subsidy on the total project cost (excluding land), up to a maximum of Rs. 50 lakh, for establishing sheep/goat breeding units.
Promotion of breed improvement and genetic upgradation in the small ruminant sector.
Encouragement of entrepreneurship and self-employment in livestock farming.
Enhancement of mutton, chevon, and milk production.
Creation of livelihood opportunities in rural areas.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: 1. Visit the National Livestock Mission (NLM) portal (nlm.udyamimitra.in).2. Register as an applicant and create a login.3. Prepare a detailed project report (DPR) for the proposed sheep/goat breeding unit.4. Fill out the online application form, uploading the DPR and all necessary supporting documents.5. Submit the application through the portal.6. The application will be appraised by the financial institution (bank) and the implementing agency.7. Upon approval, the bank will sanction the loan, and the subsidy will be released as per the scheme guidelines.
required_documents: Land ownership/lease documents for the unit location
Quotations for animals, equipment, and civil works
Business registration certificates (for FPOs, SHGs, Companies, etc.)

scheme_name: Establishment of Regional Semen Production Laboratory and Semen Bank for sheep and goat
issuing_authority: Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying (DAHD), Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India
objective: Under the Establishment of Regional Semen Production Laboratory and Semen Bank for Sheep and Goat Scheme/ the Central Government will provide assistance for the establishment of frozen semen production laboratories for goats and liquid semen production laboratories for sheep at the regional level.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance for the establishment of regional semen production laboratories and semen banks for sheep and goats, aiming to improve breeding infrastructure, enhance the genetic potential of small ruminants, and boost livestock productivity.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for establishing frozen semen production laboratories for goats.
Financial assistance for establishing liquid semen production laboratories for sheep.
Development and maintenance of regional semen banks for sheep and goats.
Improvement of genetic quality and productivity of sheep and goat breeds.
Facilitation of artificial insemination (AI) services for small ruminants.
Increased availability of high-quality germplasm to farmers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: State Governments/Union Territory Administrations, State Livestock Development Agencies, or other eligible implementing agencies need to submit detailed project reports (DPRs) and proposals to the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying (DAHD) outlining the technical, financial, and operational aspects of establishing the laboratories and semen banks. Specific guidelines and application formats are typically available through the DAHD website or relevant state animal husbandry departments.
required_documents: Detailed Project Report (DPR) including technical specifications, financial projections, and implementation plan.
Proof of land availability for the establishment of the laboratory and semen bank.
No-objection Certificates (NOCs) and approvals from relevant state authorities.
Detailed budget estimates and financial viability assessment.
Manpower details, including qualified technical personnel.
Undertakings from the implementing agency regarding adherence to guidelines.
Detailed design and layout plan of the proposed facility.

issuing_authority: Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT), Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India
objective: The objective of the EPCG Scheme is to facilitate import of capital goods for producing quality goods and services and enhance India’s manufacturing competitiveness. EPCG scheme allows import of capital goods for pre-production/ production and post-production at zero customs duty.
benefits_summary: The Export Promotion Capital Goods (EPCG) Scheme allows import of capital goods at zero customs duty for producing quality goods and services, aiming to enhance India's manufacturing competitiveness and export capabilities, subject to an export obligation.
benefits_list: Import of capital goods for pre-production, production, and post-production at zero customs duty.
Facilitates the production of quality goods and services.
Enhances India's manufacturing competitiveness in the global market.
Allows import of spares, jigs, fixtures, dies and tools up to 10% of the CIF value of imported capital goods.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: 1. Obtain an Importer-Exporter Code (IEC) if not already held. 2. Register on the official DGFT website (dgft.gov.in). 3. Submit an online application for EPCG Authorization (Application Form ANF 5A) through the DGFT portal. 4. Upload all required supporting documents. 5. Once the application is approved, an EPCG Authorization is issued. 6. Fulfill the specified export obligation within the prescribed period to avail full benefits.
required_documents: PAN Card of the applicant entity
Company Registration Documents (e.g., Memorandum & Articles of Association, Partnership Deed)
Proforma Invoice or Purchase Order of the capital goods to be imported

scheme_name: Export Promotion Council Membership Reimbursement Scheme for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India, implemented through National Small Industries Corporation (NSIC) under the National SC/ST Hub (NSSH).
objective: Export Promotion Council Membership Reimbursement Scheme provides  reimbursement to an SC/ST Micro & small enterprises(MSEs)/ on annual membership subscription fee / one-time subscription charges/entry fee charged by various Export Promotion Councils (EPC).
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial reimbursement to Micro and Small Enterprises owned by Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tribes for their annual membership subscription fees, one-time subscription charges, or entry fees paid to various Export Promotion Councils (EPCs), thereby encouraging their participation in export activities.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of annual membership subscription fee paid to Export Promotion Councils.
Reimbursement of one-time subscription charges paid to Export Promotion Councils.
Reimbursement of entry fees charged by Export Promotion Councils.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Eligible SC/ST MSEs need to apply online through the National SC/ST Hub (NSSH) portal managed by NSIC or through designated MSME Development Institutes. The application process typically involves filling out a prescribed form, uploading required documents, and submitting the claim for reimbursement with proof of payment.
required_documents: Caste certificate of the proprietor/partners/directors (proving SC/ST status)
Membership certificate from the relevant Export Promotion Council (EPC)
Proof of payment for the EPC membership fee (e.g., receipt, bank statement)
Bank details of the enterprise (e.g., cancelled cheque, bank passbook front page)

objective: The "Fellowship for Superannuated Faculty Members" scheme is a fellowship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC)/ Department of Higher Education to provide opportunities to superannuated faculty members of Universities/Institutes/Colleges/ hereinafter “Institutions” to pursue research.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance and an academic environment for superannuated faculty members to continue high-quality research and teaching, leveraging their vast experience and knowledge after retirement.
benefits_list: Contingency Grant of Rs. 50,000/- per annum.
Tenure of the fellowship is for 2 years.
No House Rent Allowance (HRA) is admissible.
Opportunity to continue research work at the parent/host institution.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 67 and 70 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired.
Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: 1. Applications are invited through an advertisement published on the UGC website.2. Eligible candidates must apply online through the designated UGC portal within the specified timeline.3. Applications are screened and evaluated by an Expert Committee.4. Selection is based on the recommendations of the Expert Committee and approval by the Commission.5. Selected candidates are informed and required to submit an acceptance letter along with other necessary undertakings.
required_documents: Certificate of Superannuation/Retirement from the institution
Consent Letter/No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Vice Chancellor/Principal of the host institution
Curriculum Vitae (CV) including academic achievements and publication list

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Allied Cultural Activities
issuing_authority: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
objective: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Allied Cultural Activities” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial support to organizations and individuals for promoting Indian art and culture through various allied cultural activities such as festivals, seminars, research, and publications.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for organizing cultural festivals and events.
Grants for conducting seminars, conferences, and workshops on art and culture.
Support for research projects in diverse fields of Indian culture and heritage.
Funding for the publication of books, journals, and educational materials related to art and culture.
Assistance for other activities contributing to the preservation and promotion of Indian cultural heritage.
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Applicants must visit the Ministry of Culture's official website or designated portal, download the scheme guidelines and application form, complete the form with all required details including project proposal and budget, attach all necessary supporting documents, and submit the complete application to the specified authority by the stated deadline. Applications are typically evaluated by an expert committee.
required_documents: Detailed project proposal outlining objectives, methodology, and expected outcomes.
Comprehensive budget estimates for the proposed cultural activity.
Registration certificate and Memorandum of Association for organizations (if applicable).
Audited financial statements for the past three years for applying organizations.
Curriculum Vitae or profile of the applicant(s) or key personnel involved.
Proof of bank account details (e.g., cancelled cheque or bank statement).
Undertakings and affidavits as per scheme guidelines.
Supporting documents demonstrating prior experience or work in the cultural field.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Cultural Activities In Performing Arts For Building Grants Including Studio Theatres
issuing_authority: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
objective: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Cultural Activities in Performing Arts for Building Grants Including Studio Theatres” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible cultural organizations for the construction of new buildings or the repair, renovation, and upgradation of existing infrastructure, including studio theatres, to facilitate performing arts activities and promote cultural development.
benefits_list: Financial support for construction of new buildings for cultural activities.
Grants for renovation and repair of existing cultural infrastructure.
Assistance for establishing and upgrading studio theatres.
Promotion of performing arts through improved facilities.
Support for organizations engaged in cultural activities.
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Organizations must submit applications in the prescribed format, along with a detailed project proposal and all required documents, to the Ministry of Culture. Applications are invited through advertisements in leading newspapers and on the Ministry's official website. An expert committee scrutinizes the applications and recommends eligible projects for grants.
required_documents: Registration certificate of the organization (e.g., under Societies Registration Act, Trust Act).
Proof of land ownership or long-term lease.
Detailed Project Report (DPR) with architectural drawings and cost estimates.
Audited accounts for the last three years.
Annual reports for the last three years.
Utilisation Certificates for previous grants (if any).
An affidavit stating non-receipt of funds for the same project from other government sources.
Details of the Governing Body/Executive Committee.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Development Of Buddhist/Tibetan Arts And Culture
issuing_authority: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
objective: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Development of Buddhist/Tibetan Arts and Culture” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India to provide financial assistance to voluntary Buddhist/Tibetan organizations including Monasteries.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to voluntary Buddhist/Tibetan organizations, including Monasteries, for the propagation and scientific development of Buddhist/Tibetan culture and tradition, and research in related fields.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for employment of scholars
Financial assistance for purchase of books
Financial assistance for construction of libraries/archives
Financial assistance for establishment of institutes
Financial assistance for maintenance of monastic institutions
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Organizations/institutions seeking financial assistance must submit their applications in the prescribed format. Applications are submitted through the concerned State Government/UT Administration where the organization's registered office is located, or through the concerned administrative Ministry for Central Government/Autonomous Bodies. Applications are accepted throughout the year.
required_documents: Audited accounts for the last three years
Annual Reports for the last three years
Recommendation of the concerned State Government/UT Administration or concerned administrative Ministry (for Central Government/Autonomous Bodies)
Details of the proposed project/activities for which financial assistance is sought
Any other document deemed necessary by the Ministry

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Procuring Mobility Equipment To Disabled Ex-Servicemen (All Ranks)
issuing_authority: Kendriya Sainik Board (KSB) Secretariat, Ministry of Defence, Government of India
objective: The scheme provides financial assistance of a maximum of ₹ 1/00/000 from the Armed Forces Flag Day Fund (AFFDF) for the procurement of modified scooters/ crutches and wheelchairs for Ex-Servicemen who are disabled after their retirement from service with a disability of 50% or more.
benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial assistance of up to ₹1,00,000 to ex-servicemen who are disabled (50% or more disability incurred after retirement) for the procurement of essential mobility equipment, including modified scooters, crutches, and wheelchairs.
benefits_list: Financial assistance up to ₹1,00,000 for procurement of mobility equipment
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
how_to_apply: 1. Obtain the application form from the respective Zila Sainik Board (ZSB) or Rajya Sainik Board (RSB). 2. Fill out the application form completely and accurately. 3. Attach all required documents. 4. Submit the completed application form along with the documents to the ZSB/RSB. 5. The ZSB/RSB will verify the application and forward it to the Kendriya Sainik Board (KSB) Secretariat for approval and sanction.
required_documents: Disability certificate (indicating 50% or more disability incurred after retirement)
Bank passbook copy (front page with account details)
Medical prescription/recommendation for specific mobility equipment
Proforma invoice or quotation for the mobility equipment
Declaration regarding non-receipt of similar assistance from other sources

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Promotion Of Guru-Shishya Parampara (Repertory Grant)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
objective: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Promotion of Guru-Shishya Parampara (Repertory Grant)” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial assistance to cultural organizations and repertories to preserve and promote rare and vanishing traditional performing art forms by training young talents under expert gurus, thereby upholding the Guru-Shishya Parampara.
benefits_list: Preservation and promotion of rare and vanishing traditional art forms.
Training of young talents in traditional performing arts under expert gurus.
Financial assistance to cultural organizations and repertories.
Support for the traditional Guru-Shishya Parampara system.
Encouragement for the sustenance and growth of traditional performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Applications are invited through advertisements published in leading national newspapers and on the Ministry of Culture's official website. Interested organizations/individuals must submit a detailed proposal along with a budget and all required supporting documents as per the scheme guidelines. The application form and detailed instructions are generally available for download from the Ministry's website during the application period. Submitted applications are then reviewed by an expert committee.
required_documents: Registration certificate of the organization (if applicable).
Audited financial statements for the last three years.
Annual reports for the last three years (if applicable).
Detailed project proposal, including work plan and objectives.
Details of the Guru(s) and Shishya(s) involved.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Cultural Organizations With National Presence
issuing_authority: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
objective: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Cultural Organizations with National Presence” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance to cultural organizations with a national presence to support their operations, maintain infrastructure, and undertake various cultural activities at national and international levels, thereby promoting and preserving Indian culture.
benefits_list: Support for maintenance and development of cultural infrastructure.
Funding for organizing cultural events and activities at national and international levels.
Assistance for promotion and preservation of diverse Indian art forms and cultural heritage.
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: 1. Download the application form and detailed scheme guidelines from the Ministry of Culture's official website. 2. Ensure the organization meets all eligibility criteria, including being a registered cultural body with a proven national presence. 3. Prepare a comprehensive project proposal outlining the planned activities, budget, and expected outcomes. 4. Gather all necessary documents as per the scheme guidelines. 5. Complete the application form accurately and submit it along with all supporting documents to the designated authority within the specified deadline, typically to the Under Secretary (Culture) at the Ministry of Culture.
required_documents: Registration Certificate of the cultural organization
Memorandum of Association, Articles of Association, or Bye-laws
Audited Annual Accounts for the last three years
Annual Reports for the last three years
Resolution passed by the Governing Body for seeking financial assistance
Proof of 'national presence' (e.g., evidence of activities across multiple states)
Bank account details (e.g., cancelled cheque, latest bank statement)
An undertaking stating that the organization has not received financial assistance for the same purpose from any other government source
Bio-data of key personnel and governing body members

scheme_name: Financial Assistance Under The National Fund For Persons With Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DoEPwDs), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India
objective: A scheme by DoEPwDs for providing financial assistance to the Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) in support of various livelihood activities. The objective of these guidelines is to provide a framework to support financial assistance under the Fund for relevant core areas  for empowerment of PwDs.
benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial assistance to Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and organizations for their empowerment, covering livelihood activities, education, skill development, rehabilitation, and creation of barrier-free environments.
benefits_list: Financial support for education and skill development programs
Assistance for livelihood activities and employment generation
Funding for rehabilitation services and provision of assistive devices
Promotion of research and development in disability sectors
Support for human resource development programs
Promotion of sports, culture, and recreation for PwDs
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
how_to_apply: Organizations working for the welfare of PwDs must submit proposals through their respective State Government/UT Administration to the Department. Individuals with disabilities apply through implementing agencies identified or approved by the Department, not directly.
required_documents: Bank account details (for both organizations and individuals)
Disability Certificate (preferably UDID card for individuals)
Relevant educational/training certificates (for individuals, if applicable)

issuing_authority: Ministry of Education, Government of India (implemented by respective Universities)
objective: With a view to extend the benefits of equity and access to quality education to the economically weak students/ the University has introduced a financial support scheme for such students which will include fee waiver of all components of fee paid by students (except examination fee & hostel fee).
benefits_summary: This scheme provides significant financial relief to economically weak students by waiving most academic fees, excluding examination and hostel charges, thereby ensuring equitable access to quality higher education.
benefits_list: Waiver of other academic fees (excluding examination fee)
Waiver of miscellaneous fees (excluding hostel fee)
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Eligible students should apply through their university's designated online portal or academic department. Applicants typically need to submit an application form along with required documents by the stipulated deadline, as announced by the university.
required_documents: Proof of Economically Weaker Section (EWS) status (if applicable)

scheme_name: Financial Support To The Students Of N.E.R For Higher Professional Courses (NEC Merit Scholarship)
issuing_authority: North Eastern Council (NEC), Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region
objective: A scholarship scheme by the North Eastern Council (NEC)/ Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region for the students of the North Eastern States undergoing studies in different disciplines at different levels in Institutions inside as well as outside the North-East but within the country.
benefits_summary: The NEC Merit Scholarship provides financial assistance to meritorious students from the North Eastern States of India pursuing various higher professional and academic courses (Diploma, Graduate, Post Graduate, M.Phil, Ph.D) within India, aiming to promote higher education and skill development in the region.
benefits_list: Monthly stipends ranging from Rs. 800/- for Diploma courses up to Rs. 3000/- for M.Phil/Ph.D courses.
Annual book grants ranging from Rs. 1000/- for Diploma courses up to Rs. 5000/- for M.Phil/Ph.D courses.
Financial support for pursuing higher professional courses both inside and outside the North-East but within the country.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: 1. Visit the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) at scholarships.gov.in during the application period. 2. Register as a new user or log in if already registered. 3. Navigate to the schemes provided by the 'Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region'. 4. Fill in the online application form with personal, academic, and bank details. 5. Upload all necessary documents as specified. 6. Review the application and submit it. 7. Take a printout of the acknowledgment for future reference.
required_documents: Domicile/Permanent Resident Certificate of any North Eastern State.
Proof of admission/Bonafide certificate from the Head of the Institution.
Mark sheets of the last qualifying examination.
Bank Passbook (first page showing account holder's name, account number, and IFSC code).

scheme_name: Food Safety & Standards Authority Of India (FSSAI) Internship Scheme
issuing_authority: Food Safety & Standards Authority of India (FSSAI)
objective: The Internship Program by the FSSAI provides interns with unique learning opportunities in various areas of food regulation of food safety administration. FSSAI is a statutory body established under the Food Safety & Standards Act/ 2006 under the aegis of the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.
benefits_summary: The FSSAI Internship Program offers interns unique learning experiences in various aspects of food regulation and food safety administration, including policy development, scientific risk assessment, and enforcement, along with a monthly stipend.
benefits_list: Unique learning opportunities in food regulation and safety administration.
Exposure to policy development and implementation.
Experience in scientific risk assessment and enforcement practices.
Opportunity to work with a statutory body under the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.
A monthly stipend of Rs. 10,000 (subject to official terms and conditions).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Interested candidates must apply online through the official FSSAI internship portal. The application process typically involves filling out an online form, submitting required documents, and may include a screening process followed by an interview for shortlisted candidates. Specific application windows and links are announced periodically on the FSSAI website.
required_documents: Statement of Purpose (SOP) explaining interest and relevance.
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the academic institution (if currently enrolled).

scheme_name: Forest And Climate Change Internship Scheme
issuing_authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC), Government of India
objective: The Scheme seeks to engage students/ with Graduation/Postgraduation Degrees/ as well as Research Scholars/ enrolled in recognized Universities / Institutions within India/ or abroad/ as “Interns”. These "Interns" shall be inducted all over the country in various Divisions/Units of the Ministry.
benefits_summary: The Forest and Climate Change Internship Scheme provides students and research scholars with an opportunity to gain practical experience and exposure to environmental policy, research, and governance within the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. Interns receive a monthly stipend and a completion certificate.
benefits_list: Certificate of internship completion from MoEFCC
Practical experience in environmental policy, research, and governance
Exposure to the functioning of various divisions and units within MoEFCC
Opportunity to contribute to ongoing projects and research
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Applications are invited through official notifications published on the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) website. Interested candidates are required to download and fill the prescribed application form, which, along with all necessary documents, must be submitted as specified in the respective notification (e.g., via email to a designated ID or through an online portal). Selection is typically based on academic merit, relevance of research interests, and suitability for the Ministry's work.
required_documents: Proof of enrollment in a recognized University/Institution
Statement of Purpose outlining interest and relevance to the scheme
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the parent institution (if currently enrolled)

issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India (implemented through Dr. Ambedkar Foundation)
objective: The scheme aims to provide good coaching quality for DNT candidates to enable them to appear in competitive examinations and succeed in obtaining an appropriate job in the Public/Private Sector.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance for quality coaching to eligible Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNT) candidates to prepare them for competitive examinations for public and private sector employment, including a monthly stipend for living expenses.
benefits_list: Financial assistance to cover coaching fees for various competitive examinations (e.g., UPSC, State PSC, SSC, RRB, Banking, etc.).
Monthly stipend/allowance for outstation candidates (e.g., Rs. 6000 for metro cities, Rs. 3000 for non-metro cities).
Monthly stipend/allowance for local candidates (e.g., Rs. 4000).
Additional special allowance for candidates with disabilities.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Eligible DNT candidates must apply to empanelled coaching institutions registered under the scheme. These institutions screen candidates based on eligibility criteria and academic merit and then forward the selected applications to the Dr. Ambedkar Foundation for approval and financial assistance. The selection of candidates for coaching is made by the institutions as per the scheme guidelines.
required_documents: Income certificate (family annual income not exceeding prescribed limit, e.g., Rs. 8 lakh per annum)
Educational qualification certificates (mark sheets and degrees)

scheme_name: Free Coaching for SCs/ OBCs and beneficiaries of PM CARES Children Scheme
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: To provide high-quality free coaching to economically disadvantaged Scheduled Castes (SCs)/ Other Backward Classes (OBCs)/ and eligible beneficiaries of the PM CARES Children Scheme for competitive exams in public and private sector employment and admission into professional courses.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance for high-quality free coaching to economically disadvantaged Scheduled Castes (SCs), Other Backward Classes (OBCs), and eligible beneficiaries of the PM CARES Children Scheme for competitive examinations and admission into professional courses.
benefits_list: Financial assistance to cover coaching fees for various competitive examinations (e.g., UPSC, SSC, Banking, Railways, State PSCs).
Financial assistance to cover coaching fees for admission to professional courses (e.g., Engineering, Medical, Management, Law).
Access to high-quality coaching institutes empanelled under the scheme.
Opportunity for skill enhancement and career advancement for eligible beneficiaries.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: OBC/SC.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Visit the official website of the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment or the designated scheme portal when applications are open. Check the official notification for specific eligibility criteria and application deadlines. Register and fill the online application form, providing personal, educational, and caste details. Upload all required documents as specified in the guidelines. Submit the application before the closing date. Selection is typically based on eligibility criteria and a merit-based process.
required_documents: PM CARES Children Scheme Beneficiary Certificate (if applicable)
Proof of Identity (e.g., Aadhaar Card, PAN Card)

scheme_name: Free Coaching for Students with Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: A Scheme "Free Coaching for Students with Disabilities" was initiated by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities under the "Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment".
benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial assistance to eligible students with disabilities to receive coaching for competitive examinations for government jobs and admission to professional courses, thereby enhancing their skills and employability.
benefits_list: Financial assistance to cover coaching fees.
Monthly stipend for daily expenses like food, lodging, and books.
Coaching for various competitive examinations such as UPSC, SSC, Banking, Railways, State PSCs.
Coaching for admission to professional courses like Engineering, Medical, and Management.
Empowerment of PwD students through skill development and employment opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
Required education level: Graduate.
how_to_apply: Eligible students apply to coaching institutions empanelled by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD). These institutions then forward the applications to DEPwD for selection. The specific application procedure, including timelines and required forms, is usually announced by the DEPwD or the empanelled institutions.

scheme_name: Free Education For Sports Medal Winners / Participants Of National/ International Events
objective: The scheme of "Free Education for Sports Medal Winners / Participants of National/ International Events" is introduced by UGC/ to provide financial assistance to the medal winners\ elite sports persons studying in universities/colleges leading to all the full-time regular courses.
