benefits_summary: This scheme provides financial assistance to medal winners and elite sports persons who have participated in national or international events, enabling them to pursue full-time regular courses in universities and colleges.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for higher education expenses.
Support for elite sports persons and medal winners.
Applicable to full-time regular courses in recognized universities and colleges.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
how_to_apply: Applicants should contact the scholarship or administrative department of their respective university or college for detailed application procedures. Applications are typically submitted through the educational institution, which then forwards them for processing, potentially via a designated national scholarship portal.
required_documents: Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets.
Sports achievement certificates (medal certificates, participation certificates for national/international events).
Proof of admission to a full-time regular course in a recognized university or college.
Bank account details for direct benefit transfer.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, in partnership with NASSCOM
objective: The scheme aims to promote a novel skilling ecosystem in the country/ the programme/scheme would incentivize the cost of the eligible course(s) concerned/ providing authentic and accredited certifications that are acceptable in the industry.
benefits_summary: The FutureSkills Prime Incentive Program promotes skilling in emerging technologies by offering financial incentives (cashback) and authentic, industry-accredited certifications to individuals completing eligible courses, thereby fostering a skilled workforce for the digital economy.
benefits_list: Financial incentive (cashback) on completion of eligible courses
Access to authentic and accredited certifications from industry-recognized platforms
Skilling in ten emerging technologies (e.g., AI, IoT, Big Data, Blockchain, Cybersecurity)
Enhancement of career opportunities in digital technology sectors
Flexible learning paths through various online platforms
Participation in a novel skilling ecosystem
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: 1. Register on the official FutureSkills Prime portal.2. Explore and select an eligible course from the empaneled learning platforms.3. Complete the chosen course and obtain the certification.4. Apply for the incentive (cashback) through the FutureSkills Prime portal by submitting the required documents and course completion details.5. Receive the incentive upon successful verification.
required_documents: Proof of Identity (e.g., Aadhaar Card, PAN Card)
Course Completion Certificate from an empaneled FutureSkills Prime platform
Bank Account Details for incentive disbursement
Any other document as specified during the application process

issuing_authority: Ministry of Textiles, Government of India
objective: The objective of the scheme is to facilitate the establishment of worksheds for shuttleless looms in an existing or new cluster/ which will provide the required scale of economy for business operations.
benefits_summary: The Group Workshed Scheme facilitates the establishment of modern worksheds with improved infrastructure for powerloom weavers, especially those using shuttleless looms. It aims to create a conducive working environment, promote cluster-based development, enhance productivity, and improve the quality of fabric production by providing financial assistance for construction.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for the construction of modern worksheds for powerloom units.
Provision of a consolidated and improved working environment for weavers.
Facilitation of common infrastructure and amenities in workshed clusters.
Promotion of economies of scale for business operations of shuttleless looms.
Enhancement of productivity and quality in the powerloom sector.
Support for modernization and technological upgrade in weaving.
Employment generation within the powerloom industry.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Application for the Group Workshed Scheme typically involves the formation of a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) by eligible powerloom weavers or a registered cooperative society/producer company. The SPV/entity then prepares a detailed project report (DPR) adhering to the scheme guidelines. The application, along with the DPR and necessary documents, is submitted to the Office of the Textile Commissioner or a designated implementing agency, usually in response to an official call for applications or notification.
required_documents: Detailed Project Report (DPR) outlining the project proposal, technical specifications, and financial plan.
Proof of constitution and registration of the Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV), cooperative society, or producer company.
Land ownership documents or lease agreements for the proposed workshed site.
List of beneficiary weavers with details such as Aadhaar, loom details, and bank account information.
Financial statements, auditor's reports, and bank solvency certificates for the applying entity.
Architectural drawings and cost estimates for the workshed construction.
Any relevant No Objection Certificates (NOCs) from local authorities.
Affidavit declaring compliance with scheme guidelines and terms.

objective: GST Exemption Certificate Scheme has been introduced by the Dept. of Heavy Industries/ for issuing certificates to Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) having a disability percentage equal to or greater than 40%. The scheme provides GSC Concession to orthopedically disabled persons on purchase of car.
benefits_summary: Provides GST concession on the purchase of a car for orthopedically disabled persons with a disability percentage of 40% or more, facilitated by a GST Exemption Certificate.
benefits_list: GST concession on purchase of car
Eligibility for Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) with 40% or greater disability
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
how_to_apply: Applicants must register on the Ministry of Heavy Industries (MHI) online portal, fill out the application form with personal and vehicle details, upload all necessary documents, and submit the application for verification and approval. Upon successful verification, the GST Exemption Certificate will be issued.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Jal Shakti, Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation
objective: GOBARdhan Scheme was launched in April 2018 as a part of the Solid and Liquid Waste Management component under Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) to positively impact village cleanliness and generate wealth and energy from cattle and organic waste. The main focus of GOBARDHAN is to keep villages clean.
benefits_summary: The GOBARdhan scheme aims to promote the conversion of organic waste, particularly cattle dung and agricultural waste, into valuable resources like biogas, Compressed Biogas (CBG), and organic manure. This initiative enhances rural sanitation, generates wealth and energy, and fosters economic growth in rural areas by providing alternative income sources and promoting a circular economy.
benefits_list: Conversion of organic waste (cattle dung, agricultural residue) into bio-gas, Compressed Bio Gas (CBG), and bio-slurry/organic manure.
Generation of wealth and energy from waste.
Creation of income opportunities for farmers and rural communities.
Promotion of a circular economy model in rural areas.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
how_to_apply: Entities such as individual entrepreneurs, cooperatives, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Panchayats, and other organizations interested in setting up biogas or Compressed Biogas (CBG) plants can register and submit their proposals through the official GOBARdhan unified portal. The application process typically involves submitting a detailed project report (DPR) and seeking financial assistance or incentives available under various government schemes linked to waste-to-wealth initiatives.
required_documents: Detailed Project Report (DPR) outlining the proposed plant, technology, capacity, and financial projections.
Land ownership or lease documents for the proposed plant site.
Identity and address proof of the applicant (e.g., Aadhaar, PAN for individuals; registration documents for entities).
Bank account details of the applicant/entity.
Necessary environmental clearances and permits from relevant authorities.
Consent to Establish and Operate from the State Pollution Control Board.

scheme_name: Garima Greh Shelter Homes For Transgender Persons
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: Garima Greh aims to provide shelter to Destitute & abandoned Transgender persons with basic amenities like shelter/ food/ medical care and recreational facilities.
benefits_summary: Garima Greh provides safe and inclusive shelter homes for destitute and abandoned transgender persons, offering essential amenities like food, medical care, recreational facilities, skill development, and counselling, to ensure their dignity, rehabilitation, and integration into society.
benefits_list: Access to medical care and psychological support
Linkage to social welfare schemes and employment opportunities
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
how_to_apply: Individuals can directly approach the Garima Greh shelter homes established and managed by Community Based Organizations (CBOs) supported by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. The application process typically involves registering at the specific shelter home and fulfilling their intake criteria.
required_documents: Proof of identity (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Voter ID)
Self-declaration of being a transgender person (if no official ID specifies gender)
Any existing medical records or disability certificates (if applicable)
Referral letter from social worker or local authorities (if available)

issuing_authority: The National Trust, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
objective: Gharaunda (Group Home for Adults) scheme aims at setting up Gharaunda Centres or Registered Organisations (RO) for for all Adult PwDs covered under the National Trust Act with adequate and quality care service with acceptable living standards including provision of basic medical care from profession
benefits_summary: The Gharaunda-Group Home for Adults Scheme provides residential group homes for adult Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) covered under the National Trust Act, ensuring adequate care services, acceptable living standards, and basic medical care.
benefits_list: Establishment of group homes (Gharaunda Centres) by Registered Organizations to provide residential care for adult PwDs.
Provision of quality and adequate care services for adult PwDs residing in the centres.
Ensuring acceptable living standards, including food, shelter, and hygiene.
Access to basic medical care and professional support for residents.
Support for adult PwDs who may not have family support or require structured residential care.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
how_to_apply: For Registered Organizations (ROs): Eligible ROs must submit a detailed proposal and application to The National Trust, outlining their plan to establish and operate a Gharaunda Centre, adhering to the scheme's guidelines. For Beneficiaries (Adult PwDs): Eligible adult PwDs or their legal guardians can apply directly to the Gharaunda Centres established by approved Registered Organizations. The specific admission criteria and application process will be provided by individual centres as per National Trust guidelines.
required_documents: For Registered Organizations: Registration certificate of the NGO/Organization, Detailed Project Proposal, Audited Financial Statements, Bank details, Declaration of adherence to National Trust guidelines.

For Beneficiaries: Disability Certificate (preferably UDID card), Proof of Identity (e.g., Aadhaar Card), Proof of Address, Guardianship Certificate (if applicable), Medical records, Application form provided by the respective Gharaunda Centre.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH), Government of India
objective: Launched on 3rd Oct 2021 by the MoRTH/ the scheme grants an award to the good samaritan who has saved the life of a victim of a fatal accident (involving a motor vehicle) by administering immediate assistance and rushing to the Hospital/Trauma Care Centre within the Golden Hour of the accident.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides a cash award and a Certificate of Appreciation to individuals who save the life of a motor vehicle accident victim by providing immediate assistance within the 'Golden Hour'.
benefits_list: Cash award of Rs. 5,000/- (Rupees Five Thousand only) for each incident
Recognition for administering immediate assistance and saving a life within the 'Golden Hour'
Encouragement for citizens to help accident victims without fear of harassment
eligibility_criteria: General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: 1. Identification: The Good Samaritan is identified by the Police or the Hospital authorities at the accident site or the hospital.2. Recommendation: The Police or Hospital forwards the details of the Good Samaritan to the District Level Appraisal Committee (DLAC).3. Appraisal: The DLAC, chaired by the District Magistrate/Collector, appraises and approves the proposal based on laid down criteria.4. Approval and Disbursement: Approved proposals are sent to the State Level Monitoring Committee (SLMC) for final approval and disbursement of the award amount directly to the Good Samaritan's bank account.
required_documents: Aadhar card or any valid government-issued ID of the Good Samaritan
Bank account details of the Good Samaritan for award disbursement
Police report/FIR related to the motor vehicle accident
Hospital certificate confirming the assistance provided by the Good Samaritan and the saving of life within the Golden Hour
Recommendation/report from the police or hospital detailing the act of the Good Samaritan

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: The "Grant for Organizing Conference (GOC)" scheme by AICTE provides financial assistance to institutions for organizing conferences in various fields of technical education. Both Onsite Conferences and Online Conferences are supported.
benefits_summary: The scheme provides financial assistance to AICTE approved institutions for organizing conferences, seminars, and workshops in various fields of technical education, covering both onsite and online formats, to promote research, innovation, and knowledge dissemination.
benefits_list: Financial support for organizing conferences, seminars, and workshops.
Support for both physical/onsite and online events.
Promotion of research, development, and innovation in technical education.
Facilitation of knowledge sharing and networking among academic and research communities.
Enhancement of the organizing institution's academic stature and visibility.
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Applicants must visit the official AICTE portal, typically through the AICTE Activity Portal or Grant Management System. Institutions need to register or log in with their credentials, locate the 'Grant for Organizing Conference (GOC)' scheme application link, and fill in the detailed online application form. This form requires comprehensive information about the proposed conference, including its theme, objectives, schedule, budget, and expected outcomes. All necessary supporting documents must be uploaded as specified in the scheme guidelines. The complete application must be submitted electronically before the stipulated deadline, after which it undergoes evaluation by expert committees.
required_documents: Detailed Conference Proposal (including theme, objectives, dates, venue, speakers, target audience, and technical program)
Comprehensive Budget Proposal with a detailed breakdown of all expected expenses
Approval Letter from the Head of the Institution
AICTE Approval letter/status of the applying institution
Details of the Organizing Committee and their relevant credentials
Bank Account details of the institution
Audited financial statements of the institution (if required for eligibility verification)
Undertaking by the institution as per AICTE format

issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: Green Business Scheme has been launched by the National Safai Karamcharis Finance and Development Corporation to provide financial assistance in the form of loans for activities that can tackle the impacts of climate change and also be income-generating.
benefits_summary: Provides low-interest loans to Safai Karamcharis and Scavengers to start eco-friendly businesses like e-rickshaws, solar energy products, or poly houses.
benefits_list: Interest rate: 4% p.a. (with rebate for prompt repayment).
Subsidy elements available based on NSKFDC guidelines.
eligibility_criteria: Target Group: Safai Karamcharis, Manual Scavengers, and their dependents.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 50 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply through the State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) nominated by the State Government or directly through NSKFDC portal.

scheme_name: Group Insurance Scheme for Powerloom Weavers
objective: The basic objective of this scheme is to provide insurance cover to the powerloom weavers in the case of natural death/ accidental death as well as partial and permanent disability due to accident.
benefits_summary: A social security scheme offering life and accident insurance coverage to powerloom weavers.
eligibility_criteria: Powerloom weavers/workers.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 59 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Wage Earner.
how_to_apply: Weavers must enroll through the Powerloom Service Centres (PSCs) or Nodal Agencies appointed by the Ministry.

scheme_name: Group Personal Accident Insurance Scheme For Coir Workers
objective: A group insurance scheme by MoMSME for coir workers. The scheme provides insurance cover against accidental death and permanent disability of the insured member.
benefits_summary: Provides insurance coverage to coir workers against accidental death and disability.
benefits_list: Premium is paid by the Coir Board.
eligibility_criteria: Coir workers engaged in the industry.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Wage earner.
Age: 18 years and above.
how_to_apply: Claims must be submitted through the Coir Board offices.
required_documents: Proof of being a Coir Worker.

scheme_name: HRIDAY: Heritage City Development & Augmentation Yojana
issuing_authority: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
objective: Launched on 21st January 2015/ the scheme "National Heritage City Development and Augmentation Yojana"/ also known as "HRIDAY" is a central sector scheme by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (formerly MoUD). The scheme was introduced to bring together urban planning/ economic growth/ and heritage conservation.
benefits_summary: Focused on the holistic development of heritage cities, preserving their character while improving civic infrastructure.
benefits_list: Conservation of monuments and heritage assets.
Improvement of sanitation, drainage, and streetlights in heritage zones.
eligibility_criteria: Selected 12 Heritage Cities (Ajmer, Amaravati, Amritsar, Badami, Dwarka, Gaya, Kanchipuram, Mathura, Puri, Varanasi, Velankanni, Warangal).
Implemented by Urban Local Bodies.
how_to_apply: This is a city-level infrastructure scheme; individuals do not apply directly.

scheme_name: Hari Om Ashram Prerit Doctor Vikram Sarabhai Research Award
objective: The Shri Hari Om Ashram Prerit Doctor Vikram Sarabhai Research Awards are biennial awards established with funds from Pujya Shri Mota of Hari Om Ashram/ Nadiad/ to recognize outstanding contributions by Indian scientists under the age of 45 in the fields of Space Sciences etc.
benefits_summary: Recognizes outstanding research in Space Sciences and related fields.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Scientists.
Age: Below 45 years.
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Fields: Space Physics, Astronomy, Planetary Science, etc.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited from Heads of Institutions/Universities. Direct applications are not accepted.

scheme_name: Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award
objective: The Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award was established to commemorate the birth centenary of Pujya Shri Mota/ the founder of the Hari Om Ashram/ Nadiad. It is awarded to an eminent Indian scientist for their innovative and outstanding contributions to the field of Science and Technology.
benefits_summary: Honors senior scientists for lifetime contributions to science.
eligibility_criteria: Eminent Indian Scientist.
Age: Above 45 years.
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Significant contribution to Science & Technology.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited from Heads of Institutions or previous awardees.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
objective: A health scheme by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for financially poor patients to defray a part of the expenditure on Hospitalization/treatment in Government Hospitals/for life threatening diseases covered under Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN)/ in cases where free medical facilities aren't there.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to poor patients for the treatment of life-threatening diseases who are not covered under other major schemes.
benefits_list: Financial assistance up to ₹1,25,000 (partially depending on cost).
Direct payment to the treating Government Hospital.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹1,25,000.
Must be undergoing treatment in a Government Hospital.
Not eligible for RAN or other state schemes.
how_to_apply: Submit the prescribed application form signed by the treating doctor and Medical Superintendent to the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.

objective: The Scheme for Funding "High Risk - High Reward Research" aims at supporting proposals that are conceptually new & risky/ & if successful/ expected to have a paradigm-shifting influence on the S&T.
benefits_summary: Supports high-risk, high-reward research proposals that have the potential to revolutionize science and technology.
benefits_list: Funding is not limited; provided based on the requirement of the project.
Covers equipment, consumables, manpower, and travel.
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Ph.D. in Science or Engineering.
Regular faculty/researcher in recognized R&D institutions in India.
Must hold a regular academic/research position.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the SERB online portal.

scheme_name: IASRI Scholarship For M.Sc And Ph.D
issuing_authority: ICAR - Indian Agricultural Statistics Research Institute (IASRI)
objective: The Indian Agricultural Statistics Research Institute is a premier Institute under the Indian Council of Agricultural Research for conducting research and education in the field of agricultural statistics and computer application in agriculture.
benefits_summary: Provides scholarships to students pursuing M.Sc. and Ph.D. degrees in Agricultural Statistics and Computer Application at IASRI.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Admitted to IASRI Post Graduate School.
Required education level: Graduate (for M.Sc) / M.Sc (for Ph.D).
how_to_apply: Selection based on merit in the entrance examination conducted by ICAR/IARI.

scheme_name: ICAR - Post Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribes Candidates
objective: The scheme "Post-Matric Scholarship to Scheduled Castes / Scheduled Tribes Candidates" was introduced by ICAR/ Dept. of Agricultural Research and Education/ to enable the students belonging to the weaker sections of society to complete undergraduate courses.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for SC/ST students pursuing undergraduate degrees in agricultural sciences.
benefits_list: Contingency grant for books and study material.
Duration: Up to the completion of the degree.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to SC or ST category.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Secondary (Pursuing Bachelor's degree).
how_to_apply: Apply through the Dean/Principal of the Agricultural University/College.

objective: The ICAR floated the Scheme of Emeritus Professor in order to provide an opportunity to the superannuated teachers/ Scientists of universities/ colleges and institutions under ICAR-AU System.
benefits_summary: Allows retired professors to continue teaching and guiding research in agricultural universities.
benefits_list: Honorarium of ₹25,000 per month (in addition to pension).
Opportunity to utilize expertise for the benefit of students.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 60 and 65 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired.
Distinguished professor with significant contribution to agricultural education.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited by ICAR. The application must be routed through the Agricultural University.

objective: The ICAR Emeritus Scientist Scheme is a structural method of tapping the Brain and skill bank of the outstanding superannuated professionals of NARS by allowing them to complete the work in hand for its fruitful conclusion/ and utilize their talent in teaching specialized courses.
benefits_summary: Enables retired scientists to complete ongoing research or initiate new projects in agricultural sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 60 and 65 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired.
Eminent Scientist in the field of Agriculture.
how_to_apply: Apply against the advertisement issued by ICAR.

scheme_name: ICAR Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) For Post-Graduate Studies
objective: ICAR awards a certain number of Junior Research Fellowships for Postgraduate Studies (course and research work) leading to a Master’s degree/ in different disciplines of agriculture and allied sciences. This is done to encourage talented students to undertake higher agricultural education.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships for students pursuing Master's degrees in Agricultural Universities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 21 and 30 years (Age criteria varies by exam notification).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate in Agriculture or allied sciences.
Must qualify the ICAR AIEEA-PG entrance exam.
how_to_apply: Apply online for the ICAR AIEEA-PG exam conducted by NTA.

scheme_name: ICAR National Professor And National Fellow
objective: To promote excellence by recognizing outstanding scientists with proven output and outcomes for creating a culture of basic research through their project work in the National Agricultural Research System (NARS).
benefits_summary: Prestigious chairs created to support cutting-edge research in agriculture by eminent scientists.
benefits_list: National Professor: Pay Scale Level 14 + Special Allowance.
National Fellow: Pay Scale Level 14 + Contingency Grant.
eligibility_criteria: Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Serving Scientist/Professor in NARS.
Proven track record of high-impact research.
how_to_apply: Nominations invited by ICAR through Agricultural Universities and Institutes.

scheme_name: ICAR Senior Research Fellowship (SRF) For Post-Graduate Studies
objective: ICAR awards a certain number of Senior Research Fellowships for Ph.D. in an Agricultural University (AU)/ in different disciplines of agriculture and allied sciences. This is done to encourage talented students to undertake higher agricultural education.
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to students pursuing Ph.D. in Agricultural Sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 20 and 35 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Master's Degree in relevant discipline.
Must qualify the ICAR AICE-JRF/SRF (Ph.D.) exam.
how_to_apply: Apply online for the ICAR AICE-JRF/SRF (Ph.D.) exam conducted by NTA.

objective: ICMR- Post Doctoral Fellowship (ICMR-PDF) Scheme is being instituted to foster high-quality research opportunities for promising fresh Ph.D./MD/MS holders in the areas of basic science/ communicable and non-communicable diseases/ & reproductive health including nutrition at ICMR Institutes/Centers.
benefits_summary: Supports fresh Ph.D./MD graduates to pursue advanced research in medical sciences.
benefits_list: Contingency Grant of ₹3 Lakhs per annum.
House Rent Allowance (HRA) and Medical Benefits.
eligibility_criteria: Fresh Ph.D./MD/MS holders (within 3 years of completion).
Age Limit: Up to 32 years.
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Must not have a permanent job.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the ICMR portal against the call for applications.
required_documents: Acceptance letter from an ICMR Institute Guide.

objective: ICMR-Junior Research Fellowship (ICMR-JRF) examination is the first step in the process of admission to the PhD/Research Programme through council's support.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for biomedical sciences and social sciences students to pursue Ph.D.
benefits_list: Opportunity to work in premier medical research institutes.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: M.Sc./M.A. (with min 55% marks).
Age Limit: Up to 28 years.
Must qualify the ICMR-JRF Entrance Exam.
how_to_apply: Apply online for the ICMR-JRF entrance exam (usually conducted by PGIMER Chandigarh/NTA).

issuing_authority: National Productivity Council (NPC) & IPL Centre for Rural Outreach (ICRO)
objective: The IPL Centre for Rural Outreach (ICRO) has launched ICRO Amrit Internship Programme focused on youth productivity for prosperity. The interns will be placed at the headquarters and Regional Offices of NPC/ IPL. The duration of the internship will be 3 months initially/ renewable up to four times.
benefits_summary: Provides internship opportunities for youth to work on rural development and productivity projects.
benefits_list: Stipend: ₹10,000 to ₹15,000 per month (varies by role/location).
Experience in rural outreach and project management.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student/Fresh Graduate.
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate in Agriculture, Economics, Social Sciences, or Management.
Proficiency in computers and local language.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NPC or ICRO website when applications are invited.

issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) offers Doctoral Fellowships to support full-time Ph.D. scholars engaged in high-quality research in the field of social and human sciences. These fellowships are awarded to Indian research scholars registered in Ph.D. programs at UGC-recognized u
benefits_summary: Financial support for doctoral students in social sciences to pursue their research without financial constraints.
eligibility_criteria: Registered for Ph.D. in UGC recognized university.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Minimum 45% marks in Graduation and 55% in Post Graduation.
Age limit: 40 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the ICSSR website.

issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social scientific Research (ICSSR)/ under the Ministry of Education/ offers a Travel Grant for participation in International Conference abroad. The aim of this programme is to assist invited social science scholars to make substantial contributions to international seminars/co
benefits_summary: Provides travel grants to Indian social scientists to present research papers at international conferences abroad.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for international air travel.
Maintenance allowance for the duration of the conference.
eligibility_criteria: Social Scientists with a Ph.D. or equivalent research experience.
Paper accepted for presentation at an international conference.
Not availed similar grant in the last 3 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the ICSSR website at least 2 months before the event.

issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) offers Postdoctoral Fellowships to encourage original and quality research by young and mid-career scholars in the field of social sciences. It is designed for researchers who have just finished their Ph.D. in advanced social science research.
benefits_summary: Supports scholars who have completed their Ph.D. to conduct advanced research in social sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D. degree in Social Sciences.
Age limit: 45 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Unemployed/Affiliated to an institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the ICSSR website.
required_documents: Consent letter from the affiliating institution.

issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social scientific Research (ICSSR)/ under the Ministry of Education/ offers a Senior Fellowship Scheme designed to assist accomplished Indian researchers in conducting advanced social science research.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for senior social scientists to conduct full-time research on themes of national importance.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding research publications.
Age: 45 to 70 years.
Regular faculty or retired researchers.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the ICSSR website.

scheme_name: IGSTC Industrial Fellowship- PhD Industrial Exposure Fellowship
objective: The Indo-German Science & Technology Centre (IGSTC) Industrial Fellowship Programme was launched by Secretary/ Department of Science and Technology/ Govt. of India on the occasion of IGSTC’s 11th Foundation Day on 14th June 2021 to support young Indian researchers for industrial exposure in Germany.
benefits_summary: Provides funding for Indian Ph.D. students to gain industrial exposure in Germany for up to 6 months.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Ph.D. student in STEM fields in India.
Completed 2 years of Ph.D.
Age limit: 30 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the IGSTC portal.
required_documents: Acceptance letter from German Industry/R&D Lab.
No Objection Certificate from Indian Institute.

scheme_name: IGSTC Industrial Fellowship- Post Doctoral Industrial Fellowship
objective: This programme is aimed at encouraging Postdoctoral researchers/fresh PhDs in S&T with appreciable track record and having aptitude for applied research and technology development.
benefits_summary: Supports Indian Post-Doctoral researchers to work in German industries for applied research.
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D. in Science/Engineering (awarded within last 3 years).
Age limit: 35 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Researcher/Student.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the IGSTC portal.

objective: The scheme aims to motivate students in the age group of 10-15 years and studying in classes 6 to 10 to target one million original ideas/innovations rooted in science and societal applications to foster a culture of creativity and innovative thinking among school children.
benefits_summary: Encourages creativity and innovation among school children by funding original science projects.
benefits_list: Direct Benefit Transfer of ₹10,000 for preparation of project/model.
Opportunity to participate in District, State, and National Level Exhibitions.
eligibility_criteria: Students studying in Class 6 to 10.
Age: 10 to 15 years.
Must have an original innovative idea.
how_to_apply: Schools must nominate students via the E-MIAS (INSPIRE Awards) web portal.

scheme_name: IVRI Institute Scholarship For M.V.Sc. And Ph.D.
objective: The Indian Veterinary Research Institute will award scholarships to MVSc and PhD students who need financial assistance.his provision will be utilized to assist as many students as possible who have good academic record and who are making diligent efforts to pursue higher education.
benefits_summary: Scholarship support for meritorious students pursuing veterinary sciences at IVRI.
eligibility_criteria: Admitted to IVRI Deemed University.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Based on merit and financial need.
how_to_apply: Apply to the Academic Section of IVRI during admission/semester registration.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: Incubation Scheme aims to promote and support untapped creativity and to promote adoption of latest technologies in MSMEs that seek the validation of their ideas at the proof-of-concept level.
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to MSMEs and individuals to validate new ideas and develop prototypes.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹15 Lakhs per idea for prototype development.
Grant up to ₹1 Crore for machinery/plant for Host Institutes.
eligibility_criteria: MSMEs, Individuals, Students.
Must have an innovative idea ready for prototype development.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Student.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the MSME Innovative Scheme portal.

objective: The Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI) Junior and Senior Scholarship shall be awarded to M.Sc. and Ph.D. students admitted under General Open Competition Scheme.
benefits_summary: Provides scholarships to M.Sc. and Ph.D. students admitted to IARI.
eligibility_criteria: Admitted to IARI via Entrance Exam.
Not receiving any other fellowship/scholarship.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
how_to_apply: Awarded automatically based on merit list during admission.

objective: A scheme by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for the welfare of Overseas Indians; assisting them in distress situations. ICWF has also been a critical support in emergency evacuation of Indian nationals from conflict zones/ countries affected by natural disasters and other challenging situations.
benefits_summary: Provides emergency financial assistance to Indian nationals in distress abroad.
benefits_list: Boarding and lodging for distressed Indians.
Air passage to India for stranded nationals.
eligibility_criteria: Indian nationals abroad in distress.
Overseas Community members in specific situations.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Contact the nearest Indian Mission/Post (Embassy or Consulate) abroad.
required_documents: Proof of distress (Police report, Medical report, etc.).

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
objective: A non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to Divyangjans aged 18 and above who belong to a poor family (BPL) in society.
benefits_summary: Provides a monthly pension to persons with severe disabilities belonging to BPL households.
benefits_list: State Governments usually add a top-up amount (Total varies by state, e.g., ₹1000-₹3000).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 18 and 79 years.
Disability level: 80% or more (Severe/Multiple Disabilities).
Must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) household.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Gram Panchayat or Municipal Corporation. Application forms available at Block/District Social Welfare Office.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
objective: A non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL) of the society.
benefits_summary: Provides a monthly pension to widows belonging to BPL households.
benefits_list: State Governments add a top-up amount (Total varies by state).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a Widow.
Age: 40 to 79 years.
Must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) household.
how_to_apply: Apply through Gram Panchayat or Municipality. Submit forms to the Block Development Officer (BDO).

scheme_name: Indirect Tax Internship Scheme In CBIC
issuing_authority: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC)
objective: The "Indirect Tax Internship Scheme" by CBIC takes Law Students/ Graduates as Interns every year. The interns are expected to study case files/ assist in legal research and drafting of petitions/ brief the counsels and extend any other related legal/general assistance to officers/counsels.
benefits_summary: Offers law students exposure to the working of the Indirect Tax department and legal research.
benefits_list: Experience in legal analysis and drafting.
No stipend is usually provided (Honorary basis).
eligibility_criteria: Law Students (pursuing 3-year or 5-year LLB) or Law Graduates.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Interest in Indirect Taxation laws.
how_to_apply: Send the application form by post/email to the Directorate of Legal Affairs, CBIC, New Delhi.

scheme_name: Indo-French Postdoctoral Research Programme for Women in Science
issuing_authority: Indo-French Centre for the Promotion of Advanced Research (IFCPAR/CEFIPRA)
objective: This programme will provide a platform for women candidates to engage & work with Indian/French scientific communities with an aim to accelerate their talented instincts towards carrying out advanced studies and research in the frontier areas of Science & Technology.
benefits_summary: Provides funding for Indian women scientists to conduct postdoctoral research in France.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Indian national.
Ph.D. in Science/Engineering (awarded within last 5 years).
No permanent position in India.
Age limit: 45 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the CEFIPRA website during the call for proposals.
required_documents: Acceptance letter from French Host Institute.

scheme_name: Indo-French Visiting/Mobility Programme for Women in Science
issuing_authority: Indo-French Centre for the Promotion of Advanced Research (IFCPAR/CEFIPRA)
objective: This programme will encourage women candidates to work with the Indian/French scientific communities with an aim to accelerate their talented instincts towards carrying out advanced studies and research in the frontier areas of Science & Technology.
benefits_summary: Facilitates mobility for Indian women scientists holding permanent positions to visit France for collaborative research.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Indian national holding a permanent position in an Indian academic/R&D institution.
Ph.D. degree.
Age limit: 60 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the CEFIPRA portal.

scheme_name: Industrial R&D Promotion Programme: Recognition Of In-House R&D Units
issuing_authority: Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR)
objective: DSIR is operating a scheme called "Recognition of In-House R&D Units" with the objective of granting recognition & registration to in-house R&D units established by the corporate industry. The In-house R&D units are expected to be engaged in innovative research & development activities.
benefits_summary: Grants recognition to in-house R&D units of companies, enabling them to avail fiscal incentives.
benefits_list: Income tax deductions on R&D expenditure (under IT Act).
Duty-free import of equipment/consumables for R&D.
Eligibility for other government R&D grants.
eligibility_criteria: Companies registered in India.
Must have a functional R&D unit.
R&D unit should be separate from production.
Must have qualified R&D staff.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the DSIR e-filing portal.
required_documents: Layout plan of the R&D unit.

objective: The sub-scheme “Infrastructure and Technology Support” aims to develop world-class infrastructure in the country to support handicraft production/ enhance product quality/ and reduce costs to enable it to compete in the global market.
benefits_summary: Provides funding for setting up Common Facility Centers (CFCs), Raw Material Banks, and testing labs for handicrafts.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for construction of CFCs.
Modern tools and technology support for artisans.
Enhancement of production capacity and quality.
eligibility_criteria: Implementing Agencies: State Govt Corps, NGOs, Cooperative Societies.
Must have land availability.
Beneficiaries: Handicraft Artisans.
how_to_apply: Implementing agencies submit proposals to the O/o DC (Handicrafts).

objective: The "INSPIRE FACULTY FELLOWSHIP" component provides opportunities to 100 persons every year with Ph.D. qualifications in the age group of 27-32 years for 5 years to carry out research in both basic and applied science areas including engineering/ medicine/ agriculture/ and veterinary sciences.
benefits_summary: Offers a contractual research position to young researchers to conduct independent research and emerge as future leaders in science.
benefits_list: Consolidated Fellowship equivalent to Assistant Professor (approx ₹1.25 Lakh/month).
Research Grant of ₹7 Lakhs per year.
eligibility_criteria: Indian citizens or PIO status holders.
Applicant's age must be between 27 and 32 years.
Ph.D. degree (awarded within last 5 years) in Science/Engineering/Medicine.
Must be within top 1% in Class 12 or a topper in University.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the INSPIRE web portal during the call for applications.

objective: INSPIRE Fellowship is one of the components of INSPIRE scheme implemented by the Department of Science and Technology (DST)/ Government of India to strengthen the Science and Technology base of the country. This Component focuses on attracting students to pursue doctoral degree.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships to University 1st Rankers and INSPIRE Scholars to pursue Ph.D. in Science and Technology.
benefits_list: Fellowship equivalent to CSIR/UGC JRF (₹31,000 pm) & SRF (₹35,000 pm).
eligibility_criteria: 1st Rank Holder in University level Post-Graduate Examination in Science/Engineering.
OR INSPIRE Scholar with min 70% marks in M.Sc.
Applicant's age must be between 22 and 27 years.
Must have valid Ph.D. admission.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the INSPIRE portal.

objective: INSPIRE INTERNSHIP aims to provide exposure to the top 1% of students at the Class X Board level by organizing Science Camps either during summer or winter which provides an opportunity for them to interact with Science icons from India and abroad/ including Nobel Laureates.
benefits_summary: Organizes science camps for bright school students to interact with global science icons and motivate them towards science careers.
benefits_list: Interaction with Nobel Laureates and top scientists.
eligibility_criteria: Students of Class 11 pursuing Science stream.
Must be within the top 1% in Class 10 Board exams.
Age: 16-17 years.
how_to_apply: Schools nominate eligible students, or students apply against advertisements by camp organizers (Universities/Institutes).

objective: The scheme "INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (SHE)" aims to attract students to study basic and natural Science subjects at undergraduate and postgraduate levels and pursue research as a career by providing scholarships to interested and deserving students.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for meritorious students pursuing Bachelor's/Master's courses in Basic and Natural Sciences.
benefits_list: ₹60,000 cash per annum + ₹20,000 mentorship grant for summer projects.
eligibility_criteria: Top 1% rankers in Class 12 Board Exams.
OR Top 10,000 rankers in JEE/NEET.
Must be pursuing B.Sc./M.Sc. (Integrated) in Basic/Natural Sciences.
Age: 17-22 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the INSPIRE portal.

issuing_authority: Defence Innovation Organization (DIO) / Ministry of Defence
objective: Aimed at creation of an ecosystem to foster innovation and technology development in Defence and Aerospace by engaging Industries (incl MSMEs/ start-ups/ individual innovators R&D institutes/ academia) and provide them grants and other supports to carry out R&D innovation for adoption in D&A Sector
benefits_summary: Provides grants and support to startups and innovators to solve critical challenges in the defence and aerospace sector.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹1.5 Crore for product development.
Potential procurement by the Armed Forces.
eligibility_criteria: Startups, MSMEs, Individual Innovators.
Must be an Indian entity.
Must address a specific challenge launched under DISC (Defence India Startup Challenge) or Open Challenge.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the iDEX portal against active challenges.

scheme_name: Institutional Support for Development and Marketing of Tribal Products/Produce
objective: The scheme “Institutional Support for development and Marketing of Tribal Products/Produce” was introduced in 2014-15 by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Supports the marketing of tribal products by providing funding for procurement, infrastructure, and market development.
benefits_list: Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Minor Forest Produce (MFP).
eligibility_criteria: State Implementing Agencies.
Tribal artisans and gatherers (Beneficiaries).
Tribal Cooperative Marketing Development Federation of India (TRIFED).
how_to_apply: Tribal artisans can register with TRIFED or sell produce at designated MSP procurement centers.

objective: Institutions of Eminence scheme has been launched in order to empower Higher Educational Institutions and to help them become world class teaching and research institutions.
benefits_summary: Selects top universities to provide them with autonomy and funding to become world-class institutions.
benefits_list: Financial assistance up to ₹1,000 Crore for public institutions.
Freedom to hire foreign faculty and admit foreign students.
eligibility_criteria: Public and Private Higher Education Institutions.
Must be ranked among top institutions globally or nationally (NIRF).
Demonstrated potential for excellence.
how_to_apply: Institutions apply against the call by the Empowered Expert Committee (EEC).

scheme_name: Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices
objective: The scheme "Export Oriented Production: Large Cardamom Certified Nursery (Unit)" is a Sub-Scheme of the Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom by the Spice Board/ Department of Commerce and Industry/ Ministry of Commerce.
benefits_summary: Promotes export of spices through quality improvement, research, and infrastructure development.
benefits_list: Subsidy for setting up spice processing units.
Support for replanting and rejuvenation of cardamom plantations.
eligibility_criteria: Spice growers, Exporters, Processors.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer/Business Owner.
Possession of land for cultivation/processing.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Spices Board website or regional offices.

objective: The Integrated Scheme on Agriculture Cooperation (ISAC) is a Central Sector Scheme of the Ministry Of Agriculture/ Government of India. It is being implemented through the National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC).
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to cooperatives for economic activities in agriculture and allied sectors.
benefits_list: Loan and subsidy for infrastructure creation (godowns, cold storage).
eligibility_criteria: Registered Cooperative Societies.
Engaged in agriculture/allied activities.
Financially viable project.
how_to_apply: Cooperatives must submit proposals to the Regional Directorate of NCDC.

scheme_name: Intellectual Property Right (IPR) Scheme Under MSME Innovative Scheme
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: Intellectual Property Right Scheme offers legal and intellectual property filing support including patents/ trademarks/ copyrights/ designs/ geographical indications (GI) etc.
benefits_summary: Reimburses the cost of filing patents, trademarks, and GIs for MSMEs.
benefits_list: Domestic Patent: Reimbursement up to ₹1 Lakh.
Foreign Patent: Reimbursement up to ₹5 Lakhs.
GI Registration: Reimbursement up to ₹2 Lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: Registered MSMEs with Udyam Registration.
Applicant's employment status: Self-employed/Business Owner.
Must have filed the IP application.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the MSME Innovative Scheme portal.

objective: The Interest Subsidy Eligibility Certificate (ISEC) Scheme is an essential mechanism of funding the Khadi programme undertaken by Khadi institutions.
benefits_summary: Provides bank loans to Khadi institutions at a subsidized interest rate of 4% p.a.
benefits_list: Interest subsidy: The difference between the actual bank interest rate and 4% is paid by KVIC.
Ensures availability of working capital for Khadi institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Institutions registered with KVIC/KVIB.
Engaged in Khadi and Polyvastra production.
Valid ISEC certificate issued by KVIC.
how_to_apply: Institutions apply to KVIC for the ISEC certificate, then approach the bank for the loan.

scheme_name: International Centre for Environment Audit and Sustainable Development (iCED) Internship
issuing_authority: Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)
objective: The "International Centre for Environment Audit and Sustainable Development (iCED)" scheme provides a platform to young students pursuing courses in Ocean Science/ Environmental Science/ Environment Management/ Environment Audit/ etc. to help develop awareness about the work of iCED with special.
benefits_summary: Internship opportunity for students to work on environmental audit and sustainable development projects.
benefits_list: Access to iCED library and resources.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing Masters/PG Diploma in Environmental Science/Policy/Management.
Good academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending CV and application form to iCED, Jaipur.

scheme_name: International Cooperation Scheme- Capacity Building of First Time MSE Exporters (CBFTE)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: The scheme “International Cooperation Scheme” was launched by the MSME/ Govt. of India with the aim to capacity build MSMEs for entering export market by providing reimbursement of various costs incurred by first time MSE exporters on export shipments.
benefits_summary: Reimburses costs for first-time Micro and Small Enterprise (MSE) exporters.
eligibility_criteria: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) with Udyam Registration.
Must be a first-time exporter.
Applicant's employment status: Business Owner.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the IC Scheme portal of MSME.

scheme_name: International Cooperation Scheme- Market Development Assistance (IC-MDA)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: The scheme “International Cooperation Scheme” was launched by the MSME/ Govt. of India with the aim to capacity build MSMEs for entering the export market by facilitating their participation in international exhibitions/fairs/conferences/seminars/buyer-seller meets abroad.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to MSMEs for participating in international trade fairs and exhibitions.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of space rent for stalls.
Maximum ceiling applies (e.g., ₹1.5 Lakhs to ₹3 Lakhs).
eligibility_criteria: Registered MSMEs with Udyam Registration.
Must be manufacturing/service providing units.
Score minimum marks on the evaluation scorecard.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the IC Scheme portal before the event.

scheme_name: Internship Policy Of Indian Council For Cultural Relations (ICCR)
objective: ICCR provides an excellent opportunity for budding scholars to familiarise themselves with the role of soft power in foreign policy formulation in the Govt of India. These ‘Interns’ would be expected to supplement the process of analysis in various fields (creative writing/ cultural mapping/ etc).
benefits_summary: Internship opportunity to work on cultural diplomacy and soft power projects.
benefits_list: Exposure to India's cultural foreign policy.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate in International Relations/Culture/History.
Age: 19 to 25 years.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending CV and application form to ICCR, New Delhi.

scheme_name: Internship Program At The Indian Patent Office
issuing_authority: Controller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks (CGPDTM)
objective: The Internship Program by the Indian Patent Office recognizes the abundance of creative and innovative energies that flow in India/ and the need to tap into and channelize these energies. The program aims to work towards the fulfilment of the objectives of the National IPR Policy.
benefits_summary: Provides hands-on experience in Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) processing and policy.
benefits_list: Exposure to patent examination and trademark registration processes.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student.
Pursuing Law/Science/Engineering degree.
Interest in IPR.
how_to_apply: Apply via email/post to the CGPDTM office when the notification is released.

scheme_name: Internship Programme At Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO)
issuing_authority: Serious Fraud Investigation Office (Ministry of Corporate Affairs)
objective: The Internship Programme at SFIO was started in 2017 to provide a platform for the interaction of talented and young scholars and researchers from reputed academic institutions who are pursuing Post-Graduation/Research Programs in Economics/ Commerce/Law/ Management and Computer Forensics/ etc.
benefits_summary: Offers internship in the field of corporate fraud investigation and white-collar crimes.
benefits_list: Practical experience in forensic auditing and corporate law.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student.
Pursuing PG/Research in Law/Commerce/Economics/Forensics.
Minimum 60% marks in graduation.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form via post to the SFIO Director.

scheme_name: Internship Programme By The Ministry Of External Affairs (MEA)
objective: The Ministry of External Affairs launched the first edition of the Internship Programme in 2022. The internship policy aims to take foreign policy closer to the people. A total of 75 internships will be offered in the year 2022 in a single term of three months during April to June 2022.
benefits_summary: Provides an opportunity to work within the MEA and understand Indian foreign policy.
benefits_list: Airfare to and from Delhi (for outstation interns).
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
Age: Below 25 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the MEA Internship Portal.

scheme_name: Internship Programme Of The Department Of Expenditure
issuing_authority: Department of Expenditure, Ministry of Finance
objective: The Internship Programme of the Department of Expenditure aims to familiarize willing and eligible undergraduates pursuing five-year integrated courses and Graduates pursuing post-Graduation/ post-Graduates pursuing Research with the overall process of formulation of economic policies of the Govt.
benefits_summary: Offers insight into government financial management and economic policy formulation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student.
Pursuing PG/Research in Economics/Finance/Management.
Strong academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply online or via email to the Department of Expenditure.

scheme_name: Internship Programme Of The Wildlife Crime Control Bureau (WCCB)
objective: Internship at Wildlife Crime Control Bureau (WCCB) provides an excellent opportunity for students/researchers to familiarize themselves with various issues related to wildlife & the functioning of WCCB. The intern shall have an orientation session and would be assigned specific areas of work.
benefits_summary: Provides exposure to wildlife laws, enforcement, and conservation efforts.
benefits_list: Hands-on experience in wildlife crime data analysis and legal aspects.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student.
Pursuing Law/Environmental Science/Wildlife Management.
Interest in wildlife conservation.
how_to_apply: Apply via email or post to the WCCB Additional Director.

scheme_name: Internship Programme for Women Students / Scholars / Social Activists / Teachers (MWCD)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Women and Child Development (MWCD)
objective: The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the Internship Programme aims to harness the talent of the trainees/ giving them an opportunity to work towards bringing more efficiency in the implementation of the schemes.
benefits_summary: Internship for women to understand and contribute to women and child development schemes.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Applicant's employment status: Student/Scholar/Activist.
Pursuing UG/PG/Research.
Age: 21 to 40 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online or send application to MWCD via post.

scheme_name: Internship Scheme By The Unique Identification Authority Of India (UIDAI)
objective: The Internship Scheme by the UIDAI enables the students to gain adequate exposure to various Technology tracks and other units within UIDAI Headquarters/ Technology Centre and Regional offices. It would be expected to work in the field of application development/ legal domains (cyber laws/IT) etc.
benefits_summary: Provides internship opportunities for students to work on real-life projects related to Aadhaar and unique identification technologies.
benefits_list: Stipend for domestic students (approx. ₹10,000 to ₹15,000/month if applicable).
Hands-on experience in Technology, Law, or Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: B.E./B.Tech/ME/M.Tech/MCA/MBA/LL.B/LL.M/Economics.
Minimum 60% marks in the last semester/year.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form and CV to the Deputy Director (HR) at UIDAI HQ or respective Regional Offices.
required_documents: Recommendation Letter from Head of Department/Principal.

scheme_name: Internship Scheme For PG/Research Students In Directorate General Of Trade Remedies (DGTR)
issuing_authority: Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR), Ministry of Commerce
objective: The Internship Scheme by the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) provides opportunities for young scholars/ especially from Law / Economics stream/ interested in learning about trade remedial investigations.
benefits_summary: Offers internship in the field of trade remedies, anti-dumping, and countervailing duties investigations.
benefits_list: Practical experience in international trade law and economics.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing PG/Ph.D. in Economics/Law/Cost Accounting.
Knowledge of Trade Laws/WTO agreements is preferred.
how_to_apply: Apply by submitting the prescribed form and CV to DGTR via email or post.

scheme_name: Internship Scheme Of Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO)
objective: The Internship Scheme of Employees’ Provident Fund Organization invites applications for Internships from students for engagement as “Interns” with EPFO. The “Interns” shall have an opportunity to know about the EPFO’s functioning and Departmental Policy issues in EPFO and Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides exposure to social security administration and policy formulation in India.
benefits_list: Experience in handling social security schemes.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing Undergraduate (final year)/Postgraduate/Research in Law, Economics, Sociology, Management.
Good academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the EPFO website when applications are open.

scheme_name: Internship Scheme Of The Department Of Economic Affairs (DEA)
issuing_authority: Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance
objective: The Department of Economic Affairs of the Ministry of Finance manages the Internship Scheme for PhD or postgraduate students working on specific issues relating to macroeconomic management/ capital Markets/ Economic Reforms/ Exchange Management/ Foreign Investment in India/ etc.
benefits_summary: Offers an opportunity to work on economic policy, capital markets, and budget-related issues.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing PG/Research in Economics/Finance/Management.
Minimum 60% marks in Graduation.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DEA Internship Portal.

scheme_name: Internship Scheme Of The Ministry Of Labour & Employment
objective: Through the Internship Scheme/ the Ministry of Labour & Employment engage eligible students as “Interns" to work for mutual benefit. The “Interns” shall have an opportunity to know about the Government functioning and Department Policy issues in the Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides students with an opportunity to understand labour laws, employment policies, and social security.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing Law/Economics/Sociology/Social Work/Management.
Strong academic background.
how_to_apply: Send the application form via email/post to the Ministry.

objective: The “J.C. Bose fellowship” is awarded to active scientists in recognition of their outstanding performance. The fellowship is scientist-specific and very selective.
benefits_summary: A prestigious fellowship for active scientists to recognize their outstanding performance.
benefits_list: Fellowship amount of ₹25,000 per month (in addition to regular income).
Research Grant of ₹15 Lakhs per annum.
Overhead charges of ₹1 Lakh p.a. to the host institute.
eligibility_criteria: Indian National residing in India.
Age: Less than 68 years.
Required education level: Postgraduate/Ph.D.
Must be an active scientist with a regular position.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited from Heads of Institutions or eminent scientists. Apply via SERB online portal.

issuing_authority: Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation, Ministry of Jal Shakti
objective: Jal Jeevan Mission/ is envisioned to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024 to all households in rural India.
benefits_summary: Aims to provide Functional Household Tap Connection (FHTC) to every rural household.
benefits_list: Safe drinking water supply at the household level.
Supply of 55 litres per capita per day (lpcd).
Improves health and reduces drudgery for women.
eligibility_criteria: Rural households in India.
Communities/Villages without tap water connection.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Implemented by Gram Panchayats/Village Water & Sanitation Committees (VWSC). Households should contact their Gram Panchayat.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW)
objective: A scheme by Ministry of MoHFW for pregnant women who access government health facilities for their delivery. to absolutely free and no expense delivery/ including caesarean section. Essential care is provided to the mother and her neonate within 48 hours.
benefits_summary: Guarantees free and cashless delivery for pregnant women and treatment for sick newborns in public health institutions.
benefits_list: Free diet during stay in hospital.
Free transport from home to health institution, between facilities, and drop back home.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
All pregnant women delivering in public health institutions.
Sick infants (up to 1 year) accessing public health institutions.
how_to_apply: Automatically applicable when visiting any Government Hospital/Health Centre for delivery or treatment.

scheme_name: Jiyo Parsi Scheme – Health of the Community (Elderly/Child Support)
objective: This scheme provides financial assistance to eligible Parsi couples to support their dependent elderly family members & children. The benefit is given only to couples who have a child after introduction of this component in 2017-18 & have an annual family income of less than ₹15 lakh from all source
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to Parsi families for childcare and elderly care to encourage population growth.
benefits_list: Monthly assistance for elderly dependents to reduce burden on couples.
Honorarium for senior citizens living with families.
eligibility_criteria: Parsi Community Members.
Couples who had a child after 2017-18.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹15,00,000.
Must have elderly dependents or young children.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Jiyo Parsi website or submit forms to the Parzor Foundation.

objective: This scheme provides financial support to the Parsi couples for fertility treatments/ including Assisted Reproductive Technologies (ART) such as In-Vitro Fertilization (IVF) and Intra Cytoplasmic Sperm Injection (ICSI)/ along with other methods/ including surrogacy/ wherever required.
benefits_summary: Reimburses costs for fertility treatments like IVF to help Parsi couples conceive.
benefits_list: Financial assistance ranges from 50% to 100% depending on income.
eligibility_criteria: Parsi couples trying to conceive.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹10,00,000 (for 100% support) - slabs exist for higher incomes.
Valid medical prescription for ART/IVF.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Jiyo Parsi website/Parzor Foundation with medical details.

objective: The Department of Biotechnology/ Ministry of Science & Technology/ Government of India provides fellowships for pursuing research in biotechnology and life-sciences through the "DBT-Junior Research Fellowship" (DBT-JRF) Programme.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for students to pursue Ph.D. in Biotechnology and Life Sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: M.Sc./M.Tech/M.V.Sc in Biotechnology or related fields.
Must qualify the Biotechnology Eligibility Test (BET).
how_to_apply: Apply online for the BET exam conducted by RCB/NTA.

scheme_name: Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) And Research Associateship (RA) For Foreign Nationals
issuing_authority: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)
objective: The scheme was initiated keeping in view the political and cultural bilateral relations of India with other developing countries: Asia/ Africa/ Latin America. The scheme has opened new vistas for foreign students and teachers/ enable them to come to India and undertake advanced studies and research.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships to foreign nationals to conduct research in CSIR laboratories in India.
benefits_list: Opportunity to work in world-class CSIR labs.
eligibility_criteria: Foreign Nationals (developing countries preferred).
Required education level: M.Sc./Ph.D.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student/Researcher.
how_to_apply: Apply through the CSIR website/International S&T Affairs Directorate (ISTAD).

scheme_name: Junior Research Fellowship In Engineering & Technology (UGC)
objective: The scheme "Junior Research Fellowship in Engineering & Technology" by the University Grants Commission (UGC)/ Department of Higher Education is meant for candidates who wish to pursue research leading to a Ph.D. degree in engineering and technology.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for engineering graduates/postgraduates to pursue Ph.D.
benefits_list: Fellowship: ₹31,000 per month (JRF) for first 2 years.
HRA and Escort Reader assistance (for PwD).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: B.E./B.Tech or M.E./M.Tech.
Must have qualified GATE or NET.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC or NTA portal (often linked with NET/GATE qualification).

scheme_name: Junior Research Fellowship In Sciences/ Humanities/ And Social Sciences (UGC-NET JRF)
objective: The scheme of Junior Research Fellowship in Sciences/ Humanities/ and Social Science by UGC/ Dept. of Education/ is open to candidates who qualify for the National Eligibility Test-Junior Research Fellowship(NET-JRF) of the UGC and the UGC-Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (UGC-CSIR).
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to students qualifying NET-JRF to pursue M.Phil/Ph.D. in Indian Universities.
eligibility_criteria: Qualified UGC-NET or CSIR-UGC NET for JRF.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D. within 3 years of result.
Age Limit: 30 years (General).
how_to_apply: Apply online for UGC-NET exam. Upon qualifying and enrolling in Ph.D., apply for activation via UGC Canara Bank portal.

objective: Jute Raw Material Bank Scheme (JRMB) will be set up in the local clusters across the country in collaboration with bonafide Agencies who have adequate experience in similar activities and have the required storage/ display/ and selling infrastructure.
benefits_summary: Ensures the availability of jute raw material at mill gate prices to small jute artisans and MSMEs.
benefits_list: Supply of raw material (jute fibre/yarn/fabric) at economic rates.
eligibility_criteria: Agencies/NGOs/Co-operatives willing to operate the bank.
Beneficiaries: Jute Artisans, WSHGs, Micro Units.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Agencies submit proposals to National Jute Board. Artisans register with the JRMB to buy material.

objective: Jute Resource-Cum-Production Centre (JRCPC) will be set up in the local clusters and localities across the country in collaboration with bonafide Agencies who have adequate experience in training and skilling programs and have required training cum-production infrastructure.
benefits_summary: Sets up centers to provide training, design development, and production support to jute artisans.
benefits_list: Training on manufacturing jute diversified products.
eligibility_criteria: Implementing Agencies (NGOs, Societies).
Beneficiaries: Jute Artisans, Women SHGs.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Student.
how_to_apply: Implementing agencies apply to NJB. Artisans enroll at the JRCPC.

objective: The Govt. of India introduced the “Kabir Puraskar Scheme” in 1990 under which a National Award designated as “KABIR PURASKAR” was instituted in three grades/ namely/ Grade One/ Grade Two and Grade Three carrying a title and cash Award for acts performed during communal riots.
benefits_summary: Awards citizens who have shown conspicuous courage in saving lives during communal riots.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens.
Must have performed an act of courage during communal, caste, or ethnic violence.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Recommendations are invited from State Governments/UT Administrations. Individuals cannot apply directly.

objective: The ‘Kera Suraksha Insurance Scheme’ is a comprehensive Group Personal Accident Insurance Scheme/ implemented by Coconut Development Board in association with Public Insurance companies for Coconut Tree Climbers/Neera Technicians/Coconut Harvesters.
benefits_summary: Provides accident insurance coverage to coconut tree climbers and harvesters.
benefits_list: Premium shared (usually Beneficiary pays small portion, CDB pays rest).
eligibility_criteria: Coconut Tree Climbers (CTC), Neera Technicians.
Age: 18 to 65 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer/Worker.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Coconut Development Board or Agriculture Office.

issuing_authority: Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) / LIC
objective: A group insurance scheme by MoMSME for khadi artisans (spinners and weavers). The scheme provides insurance cover against normal death/ accidental death and disability of the insured member.
benefits_summary: Life insurance scheme for Khadi artisans.
eligibility_criteria: Khadi Artisans (Spinners/Weavers).
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 59 years.
Affiliated with KVIC/KVIB institutions.
how_to_apply: Enrollment is done through the Khadi Institution where the artisan works.

objective: The Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme was introduced in 1998 for issue of Kisan Credit Cards to farmers on the basis of their holdings for uniform adoption by the banks so that farmers may use them to readily purchase agriculture inputs and draw cash for their production needs.
benefits_summary: Provides timely and adequate credit to farmers for cultivation, post-harvest expenses, and consumption requirements.
benefits_list: Credit limit based on land holding and scale of finance.
Interest subvention (loans effectively at 4% interest on prompt repayment).
eligibility_criteria: All Farmers (Owner cultivators, Tenant farmers, Share croppers).
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 75 years.
Must have cultivable land (or engaged in Animal Husbandry/Fisheries).
how_to_apply: Visit the nearest Bank Branch or apply online via bank portals/PM-KISAN portal.

objective: ‘Kisan Vikas Patra Scheme’ was relaunched in the year 2014 by the Department of Economic Affairs/ Ministry Of Finance/ Government of India in view of the popular demand and to revitalize Small Savings. The amount invested in Kisan Vikas Patra (KYP) doubles in 115 months at the present rate.
benefits_summary: A small savings certificate scheme that doubles the investment amount over a specific period.
benefits_list: Doubling of investment (Period varies based on interest rate, approx. 115 months currently).
Transferable and can be pledged as security for loans.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens (Adults).
Parents on behalf of minors.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Visit any Post Office or designated Bank branch.

issuing_authority: Department of Science and Technology (DST) / IISc (Note: Subsumed under INSPIRE)
objective: The KVPY is a national fellowship program in basic sciences/ initiated and funded by the Department of Science and Technology to attract exceptionally motivated students to pursue basic science courses and a research career in science.
benefits_summary: To be updated. (Note: KVPY has been subsumed under INSPIRE - SHE scheme from 2022 onwards. Refer to INSPIRE for current benefits).
eligibility_criteria: Students in Class 11, 12, or 1st year UG (Science).
Applicant's employment status: Student.
High academic performance.
