how_to_apply: Program subsumed. Applicants should now apply for INSPIRE SHE.

scheme_name: Krishonnati Yojana - Sub Mission On Seed And Planting Material (SMSP)
objective: The Sub-Mission on Seeds and Planting Material (SMSP) aims to increase the production of certified / quality seed/ increase SRR/ upgrade the quality of farm-saved seeds/ strengthen the seed multiplication chain/ promote new technologies and methodologies in seed production/ processing/ testing/ etc.
benefits_summary: Promotes production and distribution of quality seeds to farmers.
benefits_list: Subsidy on distribution of certified seeds.
Support for creation of seed storage infrastructure.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Seed producing agencies/SHGs/FPOs.
how_to_apply: Contact the State Agriculture Department or Block Agriculture Officer.

issuing_authority: Department of Justice, Ministry of Law and Justice
objective: Department of Justice introduced an Internship Programme for young law students. The purpose is to well acquaint law students in the field of research & referencing work/ in various specialized areas of justice delivery/ such as Tele-Law/ Nyaya Bandhu etc.
benefits_summary: Offers law students exposure to judicial reforms and access to justice programs.
benefits_list: Experience in legal research and policy analysis.
eligibility_criteria: Law Students (2nd/3rd year of 3-yr course or 3rd-5th year of 5-yr course).
Applicant's employment status: Student.
Interest in legal aid/judicial reforms.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Department of Justice website.

scheme_name: Land Ports Authority Of India (LPAI) Internship Scheme
objective: The Land Ports Authority of India (LPAI) is pleased to announce its annual internship program for the academic year 2025-26. This program provides a unique opportunity for students and research scholars from BBIN countries (Bhutan/ India/ Nepal/ and Bangladesh) to gain practical experience in LPAI
benefits_summary: Provides insights into border infrastructure management and trade facilitation.
benefits_list: Experience in land port management and cross-border trade.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student/Scholar.
Pursuing Graduate/PG in Engineering/Architecture/Management/Economics/Law.
Citizens of India/Bhutan/Nepal/Bangladesh.
how_to_apply: Apply by emailing the application form to LPAI.

issuing_authority: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying (DAHD)
objective: The Livestock Health and Diseases Control (LH&DC) scheme is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) implemented by the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying (DAHD)/ Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Aims to control and eradicate major livestock diseases through vaccination and disease surveillance to protect animal health.
benefits_list: Free vaccination for Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) and Brucellosis.
Control of Classical Swine Fever (CSF) and Peste des Petits Ruminants (PPR).
Establishment of Mobile Veterinary Units (MVUs) for doorstep service.
Assistance for setting up state-of-the-art disease diagnostic labs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer/Animal Rearer.
Owners of livestock (Cattle, Buffalo, Sheep, Goat, Pig).
how_to_apply: Contact the nearest Government Veterinary Hospital or Dispensary for vaccination and services.

scheme_name: Loan Based Schemes For Safai Karamchari - Education Loan Scheme (ELS)
issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: An Education Loan scheme by the Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for Safai Karamchari/ Manual Scavengers/ and their dependents aged 18 years and above.
benefits_summary: Provides education loans at concessional interest rates to the wards of Safai Karamcharis and Manual Scavengers for pursuing higher studies in India and abroad.
benefits_list: Loan for studies in India: Up to ₹10 Lakhs.
Loan for studies abroad: Up to ₹20 Lakhs.
Interest Rate: 4% p.a. (for boys), 3.5% p.a. (for girls).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Parent/Guardian must be a Safai Karamchari/Manual Scavenger/Dependent.
Admission secured in a recognized institute.
how_to_apply: Apply through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) or Nationalized Banks having MoU with NSKFDC.

scheme_name: Loan Based Schemes For Safai Karamchari - General Term Loan (GTL)
issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: A general term loan scheme by the Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for Safai Karamchari/ Manual Scavengers/ and their dependants aged 18 years and above.
benefits_summary: Provides term loans for starting any viable income-generating activity (business/service/transport sector).
benefits_list: Interest Rate: 6% p.a. (up to ₹5 Lakhs), higher slabs apply above ₹5 Lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: Safai Karamcharis, Manual Scavengers, and their dependents.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed.
Age: 18 years and above.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the State Channelizing Agency (SCA) of NSKFDC in the respective state.

scheme_name: Loan Based Schemes For Safai Karamchari - Sanitary Marts Scheme
issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: A contributory Loan scheme by Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for individual beneficiaries/self help groups of Liberated Safai Karamchari/ Manual Scavengers and their dependants aged 18 years and above/ for setting up A Sanitary Marts (SM) is a one-stop-shop for all things for sanitation
benefits_summary: Loans for setting up 'Sanitary Marts' to sell sanitation items and provide services, promoting both livelihood and hygiene.
benefits_list: Loan up to ₹15 Lakhs for setting up the mart.
Promotes supply of sanitation materials in rural/urban areas.
eligibility_criteria: Safai Karamcharis/Manual Scavengers and their dependents.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
SHGs of target group members.
how_to_apply: Apply through the State Channelizing Agency (SCA) of NSKFDC.

scheme_name: Swachhta Udyami Yojana – Swachhta Se Sampannta Ki Aur (Mechanized Cleaning)
issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: A general term loan scheme by the Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for Safai Karamchari/ Manual Scavengers and their dependants aged 18 years and above for procurement of sanitation-related vehicles.
benefits_summary: Loans for purchasing mechanized cleaning equipment (suction machines, jetting machines) to eliminate manual scavenging.
benefits_list: Loan up to ₹50 Lakhs for sanitation vehicles/equipment.
Capital Subsidy up to ₹5.00 Lakhs (for Manual Scavengers).
eligibility_criteria: Safai Karamcharis, Manual Scavengers, and their dependents.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Municipalities/ULBs can also facilitate.
how_to_apply: Apply directly to NSKFDC or through SCAs/Banks.

scheme_name: Loan based Scheme for Pay and Use Community Toilets (Swachhta Udyami Yojana)
issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: The “Pay and Use” Community Toilets Scheme under Swachhta Udyami Yojana by NSKFDC offers concessional loans to Safai Karamcharis and their dependents for setting up public toilet units/ promoting sanitation/ livelihood/ and dignity.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for construction and operation of Pay & Use Community Toilets.
benefits_list: Capital Subsidy applicable as per SRMS guidelines.
Generates steady income through user fees.
eligibility_criteria: Safai Karamcharis, Manual Scavengers, and their dependents.
SHGs/NGOs consisting of target group members.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to NSKFDC/SCA along with allotment letter of land from ULB/Gram Panchayat.

scheme_name: Long Term Fellowship under Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research
issuing_authority: Department of Health Research (DHR), Ministry of Health & Family Welfare
objective: Long Term Fellowship under the Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research aims to provide advanced training in India and abroad to medical and health research personnel in cutting-edge research areas related to medicine and health.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for mid-career scientists to undergo advanced training in foreign or Indian institutes.
benefits_list: Monthly stipend (for foreign fellowship: up to $3000/month).
eligibility_criteria: Regular faculty/researcher in medical colleges/research institutes.
Required education level: Postgraduate (MD/MS/Ph.D.).
Age limit: 45 years (relaxable for SC/ST/OBC/Women).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the DHR web portal when the call is active.

issuing_authority: National Institute of Agricultural Extension Management (MANAGE)
objective: MANAGE Internship Programme is open to students from the disciplines of Extension Education/ Economics/ Sociology/ Psychology/ MSW/ Journalism/ Communication/ and Environmental Science. The internships are open year-round/ and the duration ranges from 3 months upto 6 months.
benefits_summary: Internship opportunity for PG students to work on agricultural extension management projects.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Pursuing Ph.D. or Post Graduation in relevant disciplines.
Strong academic record.
how_to_apply: Send CV and application via email to the Director General, MANAGE.
required_documents: Recommendation from Head of the Department/Institute.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme is an extensive drive to create awareness amongst MSMEs about Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) practices and motivate and incentivize them for ZED Cerficaon while also encouraging them to become MSME Champions.
benefits_summary: Subsidizes the cost of ZED Certification (Bronze, Silver, Gold) to encourage MSMEs to adopt quality and sustainable practices.
benefits_list: Graded incentives by states/banks for ZED certified units.
eligibility_criteria: All MSMEs with valid Udyam Registration.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Business Owner.
how_to_apply: Register on the ZED portal and apply for the desired certification level.

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
objective: An employment scheme by the MoRD/ providing provide at least 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled work. Any Indian citizen above the age of 18 years and residing in a rural area can apply to this scheme.
benefits_summary: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to rural households.
benefits_list: Unemployment allowance if work is not provided within 15 days.
Work provided within 5 km of the village.
eligibility_criteria: Rural Households.
Adult members (18+ years) willing to do unskilled manual work.
Must hold a Job Card.
how_to_apply: Apply for a Job Card at the Gram Panchayat. Demand for work can be made orally or in writing to the Gram Rozgar Sahayak/Panchayat.

issuing_authority: Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance
objective: The scheme “Mahila Samman Savings Certificate” was launched by the Department of Economic Affairs/ Ministry of Finance to provide financial security to every girl and woman in India.
benefits_summary: A small savings scheme exclusively for women and girls with attractive interest rates.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female (Woman or Girl child).
Guardian can open account for a minor girl.
No age limit.
how_to_apply: Visit any Post Office or authorized Bank branch to open the account.

issuing_authority: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
objective: This is a micro-financing programme aiming towards the empowerment of women from underprivileged backgrounds or economically backward areas. Through this scheme/ these women can apply for a loan at a nominal interest rate to fulfil their dreams and goals and gain financial independence.
benefits_summary: Micro-finance scheme for SC women to start small businesses.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Must belong to Scheduled Caste (SC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) or Self Help Groups (SHGs).

objective: The Marketing Development Assistance (MDA) Scheme under the Overseas Promotion & Publicity (OPP) Scheme aims to support the promotion and marketing of Indian tourism in overseas markets.
benefits_summary: Financial support to tourism service providers for promoting India in overseas markets.
benefits_list: Assistance for participating in international travel fairs/exhibitions.
Financial support for sales tours and roadshows abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Approved Tour Operators/Travel Agents/Hoteliers.
Must be approved by the Ministry of Tourism.
Having foreign exchange earnings.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to the Ministry of Tourism at least 14 days before the event.
required_documents: Approval Letter from Ministry of Tourism.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Jal Shakti (Dept of Water Resources, RD & GR)
objective: The Mass Communication Internship Programme by the Department of Water Resources/ River Development/ and Ganga Rejuvenation. The intern program will offer work culture and environment knowledge about the functioning of the Government and its departments in information/ education/ and communication.
benefits_summary: Internship for Mass Comm students to work on IEC (Information, Education, Communication) activities related to water resources.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing Graduate/PG in Mass Communication/Journalism/Public Relations.
Good academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply online or via email/post to the Ministry when notified.

scheme_name: Maulana Azad National Fellowship (MANF) For Minority Students
objective: Maulana Azad National Fellowship For Minority Students was launched in 2009 by the Ministry of Minority Affairs for providing financial aid to students for their higher studies.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for minority students to pursue M.Phil and Ph.D. (Note: The scheme was discontinued/subsumed for new applicants from 2022-23; existing fellows continue to receive benefits).
benefits_list: Fellowship amount equivalent to UGC JRF/SRF.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Minority Community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, Jain).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000 (earlier criterion).
Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D.
Qualified UGC-NET/CSIR-NET.
how_to_apply: Previously applied via UGC/NTA. Currently, check for notifications regarding reinstatement or alternative schemes.

scheme_name: Merit Cum Means Based Scholarship For Students Belonging To The Minority Communities
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Minority Affairs for students from minority communities pursuing technical and professional courses at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in a listed institute. A total of 60/000 scholarships are targeted to be distributed as ‘Fresh’ Scholarships.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to minority students pursuing professional/technical courses.
benefits_list: Course Fee Reimbursement: Up to ₹20,000 per annum.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Minority Community.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Secured min 50% marks in previous final exam.
Pursuing Technical/Professional course.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarships For Under Graduate Studies In Agricultural Sciences (ICAR)
objective: The scheme of "Merit-cum-Means Scholarships For Under Graduate Studies In Various Branches Of Agriculture And Animal Science Subjects" was introduced by ICAR with a view to attracting meritorious undergraduate students to prosecute their studies up to graduation level.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for meritorious students from low-income families pursuing UG in Agriculture.
benefits_list: Supports students to complete their graduation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Pursuing UG in Agriculture/Allied Sciences.
Parents' annual income must be below the prescribed limit (usually ₹4.5 Lakhs).
Merit in entrance exam/academics.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Dean/Principal of the Agricultural University.

issuing_authority: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
objective: The scheme is implemented by the National Scheduled Castes Finance & Development Corporation/ Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment/ Govt. of India. Under this scheme/ loans are provided to individuals from the SC community for units costing up to ₹1/40/000/- for income-generating activities.
benefits_summary: Provides micro-loans to SC beneficiaries for small income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Caste (SC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Apply through SCAs or Channelizing Agencies.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: The scheme “Mission Solar Charkha” a pilot project was implemented by the Ministry of Micro/ Small & Medium Enterprises/ Government of India. The scheme envisages setting up of Solar Charkha Clusters which would mean a focal village and other surrounding villages in a radius of 8 to 10 Kilometres.
benefits_summary: Promotes production of khadi using solar charkhas to generate employment in rural areas.
benefits_list: Subsidy for setting up Solar Charkha Clusters (up to ₹9.60 Crore per cluster).
Supply of Solar Charkhas and Looms.
eligibility_criteria: Implementing Agencies (SPVs, NGOs, Societies).
Beneficiaries: Rural Artisans/Spinners/Weavers.
Focus on women and marginalized communities.
how_to_apply: Promoters/Agencies submit proposals to MSME Ministry. Artisans enroll with the cluster.

scheme_name: Mission on Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH)
objective: In 2014-15/ the Government of India launched Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH) subsuming the ongoing schemes viz./ NHM/ HMNEH/ CDB/ NHB/ CIH and NBM.
benefits_summary: Comprehensive scheme for the holistic growth of the horticulture sector (fruits, vegetables, spices, flowers).
benefits_list: Subsidy for planting material and area expansion (40-50%).
Assistance for polyhouses, cold storage, and packhouses.
Support for mechanization and post-harvest management.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
FPOs, SHGs, Private Entrepreneurs.
Land ownership required.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Horticulture Officer or State Horticulture Mission.

objective: The Mobility Scheme is aimed to provide an opportunity to women scientists who are facing difficulties in their present job due to relocation (marriage/ transfer of husband to any other location within the country/ attending ailing parents/ and accompanying children studying in different city).
benefits_summary: Allows women scientists holding regular positions to relocate to a new institute while retaining their job/research work.
benefits_list: Fellowship/Salary support during the relocation period.
Ensures career continuity for women scientists.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Women scientists holding regular permanent positions.
Facing relocation issues.
Required education level: Ph.D./M.Tech/MD.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DST e-PMS portal.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: MODROB scheme/ one of the AICTE Quality Improvement Scheme (AQIS)/ intends to support the development of laboratories with appropriate equipment/ technology/ tools/ software and encourages financial contribution from the industry.
benefits_summary: Provides funding to modernize laboratories in technical institutions to keep pace with technological advancements.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹20 Lakhs per lab.
Funds for purchasing modern equipment and software.
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved Technical Institutions (Engineering/Management/Pharmacy/etc.).
Must have at least 10 years of existence.
NBA Accreditation preferred.
how_to_apply: Institutions must apply online via the AICTE AQIS portal.

issuing_authority: National Backward Classes Finance & Development Corporation (NBCFDC)
objective: A general loan scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice for Indian citizens from backward classes for various income-generating activities. Loan up to ₹15 Lakh per beneficiary for pursuing income-generating activities.
benefits_summary: Provides loans at concessional interest rates to OBC individuals for starting self-employment ventures.
benefits_list: Interest Rate: 6% p.a. (up to ₹5 Lakhs), higher for above ₹5 Lakhs.
Promotes economic development of Backward Classes.
eligibility_criteria: Member of Backward Classes (OBC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) or partner banks.

scheme_name: NDRI Institute Scholarship For M.Sc. And Ph.D.
objective: “Institute Scholarship” is offered to all students of Indian Nationality admitted to Master’s and Doctoral programmes at NDRI to provide them with financial assistance.
benefits_summary: Scholarships for students pursuing M.Sc. and Ph.D. in Dairy Sciences at NDRI.
benefits_list: Support for research in dairy technology.
eligibility_criteria: Admitted to NDRI Deemed University.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Not receiving other fellowships.
how_to_apply: Awarded automatically to eligible students upon admission.

issuing_authority: National Institute of Electronics & Information Technology (NIELIT)
objective: The Internship Programme by NIELIT is an opportunity for a student to secure first-hand and practical work experience under the guidance of qualified and experienced Supervisors/Mentors. It also aims at active participation in the learning process through experimentation.
benefits_summary: Offers internship in Electronics and IT to students.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing B.E./B.Tech (Electronics/CS/IT) or M.Sc/MCA.
Minimum 60% marks.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the NIELIT website when advertised.

objective: This Scheme seeks to engage students pursuing Under Graduate/Graduate/Post Graduate Degrees or are Research Scholars enrolled in recognized University/Institution within India or abroad/ as "interns". They shall be given exposure to various Verticals/ Divisions/Units within NITI Aayog.
benefits_summary: Internship opportunity to work with the premier policy think tank of the Government of India.
benefits_list: Exposure to policy formulation and research.
Experience in working with government verticals.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Undergraduate (2nd/4th sem completed), Graduate, or Research Scholar.
Strong academic record (min 85% in Class 12, 70% in Graduation).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NITI Aayog website (Link is open from 1st to 10th of every month).

objective: National Productivity Council (NPC) provides internship training to students pursuing graduation and post-graduation. The interns shall be given exposure to related interventions being activities undertaken by NPC which will provide opportunities to learn and implement new concepts.
benefits_summary: Provides practical training in productivity enhancement, environment management, and energy efficiency.
benefits_list: Opportunity to work in Regional Directorates.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate in Engineering, Economics, Management, or Social Sciences.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form and CV to the respective Regional Directorate or HQ.

issuing_authority: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)
objective: The “NPS Vatsalya” is a pension-cum-saving scheme designed for minors/ regulated by PFRDA. It aims to secure the financial future of minors by offering a structured pension scheme/ with contributions managed by a guardian until the minor turns 18.
benefits_summary: A pension account for minors to build a retirement corpus early in life through long-term compounding.
benefits_list: Account converts to regular NPS at age 18.
Partial withdrawal allowed for education/illness (after 3 years).
eligibility_criteria: Minor Citizen of India (Age 0-18 years).
Account opened and operated by Guardian (Parent).
KYC compliance for Guardian.
how_to_apply: Open account via Point of Presence (Banks/Post Offices) or online through e-NPS portal.
required_documents: Date of Birth Proof of Minor.

scheme_name: NSAP - Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
objective: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme is one of the five sub-schemes of the National Social Assistance Programme. Under IGNOAPS/ citizens living Below Poverty Line and 60 years or above in age are eligible to apply. A monthly pension of ₹ 200 up to 79 years and ₹ 500 thereafter.
benefits_summary: Provides monthly pension to elderly citizens living below the poverty line.
benefits_list: State Governments usually provide a top-up amount (Total pension varies by state, e.g., ₹1000-₹3000).
eligibility_criteria: Age: 60 years or above.
Must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) household.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired/Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply at the Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office (BDO).

issuing_authority: Ministry of Women and Child Development
objective: Nari Adalat/ a component of the "Sambal" sub-scheme under Mission Shakti/ it aims to provide women with an alternate Grievance Redressal Mechanism for resolving cases of petty nature (harassment/ subversion/ curtailment of rights or entitlements) faced by them at Gram panchayat level.
benefits_summary: Village-level women's courts for alternative dispute resolution and grievance redressal.
benefits_list: Access to justice for women at the grassroots level.
Resolution of petty disputes (domestic, property, etc.).
Awareness about legal rights and entitlements.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Women facing domestic violence, harassment, or property disputes.
Selected women (Nyaya Sakhis) run the Adalat.
how_to_apply: Approach the local Gram Panchayat or Nari Adalat members (Nyaya Sakhis) in the village.

scheme_name: National AIDS Control Organisation (NACO) Internship Programme
issuing_authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (NACO)
objective: NACO initiated the Internship Programme for young students who wish to engage with the Government. The internship programme envisages an opportunity for young students to get familiar with and understand the various dimensions of policy-making & implementation of the National AIDS Control Programme.
benefits_summary: Provides exposure to HIV/AIDS control program implementation and policy making.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing Graduate/PG in Medical, Social Work, Public Health, Sociology, Psychology.
Interest in HIV/AIDS sector.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form to NACO via email/post.

scheme_name: National Action Plan for Skill Development of Persons with Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: A dedicated skill development program aimed at providing high-quality vocational training with a focus on PwDs. The objective is to enhance the skills of PwDs by providing quality vocational skill training/ enabling them to gain meaningful employment and become contributing members of society.
benefits_summary: Offers free vocational skill training to Persons with Disabilities to enhance employability.
benefits_list: Free skill training in various trades.
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Applicant's age must be between 15 and 59 years.
Possessing Disability Certificate (UDID).
how_to_apply: Register on the PM Daksh portal or approach Empanelled Training Partners (ETPs).

issuing_authority: Agriculture Insurance Company of India (AIC) / Ministry of Agriculture
objective: The National Agriculture Insurance Scheme (NAIS) was introduced by the Government of India to provide comprehensive insurance coverage and financial support to farmers against crop losses due to natural calamities/ pests/ and diseases. (Note: Replaced by PMFBY in many aspects).
benefits_summary: Provides insurance coverage to farmers for notified crops against natural calamities.
benefits_list: Compensation for yield loss due to drought, flood, pests, etc.
Covers food crops, oilseeds, and annual commercial/horticultural crops.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
Loanee and Non-Loanee farmers growing notified crops in notified areas.
how_to_apply: Apply through Banks/PACS (for Loanee farmers, it is often mandatory) or CSCs.

objective: Launched on 14th April 2016/ e-NAM is a pan-India electronic trading platform that links existing mandis to create a unified online market for agricultural commodities/ promoting “One Nation One Market” and enabling transparent/ efficient/ and nationwide agri-trade.
benefits_summary: An online trading platform for agricultural commodities to ensure better price discovery for farmers.
benefits_list: Real-time price discovery based on supply and demand.
Direct payment to farmers' bank accounts.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer/Trader.
Must register with the local APMC (Mandi).
how_to_apply: Register online on the e-NAM portal or mobile app, or visit the nearest e-NAM mandi.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE)
objective: To promote apprenticeship training in the country/ by providing partial stipend support to the apprentices engaged under the Apprentice Act/ 1961/ undertaking capacity building of the apprenticeship ecosystem/ and providing advocacy assistance to the stakeholders.
benefits_summary: Promotes apprenticeship training by sharing the stipend cost with employers.
benefits_list: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of stipend share to apprentices (25% of stipend up to ₹1,500/month).
eligibility_criteria: Age: Minimum 14 years (18 for hazardous industries).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student/Trainee.
Registered on the Apprenticeship Portal.
how_to_apply: Register on the Apprenticeship India portal and apply for opportunities posted by establishments.

scheme_name: Visiting Advanced Joint Research (VAJRA) Faculty Scheme
objective: VAJRA Faculty Scheme is a dedicated program exclusively for overseas scientists and academicians with emphasis on NRI and Persons of Indian Origin/Overseas Citizen of India to work as adjunct/visiting faculty for a specific period of time in Indian Public funded academic and research institutions.
benefits_summary: Invites overseas scientists to contribute to research in Indian institutions.
benefits_list: Honorarium: $15,000 USD for the first month, $10,000 USD for subsequent months.
eligibility_criteria: Overseas Scientist/Academician (NRI/OCI/PIO/Foreign National).
Active researcher in Science/Technology.
Must have a host institution in India.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the SERB VAJRA portal along with the Indian Host Institution.

objective: The Awards are given to recognize excellence in research and innovation of faculty members/researchers of the Central Universities.
benefits_summary: Honors faculty members of Central Universities for outstanding research and innovation.
benefits_list: Categories: Research (Humanities/Arts/Social Sciences/Physical Sciences/Biological Sciences) and Technology Development.
eligibility_criteria: Faculty member of a Central University (where the President is the Visitor).
Significant contribution to research/innovation.
General eligibility criteria apply.
how_to_apply: Online application via the Rashtrapati Bhavan website during the nomination period.

scheme_name: Vocational Education and Training Loan Scheme (NBCFDC)
issuing_authority: National Backward Classes Finance & Development Corporation (NBCFDC)
objective: The Vocational Education and Training Loan Scheme provides financial assistance to youth of the target group to enhance their skills & employability.
benefits_summary: Provides loans to OBC students for pursuing vocational training courses.
benefits_list: Loan coverage for course fee and expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Member of Backward Classes (OBC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Admission in a vocational course (ITI/Polytechnic/etc.).
how_to_apply: Apply through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs).

objective: The scheme will be implemented for the benefit of the Scheduled Tribes as well as PTGs and can be taken up anywhere in the country but priority will be given to remote tribal areas/ areas inhabited by particularly vulnerable tribes/ and areas affected by extremist activities.
benefits_summary: Provides free vocational training to ST youth to upgrade their skills for employment/self-employment.
benefits_list: Allowance for raw material and tools.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST).
Applicant's age must be between 15 and 45 years.
Minimum qualification depending on trade.
how_to_apply: Enroll at the Vocational Training Center (VTC) run by NGOs/State Govt supported by the Ministry.

objective: The "WISE Fellowship for Ph.D. (WISE-PhD)" of the Department of Science and Technology aims to provide an opportunity to carry out Doctoral research in basic and applied sciences to women scientists and technologists between the age group of 27-45 years who want to pursue research in STEM fields.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for women scientists to pursue Ph.D. in Science and Engineering.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Age: 27 to 45 years.
Required education level: Postgraduate (M.Sc/M.Tech).
Must have registered for Ph.D.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the DST e-PMS portal.

objective: “WISE Post-Doctoral Fellowship” is a new programme of the Department of Science & Technology that aims to provide opportunities to women scientists & technologists between the age group of 27-60 years who want to continue their research as bench-level scientists in basic and applied sciences.
benefits_summary: Supports women scientists with Ph.D. to pursue post-doctoral research.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Age: 27 to 60 years.
Required education level: Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Applicant's employment status: Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DST e-PMS portal.

scheme_name: Welfare Measures for Children of Labourers (Financial Assistance for Education)
objective: This scheme aims to provide welfare measures in the form of educational stipends/ incentives for academic excellence/ and financial support for higher education to the children of SC/ST labourers who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and pursue higher education after 10th grade.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance for education to the children of Beedi, Cine, and Mine workers.
benefits_list: Scholarship ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹25,000 per annum based on course (Class 1 to Professional Degree).
Financial assistance for school uniforms and textbooks.
eligibility_criteria: Children of Beedi/Iron Ore/Manganese/Chrome/Mica/Limestone/Dolomite workers.
Parents must have at least 6 months of service.
Monthly income limit applies (e.g., ₹10,000).
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

issuing_authority: Ministry of Women and Child Development
objective: Women Helpline provides 24X7 emergency and non-emergency response through telephonic short-code 181 to women.
benefits_summary: A 24/7 toll-free helpline to support women in distress.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Any woman or girl facing violence or in distress.
Any citizen reporting on behalf of a woman.
how_to_apply: Dial 181 from any mobile or landline.
required_documents: None required to access the helpline.

objective: The “WISE-SCOPE Fellowship” Program was started by the Department of Science & Technology/ Government of India with the aims to provide opportunity to Women Scientists and Technologists who have completed Ph.D. and want to utilize their S&T knowledge and expertise for the benefit of society.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for women scientists to address societal challenges (like agriculture, health, energy) through S&T interventions.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Age: 27 to 60 years.
Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Must address a societal challenge.
how_to_apply: Apply online via DST e-PMS portal.
required_documents: Project Proposal focusing on societal application.

scheme_name: Women Involvement in Science and Engineering Research (WISER)
objective: The scheme “Women Involvement in Science and Engineering Research (WISER)” aims to build scientific capacity/ retain and promote women researchers in India & Germany by utilizing complementary expertise in science/ technology/ innovation and research partnerships.
benefits_summary: Facilitates lateral entry for women researchers into ongoing Indo-German R&D projects.
benefits_list: Travel funds for visits to Germany/India.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Regular position in Indian/German research institution.
Must join an existing IGSTC funded project.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the IGSTC portal.
required_documents: Consent from Project Investigators (PIs) of the ongoing project.

objective: A Research Grant by MoST for female candidates to pursue research in frontier areas of science and engineering/ preferably those having a break in career (minimum two years) and not having regular employment/ to explore possibility of re-entry into the profession.
benefits_summary: Supports women scientists with a career break to return to mainstream science (Basic/Applied Research).
benefits_list: Fellowship: ₹31,000 to ₹55,000 per month (based on qualification).
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Age: 27 to 57 years.
Career break required.
Qualification: M.Sc./Ph.D. in Science or B.E./B.Tech.
how_to_apply: Apply online via DST e-PMS portal.

objective: A Research Grant by MoST for female candidates with break in their career interested in addressing societal challenges through viable S&T interventions. Women scientists who wish to apply under this scheme are required to develop their own project/proposals for scientific and technological solutions
benefits_summary: Supports women scientists to work on S&T interventions for societal benefit (Technology Development/Demonstration).
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Age: 27 to 57 years.
Career break preferred.
Qualification: M.Sc./Ph.D./B.Tech.
how_to_apply: Apply online via DST e-PMS portal.

issuing_authority: Department of Science and Technology (DST) / TIFAC
objective: A scheme by MoST for female candidates with a break in their career interested in addressing societal challenges through viable S&T interventions. 1-year training is provided in Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs)/ patent search/ know-how/ drafting/ filing/ trademarks/ trade secrets/ and copyright.
benefits_summary: Provides training in Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) to women scientists for self-employment.
benefits_list: Stipend during training: ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per month.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Age: 27 to 45 years.
Qualification: M.Sc./B.Tech/MBBS/Ph.D.
Desire to work in IPR domain.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the TIFAC website when advertised.

scheme_name: WIDUSHI (Women’s Instinct for Developing and Ushering in Scientific Heights & Innovation)
objective: 'WIDUSHI' programme aims to encourage and support senior women scientists to conduct research in interdisciplinary areas of Science and Technology.
benefits_summary: Support for senior women scientists to act as mentors and conduct interdisciplinary research.
benefits_list: Opportunity to mentor young women scientists.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Senior Women Scientists (Age 57 to 62 years, retired/retiring).
Distinguished track record in R&D.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DST portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
objective: The Youth Hostel Scheme is a joint venture of the Central and State Governments of India. It was launched in the early 1960s to promote youth travel within the country by providing low-cost budget accommodation and facilities to students and youth groups traveling on excursions or study tours.
benefits_summary: Provides safe, hygienic, and affordable accommodation to traveling youth.
benefits_list: Common facilities like dining hall, library.
Promotes national integration through youth travel.
eligibility_criteria: Youth, Students, Travelers.
Priority given to youth groups and educational tours.
No specific income or employment criteria.
how_to_apply: Bookings can be made directly at the Youth Hostel or via the Ministry's website.

scheme_name: “Ishan Uday” Special Scholarship Scheme For North Eastern Region
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Education and the University Grants Commission. Taking special interest with regard to the promotion of higher education in the NER/ i.e. for improving the GER/ promoting higher education/ and encouraging children belonging to the economically weaker sections.
benefits_summary: Provides 10,000 fresh scholarships every year to students from NER for general degree, technical, and professional courses.
benefits_list: Scholarship amount: ₹5,400 per month for general degree courses.
Scholarship amount: ₹7,800 per month for technical/medical/professional/paramedical courses.
eligibility_criteria: Domicile of North Eastern Region (NER).
Passed Class XII or equivalent in the preceding year.
Secured admission in a general degree/technical/professional course.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹4,50,000.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

objective: Restructured National Bamboo Mission was launched during 2018-19 as a Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) after the historical amendment of the Indian Forest Act during 2017 to exclude bamboo out from definition of tree.
benefits_summary: Promotes holistic growth of the bamboo sector by adopting an area-based regionally differentiated strategy.
benefits_list: Subsidy for bamboo plantation (50% of cost).
Assistance for setting up nurseries and tissue culture labs.
Support for post-harvest management and processing units.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers, FPOs, SHGs, Cooperatives.
Entrepreneurs/Private entities setting up bamboo based industries.
Government agencies.
how_to_apply: Submit proposals to the State Bamboo Mission (usually under Agriculture/Forest Department).

issuing_authority: National Bee Board (NBB), Ministry of Agriculture
objective: National Beekeeping & Honey Mission (NBHM) was announced as part of the Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyaan and aims for the overall promotion & development of scientific beekeeping in the country to achieve the goal of ‘Sweet Revolution’ which is being implemented through National Bee Board (NBB).
benefits_summary: Promotes scientific beekeeping for income generation and increased crop productivity through pollination.
benefits_list: Assistance for setting up Integrated Beekeeping Development Centres (IBDC).
Subsidy for bee boxes, colonies, and equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers, Beekeepers, SHGs, FPOs.
Societies/Cooperatives engaged in beekeeping.
Should be registered with National Bee Board.
how_to_apply: Register on the Madhukranti Portal and apply through NBB or State Horticulture Department.

scheme_name: National Centre For Communication Security (NCCS) Internship Scheme
objective: The National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS) Internship Scheme allows young talent to be associated with the NCCS work for mutual benefit. It will enable NCCS to interact with young scholars and to get fresh ideas and study/ research support from the field of academics.
benefits_summary: Internship opportunity for students to work on telecom security standards and certification.
benefits_list: Hands-on experience in telecom security testing and certification.
Duration: Minimum 2 months, up to 6 months.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens.
Final year B.E./B.Tech (Electronics/Comm/IT/CS) or M.E./M.Tech students.
Minimum 60% marks in previous semesters.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form to NCCS, Bengaluru via email/post.

scheme_name: National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS) Research Associates Scheme
objective: NCCS engages young scholars as Research Associates to contribute in development of Telecom Security eco system with in India by providing an opportunity to them to work in areas like Telecom Security Standard development/ Lab recognition and Security Certification of telecom products.
benefits_summary: Engages Research Associates to work on telecom security standards and eco-system development.
benefits_list: Opportunity to work on national telecom security projects.
eligibility_criteria: B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech/Ph.D. in Electronics/Comm/IT/CS.
Age Limit: 28 years (Level 1), 32 years (Level 2).
Relevant research/work experience.
how_to_apply: Apply against the advertisement issued by NCCS/DoT.

scheme_name: National Centre for Earth Science Studies (NCESS) Internship Scheme
objective: The “National Centre for Earth Science Studies Internship” scheme by the Earth System Science Organization/ Ministry of Earth Sciences/ provides various opportunities for school as well as college students to familiarise and/or pursue research on different branches of earth sciences.
benefits_summary: Provides internship/dissertation opportunities for students in Earth Sciences.
benefits_list: Access to NCESS labs and library.
No stipend/financial assistance is usually provided.
eligibility_criteria: Post-graduate students in Geology, Geophysics, Environmental Science, etc.
Selected B.Tech students.
Recommendation from the Head of the Department.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NCESS website.

objective: A family welfare scheme under the umbrella scheme ‘National Social Assistance Programme’ (NSAP) by MoRD. In this scheme/ financial assistance is provided to the bereaved households in case of the death of the primary breadwinner irrespective of the cause of death.
benefits_summary: Provides a one-time lump sum cash assistance to BPL families on the death of the primary breadwinner.
eligibility_criteria: Bereaved household must be Below Poverty Line (BPL).
Deceased was the primary breadwinner (male or female).
Deceased age was between 18 and 59 years.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Gram Panchayat or Municipality. Submit forms to the Block Development Officer.

scheme_name: National Fellowship & Scholarship For Higher Education Of ST Students (NFST)
objective: It is a Central Sector Scheme/ fully funded by Ministry of Tribal Affairs to grant scholarship to meritorious Scheduled Tribe (ST) students for pursuing higher education in India.
benefits_summary: Combines fellowship (for M.Phil/Ph.D.) and scholarship (for Top Class Education) for ST students.
benefits_list: Scholarship: Full tuition fee + Living expenses + Books/Stationery grant (for students in Top Class Institutes like IITs/IIMs).
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST).
For Scholarship: Annual family income < ₹6 Lakhs; Admitted to notified Top Class Institutes.
For Fellowship: Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D.; No income ceiling.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or Ministry's portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment / UGC
objective: National Fellowship for OBC Students was launched by the University Grants Commission (UGC) during the financial year 2014-15 to increase the opportunities for the students of Other Backward Classes (OBCs) to pursue higher education leading to acquiring degrees such as M.Phil. and Ph.D.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for OBC students to pursue M.Phil and Ph.D. degrees.
benefits_list: Fellowship amount equivalent to UGC JRF/SRF.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to OBC category (Non-Creamy Layer).
Qualified UGC-NET or CSIR-UGC NET.
Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D.
Transgender candidates also eligible.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC/NTA portal (selection based on NET merit).

scheme_name: National Fellowship for Students with Disabilities (NFPwD)
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: A Scheme "National Fellowship For Students With Disabilities" was initiated by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities under the "Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment".
benefits_summary: Provides fellowships to students with disabilities to pursue M.Phil and Ph.D.
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D. in UGC recognized university.
Selection often based on UGC-NET/CSIR-NET.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the NFPwD portal or through UGC/NTA notification.

objective: National Food Security Mission was launched in 2007-08 to increase the production of rice/ wheat and pulses. Later/ coarse cereals/ nutri cereals/ commercial crops (sugarcane/ jute/ and cotton) were also added to the mission.
benefits_summary: Increases production of food grains through area expansion and productivity enhancement.
benefits_list: Distribution of High Yielding Variety (HYV) seeds.
Subsidy on farm machinery and micronutrients.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers in identified districts.
Priority to small/marginal farmers.
how_to_apply: Contact the Block Agriculture Officer or District Agriculture Department.

scheme_name: National Grassroots Innovation and Outstanding Traditional Knowledge Awards
objective: National Innovation Foundation (NIF)-India/ an autonomous body of the Department of Science and Technology/ Government of India/ invites entries of unaided technological innovations and traditional knowledge developed at grassroots by an individual (Citizen of India) or group.
benefits_summary: Recognizes and rewards grassroots innovators and traditional knowledge holders.
benefits_list: Cash Awards (Lifetime Achievement, National, State, Consolation).
eligibility_criteria: Grassroots Innovators (Farmers, Artisans, Mechanics, etc.).
Must be unaided technological innovation.
No professional/technical degree holder eligible for student awards.
how_to_apply: Submit entries online via the NIF website or by post.

scheme_name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Direct Benefit to Artisans (Indigent Circumstances)
objective: The scheme "Direct Benefit to Artisans - Supports to Artisans in Indigent Circumstances" is designed to give a boost to the handicraft sector in India. Assistance of upto ₹8,000/- per month is granted to the Artisans in Indigent Circumstances in the form of monthly allowance/lump sum grant/ both.
benefits_summary: Provides monthly pension/allowance to master craftspersons in indigent circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Recipient of Shilp Guru/National Award/Merit Certificate/State Award.
Age: Above 60 years.
Annual income not exceeding ₹50,000 (criteria revised periodically).
In indigent circumstances.
how_to_apply: Apply to the DC (Handicrafts) office with supporting documents.

scheme_name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Infrastructure Support - EMPORIA
objective: In the scheme "Infrastructure and Technology Support: EMPORIA" assistance would be provided for the setting up of emporia. These would be set up in commercially viable locations in the implementing agencies' own/rented buildings.
benefits_summary: Funds the setting up or renovation of Emporia to market handicraft products.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for renovation/interior decoration of Emporia.
Maximum assistance up to ₹5 Crores depending on category.
eligibility_criteria: State Handicraft Development Corporations.
Apex Cooperative Societies.
Central/State Govt Agencies.
how_to_apply: Implementing agencies submit proposals to the O/o DC (Handicrafts).

scheme_name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Infrastructure Support - URBAN HAAT
objective: The scheme "Infrastructure & Technology Support: URBAN HAAT" is introduced is to set up a permanent marketing infrastructure in towns/ metropolitan cities to provide direct marketing facilities to handicrafts artisans/handloom weavers/ enabling them to sell their products to wider target audience.
benefits_summary: Sets up permanent marketing infrastructure (Haats) for artisans to sell directly to consumers.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for land development, stalls, dormitories.
Provides a platform for cultural tourism.
eligibility_criteria: State Govt Corporations/Agencies.
Municipal Corporations.
Must provide developed land.
how_to_apply: Agencies submit proposals to DC (Handicrafts).

objective: To motivate farmers to adopt chemical free farming and enhance the reach of natural farming/ the Government has formulated National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) as a separate and independent scheme from 2023-24 by up scaling the Bhartiya Prakritik Krishi Paddati (BPKP).
benefits_summary: Promotes chemical-free natural farming based on cow-based agriculture (Jeevamrut/Beejamrut).
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹15,000 per hectare for 3 years.
Support for capacity building and training.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers willing to adopt natural farming.
Clusters of farmers preferred.
how_to_apply: Contact the District Agriculture Officer or ATMA office.

scheme_name: National Overseas Scholarship For Scheduled Caste Etc. Candidates
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: A scholarship scheme by MoSJE for regular/ full-time students from Scheduled Castes/ Denotified Nomadic and Semi Nomadic Tribes/ Landless Agricultural Labourers and Traditional Artisans; to obtain higher education viz./ Master degree or Ph.D courses by studying abroad
benefits_summary: Scholarship for SC students to pursue Masters and Ph.D. abroad.
benefits_list: Tuition Fees + Maintenance Allowance ($15,400 USD/year approx).
Contingency Allowance, Visa Fees, Air Passage.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to SC, Denotified Nomadic Tribes, Landless Agricultural Labourers, or Traditional Artisans.
Annual family income < ₹8,00,000.
Age: Below 35 years.
Min 60% marks in qualifying exam.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the NOS portal of the Ministry.

scheme_name: National Overseas Scholarship For Scheduled Tribes (ST) Students
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs for regular/ full-time ST students to obtain higher education (post-graduate courses viz. Masters/ Ph.D. and Post-Doctoral Research) from foreign institutes/universities. The Scheme is implemented through the Indian Embassies.
benefits_summary: Financial support for ST students to pursue higher studies abroad.
benefits_list: Support for Masters, Ph.D., and Post-Doctoral research.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST).
Annual family income < ₹6,00,000.
Age: Below 35 years.
Min 55% marks in qualifying exam.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Ministry of Tribal Affairs scholarship portal.

scheme_name: National Overseas Scholarship For Students With Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment for regular/ full-time students with disabilities to obtain higher education viz./ Master's degree/ or Ph.D. courses from foreign universities/ in one of the specified fields of study.
benefits_summary: Funding for students with disabilities to study abroad (Masters/Ph.D.).
benefits_list: Covers Tuition Fees, Maintenance Allowance, Contingency.
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Age: Below 35 years.
Annual family income < ₹8,00,000 (Income ceiling revised periodically).
Min 55% marks.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DEPwD scholarship portal.

scheme_name: National Pension Scheme For Traders And Self Employed Persons (NPS-Traders)
objective: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme by the Ministry of Labour and Employment. This scheme is meant for old age protection and social security of Small Scale Traders and Retailers. The beneficiary would receive a minimum assured pension of ₹ 3000/- per month after attaining the age of 60 yrs.
benefits_summary: Voluntary pension scheme for shopkeepers, traders, and self-employed persons.
benefits_list: Assured monthly pension of ₹3,000 after age 60.
Matching contribution by the Central Government.
Social security for the unorganized sector.
eligibility_criteria: Shopkeepers/Retail Traders/Self-employed.
Annual turnover not exceeding ₹1.5 Crore.
Age: 18 to 40 years.
Should not be an income tax payer or member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS.
how_to_apply: Enroll at Common Service Centres (CSCs) or online via Maandhan portal.

objective: The “National Post Doctoral Fellowship (N-PDF)” was launched by the Science and Engineering Research Board/ Government of India to identify motivated young researchers and provide them support for doing research in frontier areas of science and engineering.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for young researchers to conduct post-doctoral research in science and engineering.
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D./M.D./M.S. degree holder.
Age: Below 35 years (relaxable for SC/ST/OBC/Women).
Must have an identified mentor and host institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the SERB portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
objective: Through this scheme/ fellowships will be provided for pursuing higher studies/ advanced research in renewable energy. The fellowship scheme is directly aligned with the overall requirement of renewable energy/ research thrust areas identified by MNRE.
benefits_summary: Fellowships for M.Tech, M.Sc, and Ph.D. students in Renewable Energy.
benefits_list: JRF/SRF/RA fellowships as per DST norms.
Support for research in Solar, Wind, Bio-energy, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing M.Tech/Ph.D. in Renewable Energy in MNRE supported institutes.
GATE/NET qualification preferred.
how_to_apply: Institutions select candidates and apply to MNRE for funding.

issuing_authority: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
objective: The "National Renewable Energy Internship Scheme" by MNRE provides internship opportunities to facilitate students pursuing undergraduate/graduate/post-graduate degrees (Engineering/ Science/ Management/ Law/ etc) or research scholars enrolled in recognized institutes/universities.
benefits_summary: Internship for students to gain exposure to the renewable energy sector.
eligibility_criteria: Pursuing Engineering/Science/Management/Law/Finance.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
Interest in Renewable Energy.
how_to_apply: Apply online or send application to HRD Division, MNRE.

objective: The scheme “National Savings Certificates (VIII-Issue) Scheme” was launched by the Department of Economic Affairs/ Ministry of Finance/ Government of India to promote a culture of long-term savings among individuals.
benefits_summary: A fixed-income investment scheme available at Post Offices with tax benefits.
benefits_list: Guaranteed Interest (currently approx 7.7% p.a., compounded annually, payable at maturity).
Can be pledged as security for loans.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens (Adults).
Minors (through guardians).
No maximum limit on investment.
how_to_apply: Visit any Post Office branch.

scheme_name: National Scheme Of Incentive To Girls For Secondary Education (NSIGSE)
issuing_authority: Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education
objective: The National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education promotes the girl child's enrolment of 14-18 years age group at secondary stage/ who passes class 8th and subsequently drops out for various socio-economic reasons.
benefits_summary: Incentivizes girl students from SC/ST communities to complete secondary education.
benefits_list: Fixed Deposit of ₹3,000 in the name of the eligible girl child.
Maturity amount (principal + interest) payable upon passing Class 10 and attaining 18 years of age.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST girls passing Class 8.
All girls passing Class 8 from Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas (KGBVs).
Enrolled in Class 9 in Government/State-aided schools.
Unmarried.
how_to_apply: Schools upload the details of eligible girls on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: National Scheme Of Welfare Of Fishermen
issuing_authority: Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
objective: The National Scheme of Welfare of Fishermen provides financial assistance to fishermen. They can use these for constructing houses and community halls for both recreation and work purposes. Further/ through the amount availed under this scheme/ fishermen can install tube wells.
benefits_summary: Provides welfare measures like housing, insurance, and savings relief to fishermen.
benefits_list: Group Accident Insurance: ₹5 Lakhs against death/permanent disability.
Saving-cum-Relief: Financial assistance of ₹4,500 during the lean period.
Housing: Assistance for constructing houses for fishermen.
eligibility_criteria: Full-time active fishermen.
Member of a Functional Fishermen Cooperative Society.
Below Poverty Line (BPL) living in coastal/inland areas.
Age: 18 to 60 years.
how_to_apply: Apply through the local Fisheries Department or Fishermen Cooperative Society.

objective: The primary goal of this scheme is to bridge the gap in practical experience for fresh Graduate Engineers/ Diploma holders/ and 10+2 Vocational pass-outs in approximately 10/000 industrial establishments. The apprentices undergo a one-year training period/ receiving a monthly stipend.
benefits_summary: Provides one-year apprenticeship training to fresh graduates and diploma holders in engineering/technology.
eligibility_criteria: Degree/Diploma in Engineering/Technology or Pharmacy or HMCT.
Pass-out within the last 3 years.
Not have undergone previous apprenticeship training.
Age: Minimum 18 years.
how_to_apply: Register and apply online through the NATS portal.

scheme_name: National Scholarship For Post Graduate Studies
objective: A scheme started by Ministry of Education for the student Higher education. It aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet their day-to-day expenses while pursuing PG studies.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for students pursuing postgraduate studies in recognized universities.
benefits_list: Duration: 10 months in a year for 2 years.
eligibility_criteria: Pursuing first PG degree in a recognized university.
Age: Below 30 years.
Merit-based selection.
Not receiving other scholarships.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

objective: The main aim of the scheme is to recognise active eminent senior resident Indian superannuated scientists for their outstanding contributions both nationally and internationally/ in the areas of Science/ Technology/ Engineering/ Mathematics (STEM) & Medicine/ to promote excellence and growth in R&D.
benefits_summary: Honorarium and research grant for distinguished retired scientists to continue leadership in science.
eligibility_criteria: Superannuated eminent Indian scientist.
Fellow of at least two National Science Academies.
Outstanding track record in R&D.
how_to_apply: Nominations invited from Presidents of Science Academies or Heads of Premier Institutes via SERB portal.

objective: The main aim of the scheme is to recognize active eminent senior resident Indian superannuated scientists for their outstanding contributions both nationally and internationally/ in the areas of Science/ Technology/ Engineering/ Mathematics (STEM) & Medicine/ to promote excellence and growth in R&D.
benefits_summary: Recognizes scientific excellence in superannuated scientists and supports their continued research.
eligibility_criteria: Superannuated eminent Indian scientist.
Fellow of at least one National Science Academy.
Proven record of excellence in scientific research.
how_to_apply: Nominations invited via SERB online portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
objective: The “National Solar Science Fellowship Programme” of the Ministry of New & Renewable Energy was launched in February 2011. The aim of the National Solar Science Fellowship Programme is to provide a platform to top-quality scientists and engineers in the area of solar energy research.
benefits_summary: High-value fellowship for scientists to conduct advanced research in solar energy.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Scientist with Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Age: Below 40 years (relaxable for women/SC/ST).
Significant research publications in Solar Energy.
how_to_apply: Apply to MNRE against the advertisement.

issuing_authority: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: The National Startup Awards by Startup India aims to recognize and reward outstanding startups and ecosystem enablers building innovative products or solutions and scalable enterprises.
benefits_summary: Recognizes and rewards outstanding startups and ecosystem enablers.
benefits_list: Cash Prize: ₹5 Lakhs for winning startup.
Cash Prize: ₹15 Lakhs for winning incubator/accelerator.
Mentorship, Pilot opportunities, and Investor connect.
eligibility_criteria: DPIIT recognized Startup.
Entity must submit Audited Financial Statements for last 3 years.
Must have a ready product/service in the market.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Startup India portal.

objective: Merit based support through National Talent Scholarship to the Under-graduates and Post graduate students seeking admission in Agricultural Universities in a State other than the State of their domicile/ through ICAR All India Entrance Examination (AIEE).
benefits_summary: Scholarship for PG students admitted in Agricultural Universities outside their home state.
benefits_list: Paid for the duration of the PG course.
eligibility_criteria: Admitted to a PG degree program in an Agricultural University located outside their domicile state.
Must have qualified ICAR-AIEEA (PG).
Maintain good academic standing.
how_to_apply: Automatically considered during ICAR counseling and admission process.

objective: National Talent Scholarship (NTS) was launched by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research for students pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree program in an ICAR-recognized agricultural university located outside of his/her state of domicile.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for UG students admitted in Agricultural Universities outside their home state.
benefits_list: Encourages students to pursue agriculture education across India.
Paid for the duration of the UG course.
eligibility_criteria: Admitted to a UG degree program in an Agricultural University located outside their domicile state.
Must have qualified ICAR-AIEEA (UG).
Maintain minimum OGPA/CGPA.
how_to_apply: Automatically considered during ICAR counseling and admission.

objective: The National Technology Awards refer to an event or program that recognizes and celebrates technological innovation and excellence across various industries within a particular country.
benefits_summary: Awards industrial concerns for successful commercialization of indigenous technology.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Industrial Concerns/Startups/MSMEs.
Must have successfully commercialized an indigenous technology.
Technology must be innovative.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the TDB website when nominations are open.

issuing_authority: Department of Water Resources, Ministry of Jal Shakti
objective: The objective of the National Water Awards is to encourage the stakeholders to adopt a holistic approach toward water resource management in the country as surface water and groundwater play a significant role in the water cycle.
benefits_summary: Recognizes exemplary work in water conservation and management by districts, states, and organizations.
benefits_list: Cash prizes for certain categories (e.g., Schools, Gram Panchayats, NGOs).
eligibility_criteria: States, Districts, Village Panchayats, ULBs, Schools, Industries, NGOs, etc.
Must have done significant work in water conservation/management.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Water Awards portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
objective: This award is to motivate young persons to achieve excellence in the field of national development or social service and to develop in them a sense of responsibility to the community and thus to improve their potential as good citizens.
benefits_summary: Awards youth for outstanding social service and contribution to national development.
eligibility_criteria: Individuals aged 15-29 years.
Voluntary Organizations working with youth for at least 3 years.
Must have rendered distinguished voluntary service.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Youth Awards portal.

objective: A volunteership scheme by Ministry of Youth Affairs And Sports to tap the potential of the disciplined and dedicated youth In the age group of 18 to 29 years who have the inclination and spirit to engage in nation building exercises/ to serve up to two years in Nation Building activities.
benefits_summary: Engages youth as volunteers for nation-building activities.
benefits_list: Opportunity to work in community development, health, literacy campaigns.
eligibility_criteria: Age: 18 to 29 years.
Education: Minimum Class 10 pass.
Unemployed youth willing to volunteer.
Not a student.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the NYKS website or submit application to the District Youth Officer.

objective: Introduced in 2009-10/ the “Netaji Subhas - ICAR International Fellowship” is a scheme by to develop competent human resource that are trained in the identified best laboratories in the world (for Indian candidates) and similarly expose overseas candidates to the best Agriculture Universities.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for Ph.D. in agriculture for Indian students abroad and overseas students in India.
benefits_list: Fellowship: $2,000 per month (for Indians abroad) / ₹40,000 per month (for foreigners in India).
eligibility_criteria: Master's degree in Agriculture/Allied Sciences.
Age limit: 35 years.
Admission in identified overseas/Indian university.
how_to_apply: Apply to ICAR when the notification is released.

issuing_authority: National Backward Classes Finance & Development Corporation (NBCFDC)
objective: A term loan scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment for women entrepreneurs from backward classes to obtain a loan of up to ₹2,00,000/- @ 5% per annum/ thereby providing them social & financial security.
benefits_summary: Provides term loans to women from Backward Classes for self-employment.
eligibility_criteria: Women belonging to Backward Classes (OBC).
Annual family income less than ₹3,00,000.
Age: 18 to 55 years.
how_to_apply: Apply through the State Channelizing Agency (SCA) of NBCFDC.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (National TB Elimination Program)
objective: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare/ Government of India has announced the scheme for incentives for nutritional support to TB patients. This scheme will be called “Nikshay Poshan Yojana”.
benefits_summary: Provides nutritional support to TB patients during the course of their treatment.
benefits_list: Paid via DBT to the patient's bank account.
Helps meet nutritional needs to aid recovery.
eligibility_criteria: All notified TB patients (Public and Private sector).
Must be registered on the Nikshay portal.
Valid bank account.
how_to_apply: Enrollment happens automatically upon notification of TB cases. Bank details must be updated in Nikshay by health staff.

issuing_authority: The National Trust (Ministry of Social Justice)
objective: Niramaya Health Insurance Scheme /launched by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities/ aims to provide affordable health insurance to the Persons with Disabilities (PwDs).
benefits_summary: Provides health insurance coverage up to ₹1 Lakh for persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation, and Multiple Disabilities.
benefits_list: Health cover of ₹1.0 Lakh per year.
eligibility_criteria: Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Intellectual Disability, or Multiple Disabilities.
Registered with the National Trust.
how_to_apply: Apply through a registered NGO (SNAC/RO) of the National Trust.

objective: One Nation/ One Ration Card is Aadhaar-based national ration card portability scheme to ensure food security for all/ including internal migrants within India/ under which beneficiaries can purchase subsidized food anywhere in India.
benefits_summary: Allows ration card holders to lift their entitled food grains from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) across India.
benefits_list: Migrant workers can buy ration anywhere.
eligibility_criteria: Valid Ration Card holder (NFSA beneficiary).
Ration Card seeded with Aadhaar.
Migrants moving to other states.
how_to_apply: No separate application needed. Ensure Aadhaar seeding with Ration Card.

objective: The objective of the scheme is to build national capacity in frontier areas of Science and Engineering/ which are of interest to India by providing research training to PhD students admitted in the Indian institutions in overseas universities / institutions of repute.
benefits_summary: Provides opportunity for Indian Ph.D. scholars to conduct research in overseas universities for a period of up to 12 months.
eligibility_criteria: Indian citizen registered for full-time Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Completed course work.
Acceptance from overseas host.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the SERB portal.
required_documents: No Objection Certificate from Indian Institute.

objective: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy/ Ministry of Education. Under this scheme/ one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government – aided schools. The Scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Education.
benefits_summary: Provides free hot cooked meals to school children to improve nutritional status and enrollment.
benefits_list: One hot cooked meal on school days.
Nutritional norms: 450 calories/12g protein (Primary), 700 calories/20g protein (Upper Primary).
eligibility_criteria: Children studying in Classes I-VIII in Government/Govt-aided schools.
Children in Madarsas/Maqtabs supported under SSA.
how_to_apply: Enrollment in the school automatically provides eligibility.

scheme_name: PM Street Vendor’s AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
objective: PM SVANidhi is a micro-credit scheme launched on 1st June 2020 by the MoHUA to provide handholding support to street vendors. The scheme facilitates a working capital collateral-free loan of ₹10,000/ with subsequent loans of ₹20,000 and ₹50,000 with a 7% interest subsidy.
benefits_summary: Collateral-free working capital loans for street vendors to restart their businesses.
benefits_list: Interest Subsidy of 7% on regular repayment.
eligibility_criteria: Street vendors vending in urban areas.
Possession of Vending Certificate/ID Card or Letter of Recommendation (LoR) from ULB.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the PM SVANidhi portal or through Common Service Centres (CSCs).

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: The scheme "PM Vishwakarma " by the Ministry of Micro/ Small & Medium Enterprises/ is a new scheme that envisages providing end-to-end holistic support to traditional artisans and craftspeople in scaling up their conventional products and services.
benefits_summary: Supports traditional artisans and craftspeople with toolkit incentives, loans, and skill training.
benefits_list: Recognition: PM Vishwakarma Certificate & ID Card.
Collateral-free Enterprise Development Loans (up to ₹3 Lakhs at 5% interest).
eligibility_criteria: Artisan/Craftsperson working in 18 notified trades (Carpenter, Blacksmith, Potter, etc.).
Age: Minimum 18 years.
Should not have availed similar loan schemes in the past 5 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PM Vishwakarma portal or at CSCs. Verification is done by Gram Panchayat/ULB.

scheme_name: PM – Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
issuing_authority: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
objective: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana is a government scheme that aims to provide free electricity to households in India. It incentivizes the installation of rooftop solar panels.
benefits_summary: Subsidizes rooftop solar installation to provide free electricity to households.
benefits_list: Subsidy: ₹30,000 for 1 kW, ₹60,000 for 2 kW systems.
Free electricity up to 300 units per month.
Loan facility available at low interest rates.
eligibility_criteria: Indian citizen owning a house with a suitable roof.
Valid electricity connection.
No previous subsidy for solar panels availed.
how_to_apply: Register on the National Portal for Rooftop Solar (pmsuryaghar.gov.in).

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC/ EBC and DNT Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to the OBC/ EBC and DNT students studying at post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education.
benefits_summary: Financial support for OBC, EBC, and DNT students pursuing higher education.
benefits_list: Group A, B, C, D course fees coverage (ceilings apply).
eligibility_criteria: Belong to OBC, EBC, or DNT category.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Studying in Class 11 to Ph.D. level in government/recognized institutes.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Pre-Matric Scholarship for OBC/ EBC and DNT Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
