objective: The objective of the Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Pre-Matric Scholarship for OBC/ EBC/ and DNT students (who are not covered under SC/ ST/ or OBC) is welfare and motivation to the children of these categories by providing financial assistance at pre-matriculation or secondary stage.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for students in Class 9 and 10 to curb dropout rates.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to OBC, EBC, or DNT category.
Studying in Class 9 or 10 in a Govt/Aided school.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or State Portal.

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Top Class College Education for OBC/ EBC and DNT Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The objective of the scheme is to recognize and promote quality education amongst Students belonging to OBC/ EBC and DNT categories by providing full financial support. The Scheme will cover OBC/EBC/DNT students for pursuing studies beyond Class XII.
benefits_summary: Provides full financial support for OBC/EBC/DNT students to pursue higher education in premier institutes (like IITs, IIMs, AIIMS).
benefits_list: Full Tuition Fee coverage (up to ₹2.00 Lakhs per annum).
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Applicant must belong to OBC/EBC/DNT category.
Admitted to notified Top Class Institutes.
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Top Class School Education for OBC/ EBC and DNT Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The objective of the scheme is to provide premium education to the meritorious students belonging to OBC/ EBC/ and DNT categories by funding their education from Class 9 onwards till they complete Class 12.
benefits_summary: Funds education for meritorious OBC/EBC/DNT students in top-class schools from Class 9 to 12.
benefits_list: Maximum Ceiling: ₹75,000 per annum (Class 9 & 10) / ₹1,25,000 per annum (Class 11 & 12).
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Belong to OBC/EBC/DNT.
Studying in a notified Top Class School.
Required education level: Secondary (Class 9-12).
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP). Selection via YASASVI Entrance Test (YET) conducted by NTA.

scheme_name: PRISM (Promoting Innovations in Individuals/ Start-ups and MSMEs): Category-1: Proof of Concept/ Prototypes/ Models
issuing_authority: Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR)
objective: The scheme aims to support individual innovators which will enable them to achieve the agenda of inclusive development - one of the thrust areas of the XIIth five-year plan (2012-2017). The proposals shall preferably be considered in the following focus sectors: Green technology/ Clean energy/ etc.
benefits_summary: Provides grants to individual innovators to convert innovative ideas into prototypes/models.
eligibility_criteria: Any Indian Citizen (Student, Professional, Individual Innovator).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student/Self-employed.
Must have an innovative idea.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to the nearest TOCIC (TePP Outreach cum Cluster Innovation Centre).

objective: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL)/ a prestigious institution under the Department of Space/ Government of India/ launched the Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) Program.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for students to pursue Ph.D. in Physics, Astronomy, Space Sciences, etc. at PRL.
eligibility_criteria: M.Sc. in Physics/Space Sciences/Atmospheric Sciences/Geology OR M.E./M.Tech.
Valid GATE/CSIR-UGC NET/JEST score.
Age Limit: 28 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PRL website during the admission cycle.

objective: The Vikram Sarabhai Postdoctoral Fellowship (VISHWAS) program is an initiative by the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) to honor the legacy of Prof. Vikram Sarabhai by supporting young doctorates to pursue groundbreaking research in fundamental science with societal impact.
benefits_summary: Prestigious postdoctoral fellowship for independent research at PRL.
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D. degree (thesis submitted or awarded).
Excellent academic record and research publications.
Age Limit: 35 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the PRL website.

scheme_name: Padho Pardesh (Interest Subsidy on Educational Loans for Overseas Studies)
objective: Padho Pardesh is a scheme of interest subsidy on educational loans for overseas studies for the students belonging to the minority communities. The scheme was launched by the Ministry of Minority Affairs in 2006 under the Prime Minister's New 15 Point Programme for the Welfare of Minorities.
benefits_summary: Provides interest subsidy on education loans for minority students studying abroad (Note: Scheme discontinued from 2022-23; benefits continue for existing beneficiaries).
benefits_list: 100% Interest Subsidy on Education Loan during the moratorium period.
Reduces financial burden of overseas education.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Minority Community.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000.
Admitted to Masters/M.Phil/Ph.D. abroad.
availed loan from IBA member bank.
how_to_apply: Apply through the lending bank. Bank claims subsidy from Canara Bank (Nodal Bank).

scheme_name: Paired Early Career Fellowship in Applied Research (PECFAR)
objective: PECFAR is a fellowship to facilitate a two-way exchange of young early-career Indian and German researchers through short visits for networking and exploring avenues for collaboration and innovation in applied research.
benefits_summary: Supports short research visits for early career researchers between India and Germany.
benefits_list: Opportunity to find a research partner.
eligibility_criteria: Early Career Researchers (Ph.D. or Post-Doc).
Holding a regular position in academic/research institution.
Age limit: 35 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the IGSTC portal.

scheme_name: Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National Welfare Fund For Sportspersons (PDUNWFS)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
objective: The scheme of PDUNWFS is being implemented with the objective of providing financial assistance to outstanding sportspersons/family members living in indigent circumstances for various purposes like ameliorate condition/ medical treatment/ injuries/ procurement of sports equipment etc.
benefits_summary: Financial aid to sportspersons in distress or indigent conditions.
benefits_list: Assistance up to ₹5 Lakhs for medical treatment/injuries.
Monthly pension for retired sportspersons in indigent conditions.
Assistance to families of deceased sportspersons.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding Sportspersons (National/International level).
Living in indigent circumstances (Income criteria applies).
Injured during training/competition.
how_to_apply: Apply to the Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports via prescribed proforma.

objective: The Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)/ launched in 2015/ is an extended component of Soil Health Management (SHM) under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS)/ National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA).
benefits_summary: Promotes organic farming through cluster approach and Participatory Guarantee System (PGS) certification.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹50,000 per hectare for 3 years.
₹31,000 (of the above) directly for organic inputs (bio-fertilizers, etc.).
Support for packing, marketing, and branding.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers willing to adopt organic farming.
Must form a cluster of 20 hectares (min 50 farmers).
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
how_to_apply: Contact the Regional Council or District Agriculture Department to join a cluster.

scheme_name: Pension Scheme for the Tappers in Small Rubber Plantations
issuing_authority: Rubber Board, Ministry of Commerce & Industry
objective: The scheme “Pension Scheme for the Tappers in Small Rubber Plantations” was launched by the Rubber Board/ Ministry of Commerce & Industry/ Government of India to motivate tappers and retain them in the tapping job.
benefits_summary: Social security pension for rubber tappers working in the unorganized sector.
eligibility_criteria: Rubber Tapper working in small rubber holdings.
Registered with the Rubber Board.
Age: 18 to 55 years (for enrollment).
how_to_apply: Enroll through the Rubber Board Field Office or Rubber Producers' Society (RPS).

scheme_name: Persons With Disabilities Scheme In Colleges: Financial Assistance To Visually Challenged Teachers
objective: The Scheme has been formulated to help visually challenged permanent teachers to pursue teaching and research with the help of a Reader and by using teaching and learning aids by way of providing Reader’s Allowance and funds for the purchase of Braille books/ recorded materials etc.
benefits_summary: Financial support to visually challenged teachers for hiring readers and purchasing aids.
benefits_list: Grant for instructional aids (Braille books, software, etc.).
eligibility_criteria: Permanent Teacher in a UGC recognized College/University.
Visually Challenged (Blind/Low Vision).
Age: Below 50 years.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Principal/Registrar of the College/University to UGC.

scheme_name: Persons With Disabilities Scheme In Colleges: Higher Education For Persons With Special Needs (HEPSN)
objective: The Persons with Disabilities Act 1995 indicates that differently-abled persons should have access to education at all levels. In the higher education sector/ the University Grants Commission (UGC) is supporting universities and colleges in the country to involve in special education activities.
benefits_summary: Supports colleges to create an enabling environment for students with disabilities.
benefits_list: Making campus barrier-free (ramps, rails, special toilets).
eligibility_criteria: UGC recognized Colleges/Universities.
Must have PwD students enrolled.
Beneficiaries: Students with disabilities.
how_to_apply: Colleges submit proposals to UGC.

scheme_name: Phase-II Of The Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Programme
issuing_authority: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
objective: Phase-II of the Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Programme/ this programme is introduced by Ministry of New & Renewable Energy(MNRE)/ GoI with JAKEDA as a nodal agency in the UT of J&K for achieving cumulative capacity of 220MW under 20MW Residential Scheme and 200 MW Jammu Solar City “Solar City.
benefits_summary: Promotes installation of Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Systems in the residential sector.
benefits_list: Central Financial Assistance (Subsidy) up to 40% for capacity up to 3 kW.
20% subsidy for capacity above 3 kW up to 10 kW.
eligibility_criteria: Residential consumers.
Group Housing Societies/RWAs.
Must use indigenous solar cells/modules.
how_to_apply: Apply through the National Portal for Rooftop Solar or DISCOM portal.

objective: The PRL Award/ instituted by the late Prof. Devendra Lal/ former Director of the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL)/ is awarded biennially to recognize outstanding Indian scientists for original work in Earth & Planetary systems/ including areas like Oceanography/ Atmospheric Sciences & Meteorology.
benefits_summary: Biennial award for outstanding contributions to Earth and Planetary Sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Scientists.
Age: Below 45 years.
Significant contribution to Earth & Planetary Systems research.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited from Heads of Institutions/Universities.

scheme_name: Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD)
objective: Launched in 2014–2015/ the scheme "Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual Augmentation Drive (PRASAD)" is a Central Sector Scheme by the Ministry of Tourism/ Government of India. The scheme focuses on developing and identifying pilgrimage sites across India to enrich the religious tourism experience.
benefits_summary: Develops infrastructure at pilgrimage sites to improve tourist experience.
benefits_list: Development of basic amenities (toilets, waiting rooms, parking).
Beautification and illumination of pilgrimage sites.
eligibility_criteria: Identify pilgrimage destinations.
Implemented by State Governments/UT Administrations.
No direct application for individuals.
how_to_apply: State Governments submit project proposals to the Ministry of Tourism.

scheme_name: Post Doctoral Fellowship To Women Candidates (UGC)
objective: The UGC has initiated a scheme of “Post Doctoral Fellowship for Women” to those candidates/ who are unemployed holding Ph.D. degree in their respective subject areas with an aim to accelerate the talented instincts of the women candidates to carry out the advanced studies and research.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for unemployed women Ph.D. holders to conduct advanced research.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Unemployed Ph.D. degree holder.
Age: Below 55 years (General), 60 years (Reserved).
Minimum 55% marks in PG.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC/NTA portal.

scheme_name: Post Doctoral Fellowship to Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Candidates (UGC)
objective: This scheme has been initiated keeping in view social background of candidates from the deprived section of society and to provide them an opportunity to undertake advanced studies and research in Science/ Engineering & Technology/ Humanities and Social Sciences in Indian Universities/Institutions.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for SC/ST Ph.D. holders to pursue post-doctoral research.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to SC/ST category.
Unemployed Ph.D. degree holder.
Age: Below 50 years (male), 55 years (female).
Minimum 50% marks in PG.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC portal.

scheme_name: Post Graduate Indira Gandhi Scholarship For Single Girl Child
objective: A scholarship scheme by the University Grants Commission for Girl students who are admitted in the first year of PG courses in any designated university or a postgraduate college/ and happen to be the only girl child in the family. The amount of fellowship is ₹ 36/200/- per annum.
benefits_summary: Supports single girl children to pursue postgraduate education.
benefits_list: Paid for a period of 2 years.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Must be the ONLY child in the family (Single Girl Child).
Admitted to 1st year of non-professional PG course.
Age: Up to 30 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
required_documents: Affidavit for Single Girl Child (₹50 Stamp Paper).

scheme_name: Post Graduate Merit Scholarship Scheme For University Rank Holders
objective: UGC/ Dept. of Higher Education introduced a Post-Graduate Merit Scholarship Scheme for University rank holders both in General and Honours Courses at the UG level. The scholarship may be tenable for a period of two years.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for 1st and 2nd rank holders in Undergraduate courses to pursue PG.
eligibility_criteria: 1st or 2nd Rank holder in UG (General/Honours) at University level.
Admitted to a PG course.
Minimum 60% marks in UG.
Age: Up to 30 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Post Graduate Scholarship Scheme for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students-AICTE
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: AICTE in order to ensure the development of technical education in India awards Post Graduate Scholarship of ₹12/400/- per month to full-time GATE/CEED qualified students admitted to AICTE approved post-graduate programs in AICTE approved Institutions/University Dept. as per AICTE approved intake.
benefits_summary: Monthly scholarship for GATE/GPAT/CEED qualified students pursuing M.Tech/M.Pharm/M.Arch/M.Des.
benefits_list: Duration: 24 months or course duration (whichever is less).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Qualified GATE/GPAT/CEED score.
Admitted to AICTE approved PG program.
Must not receive salary/stipend from other sources.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the AICTE PG Scholarship Portal. Institute verifies the application.

scheme_name: Post Graduate Scholarship for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students (UGC)
objective: University Grants Commission is implementing the scheme of Post Graduate Scholarship for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students for Pursuing M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm courses (including integrated dual degree courses).
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to GATE/GPAT qualified students pursuing PG in UGC funded Universities.
eligibility_criteria: Qualified GATE/GPAT.
Admitted to M.E./M.Tech/M.Pharm in UGC funded University/College.
Not receiving AICTE scholarship.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC/Canara Bank portal.

objective: A scholarship scheme by Ministry of Minority Affairs for students from minority communities pursuing a post-matric course from a notified institution in India in a government or private school. A total of five (5) lakh scholarships are targeted to be distributed as ‘Fresh’ Scholarships.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for minority students pursuing education from Class 11 to Ph.D.
benefits_list: Admission & Tuition Fee (Actual or capped, e.g., ₹7,000/₹10,000/₹3,000 p.a.).
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Minority Community.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,00,000.
Secured min 50% marks in previous exam.
Pursuing Class 11, 12, ITI, Diploma, Graduation, PG.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme For ST Students
objective: This is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs/ for the students of Scheduled Tribe (ST) for studies in India. This scheme is for pursuing higher education starting from class XI to Post Graduation courses in India.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to ST students for post-matriculation studies.
benefits_list: Study Tour charges, Thesis typing charges.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Pursuing recognized post-matric course.
Not receiving other scholarships.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or State Portal.

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for Students With Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: A scholarship scheme by DoEPwD for students with disabilities pursuing a post-matric qualification from UGC/AICTE recognized universities/ i.e. students of Class 11th/ Class 12th/ including Polytechnics/ Post-Matriculation Diploma/Certificates and Bachelor’s/Master’s Degree/Diploma in India.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for students with disabilities pursuing higher education.
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Pursuing Post-Matric courses.
Valid UDID/Disability Certificate.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

objective: POMIS is an investment scheme recognized and validated by the Ministry of Finance. It is one of the highest-earning schemes with an interest rate of 6.6%. The interest in this scheme is disbursed monthly. After opening a POMIS account/ individuals can invest a minimum amount of ₹1500.
benefits_summary: A guaranteed monthly income investment scheme for investors seeking regular returns.
benefits_list: Guaranteed monthly interest payment (Interest rate approx 7.4% p.a.).
eligibility_criteria: Resident Individual (Adult).
Minor (above 10 years).
Maximum Investment: ₹9 Lakh (Single), ₹15 Lakh (Joint).
how_to_apply: Visit the nearest Post Office branch.

objective: The fellowship offers candidates the opportunity to pursue research in PRL’s cutting-edge science & engineering research areas. The tenure of the fellowship is for a maximum of two years/ subject to annual progress reviews. The fellowship provides a competitive stipend/ research opportunities.
benefits_summary: Provides fellowship for post-doctoral research in Physics, Astronomy, Space Sciences at PRL.
benefits_list: Fellowship: ₹47,000 to ₹54,000 per month (depending on experience).
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D. degree in Science/Engineering/Technology.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student/Researcher.
Outstanding academic record and research publications.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PRL website against rolling advertisements.

scheme_name: Post-Graduate Scholarships for Professional Courses for SC/ST Candidates
objective: The scheme has been initiated to provide opportunity to deprived sections of the society/ viz. SC/ST for undertaking postgraduate level studies in Professional subjects like Engineering & Technology/ Management/ Pharmacy etc. in recognised Indian Universities/Institutions/Colleges.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to SC/ST students pursuing professional PG courses.
benefits_list: Scholarship: ₹7,800 per month (ME/MTech) / ₹4,500 per month (Other Professional courses).
Duration: 2 or 3 years depending on the course.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to SC/ST category.
Admitted to a professional PG course.
Course must be recognized by UGC/AICTE/MCI etc.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Scheduled Caste (SC) Students is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides financial support to SC students for pursuing education from Class 11 to Ph.D.
benefits_list: Academic Allowance (₹2,500 to ₹13,500 per annum depending on course).
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Caste (SC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Pursuing recognized post-matric course.
Not receiving other scholarships.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or State Portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: With a view to enabling an area-based development approach/ a new scheme Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAGY) was launched during 2009-10. The Scheme aims at the integrated development of Scheduled Castes majority villages.
benefits_summary: Integrated development of villages with >50% SC population to ensure access to basic services.
benefits_list: Gap funding of ₹20 Lakhs per village for development activities.
Convergence with other schemes for water, sanitation, electricity, roads.
eligibility_criteria: Villages with >50% SC population.
Identified by the State Government/Ministry.
Beneficiaries: Residents of Adarsh Grams.
how_to_apply: Implemented by Gram Panchayats. Village Development Plan (VDP) is prepared.

objective: Launch on 1st April 2016/ Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana Gramin (PMAY-G) is centre's flagship mission by the Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD)/ implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA). PMAY-G addresses the rural housing shortage and bridges the housing deficit in rural areas
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to rural poor for constructing pucca houses.
benefits_list: Additional support for toilet construction (SBM-G) and MGNREGA wages.
eligibility_criteria: Houseless or living in kutcha/dilapidated houses.
Identified based on SECC 2011 data and Awaas+ survey.
Exclusion criteria (e.g., owning motorized vehicle, paying income tax) applies.
how_to_apply: Selection is based on SECC list. Beneficiaries are contacted by Gram Panchayat. No direct application.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
objective: A flagship mission implemented by MoHUA/ addressing urban housing shortage. The beneficiaries include Economically Weaker Section (EWS) / Low Income Group (LIG) and Middle Income Group (MIG) categories/ including slum dwellers/ by ensuring a pucca house to all eligible urban households by 2022.
benefits_summary: Provides central assistance for affordable housing in urban areas.
benefits_list: Assistance: ₹1.5 Lakhs for Beneficiary Led Construction (BLC) / Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP).
Interest Subsidy (CLSS) for EWS/LIG (MIG component ended).
Slum redevelopment grant of ₹1 Lakh per house.
eligibility_criteria: Urban poor (EWS/LIG).
Annual family income: Up to ₹3 Lakhs (EWS), ₹3-6 Lakhs (LIG).
Must not own a pucca house in India.
how_to_apply: Apply online via PMAY-U MIS portal or through Common Service Centres (CSCs).
required_documents: Affidavit of not owning a pucca house.

issuing_authority: Pharmaceuticals & Medical Devices Bureau of India (PMBI)
objective: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana was launched by the Department of Pharmaceuticals/ in November 2008. Under the scheme/ dedicated outlets known as Janaushadhi Kendras are opened to make quality generic medicines available to all at affordable prices.
benefits_summary: Provides quality generic medicines at affordable prices through Janaushadhi Kendras. Also offers opportunity to open Kendras.
benefits_list: Generic medicines at 50-90% lower cost than branded medicines.
Incentive of ₹5 Lakhs for store owners.
Special incentive for women/SC/ST/North East store owners.
eligibility_criteria: For Medicine Purchase: All citizens.
For Opening Kendra: Pharmacists, Doctors, Registered Medical Practitioners, NGOs, Trusts.
Must have 120 sq. ft. space.
how_to_apply: To open a Kendra, apply online via the PMBI website. To buy medicines, visit nearest Kendra.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Dakshta Aur Kushalta Sampann Hitgrahi (PM-DAKSH)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The scheme "PM-DAKSH"/ launched by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MoSJ&E)/ aims to empower socially/ educationally/ and economically marginalized groups like SCs/ OBCs/ EWS/ DNTs/ Safai Mitras/ and Waste Pickers by enhancing their skills through high-quality training.
benefits_summary: Skill development training for marginalized groups to enhance employability.
benefits_list: Free Short Term Training and Up-skilling/Reskilling.
Long Term Training and Entrepreneurship Development Programs.
Stipend to trainees (approx ₹1,000-₹3,000 depending on course/attendance).
eligibility_criteria: SC, OBC (Annual income < ₹3 Lakh), EWS (Annual income < ₹1 Lakh), DNT, Safai Karamcharis.
Age: 18-45 years.
Unemployed youth.
how_to_apply: Register on the PM-DAKSH portal or PM-DAKSH mobile app.

objective: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana was launched in 2016 with the aim to support farmers by providing an affordable comprehensive risk cover for crops.
benefits_summary: Comprehensive crop insurance scheme to protect farmers against non-preventable natural risks.
benefits_list: Full Sum Insured coverage for crop loss.
eligibility_criteria: All farmers (Loanee and Non-Loanee) growing notified crops in notified areas.
Sharecroppers and Tenant Farmers eligible.
how_to_apply: Apply through Banks, CSCs, or the PMFBY Portal/App. Mandatory for Loanee farmers in some states (unless opted out).

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Formalisation Of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
objective: Launched on 29th June 2020/ PMFMPE is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme by the MoFPI/ designed to address the challenges faced by the micro enterprises and to tap the potential of groups and cooperatives in supporting the upgradation and formalization of these enterprises.
benefits_summary: Provides financial, technical, and business support for upgradation of micro food processing enterprises.
benefits_list: Credit-linked subsidy @35% of project cost (Max ₹10 Lakhs).
Seed capital of ₹40,000 per member for SHGs.
eligibility_criteria: Existing Micro Food Processing units.
Individual Entrepreneurs, FPOs, SHGs, Cooperatives.
One District One Product (ODOP) approach preferred.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PMFME portal.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha Evam Utthan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
issuing_authority: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
objective: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Surakshaevam Utthaan Mahabhiyan Yojana (PM-KUSUM Scheme) was launched in March 2019 by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)/ to subsidize farmers to install solar irrigation pumps for cultivation. Each farmer will receive a 60% subsidy.
benefits_summary: Promotes solar energy use in agriculture to provide energy security to farmers.
benefits_list: Component A: Set up solar power plants on barren land (Income generation).
Component B: Install standalone solar agriculture pumps (60% Subsidy).
Component C: Solarize existing grid-connected pumps (Sell surplus power).
eligibility_criteria: Farmers, Group of Farmers, FPOs, Water User Associations.
Land ownership required.
how_to_apply: Apply through the State Renewable Energy Agency or designated portal.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana: Per Drop More Crop (PDMC)
objective: The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana: Per Drop More Crop” was launched by the Dept. of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare/ Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare/ Govt. of India on 1st July 2015. The scheme mainly focuses on water use efficiency at farm level through Micro Irrigation.
benefits_summary: Promotes micro-irrigation technologies like Drip and Sprinkler irrigation.
benefits_list: Assistance: 55% for Small/Marginal farmers, 45% for other farmers.
Improves water use efficiency and crop yield.
eligibility_criteria: All farmers with cultivable land.
Priority to water-stressed areas.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
how_to_apply: Apply through the State Horticulture/Agriculture Department or online portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Women and Child Development
objective: The scheme is to provide maternity benefits to women belonging to socially and economically disadvantaged sections of society. For first child the maternity benefit of ₹5,000 is provided in two instalments under PMMVY and second child/ it is ₹6,000 is to be provided subject to second child is girl.
benefits_summary: Provides cash incentive to pregnant women and lactating mothers for wage loss compensation and nutrition.
benefits_list: ₹6,000 for 2nd child (if it is a girl, in 1 instalment).
Promotes health seeking behavior and institutional delivery.
eligibility_criteria: Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers (PW&LM).
Must meet specific socio-economic criteria (e.g., E-Shram card, PMJAY card, BPL ration card, etc.).
Exclude Govt/PSU employees.
how_to_apply: Register at Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or online via PMMVY Soft portal.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card of Mother and Husband.

scheme_name: PM-MKSSY: COMPONENT 1A: Formalization of Fisheries Sector and Capital Financing
objective: The scheme aims to formalise fisheries microenterprises and facilitate their access to institutional working capital finance through small grants and credit intermediaries. It benefits fisheries sector stakeholders registered on NFDP with verifiable credit needs and valid bank accounts.
benefits_summary: Encourages formalization of fisheries micro-enterprises and improves access to credit.
benefits_list: Interest Subvention on Working Capital Loans.
Incentivizes digital transactions and formal registration.
eligibility_criteria: Fishers, Fish Farmers, Fish Workers, Vendors.
Micro-enterprises in fisheries sector.
Registered on the National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP).
how_to_apply: Register on the NFDP portal and apply for benefits.

scheme_name: PM-MKSSY: COMPONENT 1B: One-Time Incentive for Aquaculture Insurance
objective: The Component 1B of PM-MKSSY provides a one-time incentive to eligible applicants who have availed institutional credit for fisheries-related activities. The objective is to encourage formal credit adoption and support the repayment capacity of fisheries microenterprises.
benefits_summary: Incentivizes adoption of aquaculture insurance by farmers availing institutional credit.
benefits_list: One-time incentive of ₹15,000 or 20% of the insurance premium (whichever is lower).
eligibility_criteria: Fish Farmers/Aqua-farmers.
Must have availed institutional credit (KCC/Loan).
Purchased aquaculture insurance policy.
how_to_apply: Apply through the lending bank or NFDP portal.

objective: Introduced by the Department of Fisheries/ Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) is a scheme to bring about ecologically healthy/ economically viable/ and socially inclusive development of the fisheries sector of India.
benefits_summary: Comprehensive scheme for the development of fisheries sector.
benefits_list: Subsidy for new ponds, biofloc units, RAS, cages, etc. (40% General, 60% SC/ST/Women).
Assistance for boats, nets, and safety kits.
Support for cold chain, processing units, and marketing infrastructure.
eligibility_criteria: Fishers, Fish Farmers, SHGs, JLGs, FPOs.
Entrepreneurs/Private firms in fisheries.
SC/ST/Women beneficiaries given priority.
how_to_apply: Submit Detailed Project Report (DPR) to the District Fisheries Office.

objective: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY) is a flagship scheme of Government of India. It facilitates micro credit/Loan upto Rs. 20 lakhs to income generating micro enterprises engaged in the non farm sector in manufacturing/ processing/ trading or service sector including activities allied to agriculture
benefits_summary: Provides collateral-free loans to micro and small enterprises.
benefits_list: Tarun Loan: ₹5 Lakhs to ₹10 Lakhs (Limit raised to ₹20 Lakhs for Tarun Plus).
eligibility_criteria: Non-Corporate Small Business Segment (NCSBS).
Proprietorship/Partnership firms running small manufacturing/trading/service units.
Also covers allied agri-activities.
how_to_apply: Apply at any Bank, NBFC, or MFI. Online application via UdyamiMitra portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Women and Child Development
objective: The Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar (PMRBP) is organized to celebrate the energy/ determination/ ability/ zeal/ and enthusiasm of our children.
benefits_summary: Awards children for exceptional achievement in Innovation, Scholastic, Sports, Arts & Culture, Social Service, and Bravery.
eligibility_criteria: Children residing in India.
Age: 5 to 18 years.
Exceptional achievement in any of the specified fields.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Awards Portal (awards.gov.in).

objective: An Accident Insurance Scheme offering accidental death and disability cover for death or disability on account of an accident.
benefits_summary: Low-cost accidental insurance cover for all bank account holders.
eligibility_criteria: Bank Account holders.
Age: 18 to 70 years.
Consent for auto-debit of premium.
how_to_apply: Enroll through Bank/Post Office (Net banking or form).

scheme_name: PM-USP: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
issuing_authority: Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Dept. of Higher Education to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies. The scholarships are awarded on the basis of results of Higher Secondary / Class 12th Board Exam
benefits_summary: Scholarship for meritorious students pursuing college/university education.
eligibility_criteria: Top 20th percentile in Class 12 Board Exam.
Annual family income up to ₹4.5 Lakhs.
Pursuing regular degree course in recognized college/university.
Age: 18-25 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: PM-USP: Special Scholarship Scheme for Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh (PM-SSS)
objective: This initiative provides academic fees and maintenance allowances to support students from Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh pursuing higher education outside the Union Territories
benefits_summary: Scholarship for students of J&K and Ladakh to study in colleges outside the UTs.
benefits_list: Academic Fee: Up to ₹30,000 (General), ₹1.25 Lakh (Engineering), ₹3 Lakh (Medical).
Maintenance Allowance: ₹1 Lakh per annum (paid in instalments).
eligibility_criteria: Domicile of J&K or Ladakh.
Passed Class 12 from JKBOSE or CBSE affiliated schools in J&K/Ladakh.
Annual family income up to ₹8 Lakhs.
Admitted to institute outside J&K/Ladakh via AICTE counseling or premier institute entrance.
how_to_apply: Register on the AICTE PM-SSS portal.

scheme_name: PM-USP: Central Sector Interest Subsidy Scheme (CSIS)
issuing_authority: Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education
objective: The scheme provides full interest subsidy during the moratorium period on loan availed under Model Education Loan Scheme of Indian Banks’ Association (IBA) to students belonging to economically weaker sections whose annual parental income is up to ₹4.5 Lakhs from all sources.
benefits_summary: Provides interest subsidy on education loans for EWS students pursuing professional courses.
benefits_list: Full interest subsidy during the course period + 1 year (Moratorium period).
Applicable for technical/professional courses in India.
eligibility_criteria: Annual family income up to ₹4.5 Lakhs.
Admitted to professional/technical course in recognized institute (NAAC/NBA/CFTI).
Availed education loan from scheduled bank.
how_to_apply: Apply for the subsidy through the lending Bank Branch.
required_documents: Income Certificate issued by competent authority.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
objective: A scheme by Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas for Providing free LPG connections to women belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) households.
benefits_summary: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG) to poor households to improve women's health.
benefits_list: Cash assistance of ₹1,600 for connection cost.
First refill and stove provided free of cost (under Ujjwala 2.0).
Subsidy on refills (currently ₹300 per cylinder for 12 cylinders/year).
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female (Adult woman).
Belong to poor household (SC/ST/PMAY/AAY/Forest Dwellers/etc.).
No LPG connection in the household.
how_to_apply: Apply at the nearest LPG Distributor or online via PMUY portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
objective: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MOPNG)/ introduced the ‘Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana 2.0’ in India. The Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana (PMUY) is a scheme launched by the Government of India in 2016 to provide free LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) connections to women from below-poverty-line.
benefits_summary: Extended version of PMUY covering migrants and additional beneficiaries.
benefits_list: Simplified KYC for migrants (Self-declaration accepted for address proof).
eligibility_criteria: Adult woman from poor household.
Migrant workers without address proof.
Households left out in PMUY 1.0.
how_to_apply: Apply online or at LPG distributor. Migrants can submit self-declaration for address.
required_documents: Ration Card / Self-declaration for family composition (for migrants).

issuing_authority: Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education
objective: The "Pradhan Mantri Vidyalaxmi (Pm-vidyalaxmi) Scheme" aims to provide financial support for higher education through education loans. Under this scheme/ financial assistance of up to ₹10,00,000/- is provided to meritorious students seeking admission to domestic institutions.
benefits_summary: Facilitates collateral-free education loans for students admitted to top-quality higher education institutions.
benefits_list: Collateral-free loan up to ₹7.5 Lakhs (with 75% credit guarantee by GoI).
Interest subvention of 3% on loans up to ₹10 Lakhs for students with family income < ₹8 Lakhs.
Covers tuition fees and other expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Admitted to top 860 QHEIs (NIRF ranked).
Annual family income < ₹8 Lakhs (for interest subvention preference).
Applicant's employment status: Student.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PM-Vidyalaxmi portal (unified portal for education loans).

scheme_name: Pragati Scholarship Scheme For Girl Students (Technical Degree)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: A scholarship scheme by MoE for Girl Students admitted in 1st or 2nd Year of the technical degree course in an AICTE approved institution. The benefits are provided for max. 4 years for first-year admitted students and max. 3 years for second-year admitted students through lateral entry.
benefits_summary: Financial support for girl students pursuing technical degree courses to encourage higher education.
benefits_list: Paid as a lump sum amount towards college fees, purchase of computer, stationeries, books, etc.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹8,00,000.
Admitted to 1st year (or 2nd year via lateral entry) of Technical Degree Course in AICTE approved institution.
Maximum two girl children per family.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Pragati Scholarship Scheme For Girl Students (Technical Diploma)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: A scholarship scheme by AICTE for Girl Students admitted in 1st or 2nd Year of the technical diploma course in an AICTE approved institution. The benefits are provided for a period of max. of 3 years for first-year students and a max. of 2 years for second-year admitted students (later entry).
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to girl students pursuing technical diploma courses.
benefits_list: Paid as a lump sum amount towards college fees, equipment, books, etc.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹8,00,000.
Admitted to 1st year (or 2nd year lateral entry) of Technical Diploma Course in AICTE approved institution.
Maximum two girl children per family.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Pratyaksh Hanstantrit Labh (PAHAL) / Direct Benefits Transfer For LPG (DBTL)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
objective: PAHAL (DBTL) has been introduced to curb the diversion of subsidized LPG. Under the scheme/ Consumers will pay the market price for the domestic cylinder and the subsidy will be transferred directly to their bank account.
benefits_summary: Direct transfer of LPG subsidy to the consumer's bank account.
benefits_list: Subsidy amount transferred directly to bank account.
Prevents leakage and diversion of subsidized LPG.
Ensures subsidy reaches the intended beneficiary.
eligibility_criteria: LPG Consumer with a valid connection.
Must have linked Aadhaar with Bank Account and LPG Consumer ID.
how_to_apply: Link Aadhaar with Bank Account and LPG ID (submit Form 1/2 to Distributor/Bank).

objective: A mandatory insurance scheme by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for safeguarding the interests of Indian emigrant workers falling under Emigration Check Required (ECR) category going for overseas employment to ECR countries.
benefits_summary: Insurance scheme for Indian workers going abroad for employment.
benefits_list: Insurance cover of ₹10 Lakhs in case of accidental death/permanent disability.
eligibility_criteria: Indian workers with Emigration Check Required (ECR) passports.
Going for employment to ECR countries.
Age: 18 to 65 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via e-Migrate portal or through registered recruitment agents.

objective: A scholarship scheme by Ministry of Minority Affairs for students from minority communities studying in Class 1st to Class 10th in India in a government or private school/ including such residential Government institutes and eligible private institutes selected and notified in a transparent manner.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for minority students in Class 1 to 10 to encourage school education.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Minority Community.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹1,00,000.
Previous exam marks: Minimum 50%.
Studying in Class 1 to 10.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Pre Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Tribe (ST) Students
objective: A scholarship scheme by Ministry of Tribal Affairs for regular/ full time students from Scheduled Tribe studying in Class 9th and 10th/ in a Government School or in a School recognized by Govt. or a Central/State Board of Secondary Education.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to ST students in Class 9 and 10 to minimize dropouts.
benefits_list: Books and Ad-hoc Grant: ₹750/1000 per annum.
Additional allowance for students with disabilities.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,00,000.
Studying in Class 9 or 10.
Not receiving other scholarships.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or State Portal.

scheme_name: Pre Matric Scholarship For Students With Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: A scholarship scheme by DoEPwDs for Students with Disabilities (SwDs) studying in class 9th and class 10th in a government school or a school recognized by the government or CBSE or State Board. This includes persons with visual/ hearing/ speech/ loco-motor/ mental retardation & other disabilities.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for students with disabilities in Class 9 and 10.
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Studying in Class 9 or 10.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarships Scheme for Scheduled Castes & Others
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Scheduled Castes & others is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Financial support for SC students in Class 9 and 10.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Caste (SC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Studying in Class 9 or 10.
how_to_apply: Apply online through NSP or respective State Scholarship Portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The scholarship is designed to provide OBC students with the necessary financial resources studying at pre-matriculation stage to enable them to complete their education.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for OBC students in Class 1 to 10.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to OBC category.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Studying in Class 1 to 10 in Govt/Aided school.
how_to_apply: Apply online through NSP or State Portal.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarships to the Children of Those Engaged in Occupations Involving Cleaning and Prone to Health Hazards
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to children whose parents/guardians belong to one of the following categories/ to pursue Pre-Matric education: Tanners/ Flayers/ Waste Pickers and Persons who are either presently engaged in Manual Scavenging.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for children of parents in unclean occupations (e.g., manual scavenging, tanning, waste picking).
eligibility_criteria: Child of Manual Scavenger/Tanner/Flayer/Waste Picker.
Studying in Class 1 to 10.
No caste restriction.
how_to_apply: Apply through NSP or State Portal.

scheme_name: Prime Minister Early Career Research Grant (PM-ECRG)
objective: This programme is initiated by the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) to assist researchers to initiate their research career in a new institution with a flexible budget and progressive initiatives for ease of doing research.
benefits_summary: Provides research grants to early-career researchers (formerly SERB Start-up Research Grant).
benefits_list: Research Grant up to ₹60 Lakhs (for 2 years).
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Hold a regular academic/research position.
Apply within 2 years of joining the institution.
Age: Below 42 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the ANRF/SERB portal.

issuing_authority: Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) / Ministry of MSME
objective: Launched in August 2008/ Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) is a credit-linked subsidy scheme/ administered by the Ministry Of MSME (MSME). PMEGP aims to generate employment opportunities through the establishment of micro-enterprises in
benefits_summary: Credit-linked subsidy scheme for setting up new micro-enterprises.
benefits_list: Margin Money Subsidy: 15-35% of project cost.
eligibility_criteria: Any individual above 18 years.
At least Class 8 pass for projects > ₹10 Lakhs (Mfg) / ₹5 Lakhs (Service).
SHGs, Cooperative Societies, Trusts.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PMEGP e-portal (KVIC).

scheme_name: Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme (PMSS) for Ex-Servicemen
objective: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme (PMSS) was introduced in the academic year 2006-07 by the Department of Ex-servicemen Welfare/ to encourage higher technical and professional education for the dependent wards of Ex-Servicemen / Ex-Coast Guard personnel and their widows.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for wards/widows of Ex-Servicemen pursuing professional degree courses.
benefits_list: Covers professional courses like Engineering, Medical, MBA, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Wards/Widows of Ex-Servicemen/Ex-Coast Guard.
Admitted to professional degree course.
Minimum 60% marks in MEQ (Class 12/Diploma/Graduation).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the KSB website.

scheme_name: Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme for Railway Protection Force (PMSS-RPF)
objective: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme (PMSS) for RPF was announced by Hon’ble Prime Minister of India in his address to the nation from the historical Red Ford on 15th August 2005 to encourage higher technical and professional education for the dependent wards of Ex/Serving RPF/RPSF personnel& widows.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for wards of RPF/RPSF personnel pursuing professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: Wards of Serving/Retired RPF/RPSF personnel.
Admitted to professional degree course.
Minimum 60% marks in MEQ.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme for Central Armed Police Forces (PMSS-CAPF)
objective: The scheme “Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme for the Wards/Widows of Central Police Force Personnel” was introduced by the Ministry of Home Affairs/ Govt. of India in the Academic year 2006-07 to encourage higher technical and professional education for the wards of widows and ex-CPFs personnel.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for wards/widows of CAPF & AR personnel pursuing professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: Wards/Widows of CAPF & Assam Rifles personnel (Serving/Retired/Deceased).
Admitted to professional degree course.
Minimum 60% marks in MEQ.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Prime Minister’s Fellowship for Doctoral Research
issuing_authority: Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB) & CII
objective: Prime Minister’s Fellowship for Doctoral Research scheme is a prestigious initiative of Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB)/ Department of Science & Technology/ Government of India towards the advancement of university research engagements in line with industry requirement.
benefits_summary: Offers double scholarship (Govt + Industry) to Ph.D. scholars for industry-relevant research.
benefits_list: Monthly Fellowship: Approx ₹87,000 (JRF level) to ₹95,000 (SRF level) [Doubled amount].
Govt pays half, Industry pays half.
eligibility_criteria: Registered for full-time Ph.D.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
Must have an industry partner willing to sponsor 50% fellowship.
Research topic must be industry-relevant.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PM Fellowship Portal.

objective: The scheme aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore youth in top 500 companies in five years. Through this Scheme/ youth will gain exposure for 12 months to real-life business environment/ across varied professions and employment opportunities.
benefits_summary: 12-month internship in top 500 companies to gain real-world business experience.
benefits_list: Monthly assistance of ₹4,500 by Govt + ₹500 by Company (Total ₹5,000).
eligibility_criteria: Age: 21-24 years.
Not employed full-time; Not a full-time student.
Educational Qualification: 10th/12th pass, ITI, Diploma, Graduate.
Family income < ₹8 Lakhs.
how_to_apply: Register on the PM Internship Scheme portal (pminternship.mca.gov.in).

scheme_name: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme for the Wards of Central Armed Police Forces & Assam Rifles
objective: The scheme was introduced by the Ministry of Home Affairs/ Government of India from the academic year 2006-07 to encourage higher technical & professional education for the dependent wards & widows of Central Armed Police Forces & Assam Rifles (CAPFs & AR) Personnel.
benefits_summary: Duplicate of C390. Scholarship for wards/widows of CAPF & AR personnel.
eligibility_criteria: Wards/Widows of CAPF & AR personnel.
Admitted to professional degree course.
Minimum 60% marks in MEQ.
how_to_apply: Apply online via NSP.

scheme_name: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme for the Wards of States/UTs Police Personnel
objective: The scheme was introduced by the Ministry of Home Affairs/ Government of India from the academic session 2019-20 to encourage higher technical & professional education for the dependent wards of States/UTs Police Personnel who are martyred during Terror/Naxal attacks.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for wards of State Police Personnel martyred in terror/naxal attacks.
eligibility_criteria: Wards of State/UT Police Personnel martyred in Terror/Naxal attacks.
Admitted to professional degree course.
how_to_apply: Apply online via NSP.
required_documents: Martyr Certificate issued by State Govt.

scheme_name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme (PLI) For Telecom & Networking Products
objective: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing/ investments and export in the telecom and networking products The PLI Scheme will be implemented within the overall financial limits of ₹ 12/195 Crores only.
benefits_summary: Incentivizes domestic manufacturing of telecom and networking products.
benefits_list: Incentive of 4% to 7% on incremental sales of manufactured goods.
eligibility_criteria: Global and Domestic Manufacturers.
MSMEs.
Must meet investment and incremental sales thresholds.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the PLI portal for Telecom.

scheme_name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme (PLI) For Automobile And Auto Component Industry
objective: The government of India has launched the scheme “Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Automobile and Auto Components Industry” in India to enhance India’s Manufacturing Capabilities for Advanced Automotive Products.
benefits_summary: Incentivizes manufacturing of Advanced Automotive Technology (AAT) products (EVs, Hydrogen Fuel Cell vehicles, etc.).
benefits_list: Incentives based on determined sales value.
Range from 8% to 18% depending on the component/vehicle.
Promotes clean and advanced technology vehicles.
eligibility_criteria: Automobile and Auto Component Manufacturers.
Must meet criteria of Global group revenue and investment.
New Non-Automotive investors also eligible.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the MHI PLI portal.

scheme_name: Programme For Internship For LL.B./ LL.M./ Environmental Studies/ Research Students (NGT)
objective: The National Green Tribunal provides opportunities to college students by holding Summer Internship Programs (March/ April/ May and July ordinarily) and Winter Internship Programs (November/ December and January ordinarily) Internship Programs for LL.B./ LL.M./ Env. Studies/ Research Students.
benefits_summary: Internship to understand environmental law and justice.
eligibility_criteria: Student of LL.B., LL.M., or Environmental Studies.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
Interest in Environmental Law.
how_to_apply: Submit application form and CV to the Registrar General, NGT.

issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: The primary objective of this scheme is to enhance the expertise and capabilities of faculty members in degree and diploma-level institutions across the country. The aim is to enable teachers to acquire Master's and Doctoral degrees and instil in them a culture of research.
benefits_summary: Provides opportunities for faculty members to upgrade their qualifications (M.Tech/Ph.D.).
benefits_list: Study leave with salary (from parent institute).
eligibility_criteria: Regular faculty member of AICTE approved institution.
Minimum teaching experience (e.g., 3 years for Ph.D.).
Sponsorship from employer.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the QIP portal of the coordinating IIT/IISc.

scheme_name: RKVY Soil Health and Fertility - Soil Health Card
objective: Soil Health Card is designed to give each farmer soil nutrient status of his/her holding and advice him/her on the dosage of fertilizers and also the needed soil amendments/ that s/he should apply to maintain soil health in the long run.
benefits_summary: Provides Soil Health Cards to farmers with nutrient status and fertilizer recommendations.
benefits_list: Free Soil Health Card every 3 years.
Reduces input cost and improves yield.
eligibility_criteria: All Farmers.
Land holding required for soil sample collection.
how_to_apply: Soil samples collected by Agriculture Department/STL staff. Card delivered to farmer.

scheme_name: RKVY Soil Health and Fertility Village level Soil Testing Lab (VLSTL)
objective: In order to promote soil test analysis in a campaign mode and to create awareness among farmers about soil health for judicious use of chemical fertilisers/ organic manure/ the component of “Setting of Village-level Soil Testing Laboratories” is being implemented under the scheme.
benefits_summary: Assistance for setting up village-level soil testing labs by rural youth/entrepreneurs.
benefits_list: Financial assistance up to ₹5 Lakhs (75% of cost) for setting up VLSTL.
eligibility_criteria: Rural Youth (Age 18-27 years).
Self Help Groups (SHGs), FPOs.
10+2 Science pass (for individual entrepreneur).
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to the District Agriculture Officer.

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Education Of Children & Widows Of Ex-Servicemen (Education Grant)
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to Ex-Servicemen and their widows for helping them in educating their child (for maximum two children) up to graduation and also for widows to pursue Post Graduation degree.
benefits_summary: Education grant for children and widows of Ex-Servicemen (ESM) up to the rank of Havildar.
benefits_list: Grant of ₹1,000 per month per child.
Applicable for Class 1 to Graduation (or Post Graduation for widows).
eligibility_criteria: Ex-Servicemen (up to rank of Havildar/Equivalent) and their widows.
Applicant's employment status: Retired/Widow.
Child/Widow must be studying in a recognized school/college.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the KSB website (ksb.gov.in). Hard copies to be submitted to Zila Sainik Board (ZSB).

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Ex-Servicemen In Penury
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen up to rank of Havildar /equivalent and their widows who are living in extreme poverty.
benefits_summary: Monthly financial assistance for non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen living in poverty.
eligibility_criteria: Non-Pensioner Ex-Servicemen (up to Havildar rank) or their widows.
Should be above 65 years of age.
Should not be in receipt of any other pension/grant.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KSB website. Verification by ZSB/RSB.

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Medical Treatment Of Ex-Servicemen
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to a non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen/widows of ranks up to Havildar/equivalent to meet routine medical expenses.
benefits_summary: Financial aid for medical treatment of non-pensioner ESM and their widows.
benefits_list: Covers treatment for general diseases in government/empanelled hospitals.
eligibility_criteria: Non-Pensioner ESM (up to Havildar) and Widows.
Not covered under ECHS.
Applicant's employment status: Retired.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KSB website. Submit original medical bills.

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Vocational Training Of Widows Of Ex-Servicemen
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to a widow of Ex-Servicemen to settle in life through vocational training.
benefits_summary: One-time grant for vocational training to help widows of ESM become self-reliant.
eligibility_criteria: Widows of Ex-Servicemen (up to Havildar rank).
Must have undergone vocational training.
Applicant's employment status: Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KSB website after completion of training.

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance To 100% Disabled Child Of Ex-Servicemen
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to Ex-Servicemen or their widows for their 100% disabled child.
benefits_summary: Monthly grant for the care of 100% disabled children of Ex-Servicemen.
benefits_list: Support for upbringing and medical needs.
eligibility_criteria: Child of Ex-Servicemen (up to Havildar rank).
Child must have 100% disability certification.
Not in receipt of any other disability pension/grant.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KSB website.

objective: Rainfed Area Development (RAD) scheme was launched in 2014-15 to mainstream development of rainfed areas in a sustainable manner. It adopts an area-based approach/ focuses on Integrated Farming System (IFS) for enhancing productivity and minimizing risks associated with climatic variability.
benefits_summary: Promotes Integrated Farming Systems (IFS) in rainfed areas to minimize crop failure risk.
benefits_list: Assistance for IFS components (Crops + Livestock + Fishery + Horticulture).
Subsidy up to 50% of the cost (Max ₹1 Lakh/beneficiary).
Support for water harvesting and conservation.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers in rainfed areas.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
Willing to adopt multi-cropping/rotational cropping/mixed farming.
how_to_apply: Contact the District Agriculture Officer or ATMA office.

scheme_name: Rajbhasha Gaurav Puraskar Scheme for writing original book in Hindi
issuing_authority: Department of Official Language, Ministry of Home Affairs
objective: The scheme “Rajbhasha Gaurav Puraskar Scheme for writing original book in Hindi” was launched by the Department of Official Language/ Ministry of Home Affairs/ Government of India with an objective to encourage writing books originally in Hindi and to promote Official Language.
benefits_summary: Awards authors for writing original books in Hindi on technical/science subjects.
benefits_list: Promotes Hindi literature in knowledge domains.
eligibility_criteria: Indian Citizens.
Book must be originally written in Hindi (not translation).
Subject: Science, Engineering, Technology, Law, etc.
how_to_apply: Send the book and application form to the Department of Official Language.

scheme_name: Rajiv Gandhi National Fellowship For Scheduled Caste Candidates (RGNF-SC)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment / UGC
objective: The Rajiv Gandhi National Fellowship (RGNF) Scheme for Scheduled Caste is formulated and funded by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment. The scheme is open to candidates who belong to Scheduled Caste and are pursuing higher studies such as regular and full-time M.Phil. and Ph.D. degrees. (Renamed as National Fellowship for SC - NFSC).
benefits_summary: Fellowship for SC students to pursue M.Phil/Ph.D.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Caste (SC).
Registered for M.Phil/Ph.D. in UGC recognized university.
Selection based on UGC-NET/CSIR-NET.
how_to_apply: Apply online via UGC/NTA portal (NFSC).

objective: The Department of Biotechnology has instituted a scheme known as the “Ramalingaswami Re-Entry Fellowship”. The scheme is aimed at Indian Nationals who are working overseas in various fields of biotechnology and life sciences and are interested in taking up scientific research positions in India.
benefits_summary: Attracts Indian scientists working abroad to return and pursue research in India.
eligibility_criteria: Indian National working overseas in Biotechnology/Life Sciences.
Ph.D./M.D./M.S. degree.
Age Limit: 45 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the DBT eProMIS portal.

issuing_authority: Indo-French Centre for the Promotion of Advanced Research (IFCPAR/CEFIPRA)
objective: The “Raman-Charpak Fellowship Programme” is in honour of two Nobel Laureates in Physics/ Prof C.V. Raman/ Indian Nobel Laureate (1930) & Prof Georges Charpak/ French Nobel Laureate (1992). The Fellowship was launched during the State visit of the President of France to India in February/ 2013.
benefits_summary: Facilitates exchange of Ph.D. students between India and France.
benefits_list: Fellowship: €1500 per month (for Indian students in France).
eligibility_criteria: Registered Ph.D. student in India or France.
Age: Up to 30 years.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the CEFIPRA website.

objective: Ramanujan Fellowship was started by the Science and Engineering Research Board/ Govt. of India for brilliant Indian scientists and engineers from outside India to take up scientific research positions in India/ those Indian scientists/engineers who want to return to India from abroad.
benefits_summary: Attracts brilliant Indian scientists from all over the world to take up research positions in India.
eligibility_criteria: Indian scientist/engineer working abroad.
Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Excellent track record.
Age: Below 40 years.
how_to_apply: Nominations are invited via the SERB online portal.

objective: Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) was launched on 24th April 2018 'National Panchayat Day' by the Hon'ble Prime Minister/ an umbrella scheme of the Ministry of Panchayati Raj. It is a unique scheme proposed to develop and strengthen the Panchayati Raj System across India in rural areas.
benefits_summary: Strengthens Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) to achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
benefits_list: Capacity building and training for Panchayat representatives.
eligibility_criteria: Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).
Elected Representatives and Functionaries of PRIs.
how_to_apply: Implemented by the Ministry through State Governments. PRIs are beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan - Internship Program
objective: The 'Internship Program' as part of the Framework for Implementation of Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Revamped Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) (2022-23 to 2025-26) of the Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR)/ Government of India (Gol)/ seeks to engage 'Students'.
benefits_summary: Internship for students to work on strengthening Panchayati Raj Institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student.
Pursuing Graduate/PG in Rural Development/Social Work/Public Policy.
Age: 18-30 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Ministry of Panchayati Raj website.

scheme_name: Rashtriya Parivar Sahayata Yojana (National Family Benefit Scheme)
objective: This scheme is being executed for the family members who are unable to make their livelihood after the death of the primary breadwinner of the family.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to BPL families upon the death of the primary breadwinner (Same as NFBS - C282).
eligibility_criteria: BPL Household.
Death of primary breadwinner (age 18-59 years).
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat/Municipality.

issuing_authority: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) / Ministry of Science & Technology
objective: The objective of the Rashtriya Vigyan Puraskar (RVP) is to recognise the notable and inspiring contribution made by the scientists/ technologists and innovators individually or in teams in various fields of science/ technology and technology- led innovation.
benefits_summary: National awards recognizing excellence in Science, Technology, and Innovation.
benefits_list: Awards in 4 categories: Vigyan Ratna, Vigyan Shri, Vigyan Yuva-SSB, Vigyan Team.
eligibility_criteria: Scientists/Technologists/Innovators working in India.
PIOs usually eligible for Vigyan Ratna.
Significant contribution to S&T.
how_to_apply: Nominations invited on the Rashtriya Awards Portal (awards.gov.in).

scheme_name: Rastriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN) - Health Minister’s Cancer Patient Fund
issuing_authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
objective: A scheme to provide financial assistance to poor patients living below poverty line and suffering from cancer/ for their treatment at 27 Regional cancer centers (RCCs).
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance for treatment of cancer patients living below poverty line.
benefits_list: Amount paid directly to the treating Regional Cancer Centre (RCC).
eligibility_criteria: Living Below Poverty Line (BPL).
Suffering from Cancer.
Treatment in Government Hospital/RCC.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Medical Superintendent of the treating hospital/RCC.

objective: Raw Material Assistance Scheme aims to help MSMEs by way of financing the purchase of Raw Material (Both indigenous & imported). This gives an opportunity to MSMEs to focus better on manufacturing quality products.
benefits_summary: Financing for purchase of raw materials for MSMEs.
benefits_list: Bulk purchase of raw material at concessional rates.
Facilitates cash discount on raw material.
eligibility_criteria: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
Applicant's employment status: Business Owner/Self-employed.
Udyam Registration.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NSIC portal or nearest NSIC branch.

scheme_name: Reimbursement of membership Fee in Government promoted E-Commerce Portals (NSSH)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (NSSH)
objective: Reimbursement of Membership fee of Government promoted e-commerce portals Scheme is a sub scheme under National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) scheme.
benefits_summary: Reimburses membership fees for SC/ST MSEs joining Govt e-commerce portals like GeM, e-MSME.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of 80% or ₹25,000 (whichever is less) of the membership fee.
Maximum reimbursement for 2 portals per year.
Helps SC/ST entrepreneurs access online markets.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST Entrepreneurs (MSEs).
Udyam Registration.
Paid membership fee for eligible portal.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National SC-ST Hub (NSSH) portal.

scheme_name: Relief and Rehabilitation Assistance to Sri Lankan Refugees
objective: The scheme provides relief materials/ which among other things include Feeding Charges/ Subsidized Rice/ Clothing Materials/ Utensils/ Transportation Charges (on arrival)/ Porterage and Transport (on repatriation)/ Cash Doles/ Infrastructure Facilities (at camps)/ Electricity/ Drinking Water/ etc.
benefits_summary: Provides cash doles and subsidized rations to Sri Lankan refugees living in camps in India.
benefits_list: Cash Dole: Varying amounts for Head of family and members.
eligibility_criteria: Sri Lankan Refugees living in camps in Tamil Nadu and Odisha.
Must be registered with the camp authorities.
how_to_apply: Administered by the Department of Rehabilitation, Government of Tamil Nadu.

objective: The Research Associates (RAs) of the Department of Biotechnology (DBT) in India are individuals who engage in research work under the DBT-Research Associateship Program in Biotechnology and its was started in 1999.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for Ph.D. holders to conduct post-doctoral research in Biotechnology.
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D./MD/MS in Biotechnology or Life Sciences.
Applicant's employment status: Student/Researcher.
Age Limit: 40 years (male), 45 years (female).
how_to_apply: Apply online via DBT-RA portal.

scheme_name: Research Fellowship In Sciences For Meritorious Students (RFSMS)
objective: The Research Fellowship in Sciences for meritorious students (RFSMS) scheme of the UGC is open to candidates who have registered for Ph.D. in Science in Universities with Potential for Excellence/Centres with Potential for Excellence/Centres of Advance Studies and Department of Special Assistance.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for meritorious science students registered for Ph.D. in UGC recognized centers of excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Registered for Ph.D. in Science in eligible UGC centers (UPE/CPE/CAS/DSA).
Applicant's employment status: Student.
how_to_apply: Selection is done by the University/Department. No direct application to UGC.

scheme_name: Research Grant For In-Service Faculty Members
objective: The scheme "Research Grant for In-Service Faculty Members" was introduced by the University Grants Commission (UGC)/ Department of Higher Education (DoHE). The objective of this research grant is to provide opportunities to regularly appointed faculty members of Universities/ Institutes/ Colleges.
benefits_summary: Provides research grants to permanent faculty members to undertake research projects (Start-up Grant).
benefits_list: Funds for equipment, field work, and contingencies.
eligibility_criteria: Regularly appointed faculty in UGC recognized Universities/Colleges.
Ph.D. degree holder.
Within 10 years of appointment.
Age: Below 50 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC portal.

objective: Under this scheme/ DOPT proposes to offer four short-term fellowships to researchers from the field of media / Civil Society professionals / RTI Trainers to conduct field-based research on themes relating to RTI. It is hoped that the research output will enhance their understanding of the RTI Act.
benefits_summary: Fellowship to researchers to study and analyze the implementation of the RTI Act.
benefits_list: Opportunity to contribute to RTI policy research.
eligibility_criteria: Age: 25 to 40 years.
Graduates in Law/Social Sciences/Journalism.
Experience in RTI related work.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to DoPT when advertised.

scheme_name: SAHAKAR MITRA: NCDC’s Scheme on Internship Programme
objective: The programme aims at providing an opportunity to professional graduates to get experience in areas of functioning of NCDC and related aspects of cooperatives.
benefits_summary: Internship for young professionals to learn about the cooperative sector.
benefits_list: Practical experience in cooperative finance and management.
eligibility_criteria: Professional Graduates (Agriculture/Management/IT/Finance/Law).
Applicant's employment status: Student/Fresh Graduate.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NCDC website.

issuing_authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
objective: The SEBI ITD Internship Program provides hands-on training to the students of the institutes based in and around Mumbai. The program accepts up to 15 interns. The Interns accepted under this program would be given an opportunity to work on different Information Technology (IT) projects.
benefits_summary: IT internship for students to work on SEBI's technology projects.
benefits_list: Hands-on experience in FinTech and regulatory technology.
eligibility_criteria: Students pursuing full-time MBA/MCA/M.Tech (IT/CS) from recognized institutes in/around Mumbai.
Completed at least 1 year of the course.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Placement Cell of the Institute to SEBI.

objective: The SERB - POWER Fellowship is designed to identify and reward excellent women researchers in Indian academic institutions and R&D laboratories/ holding Ph.D. degrees in any branch of science and engineering.
benefits_summary: Recognizes and supports outstanding women researchers with a fellowship and research grant.
benefits_list: Personal Fellowship of ₹15,000 per month (in addition to regular income).
Research Grant of ₹10 Lakhs per annum.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 35 and 55 years.
This scheme is available for: Female.
Must hold a regular academic/research position in a recognized institution.
Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Should be an active researcher.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SERB online portal. Nominations are also invited.

objective: The aim of the ‘SERB - POWER Research Grants’ is to encourage emerging and eminent women researchers for individual-centric and competitive mode of research funding to undertake R&D activities in frontier areas of science and engineering.
benefits_summary: Provides research grants to women scientists in two categories: Level I (upto ₹60 Lakhs) and Level II (upto ₹30 Lakhs).
benefits_list: Level I: Grant up to ₹60 Lakhs for 3 years (Institutes like IITs, IISc, etc.).
Level II: Grant up to ₹30 Lakhs for 3 years (Other Universities/Colleges).
Covers equipment, consumables, travel, and overheads.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's age must be between 35 and 55 years.
This scheme is available for: Female.
Required education level: Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Regular faculty/researcher in recognized R&D institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the SERB portal.

objective: The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB)/ a statutory body under the Department of Science and Technology/ Government of India has launched "SERB International Research Experience (SIRE)".
benefits_summary: Facilitates overseas research exposure for Indian researchers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Faculty/Researcher (Permanent position).
Required education level: Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Age Limit: 40 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the SERB portal.

objective: SERB Board has launched a new ‘SERB Research Scientist (SRS) Scheme’ for the awardees of INSPIRE Faculty and Ramanujan Fellowships/ who fail to secure regular positions after completion of the regular tenure of five years in the respective schemes.
benefits_summary: Provides a bridge opportunity for INSPIRE Faculty/Ramanujan Fellows to continue research if they haven't secured a permanent job.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Research Scientist (Contractual).
Must be an INSPIRE Faculty or Ramanujan Fellow completing their 5-year tenure.
Required education level: Ph.D.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SERB portal before the completion of current fellowship tenure.

scheme_name: SERB Science and Technology Award for Research (SERB-STAR)
objective: “SERB Science and Technology Award for Research (SERB-STAR)” is a prestigious award instituted by Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB) to recognize and reward outstanding performance of Principal Investigators (PIs) of SERB Projects.
benefits_summary: Rewards outstanding PIs of SERB projects to recognize their contribution.
benefits_list: Fellowship: ₹15,000 per month (in addition to salary).
Exemption from submitting research proposal for the grant.
eligibility_criteria: Principal Investigator (PI) of a completed SERB project.
Project must be rated 'Excellent'.
Regular faculty/researcher.
Age: 40 to 50 years.
