how_to_apply: Nominations are invited from eligible PIs via SERB portal.

objective: The “SERB-Technology Translation Award (SERB-TETRA)” is a new scheme launched by the Science and Engineering Research Board/ Government of India for catalyzing technology translation in academic settings.
benefits_summary: Supports translation of research into prototypes or products with commercial potential.
benefits_list: Helps in patenting, prototyping, and technology transfer.
eligibility_criteria: PIs with ongoing or completed SERB projects.
Must have a patent filed/granted or technology ready for transfer.
Regular academic/research position.
how_to_apply: Apply online through SERB portal.

issuing_authority: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) / SRISTI
objective: SITARE-Appreciation Grant: Residential workshops called as Biotech Innovation Ignition School (BIIS) for undergraduate students are organized to provide hands-on technical training and mentorship for problem identification in 3-4 weeks duration.
benefits_summary: Provides grants to students participating in the Biotech Innovation Ignition School (BIIS).
benefits_list: Grant of ₹1 Lakh for developing a proof of concept.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing Undergraduate in Science/Tech/Biotech.
Innovative idea in biotechnology.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the SITARE website (SRISTI).

scheme_name: SITARE-Gandhian Young Technological Innovation (GYTI) Award Grant
issuing_authority: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) / SRISTI
objective: Under SITARE-GYTI scheme component/ 15 innovative student projects will receive funding support of upto INR 15 lakhs each. The scheme encourages postgraduate & doctoral students to undertake projects with commercial potential leading to the creation of biotech startups.
benefits_summary: Awards grants for innovative biotech projects with commercial potential.
benefits_list: Support to start a biotech venture.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate/Postgraduate/Ph.D. in Science/Biotech.
Indian citizen.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the SITARE-GYTI portal.

scheme_name: SMILE - Comprehensive Rehabilitation For Welfare Of Transgender Persons (Scholarship)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: A scheme to provide Financial Assistance in the form of scholarships is provided to Transgender students studying in secondary school (IX & X) & senior secondary school (XI & XII) to reduce the incidence of dropout and support their educational aspirations.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for Transgender students to support their education.
benefits_list: Covers tuition fees and other allowances.
eligibility_criteria: Transgender Students.
Studying in Class 9 to 12.
Annual family income < ₹2.5 Lakhs.
Valid Transgender Certificate.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or National Portal for Transgender Persons.

scheme_name: SMILE - Comprehensive Rehabilitation For Welfare Of Transgender Persons – Skill Development
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The scheme aims to provide employment opportunities to the person belonging to transgender person community by providing them market-oriented skills.
benefits_summary: Skill development training for Transgender persons to enhance employability.
benefits_list: Free Short Term Training and Long Term Training.
eligibility_criteria: Transgender Persons.
Valid Transgender Certificate (UDID not applicable, Transgender ID required).
Age: 18-60 years.
how_to_apply: Register on the PM-DAKSH portal or SMILE portal.

objective: SVAMITVA/ a Central Sector scheme of Ministry of Panchayati Raj to provide ‘Record of Rights’ to village household owners with issuance of legal ownership cards (Property cards/Title deeds) to the property owners by mapping of land parcels using drone technology.
benefits_summary: Provides property cards (Sanad) to rural households for inhabited land.
benefits_list: Enables use of property as a financial asset for loans.
eligibility_criteria: Rural Households inhabiting 'Abadi' land (populated areas).
General eligibility criteria apply (Landowners in survey areas).
how_to_apply: Implemented by State Revenue Department and Survey of India. Property owners participate during drone survey.

objective: Saansad Adarsh Gram Yojana aims to translate the comprehensive vision of Mahatma Gandhi about an ideal Indian village into reality/ keeping in view the present context. Each Member of Parliament adopts a Gram Panchayat and guides its holistic progress giving importance to social development.
benefits_summary: Holistic development of villages adopted by Members of Parliament.
eligibility_criteria: Gram Panchayats adopted by MPs.
Beneficiaries: Residents of the adopted village.
how_to_apply: Implemented by the Gram Panchayat under the guidance of the MP. Villagers participate in Gram Sabhas.

issuing_authority: The National Trust (Ministry of Social Justice)
objective: Samarth (Respite Care) scheme/ launched by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities/ aims at setting up Samarth Centres for providing respite and residential care for the specified categories of Persons with Disability (PwD).
benefits_summary: Provides temporary residential care for orphans, families in crisis, and PwDs needing respite.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (Autism, Cerebral Palsy, MR, Multiple Disabilities).
BPL/LIG families get priority.
how_to_apply: Contact registered Samarth Centres (NGOs registered with National Trust).

scheme_name: Savitribai Jyotirao Phule Fellowship For Single Girl Child
objective: Savitribai Jyotirao Phule Fellowship for Single Girl Child (SJSGC) is a Fellowship Scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC)/ Department of Higher Education/ for pursuing research work leading to the award of a Ph.D. degree. The target group of this scheme is the ‘single girl child'.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for Single Girl Child to pursue Ph.D. full-time.
eligibility_criteria: This scheme is available for: Female (Single Girl Child).
Registered for regular Ph.D. program.
Age: Up to 40 years (45 for SC/ST/PwD).
how_to_apply: Apply online via the UGC/NTA portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Women and Child Development
objective: A support scheme by Ministry of Women & Child Development enabling self-development and empowerment of out-of-school adolescent girls/ and improving their nutritional/ health & hygiene status. All out-of-school adolescent girls in the 11 to 14 years age group are eligible for this scheme.
benefits_summary: Nutritional and non-nutritional support for out-of-school adolescent girls (now part of Mission Poshan 2.0).
benefits_list: Nutrition support (Take Home Ration or Hot Cooked Meal).
eligibility_criteria: Out-of-school Adolescent Girls (11-14 years).
This scheme is available for: Female.
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC).
required_documents: School Leaving Certificate (to prove out-of-school status).

scheme_name: Scheme For Award Of Financial Assistance For Education To The Wards Of Beedi/Cine/IOMC/LSDM Workers – Pre & Post-Matric
objective: A scholarship scheme by the Directorate of Social Welfare/ Ministry of Labour and Employment wherein financial assistance for education is provided to the Wards of Beedi/IOMC/LSDM Workers/ varying from ₹1,000/- to ₹25,000/- per student per annum. The benefits are transferred through the DBT system.
benefits_summary: Duplicate of C267. Provides scholarships to children of Beedi, Cine, and Mine workers.
eligibility_criteria: Wards of Beedi/Cine/IOMC/LSDM workers.
Worker's monthly income ≤ ₹10,000.
Studying in Class 1 to Professional Degree.
how_to_apply: Apply online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Scheme For Award Of Senior/Junior Fellowships To Outstanding Persons In The Fields Of Culture
issuing_authority: Centre for Cultural Resources and Training (CCRT), Ministry of Culture
objective: The scheme “Scheme for Award of Senior/Junior Fellowships to Outstanding Persons in the Fields of Culture” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Fellowships for artists and scholars to undertake advanced research in culture.
eligibility_criteria: Artists/Scholars in the field of Culture.
Age: 25-40 (Junior), 40+ (Senior).
Not employed or receiving other pensions > ₹2000.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the CCRT/Ministry of Culture website.

scheme_name: Scheme For Campus Accommodation & Facilities Enhancing Social Experience (CAFES)
issuing_authority: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
objective: Scheme for Campus Accommodation & Facilities Enhancing Social Experience (CAFES) was introduced by AICTE to provide financial assistance for construction of hostel to create a congenial study atmosphere free from the shackles of domestic shores so as to encourage students to purse their education.
benefits_summary: Provides funding for construction of hostels in AICTE approved institutions.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹3 Crores (for SC/ST/NER) or ₹2 Crores (General).
eligibility_criteria: AICTE approved Govt/Govt-aided Institutions.
Must have land availability.
Institutions in existence for at least 5 years.
how_to_apply: Institutions apply via AICTE AQIS portal.

scheme_name: Scheme For Financial Assistance For Veteran Artists
objective: A pension scheme by MoC improves the financial and socio-economic status of the old artists and scholars who have contributed significantly in their specialized fields. Still/ due to old age/ they have to lead a miserable life or are in penury condition.
benefits_summary: Monthly pension for elderly artists living in poverty.
benefits_list: Recognizes contribution to art and culture.
eligibility_criteria: Artist/Scholar aged 60 years or above.
Annual income not exceeding ₹48,000.
Must have contributed significantly to art/culture.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Ministry of Culture website. Recommendation from Centre/State Govt required.

scheme_name: Scheme For Grant Of Rewards To The Children Of Salt Labourers
issuing_authority: Office of the Salt Commissioner, DPIIT
objective: The scheme “Scheme for Grant of Rewards to the Children of Salt Labourers” was launched by Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)/ Ministry of Commerce and Industry in the year 1985.
benefits_summary: Cash rewards for meritorious children of salt labourers.
benefits_list: Cash Reward based on marks secured in Class 10/12 exams.
Encourages education among salt worker families.
eligibility_criteria: Children of Salt Labourers.
Passed Class 10/12 Board Exams.
Selection based on merit.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Salt Commissioner's Office regional divisions.

scheme_name: Scheme Of Assistance To Disabled Persons For Purchase/Fitting Of Aids/Appliances (ADIP)
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: The main objective of the Scheme is to assist needy disabled persons in procuring durable/ sophisticated/ scientifically manufactured/ modern/ standard aids and appliances that can promote their physical/ social and psychological rehabilitation by reducing the effect of disabilities.
benefits_summary: Provides free or subsidized aids and assistive devices to Persons with Disabilities.
benefits_list: Includes Wheelchairs, Hearing Aids, Braille Kits, Artificial Limbs, Cochlear Implants.
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Monthly income up to ₹30,000.
Not received similar aid in last 3 years (1 year for children).
how_to_apply: Apply through ALIMCO camps, District Disability Rehabilitation Centres (DDRC), or NGOs.

scheme_name: Scheme Of Financial Assistance For The Preservation And Development Of Cultural Heritage Of The Himalayas
objective: The scheme “Scheme of Financial Assistance for the Preservation and Development of Cultural Heritage of the Himalayas” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Grants to NGOs for preserving the cultural heritage of the Himalayan region.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹10 Lakhs per year.
Funding for research, documentation, and dissemination of Himalayan culture.
eligibility_criteria: Voluntary Organizations (NGOs), Universities, Museums.
Located in Himalayan States/UTs (J&K, Ladakh, HP, UK, Sikkim, NE, West Bengal - Darjeeling).
Registered for at least 3 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Ministry of Culture scheme portal.

scheme_name: Scheme for Grant of Financial Assistance for Administering Road Safety Advocacy
issuing_authority: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH)
objective: The objective of the Scheme is to stimulate and encourage opportunities for various agencies through participation/ in order to create awareness on road safety.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to NGOs for conducting road safety awareness programs.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹5 Lakhs per project.
Supports campaigns, workshops, and training on road safety.
eligibility_criteria: Registered NGOs/Trusts/Societies.
Minimum 3 years of experience.
Working in the field of road safety/social welfare.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the MoRTH portal.

issuing_authority: Rubber Board, Ministry of Commerce & Industry
objective: The Scheme is intended to increase the production of natural rubber in India by accelerating new planting and replanting of rubber on scientific lines. Accelerated new planting/replanting would be achieved by giving proper technical guidance and financial assistance to growers.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for new planting and replanting of rubber.
benefits_list: Planting Subsidy: ₹25,000 per hectare (Traditional areas) / Higher for Non-Traditional/NE.
eligibility_criteria: Rubber Growers with land ownership.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
Registered with Rubber Board.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Rubber Board Field Officer.

scheme_name: Scheme for Supportive Money to the Parents of Transgender Children
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The "Scheme for Supportive Money to the Parents of Transgender Children" aims to provide financial assistance of ₹1,000/- to the parents of transgender children. Transgender persons face stigma and discrimination in almost every sphere of their life including within the group/ health facilities.
benefits_summary: Financial support to parents of transgender children to prevent abandonment and ensure care.
benefits_list: Promotes social inclusion within the family.
eligibility_criteria: Parents of Transgender Child.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹1,50,000.
Child must have Transgender ID/Certificate.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Social Welfare Officer.

scheme_name: Scheme of Assistance for the Prevention of Alcoholism & Substance (Drugs) Abuse and for Social Defence Services
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The scheme aims to meet needs falling within the mandate of the Ministry which cannot be met under its regular schemes and support such initiatives of an innovative/pilot nature in the area of welfare and empowerment of the Ministry’s target groups/ as cannot be supported under its regular schemes.
benefits_summary: Provides financial assistance to NGOs and other eligible organizations for running Integrated Rehabilitation Centres for Addicts (IRCAs) and awareness programs.
benefits_list: Grant-in-aid up to 90% of the approved expenditure.
Funding for awareness generation, counseling, treatment, and rehabilitation of addicts.
Supports setting up of De-addiction centres.
eligibility_criteria: Registered NGOs/Voluntary Organizations with at least 3 years of experience.
Trusts, Red Cross Societies, Panchayati Raj Institutions.
Must have necessary infrastructure.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the e-Anudaan portal of the Ministry.

scheme_name: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries (SFURTI)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME)
objective: To make the traditional industries more productive and competitive and facilitating their sustainable development/ the Govt. of India announced in 2005-06/ setting up of a fund for the regeneration of traditional industries. Pursuant to this announcement/ a Central Sector Scheme titled the “SFURTI".
benefits_summary: Develops clusters of traditional industries (Khadi, Coir, Village industries) to make them competitive and sustainable.
benefits_list: Financial assistance up to ₹2.50 Crores (Regular Cluster) or ₹5.00 Crores (Major Cluster).
Support for infrastructure (Common Facility Centers), skill training, and design intervention.
eligibility_criteria: Implementing Agencies (NGOs, Central/State Govt agencies, Panchayati Raj Institutions).
Beneficiaries: Artisans in traditional industries (Khadi, Coir, etc.).
Cluster must have minimum 500 artisans.
how_to_apply: Implementing Agencies submit proposals to Nodal Agencies (KVIC, Coir Board, etc.).

scheme_name: Scheme of Grant-in-Aid for Professional Associations/ Organisations of Social Scientists
issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)/ under the Ministry of Education/ Government of India/ provides Grants-in-Aid to professional associations/ organisations of social scientists/ universities/ colleges/ and research institutes
benefits_summary: Financial support for maintenance and development of professional associations in social sciences.
benefits_list: Maintenance grant for infrastructure and library.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Professional Associations of Social Scientists.
Research Institutes/Universities/Colleges.
Must be registered for at least 3 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the ICSSR website.

scheme_name: Scheme of Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary Organisations Working for the Welfare of Scheduled Tribes
objective: The Central Sector Scheme “Scheme of Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary Organisations Working for the Welfare of Scheduled Tribes” was launched by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs/ Government of India.
benefits_summary: Provides grants to NGOs for projects benefiting Scheduled Tribes in education, health, and livelihood.
benefits_list: Funding for residential schools, hostels, mobile dispensaries.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Voluntary Organizations/NGOs functioning for at least 3 years.
Must be registered on NGO Darpan portal.
Experience in working for ST welfare.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Ministry's NGO Grants portal.

scheme_name: Scheme of Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary and other Organizations Working for Scheduled Castes
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: The "Scheme aims to enhance the reach of development interventions of the Government and fill the gaps in service-deficient Scheduled Caste-dominant areas in sectors such as education/ health/ vocational training/ etc./ through the efforts of voluntary organizations and other organizations.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to NGOs for running schools, hostels, and training centers for SC beneficiaries.
benefits_list: Grants for running residential/non-residential schools and hostels.
eligibility_criteria: Registered NGOs/VOs with 2 years of experience.
Beneficiaries must be Scheduled Castes.
Registered on e-Anudaan portal.
how_to_apply: Apply online via e-Anudaan portal.

scheme_name: Scheme of Grants-in-Aid for supporting professional associations/ organisations of social scientists (Seminars)
issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)/ under the Ministry of Education/ Government of India/ provides Grants-in-Aid for supporting professional associations/ organisations of social scientists/ companies and trusts for their development. (Specifically for Seminars/Conferences).
benefits_summary: Grants for organizing national and international seminars/conferences in social sciences.
benefits_list: Funding for venue, travel, boarding, and publication of proceedings.
Promotes exchange of ideas among social scientists.
eligibility_criteria: Universities, Colleges, Research Institutes.
Registered Professional Associations.
Seminar topic must be relevant to social sciences.
how_to_apply: Apply online via ICSSR website.

scheme_name: Scheme of Internship for Post-Graduate/Research Students (MoSPI)
issuing_authority: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)
objective: MoSPI is implementing the scheme of Internship for Post-graduate/Research Students of Statistics/ Mathematical Statistics/ Operations Research/ Economics/ Demography or any of the applied field of Statistics of recognized Universities//Institutions.
benefits_summary: Internship to expose students to the official statistical system of India.
benefits_list: Experience in data collection, analysis, and report writing.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing PG/Research in Statistics, Economics, or related fields.
Enrolled in a recognized University/Institute.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form to MoSPI or Regional Offices.

scheme_name: Scheme of Internship of Students to DRDO Labs/Estts for Project Training
objective: The scheme aims to provide an opportunity and exposure to future graduates and postgraduates in Engineering and General Sciences to the research and development activities carried out by DRDO in state-of-the-art technology in fields relevant to the defense sector.
benefits_summary: Offers project training/internship to engineering and science students in DRDO labs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech/M.Sc.
Indian Citizen.
how_to_apply: Apply directly to the Director of the specific DRDO Lab.

issuing_authority: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
objective: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)/ under the Ministry of Education/ Government of India/ provides financial assistance for the publication of high-quality doctoral theses/ research project/fellowship reports/ and seminar/conference papers in the field of social sciences.
benefits_summary: Provides subsidy for publishing high-quality research work in social sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Ph.D. degree holder or Project Director.
Manuscript accepted by a reputed publisher.
Work must be in social sciences.
how_to_apply: Apply online via ICSSR website.

scheme_name: Scholarship Scheme for Girl Children of Jute Mills / MSMEs Workers
objective: National Jute Board Act/ 2008 provides to “secure better working conditions and provisions and improve amenities and incentives for workers engaged in the jute industry.”
benefits_summary: Scholarship for girl children of workers in Jute Mills/Jute MSMEs who pass Class 10 and 12.
eligibility_criteria: Girl child of Jute Mill/MSME worker.
Passed Secondary/Higher Secondary Exam.
Worker must be registered with Jute Mill/Unit.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Jute Mill/Unit management to NJB.

scheme_name: Security Related Expenditure (Relief and Rehabilitation) Scheme - J&K
objective: The scheme aims to support the relief and rehabilitation of the Kashmiri migrants who have been uprooted from the Valley during the militancy period besides other relief & rehabilitation measures. "SRE (R&R) Scheme" is a component of the Umbrella scheme/ 'Modernization of Police Forces (MPF)".
benefits_summary: Provides relief assistance to Kashmiri Migrants.
benefits_list: Cash Relief: ₹3,250 per person per month (Max ₹13,000 per family).
Dry Ration: 9 kg Rice, 2 kg Atta per person per month.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Kashmiri Migrants living in Jammu/Delhi/NCR.
General eligibility criteria apply (Displaced due to militancy).
how_to_apply: Register with the Relief and Rehabilitation Commissioner (Migrants), J&K.

scheme_name: Self Employment Scheme For Rehabilitation Of Manual Scavengers (SRMS)
issuing_authority: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
objective: The Self Employment Scheme for Rehabilitation of Manual Scavengers (SRMS) was introduced in January 2017 by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment/ to assist the identified manual scavengers and their dependants in rehabilitation in alternative occupations.
benefits_summary: Comprehensive rehabilitation package for identified manual scavengers.
benefits_list: Skill Development Training with stipend of ₹3,000/month.
Capital Subsidy up to ₹5 Lakhs for self-employment projects.
eligibility_criteria: Identified Manual Scavengers and their dependents.
Listed in the survey list of Manual Scavengers.
how_to_apply: Apply through NSKFDC or State Channelizing Agencies.

objective: Introduced in 2004 by the Govt. of India/ SCSS offers a steady stream of income for individuals over 60 years old. As it is a government-backed scheme/ there is minimal risk associated with SCSS. Individuals can apply for this scheme in post offices and public and private banks.
benefits_summary: A secure investment scheme for senior citizens with regular interest payouts.
eligibility_criteria: Individual aged 60 years or above.
Retired employees aged 55-60 years (invest within 1 month of retirement benefit receipt).
Retired Defence Personnel aged 50+.
how_to_apply: Visit any Post Office or Authorized Bank Branch.

objective: The scheme “Seva Bhoj Yojna” was launched by the Ministry of Culture/ Government of India and it has been made operational with effect from the 1st of August/ 2018. The scheme shall be applicable within the territorial jurisdiction of India and will remain open from the 1st to 15th of every month.
benefits_summary: Reimburses CGST and IGST on food items used by charitable institutions for providing free food (Langar/Prasad).
benefits_list: Reimbursement of Central GST and Integrated GST on raw food ingredients (Ghee, Oil, Rice, Atta, etc.).
Reduces financial burden on charitable institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Charitable Religious Institutions (Temples, Gurudwaras, Mosques, Churches, etc.).
Must distribute free food to at least 5000 people per month.
Registered under Darpan Portal and Section 12AA of IT Act.
how_to_apply: Enroll online on the Darpan Portal/Ministry of Culture website.

issuing_authority: Central Waqf Council, Ministry of Minority Affairs
objective: Under the scheme/ interest free loan to Waqf Boards (WBs)/waqf institutions is granted to various Waqf Boards and Waqf Institutions in the country for construction of economically viable buildings on the Waqf land/ such as commercial complex/ marriage halls/ hospitals/ cold storage etc..
benefits_summary: Provides interest-free loans to develop Waqf properties for generating income.
benefits_list: Interest-free loan for construction of commercial complexes, hospitals, etc. on Waqf land.
eligibility_criteria: Waqf Boards/Waqf Institutions.
Must have registered Waqf land.
Project must be economically viable.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to the Central Waqf Council.

objective: The “Shilp Guru Award” for master craftspersons was introduced by the Ministry of Textiles to honor legendary master craftspersons of handicrafts whose work and dedication have contributed not only to the preservation but also to the promotion of the rich and diverse craft heritage of the country.
benefits_summary: Highest award for master craftspersons in the handicrafts sector.
eligibility_criteria: Master Craftsperson with at least 20 years of experience.
Age: Minimum 50 years.
Must have trained at least 10 disciples.
Extraordinary contribution to the craft.
how_to_apply: Apply through the DC (Handicrafts) website or regional offices.

scheme_name: Short Term Fellowship under Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research
objective: Short Term Fellowship under the Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research aims to provide advanced training in India & abroad to medical and health research personnel in cutting-edge research areas related to medicine & health.
benefits_summary: Short-term training fellowship for medical researchers to upgrade skills.
benefits_list: Travel cost and course fee coverage.
eligibility_criteria: Faculty/Researchers in medical colleges/institutes.
Age Limit: 45 years.
Regular employment in a recognized institute.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the DHR web portal.

objective: National Small Industries Corporation enlists Micro & Small Enterprises under Single Point Registration scheme for participation in Government Purchases. The units enlisted under this scheme of NSIC are eligible to get the benefits under Public Procurement Policy for Micro & Small Enterprises.
benefits_summary: Registers MSEs to participate in government procurement with benefits.
benefits_list: Exemption from payment of Earnest Money Deposit (EMD).
Issue of Tender Sets free of cost.
eligibility_criteria: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) with Udyam Registration.
Must have started commercial production.
Applicant's employment status: Business Owner.
how_to_apply: Apply online via NSIC SPRS portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE)
objective: A Skill loan scheme by Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship to provide loan upto Rs. 1/50/000 to student who has secured admission in a course run by Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs)/ Polytechnics or in a school recognized by Central or State education Boards or in a college affil
benefits_summary: Provides loans to students for pursuing skill development courses.
eligibility_criteria: Indian National.
Admitted to a course in ITI, Polytechnic, or recognized skill training institute.
No specific age limit (usually for youth).
how_to_apply: Apply at any Nationalized Bank.

scheme_name: Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI) Internship
objective: The SECI Internship Policy is designed to provide practical experience to students in the renewable energy sector. The program aims to give interns exposure to SECI's various divisions/ helping them understand the renewable energy sector.
benefits_summary: Internship for students to gain exposure to solar energy projects and management.
benefits_list: Practical training in Renewable Energy sector.
eligibility_criteria: Pursuing Engineering (Renewable Energy/Electrical/Civil/Mechanical) or MBA (Power Management/Finance) or CA/CS/Law.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
how_to_apply: Send application to HR Department, SECI via email/post.

scheme_name: Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (SCLCSS) for SC/ST
issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (NSSH)
objective: Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (SCLCSS) for Scheduled Caste / Scheduled scheme aims to facilitate capital subsidy to SC/ST MSEs for purchasing of plant & machinery/equipment through institutional finance (bank loan).
benefits_summary: Provides capital subsidy to SC/ST MSEs for technology upgradation.
benefits_list: 25% capital subsidy on institutional finance for plant & machinery.
eligibility_criteria: MSEs owned by SC/ST entrepreneurs.
Must have availed term loan for technology upgradation.
Valid Udyam Registration.
how_to_apply: Apply through the lending bank/Primary Lending Institution (PLI).

objective: A scheme by Ministry of Finance for financing SC/ST and Women Entrepreneurs by facilitating bank loans for setting up a greenfield project Enterprise in manufacturing/ services/ trading sector and activities allied to agriculture.
benefits_summary: Facilitates bank loans for SC/ST and Women entrepreneurs to set up new businesses.
benefits_list: No collateral required (covered under Credit Guarantee Scheme).
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST or Woman Entrepreneur (above 18 years).
Setting up a Greenfield Project (First-time venture).
Manufacturing, Service, Trading, or Agri-allied sector.
how_to_apply: Apply at bank branch, through Stand-Up India portal, or via Lead District Manager.

objective: The Dept. of Biotechnology has launched "Star College Scheme for Strengthening of Science Education and Training at Undergraduate Level" for improving critical thinking and 'hands-on' experimental work at the undergraduate (college) level in sciences.
benefits_summary: Grants to colleges to improve science teaching and laboratory infrastructure.
benefits_list: One-time non-recurring grant (₹10 Lakhs per dept).
Recurring grant (₹3 Lakhs per dept per year).
eligibility_criteria: Colleges offering UG courses in Life Sciences/Physical Sciences.
Private/Govt colleges.
Must have adequate faculty and infrastructure.
how_to_apply: Colleges apply online through DBT eProMIS.

scheme_name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme- For Incubators
issuing_authority: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: The scheme “Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)” was launched by the DPIIT on 19th April 2021 to provide financial assistance to startups for proof of concept/ prototype development/ product trials/ market-entry/ and commercialization. (Component for Incubators).
benefits_summary: Provides funds to eligible incubators to disburse as seed funding to startups.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹5 Crores to the Incubator.
Enables incubators to support up to 20-25 startups.
eligibility_criteria: Incubator must be a legal entity.
Operational for at least 2 years.
Must have seating capacity for 25 startups.
Supported by Central/State Govt.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the SISFS portal.

scheme_name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme- For Startups
issuing_authority: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: The scheme “Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)” was launched by the DPIIT on 19th April 2021 with an outlay of INR 945 Crore to provide financial assistance to startups for Proof of Concept/ prototype development/ product trials/ market-entry/ and commercialization.
benefits_summary: Seed funding for early-stage startups for proof of concept and market entry.
benefits_list: Up to ₹20 Lakhs as Grant for Proof of Concept/Prototype.
Up to ₹50 Lakhs as Debt/Convertible Debentures for Commercialization/Market Entry.
eligibility_criteria: DPIIT recognized Startup.
Incorporated not more than 2 years ago.
Must have a business idea with market potential.
Preference for sectors like social impact, waste management, etc.
how_to_apply: Apply online on the SISFS portal to selected incubators.

issuing_authority: Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)
objective: The Student Internship Program/ a unique learning opportunity aims to provide the Interns exposure to the functioning of the CAG of India and the Indian Audit & Accounts Department.
benefits_summary: Internship for students to gain exposure to public audit and accounting processes.
benefits_list: Hands-on experience in public finance and audit.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing PG/Research in Economics, Finance, Management, Commerce.
Good academic record.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form and CV to the CAG office or Regional Training Institutes.

scheme_name: Students READY (Rural Entrepreneurship Awareness Development Yojana)
objective: The Student Rural Entrepreneurship Awareness Development Yojana (READY) was launched by Hon'ble Prime Minister on 25 July 2015. It aims to provide rural entrepreneurship awareness, practical training in real-life situations in rural agriculture, and creating awareness to undergraduate students.
benefits_summary: Provides practical hands-on training to agricultural graduates to build entrepreneurship skills.
benefits_list: Stipend of ₹3,000 per month for 6 months.
Skill development in rural agriculture and allied sectors.
Components: RAWE, AIA, ELP, Hands-on Training.
eligibility_criteria: Final year Undergraduate students of Agricultural Universities.
Applicant's employment status: Student.
Course: Agriculture and Allied Sciences.
how_to_apply: Implemented as part of the course curriculum in Agricultural Universities.

objective: The Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization was launched in 2014-15 by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare. It aims to increase the reach of farm mechanization to small and marginal farmers and to the regions where availability of farm power is low.
benefits_summary: Provides subsidy for purchasing agricultural machinery and equipment.
benefits_list: Subsidy of 40-50% on agricultural machinery (Tractors, Power Tillers, etc.).
Demonstration and training on farm machinery.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers, SHGs, FPOs, Cooperatives.
Small and Marginal Farmers given priority.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Direct Benefit Transfer in Agriculture Mechanization (DBT-AgriMachinery) portal.

scheme_name: Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry
issuing_authority: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying
objective: The Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying introduced the National Livestock Mission as umbrella scheme. The "Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry" is the realigned National Livestock Mission/ which is the scheme's sub-mission.
benefits_summary: Focuses on genetic upgradation of livestock and poultry for higher productivity.
benefits_list: Subsidy for setting up breeding farms (Sheep, Goat, Pig, Poultry).
Assistance for semen stations and IVF technology.
eligibility_criteria: Individual Farmers, SHGs, FPOs, JLGs, Section 8 Companies.
Entrepreneurs willing to set up breeding units.
State Governments/Cooperatives.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NLM portal.

objective: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) Summer Internship Programme aims to provide a platform for highly motivated students & teachers to interact with PRL’s scientists/ gaining exposure to the lab’s research areas and facilities. Participants will work on projects in fundamental science/ technology
benefits_summary: Internship for students to work on research projects in Physics, Space Sciences, etc.
benefits_list: Access to PRL facilities and library.
eligibility_criteria: Students pursuing M.Sc./Integrated M.Sc./B.Tech/M.Tech.
Minimum 60% marks in previous exams.
Interest in Physics, Astronomy, Space Sciences, Geosciences.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the PRL website when applications open (usually Jan-Feb).

objective: The Summer Internship Scheme by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade is for the interaction of the officers of the Directorate with young scholars and researchers with brilliant academic backgrounds from reputed academic institutions pursuing Studies/Research in Public Policy/Economics/Law etc.
benefits_summary: Internship to understand foreign trade policy and international trade dynamics.
eligibility_criteria: Students pursuing Law (Final Year), Economics, Finance, Management (Grad/PG).
Applicant's employment status: Student.
From recognized National Law Universities/Reputed Institutes.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DGFT website.

scheme_name: Supporting Community Radio Movement in India
objective: The GOI has a policy for grant of License to eligible organisations for setting up of Community Radio Stations. To support the Sector/ the Scheme provides financial assistance once the Community Radio is made operational by eligible organisation. The Scheme also has components for capacity building.
benefits_summary: Financial support for setting up and sustaining Community Radio Stations (CRS).
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹10 Lakhs for equipment purchase.
Grant for content creation and technical upgradation.
Promotes local voice and community development.
eligibility_criteria: Educational Institutions, NGOs, Agricultural Universities (SAUs).
Registered for at least 3 years.
Must have obtained LOI/GOPA for setting up CRS.
how_to_apply: Submit proposal to the Ministry of Information & Broadcasting.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
objective: SUMAN Yojana is a maternity benefit initiative by MoH&FW. This program provides affordable and quality healthcare solutions to pregnant women and newborns. The scheme provides zero expense access up to six months after delivery. Upto antenatal check-ups during the 1st trimester are provided.
benefits_summary: Ensures zero-cost, dignified, and respectful delivery care for mothers and newborns.
benefits_list: Free transport, drugs, diagnostics, and diet.
Zero tolerance for denial of services.
eligibility_criteria: All Pregnant Women.
All Newborns (up to 6 months).
Visit any Public Health Facility.
how_to_apply: Available automatically at all Government Hospitals/Health Centres.

issuing_authority: Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation
objective: A sanitation scheme for Visual cleanliness through Open Defecation Free (ODF) sustainability and effective management of solid and liquid waste in villages.
benefits_summary: Focuses on ODF Plus status: Sustaining toilet usage and managing Solid & Liquid Waste (SLWM).
benefits_list: Community Sanitary Complexes (CSC) funding: Up to ₹3 Lakhs.
Funds for SLWM projects (compost pits, soak pits, drainage) at GP level.
eligibility_criteria: Rural Households without toilets (BPL/Identified APL).
Gram Panchayats for community projects.
how_to_apply: For IHHL: Apply via Gram Panchayat or SBM-G portal. For Community projects: GP prepares plan.

scheme_name: Swachh Bharat Mission – Grameen PHASE I
issuing_authority: Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation
objective: The scheme aims to eliminate open defecation and improve rural sanitation by supporting toilet construction and promoting usage among poor and marginalized rural households.
benefits_summary: Aimed at achieving Open Defecation Free (ODF) India by constructing toilets. (Phase I completed in 2019, now in Phase II).
benefits_list: Incentive of ₹12,000 for constructing household toilets.
eligibility_criteria: Rural households without toilets (BPL/SC/ST/PwD/Small Farmers).
Left-out beneficiaries (LOBs).
how_to_apply: Contact Gram Panchayat or apply online for LOB/NOLB category.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
objective: A sanitation scheme for the construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL) for urban households. Also focuses on Garbage Free Cities.
benefits_summary: Supports urban sanitation and solid waste management.
benefits_list: Subsidy for Individual Household Latrine (IHHL): Central share ₹4,000 + State share.
Funds for Solid Waste Management projects.
eligibility_criteria: Urban households without toilets.
Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) for waste management projects.
how_to_apply: For IHHL: Apply online via SBM-Urban portal or at ULB/Ward office.

scheme_name: Swami Vivekananda Single Girl Child Fellowship for Research in Social Sciences
objective: A fellowship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC) for Single Girl Child for research in Social Sciences with an aim to compensate for direct costs of higher education/ especially for such girls who happen to be the only girl child in their family.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for Single Girl Child to pursue Ph.D. in Social Sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Only Girl Child in the family.
Registered for regular full-time Ph.D. in Social Sciences.
Age: Up to 40 years (General), 45 years (Reserved).
how_to_apply: Apply online via UGC/NTA portal.

objective: Under this scheme/ a selected number of young scientists/ with proven track record/ are provided special assistance and support to enable them to pursue basic research in frontier areas of science and technology.
benefits_summary: Awards fellowships to outstanding young scientists for independent research.
benefits_list: Monthly Fellowship of ₹25,000 (in addition to salary).
Research Grant of ₹5 Lakhs per annum for 5 years.
eligibility_criteria: Indian National.
Ph.D. in Science/Engineering/Medicine.
Age: 30 to 40 years.
Must have a regular position in a recognized Indian institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the DST e-PMS portal against the call for proposals.

objective: Launched on 15th August 1972/ "Swatantrata Sainik Samman Pension Scheme" is a Pension Scheme by the Ministry of Home Affairs. The scheme provides the grant of pension to living freedom fighters and their families; if they are no more alive/ and to the families or martyrs.
benefits_summary: Provides monthly pension to Freedom Fighters and their eligible dependents.
benefits_list: Monthly Pension: Approx ₹30,000+ DA (varies by category).
Free Railway Pass (AC 2 Tier/1st Class) for self and companion.
eligibility_criteria: Freedom Fighter who suffered imprisonment for at least 6 months.
Spouse/Unmarried Daughters/Dependent Parents (if FF is deceased).
Income limits apply for dependents.
how_to_apply: Apply through the State Government with necessary proofs.

objective: The ‘TEC Internship Scheme’ intends to engage 25 Interns in order to study/formulate/operationalize/assess various works/projects/policies etc. The internship will provide an opportunity for young scholars to contribute to the TEC work and have insight into the related technical work.
benefits_summary: Internship for students to work on telecom standards and engineering.
benefits_list: Exposure to telecom standardization and testing.
eligibility_criteria: Final year B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech (Electronics/Comm/IT/CS).
Minimum 60% marks.
Age: 18-30 years.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application to ADG (Estt), TEC, New Delhi.

scheme_name: Tagore National Fellowship for Cultural Research
objective: The Scheme has been introduced to invigorate and revitalize the various institutions under the Ministry of Culture and other identified cultural institutions in the country/ by encouraging Fellows/academicians to affiliate themselves with these institutions to work on projects of mutual interest.
benefits_summary: High-value fellowship for eminent scholars to research in cultural institutions (Museums, Archives, Libraries).
eligibility_criteria: Eminent Scholars/Researchers with established reputation.
Strong publication record in Culture/History/Art.
Proposed project must benefit the host institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Ministry of Culture website or Nodal Institutions.

scheme_name: Tagore National Scholarship for Cultural Research
objective: The Scheme has been introduced to invigorate and revitalize the various institutions under the Ministry of Culture and other identified cultural institutions in the country/ by encouraging Scholars/academicians to affiliate themselves with these institutions to work on projects of mutual interest.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for young researchers to work in cultural institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Scholars with good academic credentials.
Significant research work/publications.
Age: No specific bar, generally for mid-career scholars.
how_to_apply: Apply via Ministry of Culture website.

scheme_name: Tea Development and Promotion Scheme (TDPS): Plantation Development for Small Tea Growers
objective: The objective of this component is to increase the production/ productivity and quality of tea/ overall development of small tea growers to help them move up in the value chain/ sustainability of production by promoting organic tea cultivation& climate resilient tea and to make them self-reliant.
benefits_summary: Provides assistance to small tea growers for replanting, mechanization, and quality improvement.
benefits_list: Assistance for purchasing pruning machines and harvesters.
Support for setting up mini tea factories.
eligibility_criteria: Small Tea Growers (holding up to 10.12 hectares).
SHGs/FPOs of small growers.
Registered with Tea Board.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the nearest Tea Board office.

objective: The scheme “Teachers Associateship for Research Excellence (TARE)” was launched by Science & Engineering Research Board/ Department of Science & Technology/ Government of India which aims to facilitate mobility of faculty members working in State Universities /Colleges/Private Academic institutions.
benefits_summary: Allows faculty from state universities/colleges to carry out research in premier institutes (IITs/IISc/CSIR labs).
benefits_list: Fellowship: ₹5,000 per month (in addition to salary).
eligibility_criteria: Regular Faculty in State Universities/Colleges/Private Institutions.
Ph.D. in Science/Engineering.
Age: Below 45 years.
Must have a mentor in a Central Institution.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SERB portal.

scheme_name: Technology Development And Utilization Programme For Women (TDUPW)
issuing_authority: Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR)
objective: In order to meet the specific needs of women and to enhance the contribution of women towards technology capability building/ the “Technology Development & Utilization Programme for Women (TDUPW)” has been formulated by the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR).
benefits_summary: Supports projects for technology development and dissemination specifically benefiting women.
benefits_list: Grants to institutions/NGOs for women-centric technology projects.
Promotes adoption of new technologies by women.
eligibility_criteria: R&D Institutions, Universities, IITs.
NGOs/Voluntary Organizations working for women.
Beneficiaries: Women.
how_to_apply: Submit project proposal to DSIR.

objective: Technology Development Fund (TDF) has been established to promote self-reliance in Defence Technology as a part of the 'Make in India' initiative.
benefits_summary: Grants to MSMEs and Startups for developing defence technologies.
benefits_list: Grant up to ₹10 Crores (90% of project cost).
Opportunity to become a defence supplier.
eligibility_criteria: Indian MSMEs and Startups.
Registered as a company under Companies Act.
Maximum foreign shareholding 49%.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the TDF portal against specific problem statements.

objective: The Telecom Technology Development Fund Scheme (hereinafter referred to as ‘Scheme’) has been notified for domestic companies and institutions involved in technology design/ development/ commercialization of telecommunication products and solutions to enable affordable broadband and mobile services.
benefits_summary: Funds R&D in telecom technologies for affordable broadband and mobile services.
benefits_list: Promotes indigenous manufacturing and IPR creation.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic Companies, Startups, R&D Institutions, Academia.
Indian entities working in telecom technology.
how_to_apply: Submit proposals via the TTDF portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
objective: MYAS confers the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award in order to recognize the achievements of persons associated with the field of adventure and to encourage young people to develop the spirit of endurance/ risk-taking/ cooperative teamwork/ and quick/ ready/ and effective reflexes.
benefits_summary: Highest national award for adventure sports (Land, Sea, Air).
benefits_list: Categories: Land Adventure, Water Adventure, Air Adventure, Life Time Achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Individuals with outstanding achievement in adventure sports.
Indian Citizens.
Consistent performance over last 3 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Awards Portal.

issuing_authority: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (NSSH)
objective: Testing Fee Reimbursement Scheme for Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe is a sub scheme under National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) scheme.
benefits_summary: Reimburses testing charges paid by SC/ST MSEs to NABL accredited labs.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of 80% or ₹1,00,000 (whichever is less) of testing fees.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST owned MSEs.
Udyam Registration.
Testing done at NABL/BIS accredited labs.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National SC-ST Hub (NSSH) portal.

scheme_name: The Inland Waterways Authority of India Internship Scheme
objective: The purpose of this scheme is to provide with Selected Candidates’ short term exposure to different verticals/ divisions/ units of IWAI as “Interns”. An intern may be required to work at Head Office or Project Office depending upon the requirement of the Authority.
benefits_summary: Internship to gain exposure to inland waterways transport and management.
benefits_list: Practical experience in logistics and waterways.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status: Student.
Pursuing Civil Engg, Naval Architecture, Logistics, Law, Finance, etc.
From recognized Universities.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the form to IWAI Secretary via email/post.

scheme_name: The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme
objective: The Lokpal of India launched the Internship Scheme for the interns to learn about the implementation of the Lokpal & Lokayuktas Act/ the Prevention of Corruption Act/ the Central Vigilance Commission Act/ & they can contribute towards the effective functioning of the Lokpal of India with fresh ideas
benefits_summary: Internship opportunity to understand the anti-corruption framework of India.
benefits_list: Experience in legal research and anti-corruption laws.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing LL.B./LL.M./MBA/Economics/Public Policy/Management.
Required education level: Undergraduate (Law - 2nd/4th year) or Postgraduate.
how_to_apply: Apply by sending the application form to Lokpal of India via email or post.

issuing_authority: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
objective: The NSFDC Internship Scheme 2024 is implemented by the National Scheduled Castes Finance & Development Corporation/ Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment/ Govt. of India. The internship will expose students to various programs & schemes undertaken by NSFDC/ offering them hands-on experience in
benefits_summary: Provides internship to students to gain experience in financial inclusion and social welfare schemes.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Pursuing MBA/PGDM/MSW/Economics/Commerce.
Minimum 60% marks in Graduation.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the NSFDC portal or send application to NSFDC office.

scheme_name: Top Class Education For Scheduled Caste Students
issuing_authority: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
objective: A scholarship scheme by Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment for Scheduled Caste students pursuing studies beyond class 12th admitted in a full-time course in one of the notified institutions. The total annual family income from all sources must be less than or equal to Rs. 8.00 lakh.
benefits_summary: Financial support to SC students for pursuing higher education in premier institutes (IITs, IIMs, NITs, etc.).
benefits_list: Full Tuition Fee coverage (up to ₹2.00 Lakhs per annum for private institutes, full for Govt).
eligibility_criteria: Belong to Scheduled Caste (SC).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹8,00,000.
Secured admission in a notified Top Class Institution.
Selected based on merit (entrance exam rank).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Top Class Education For Students With Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
objective: A scholarship scheme by DoEPwDs for Students with Disabilities (SwDs) pursuing studies beyond class 12th admitted in a full-time course in one of the 'notified institutions'.
benefits_summary: Scholarship for students with disabilities to study in top-tier institutions.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of Tuition Fees (up to ₹2.00 Lakhs per annum).
eligibility_criteria: Person with Disability (min 40%).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹8,00,000.
Admitted to a notified Top Class Institute.
Pursuing Graduate/Postgraduate degree/diploma.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

scheme_name: Travel Grant Scheme For College Teachers / Librarians / Vice Chancellors (UGC)
objective: This scheme provides financial assistance to permanent teachers and librarians of colleges and vice-chancellors of state universities/ deemed to be universities and central universities to present research papers at international conferences abroad.
benefits_summary: Reimbursement of travel expenses for attending international conferences.
eligibility_criteria: Permanent Teacher/Librarian in UGC recognized College/University.
Paper accepted for presentation at an International Conference.
Not availed travel grant in the last 3 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via UGC portal at least 2 months prior to the conference.

scheme_name: Upgrading the Skills and Training in Traditional Arts/Crafts for Development (USTTAD)
objective: The Upgrading the Skills and Training in Traditional Arts/Crafts for Development (USTTAD) scheme is a program launched by the Ministry of Minority Affairs/ Government of India/ in 2015. It aims to preserve the rich heritage of traditional arts/crafts of minorities.
benefits_summary: Preserves traditional arts/crafts of minorities and builds capacity of traditional artisans.
benefits_list: Training for master craftsmen and artisans.
Support for design development and marketing linkage (Hunar Haat).
eligibility_criteria: Artisans belonging to Minority Communities.
Traditional craftsmen/weavers.
Age: 14 to 45 years.
Family income criteria may apply for trainees.
how_to_apply: Enroll through Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs) selected by the Ministry.

objective: VAIBHAV Fellowship envisages collaboration between scientists of Indian Diaspora with Indian Higher Educational Institutions/Universities/Public Funded Scientific Institutions. Fellow would identify an Indian Institution for collaboration & may spend upto two months in a year for a max. of 3 years.
benefits_summary: Fellowship for Indian Diaspora scientists to collaborate with Indian institutions.
benefits_list: Fellowship: ₹4 Lakhs per month (for the duration of stay in India).
eligibility_criteria: NRI/OCI/PIO Scientist/Academician.
Active researcher in Science/Technology.
Must have a collaborator in an Indian Institute (Host).
Ph.D./M.D. degree.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the VAIBHAV portal.
required_documents: Consent Letter from Indian Host Institute.

scheme_name: Vanchit Ikai Samooh Aur Vargon Ko Aarthik Sahayata (VISVAS) Yojana
issuing_authority: National Backward Classes Finance & Development Corporation (NBCFDC) / NSFDC
objective: The VISVAS (Vanchit Ikai Samooh aur Vargon ko Aarthik Sahayata) Scheme aims to provide interest subvention to OBC/SC Self Help Groups (SHGs) or individuals. It is being implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
benefits_summary: Interest subvention scheme for SC and OBC Self Help Groups (SHGs) to access bank loans at lower interest rates.
benefits_list: Interest Subvention of 5% p.a. on bank loans.
Maximum loan amount eligible for subvention: ₹4 Lakhs per SHG / ₹2 Lakhs per individual.
eligibility_criteria: SC/OBC Self Help Groups (SHGs) or Individuals.
Annual family income < ₹3 Lakhs.
Must have availed loan from Bank/SHG Federation.
how_to_apply: Interest subvention is directly credited to the beneficiary's loan account through DBT. Lending institutions submit claims.

scheme_name: Vice Chancellor Internship Scheme (VCIS) - University of Delhi
objective: The Vice Chancellor Internship Scheme (VCIS) by the University of Delhi aims to provide students with experiential learning opportunities/ integrating academic knowledge with practical work experience. This initiative aligns with the objectives of "Samagra Shiksha" (holistic education) as outlined..
benefits_summary: Part-time internship for students of Delhi University to work in various departments/centers of the University.
benefits_list: Flexible working hours (8-10 hours per week).
eligibility_criteria: Regular student of University of Delhi (UG/PG).
Not applicable for first-year/first-semester students.
Minimum CGPA requirements may apply.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the VCIS portal of Delhi University.

scheme_name: Vikaas-Day Care Scheme For Person with Disability Children
issuing_authority: The National Trust (Ministry of Social Justice)
objective: Vikaas is a Day care scheme/ primarily to expand the range of opportunities available to a person with disability attaining the age of 10 years for enhancing interpersonal and vocational skills as they are on a transition to higher age groups.
benefits_summary: Day care facilities for PwDs (above 10 years) to enhance interpersonal and vocational skills.
benefits_list: Day care for at least 6 hours per day.
eligibility_criteria: Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, MR, Multiple Disabilities.
Age: 10 years and above (No upper age limit).
Registered with the National Trust.
how_to_apply: Enroll at a registered Vikaas Centre (NGO).

issuing_authority: Department of Women Development and Child Welfare
objective: A financial assistance scheme under the 'Super Six' guarantees to support women aged 18-59 years with a monthly stipend to ensure their economic independence.
benefits_summary: Provides monthly financial aid to eligible women to support their livelihood and daily needs.
benefits_list: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the beneficiary's bank account.
Free bus travel for women in RTC buses is also part of this welfare umbrella.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Female aged between 18 and 59 years.
Annual family income must be below the BPL limit (approx ₹2.5 Lakhs in rural / ₹3 Lakhs in urban areas).
how_to_apply: Applications will be accepted through the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or the GSWS portal.

objective: To enable students from SC, ST, BC, EBC, and Minority communities to pursue higher education (Masters/Ph.D.) in top universities abroad by providing financial assistance.
benefits_summary: Financial grant for tuition fees and living expenses for studying abroad.
benefits_list: Grant of up to ₹15 Lakhs (or as per actuals) for tuition and living expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Annual family income limit: ₹6,00,000.
Valid admission offer from a recognized foreign university.
Qualifying score in GRE/GMAT/TOEFL/IELTS.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Jnanabhumi portal.

scheme_name: AMRUT / Smart Cities Mission - State Share
issuing_authority: Municipal Administration and Urban Development Department
objective: State's contribution to the central AMRUT and Smart Cities missions to improve urban infrastructure, ensuring water supply, sewerage, and digital governance in cities.
benefits_summary: Development of core urban infrastructure for better quality of life in cities.
benefits_list: Development of green spaces and parks.
Smart city solutions for traffic and safety.
eligibility_criteria: Residents of designated AMRUT and Smart Cities in AP.
how_to_apply: Implemented by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs). Citizens benefit directly.

issuing_authority: Department of Women Development and Child Welfare
objective: To improve the nutritional and health status of children (0-6 years), pregnant women, and lactating mothers through a package of six services.
benefits_summary: Provides nutrition, health check-ups, and pre-school education.
benefits_list: Supplementary Nutrition (Hot cooked meals/Take Home Ration).
eligibility_criteria: Children (0-6 years).
Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers.
Resident of the Anganwadi catchment area.
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre.

objective: A farmer welfare scheme providing financial investment support of ₹20,000 per annum to farmers to help them purchase agricultural inputs like seeds and fertilizers.
benefits_summary: Direct financial assistance to farmers before the crop season.
benefits_list: Annual financial assistance of ₹20,000 per farmer family.
Includes the Central Government's PM-KISAN component.
Timely support for Kharif and Rabi seasons.
eligibility_criteria: Farmer family owning cultivable land.
Tenant farmers (subject to CCRC card eligibility).
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
how_to_apply: Enroll through Rythu Seva Kendras (RSKs) or Village Agriculture Assistants.

scheme_name: AP State Skill Development Corporation (APSSDC) Training
objective: To provide industry-relevant skill training to unemployed youth and students to enhance their employability and promote entrepreneurship.
benefits_summary: Skill training, certification, and placement support.
benefits_list: Training in high-demand sectors (IT, Electronics, Healthcare, etc.).
eligibility_criteria: Unemployed youth or students.
Age: 18-35 years.
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
how_to_apply: Register on the APSSDC portal or visit Skill Hubs.

objective: Provides post-operative sustenance allowance to patients from poor families who undergo surgery/treatment under the state health insurance scheme, compensating for wage loss during recovery.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance during the recovery period after surgery.
benefits_list: Daily allowance of ₹225 during the recovery period.
Paid directly to the bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Must have undergone treatment under Dr. NTR Vaidya Seva (formerly Aarogyasri).
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Doctor's recommendation for rest period.
how_to_apply: Processed automatically by the hospital upon discharge.

scheme_name: BC/ EBC/ Kapu/ Brahmin Welfare Corporation Schemes
objective: These corporations implement specific welfare schemes for their respective communities, focusing on economic upliftment, education, and livelihood support.
benefits_summary: Community-specific loans, scholarships, and self-employment grants.
benefits_list: Subsidized loans for setting up businesses.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to the specific community (BC, EBC, Kapu, Brahmin).
Annual family income limits apply (e.g., < ₹2.5 Lakhs).
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
how_to_apply: Apply through the GSWS NBM portal or respective Corporation websites.

objective: Provides insurance coverage to unorganized sector workers and their families in case of accidental death or permanent disability.
benefits_summary: Accident insurance cover for unorganized workers.
benefits_list: ₹10 Lakhs for Accidental Death or Full Disability.
₹1 Lakh for Natural Death (for specified age group).
eligibility_criteria: Unorganized worker resident in AP.
Age: 18 to 70 years.
White Ration Card holder.
how_to_apply: Enrollment via Grama/Ward Volunteers. Claim submission at Village Secretariat.

objective: Financial assistance to traditional artisans such as Tailors, Washermen (Rajakas), and Barbers (Nayee Brahmins) to support their livelihood and working capital needs.
benefits_summary: Annual financial aid to traditional service providers.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Tailor, Washerman, or Barber by profession.
Own a shop/establishment.
Age: 21 to 60 years.
BPL family.
how_to_apply: Apply at Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.

objective: Provides financial assistance for the marriage of girls belonging to SC, ST, BC, Minority, and Differently-abled communities to prevent child marriage and support poor families.
benefits_summary: Marriage incentive for brides from weaker sections.
eligibility_criteria: Bride age > 18, Groom age > 21.
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
White Ration Card holder.
Belong to specified categories.
how_to_apply: Register at the Village/Ward Secretariat before the marriage.

objective: Provides annual financial assistance of ₹10,000 to self-employed owner-cum-drivers of Autos, Taxis, and Maxi Cabs to cover expenses like insurance, fitness certificate, and repairs.
benefits_summary: Financial support for auto and taxi drivers for vehicle maintenance.
eligibility_criteria: Owner-cum-driver of Auto/Taxi/Maxi Cab.
Valid Driving License and Vehicle RC.
BPL family (White Ration Card).
Resident of AP.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.

objective: Provides 3 free LPG gas cylinders per year to eligible BPL households to promote the use of clean cooking fuel and reduce the financial burden on women.
benefits_summary: Free LPG refills for poor households.
benefits_list: Subsidy amount credited directly to bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
White Ration Card holder.
Active LPG connection.
This scheme is available for: Female.
how_to_apply: Book cylinder as usual. Subsidy is processed via the GSWS system.

issuing_authority: Department for Welfare of Differently Abled & Senior Citizens
objective: Provides comprehensive welfare measures including monthly pensions, aids and appliances, and marriage incentives to persons with disabilities.
benefits_summary: Social security and rehabilitation support for PwDs.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Minimum 40% disability (SADAREM certificate).
Income limits apply for specific benefits.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the department portal or Grama Sachivalayam.

objective: Provides subsidized diesel to registered mechanized and motorized fishing boats to reduce the operational costs for fishermen.
benefits_summary: Diesel subsidy for fishing boats.
benefits_list: Available at selected petrol bunks in coastal districts.
eligibility_criteria: Registered owner of mechanized/motorized fishing boat.
Valid fishing license.
Resident of AP.
how_to_apply: Apply for 'Smart Card' at Fisheries Department. Use card at designated bunks.

objective: Provides nutritious mid-day meals to students in government and aided schools to improve health, enrollment, and attendance.
benefits_summary: Daily nutritious meal for school children.
benefits_list: Hot cooked meal with egg, rice, dal, and vegetables.
eligibility_criteria: Students of Class 1 to 10.
Studying in Government/Aided schools.
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
how_to_apply: Automatically available to enrolled students.

objective: An extension program where progressive farmers teach best practices in agriculture (Integrated Crop Management) to fellow farmers in their own fields.
benefits_summary: Field-based training for farmers to reduce costs and increase yield.
benefits_list: Practical training on pest management and organic farming.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Farmers willing to adopt new technologies.
Registered in the Polambadi group.
how_to_apply: Contact Village Agriculture Assistant (VAA) at Rythu Seva Kendra.

scheme_name: Free Bus Travel for Women (Maha Lakshmi)
objective: Offers free travel for all women on state-run RTC buses (Palle Velugu and Express services) across Andhra Pradesh.
benefits_summary: Zero-cost bus travel for women within the state.
benefits_list: Free ticket for women in ordinary and express buses.
Enhances mobility and safety for women.
eligibility_criteria: All women residents of Andhra Pradesh (of all ages).
Transgender persons are also eligible.
how_to_apply: Show valid ID proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID) to the conductor to get a zero-fare ticket.

objective: A decentralized service delivery system where a volunteer is assigned to approx. 50 households to ensure door-to-door delivery of government schemes.
benefits_summary: Ensures last-mile delivery of welfare benefits to citizens' doorsteps.
benefits_list: Doorstep delivery of pension, rice card, and other services.
Assistance in applying for government schemes.
eligibility_criteria: Any resident of Andhra Pradesh can avail services.
To be a volunteer: Age 18-35, 10th/Inter pass, Local resident.
how_to_apply: Citizens are automatically mapped. Volunteer recruitment via notification.

objective: Provides financial rewards to UPSC aspirants from weaker sections who clear Prelims and Mains stages to support their preparation.
benefits_summary: Cash incentives for clearing UPSC Civil Services stages.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Cleared UPSC Prelims/Mains.
Belong to SC/ST/BC/Minority (income limits apply).
how_to_apply: Apply online via Jnanabhumi portal after results.

objective: Direct benefit transfer to farmers as compensation for crop damage (>33%) due to natural calamities like floods, cyclones, or drought.
benefits_summary: Compensation for crop loss paid directly to farmers.
benefits_list: Financial assistance credited to bank account.
Disbursed within the same season to help farmers restart.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers with >33% crop damage.
Crop registered in e-Panta (Digital Crop Booking).
Actual cultivators (Owners/Tenants).
how_to_apply: Enumeration by Village Agriculture Assistants. Check status at RBK.

objective: Facilitates interest-free bank loans of ₹10,000 to street vendors and traditional artisans to support their businesses (aligned with PM SVANidhi).
benefits_summary: Interest-free working capital loan for street vendors.
benefits_list: Government reimburses the total interest to the bank.
eligibility_criteria: Street Vendors, Traditional Artisans.
Valid Vending ID/Certificate.
Resident of Andhra Pradesh.
how_to_apply: Apply through Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.

objective: A flagship mission to provide a functional household tap water connection to every rural home for a safe and adequate water supply.
benefits_summary: Ensures potable drinking water supply to every household via tap.
benefits_list: Regular supply of safe drinking water (55 LPCD).
eligibility_criteria: Rural households in Andhra Pradesh.
Households without functional tap connection.
how_to_apply: Contact Gram Panchayat or Village Water Committee.

objective: A long-term state focus on completing pending and starting new irrigation projects to ensure water security for agriculture and drinking purposes.
benefits_summary: Development of major and medium irrigation infrastructure.
benefits_list: Construction of dams, canals, and lift irrigation schemes.
Drinking water supply to drought-prone areas.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers in the command areas of the projects.
General public benefiting from water supply.
how_to_apply: Not applicable for individual application. Community benefit scheme.

objective: Provides a monthly stipend of ₹5,000 to junior lawyers during their first three years of practice to support them financially.
benefits_summary: Financial support to junior advocates during early practice years.
eligibility_criteria: Junior Advocates (less than 3 years practice).
Enrolled in AP Bar Council.
Age: Under 35 years.
Non-practicing advocates ineligible.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Law Nestham portal.

objective: Comprehensive program for upgrading infrastructure in all government schools to provide a conducive learning environment.
benefits_summary: Transforms government schools with basic infrastructure components like clean water, toilets, furniture, and English labs (formerly known as Nadu-Nedu).
benefits_list: Construction of compound walls, toilets, and classrooms.
Supply of dual desk benches and smart TVs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Target Group: Government Schools and students studying there.
how_to_apply: Implemented by School Management Committees (SMCs) and the Education Department. No individual application.

objective: Provides financial aid of ₹10,000 to fishermen families during the annual marine fishing ban period.
benefits_summary: Financial sustenance for marine fishermen during the 61-day fishing ban period to prevent loss of livelihood.
benefits_list: Ex-gratia of ₹10 Lakhs to families of fishermen who die while fishing.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Must be a registered marine fisherman with a boat.
Must have a valid fishing license/boat registration.
how_to_apply: Enroll through the Village/Ward Sachivalayam (Fisheries Assistant).

objective: A digital portal providing public access to land records, enabling citizens to view details of their property online.
benefits_summary: Provides transparent access to land records, Adangal, and 1B documents online.
benefits_list: View and download Adangal and 1-B (ROR) documents.
Check village maps and land conversion status.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Must own land in Andhra Pradesh.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer/Landowner.
how_to_apply: Visit the MeeBhoomi portal or Grama Sachivalayam to access records or request corrections.

issuing_authority: Panchayat Raj and Rural Development Department
objective: State-level implementation of the central scheme that guarantees 100 days of wage employment to rural households.
benefits_summary: Provides guaranteed wage employment and creates durable rural assets.
benefits_list: 100 days of wage employment per household per year.
Wages paid directly to bank/postal accounts.
Work includes water conservation, drought proofing, and land development.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh (Rural area).
Adult members of rural households willing to do unskilled manual work.
Must possess a Job Card.
how_to_apply: Apply for a Job Card at the Grama Sachivalayam or Field Assistant.

objective: Includes financial support for Dargahs/Masjids, scholarships, and assistance for Christian pilgrimages to Jerusalem.
benefits_summary: Holistic welfare for minority communities including financial aid for pilgrims and honorarium for religious service providers.
benefits_list: Honorarium to Imams, Muezzins, and Pastors.
Financial assistance for Hajj and Jerusalem pilgrimage.
Shadi Mubarak/Dulhan scheme for marriage assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Must belong to a Minority community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, etc.).
Annual family income limits apply (usually < ₹3 Lakhs).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Minorities Welfare portal or at Grama Sachivalayam.

issuing_authority: Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare
objective: State-level implementation of various national health programs for maternal health, child health, and disease control.
benefits_summary: Strengthens the public health system to provide universal access to equitable and quality healthcare.
benefits_list: Free drugs and diagnostics in government hospitals.
Maternal and Child Health services (JSSK, JSY).
Control of communicable and non-communicable diseases.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Patients visiting government health facilities.
how_to_apply: Visit any Government Hospital, PHC, or CHC to avail services.

issuing_authority: Society for Elimination of Rural Poverty (SERP)
objective: Provides a monthly social security pension to the elderly, widows, disabled persons, and other vulnerable groups.
benefits_summary: Monthly pension delivered at the doorstep/bank for social security (Formerly YSR Pension Kanuka).
benefits_list: Monthly Pension of ₹4,000 for Old Age, Widows, Weavers, Toddy Tappers, Fishermen, Single Women.
Monthly Pension of ₹6,000 for Disabled persons.
Enhanced pension for chronic kidney patients (₹10,000).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Annual family income < ₹1.2 Lakh (Rural) / ₹1.44 Lakh (Urban).
White Ration Card holder.
Age: 60+ (OAP), 18+ (Single Women/Widows/Disabled).
how_to_apply: Apply at the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam. Verification done by Welfare Assistant.
