required_documents: Age Proof/Death Certificate of Husband/SADAREM Certificate.

objective: Aims to provide affordable, permanent housing for the economically weaker sections in urban and rural areas.
benefits_summary: Provides free house sites and financial assistance for the construction of pucca houses.
benefits_list: Free house site pattas registered in the name of women beneficiaries.
Financial assistance of ₹1.80 Lakhs for construction.
Provision of basic infrastructure (water, electricity) in housing colonies.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Must not own a pucca house.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
White Ration Card holder.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Grama/Ward Volunteer or Sachivalayam.

issuing_authority: Dr. YSR Aarogyasri Health Care Trust
objective: The state's flagship health insurance scheme providing free, cashless healthcare services for serious illnesses to BPL families, with an enhanced coverage limit.
benefits_summary: Cashless treatment for identified diseases in empanelled hospitals (Formerly Aarogyasri).
benefits_list: Coverage up to ₹25 Lakhs per family per annum.
Covers 3257 procedures including cancer and cardiac care.
End-to-end cashless service from registration to discharge.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000.
Holders of Health Card/White Ration Card.
how_to_apply: Visit any Network Hospital with Aarogyasri Card or Aadhaar Card.

objective: Subsidized distribution of high-yield cattle, sheep, and goats to enhance the livelihood of rural families.
benefits_summary: Promotes livestock rearing as a livelihood through subsidy and support.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer/SHG Member.
Priority to SC/ST/BC communities.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Animal Husbandry Assistant at Grama Sachivalayam.

objective: Comprehensive scheme for full tuition fee reimbursement (RTF) and maintenance/hostel charges (MTF) for students pursuing higher education.
benefits_summary: Ensures that poor students can pursue higher education without financial burden.
benefits_list: Fee Reimbursement (RTF): Full fee reimbursement credited to college or mother's account.
Maintenance Fee (MTF): Annual amount for boarding/lodging (Vasathi Deevena).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000.
Pursuing ITI, Polytechnic, Degree, PG, or Professional courses.
75% attendance is mandatory.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Jnanabhumi portal.

objective: The foundational system for distributing subsidized essential commodities like rice, sugar, and pulses to BPL families through ration cards.
benefits_summary: Ensures food security by providing essential commodities at subsidized rates via Rice Cards.
benefits_list: Rice at ₹1 per kg (or free under specific schemes).
Sugar, Red Gram, and Palmoil at subsidized rates.
Home delivery of ration (available in select areas).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Annual family income < ₹1.2 Lakh (Rural) / ₹1.44 Lakh (Urban).
Total land holding < 10 acres (Wet+Dry).
how_to_apply: Apply for a new Rice Card at the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.
required_documents: Aadhaar Cards of all family members.

objective: Village-level centers providing services like soil testing, quality seeds, fertilizers, and technical advice to farmers.
benefits_summary: A one-stop shop for all agricultural needs of farmers at the village level (also known as Rythu Bharosa Kendras).
benefits_list: Supply of certified seeds, fertilizers, and pesticides.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Farmer.
how_to_apply: Visit the local RSK/RBK in the village.

issuing_authority: Department of Women Development and Child Welfare
objective: A nutrition scheme for pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children (up to 6 years) to combat malnutrition through Anganwadi centers.
benefits_summary: Provides supplementary nutrition to prevent anemia and malnutrition.
benefits_list: Nutritious food including milk, eggs, and ragi malt.
Hot cooked meals for children at Anganwadis.
Additional benefits in tribal areas (Sampoorna Poshana Plus).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Pregnant Women, Lactating Mothers.
Children aged 6 months to 6 years.
Registered at Anganwadi Centre.
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre.

objective: Annual distribution of kits with school essentials (uniforms, books, bags, shoes) to students in government schools.
benefits_summary: Provides a student kit at the start of the academic year to ensure students are ready for school (Formerly Jagananna Vidya Kanuka).
benefits_list: Kit includes: 3 pairs of uniforms, school bag, text books, note books, work books.
One pair of shoes, two pairs of socks, and a belt.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Students studying in Class 1 to 10 in Government/Aided schools.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
how_to_apply: Distributed automatically to enrolled students in schools on reopening day.

objective: A single-window clearance system for investors to get all required approvals and licenses for setting up industries.
benefits_summary: Streamlines the process of starting a business by providing all approvals within 21 days.
benefits_list: Online application for all industrial approvals.
eligibility_criteria: Entrepreneurs/Investors planning to set up industries in AP.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Investor.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the AP Single Desk Portal.

objective: A set of policies providing incentives like tax breaks, capital subsidies, and power subsidies to attract new industries and support MSMEs.
benefits_summary: Provides fiscal incentives to promote industrial growth and employment.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of Stamp Duty and SGST.
Power cost reimbursement (₹1.00 per unit for 5 years).
Investment subsidy for MSMEs and Women Entrepreneurs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
New and expanding industrial units in AP.
MSMEs, Large & Mega Industries.
Women/SC/ST entrepreneurs get additional incentives.
how_to_apply: Apply for incentives via the AP Industries Department portal.

issuing_authority: Municipal Administration and Urban Development / PR&RD
objective: State's initiative, aligned with the Swachh Bharat Mission, focusing on sanitation, waste management, and making the state Open Defecation Free (ODF).
benefits_summary: Promotes sanitation through CLAP (Clean Andhra Pradesh) initiative.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Households without toilets.
Gram Panchayats/ULBs.
how_to_apply: Contact the Grama/Ward Volunteer or Secretariat for toilet construction or waste collection issues.

issuing_authority: Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare
objective: Provides free transportation for mothers and newborns from government hospitals back to their homes after delivery.
benefits_summary: Ensures safe and free drop-back service for post-natal women and infants.
benefits_list: Free drop-back facility in AC vehicles.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Women who deliver in Government Hospitals.
This scheme is available for: Female.
how_to_apply: Hospital administration arranges the vehicle upon discharge.

objective: Provides financial assistance of ₹15,000 annually to mothers for their children's education (Class 1 to Intermediate).
benefits_summary: Incentivizes mothers to send their children to school by providing financial support (Replaces Amma Vodi).
benefits_list: Deposited directly into the mother's bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Mother/Guardian of a child studying in Class 1 to 12 (Govt/Private/Aided).
Annual family income < ₹1.2 Lakh (Rural) / ₹1.44 Lakh (Urban).
White Ration Card holder.
how_to_apply: School Headmaster/Principal enrolls eligible students. Verification by Grama Sachivalayam.

issuing_authority: Labour, Employment, Training and Factories Department
objective: Provides a monthly allowance of ₹3,000 to eligible unemployed youth to support them while they search for jobs.
benefits_summary: Financial support for unemployed youth (Status subject to current government policy).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.
Applicant's age must be between 22 and 35 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
Required education level: Graduate/Diploma holder.
Family income criteria applies.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the designated Unemployment Allowance portal or Employment Exchange.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: This scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department Telangana financial assistance is provided to construction workers who suffer temporary disabilities due to accidents or diseases & hospitalized for five or more days.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for registered construction workers during hospitalization due to accident or disease.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹3,000 per month during hospitalization.
Payable for a maximum of 3 months.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Telangana.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Must be a registered construction worker with the Board.
Hospitalized for 5 or more days due to accident/disease.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Assistant Labour Commissioner (ALC) within 3 months of discharge.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Maternity Benefit Scheme" was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department, Telangana. The scheme provides financial assistance to registered women workers, as well as the wives & daughters of registered workers to support them.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for maternity expenses for female construction workers or wives/daughters of male workers.
benefits_list: Additional ₹5,000 if the newborn is a girl child (in some cases).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Telangana.
Registered construction worker or wife of a registered worker.
Limited to two deliveries.
This scheme is available for: Female.
how_to_apply: Submit claim application to the ALC/Labour Department.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Marriage Gift Scheme" was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Dept. Telangana. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance as a marriage gift to unmarried registered women workers & the daughters of registered building & construction
benefits_summary: Provides financial gift for the marriage of unmarried female workers or daughters of registered workers.
benefits_list: Paid to the female worker or the daughter of the male worker.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Telangana.
Registered worker with active membership.
Marriage should be legally registered.
Limited to two daughters per family.
how_to_apply: Apply to the ALC office with marriage proof within 3 months of marriage.

scheme_name: ₹ 5 Lakh Insurance Cover To Farmers (Rythu Bima)
objective: Launched on 15 August 2018, the scheme "₹5 Lakh Insurance Cover for Farmers" is an Insurance Scheme by the Department of Agriculture, Government of Telangana. This scheme provides ₹5 lakh insurance coverage to all farmers aged between 18 and 59 years.
benefits_summary: Group Life Insurance scheme for farmers, providing financial security to family members.
benefits_list: Insurance cover of ₹5 Lakhs in case of death (natural or accidental).
Amount settled to the nominee within 10 days.
Entire premium paid by the State Government.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Telangana.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 59 years.
Must own agricultural land (Pattadar Passbook holder).
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
how_to_apply: Enrollment is done by Agriculture Extension Officers (AEO) based on land records.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Disability Relief" scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Dept. Telangana aimed at providing financial support to registered building & other construction workers who suffer from total or partial permanent disabilities.
benefits_summary: Financial compensation for registered workers suffering permanent disability.
benefits_list: Partial Permanent Disability: Up to ₹4,00,000 (based on percentage).
Relief for loss of earning capacity.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Telangana.
Registered construction worker.
Disability occurred due to accident during employment.
Disability percentage assessed by Medical Board.
how_to_apply: Submit claim to Assistant Labour Commissioner with medical proof.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Disability Aids and Appliances" scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Dept. Telangana to provide artificial limbs, wheelchairs, tricycles, & other fabricated appliances to registered construction workers who have lost their limb
benefits_summary: Provides free assistive devices to disabled construction workers.
benefits_list: Improving mobility and quality of life.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Telangana.
Registered construction worker with disability.
Recommendation from Medical Officer.
how_to_apply: Apply through the ALC office.

scheme_name: Chief Minister's Overseas Scholarship Scheme for Minorities
objective: The scheme “Chief Minister's Overseas Scholarship Scheme for Minorities” was launched by the Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Telangana in the year 2015-16 for students belonging to the Minority community to pursue Higher Studies in Post Graduate Courses in Foreign Universities.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for minority students to pursue PG/Ph.D. abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Telangana.
Belong to Minority Community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, etc.).
Annual family income < ₹5,00,000.
Valid admission in recognized foreign university.
Age: 18-35 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the Telangana e-Pass portal.

issuing_authority: Professor Jayashankar Telangana State Agricultural University (PJTSAU)
objective: The "Feed for Seed" program is an initiative launched by the Government of Telangana in India, aimed at promoting agriculture and supporting farmers in the state. The program is designed to encourage farmers to produce their own seeds by providing them with free, high-quality seeds of various crops.
benefits_summary: Encourages farmers to produce their own quality seeds.
eligibility_criteria: Resident Farmer of Telangana.
Willing to undertake seed production.
Must have suitable land and irrigation facilities.
how_to_apply: Contact local Agriculture Extension Officer (AEO).

scheme_name: Mahatma Jyothiba Phule Overseas Vidya Nidhi for BC and EBC Students
objective: The scheme “Mahatma Jyothiba Phule Overseas Vidya Nidhi” was launched by the Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of Telangana for meritorious students belonging to Backward Classes & Economically Backward Classes to pursue Higher Studies in foreign universities.
benefits_summary: Financial grant for BC and EBC students to study abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Telangana.
Belong to BC or EBC category.
Annual family income < ₹5,00,000.
Age: 18-35 years.
Valid GRE/GMAT/TOEFL/IELTS scores.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Telangana e-Pass portal.

issuing_authority: Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare
objective: To work towards “Avoidable Blindness Free Telangana”, the Government has decided to take up universal eye screening by covering the entire population of the state under the name “Kanti Velugu”.
benefits_summary: Universal eye screening program for all citizens of Telangana.
benefits_list: Free medicines and cataract surgeries if required.
eligibility_criteria: All residents of Telangana.
No age or income restriction.
how_to_apply: Visit the Kanti Velugu camp in your village/ward during the campaign dates.

objective: Rythu Bandhu Farmer Group Life Insurance Scheme for Farmers” is a life insurance scheme by the Government of Telangana that provides insurance coverage to the nominee of a farmer in case of the farmer’s death. It is free for farmers and is implemented through LIC.
benefits_summary: Life insurance coverage for farmers.
benefits_list: Sum Assured: ₹5 Lakhs payable to nominee on farmer's death.
eligibility_criteria: Farmer with Pattadar Passbook.
Age: 18 to 59 years.
Enrolled in the scheme via Agriculture Department.
how_to_apply: Enrollment done by AEOs using land records. Claim filed by nominee with AEO.

issuing_authority: Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare
objective: KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme is a welfare initiative launched by the Government of Telangana, India, aimed at providing nutritious food to pregnant. The primary objective of the scheme is to improve the nutritional status of pregnant women.
benefits_summary: Provides nutrition kits to pregnant women to combat anemia.
benefits_list: Kit contains nutritional mix powder, dates, ghee, and iron syrup.
Distributed twice during pregnancy (during antenatal checkups).
eligibility_criteria: Pregnant women (specifically with anemia).
Targeted at 9 high-prevalence districts initially.
Registered at Govt Health Centre.
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest PHC/Anganwadi.

scheme_name: Skill Development Training - 15 Days Residential (Construction Workers)
issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board offers a comprehensive 15-day residential skill up-gradation training program designed to enhance the skills of registered construction workers.
benefits_summary: Skill upgradation for construction workers.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Construction Worker.
Age: 18-60 years.
Resident of Telangana.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Board or ALC office.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Fatal Accident Relief" scheme is a welfare initiative by the Telangana Government aimed at providing financial support to the nominee, dependents, or legal heirs of registered building and other construction workers in the unfortunate event of their accidental death.
benefits_summary: Financial compensation for accidental death of construction worker.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Construction Worker.
Death due to accident during course of employment.
Resident of Telangana.
how_to_apply: Nominee submits claim to Assistant Labour Commissioner.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board offers offers a 15-day Recognition of Prior Learning training programme for registered construction workers. The programme aims to enhance the employability of workers, ensuring they meet industry standards for future opportunities.
benefits_summary: On-site training and certification for construction workers.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Construction Worker.
Minimum 1 year experience.
Age: 18-60 years.
how_to_apply: Apply at the Labour Department office.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Relief to the Unregistered Workers" scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F Dept. Telangana aimed to provide financial assistance to the dependents of unregistered building & construction workers who die in accidents at construction sites.
benefits_summary: Ex-gratia for accidental death of unregistered workers at construction sites.
benefits_list: Financial assistance of ₹50,000 to the dependents.
Humanitarian aid for families of unregistered workers.
eligibility_criteria: Unregistered Construction Worker.
Death must occur at the construction work site.
Resident of Telangana.
how_to_apply: Legal heir applies to ALC with proof of accident.

objective: The scheme is designed to support economically disadvantaged families belonging to the SC/ST/BC/EBC communities by providing one-time financial assistance for the marriage of their daughters.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for the marriage of girls from SC, ST, BC, and EBC communities.
benefits_list: Paid to the bride's mother's bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Bride must belong to SC/ST/BC/EBC category.
Age of Bride: 18+ years.
Resident of Telangana.
Income Limit: ₹2 Lakhs (Urban), ₹1.5 Lakhs (Rural).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Telanagna e-Pass portal/Kalyana Lakshmi portal.

objective: In order to prevent child marriages and support marriage expense of financially distressed families, on 02nd October 2014, Government of Telangana (GoTS) introduced a welfare scheme called “Shaadi Mubarak’’, providing financial assistance for marriages of minority community girls.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for the marriage of girls from Minority communities.
eligibility_criteria: Bride must belong to a Minority Community.
Age: 18+ years.
Annual family income < ₹2,00,000.
Resident of Telangana.
how_to_apply: Apply online via e-Pass Shaadi Mubarak portal.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The scheme is effective from 01.05.2018 and aims to support the families of deceased workers with financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- to help cover funeral expenses and provide additional relief. The Telangana Government has enhanced the relief amount under the welfare scheme "Natural Death".
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to the nominee of a registered worker in case of natural death.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Construction Worker.
Natural death.
Active membership.
how_to_apply: Nominee submits application to ALC.

issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board offers a 90-day residential skill development training program designed for unemployed youth who are dependents of registered construction workers.
benefits_summary: Skill training for children/dependents of construction workers.
eligibility_criteria: Dependent of Registered Construction Worker.
Unemployed Youth.
Age: 18-35 years.
how_to_apply: Apply at the Board office or training center.

scheme_name: Skill Development - 90 Days Training in Curtain & Garment Stitching
issuing_authority: Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board offers a 90-day non-residential skill development training programme specifically designed for unemployed girls and women who are dependents of registered construction workers.
benefits_summary: Tailoring and stitching training for female dependents of workers.
benefits_list: Sewing machine provided upon completion (often subsidized/free).
eligibility_criteria: Female dependent of Registered Worker.
Unemployed.
Age: 18-45 years.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Labour Department.

objective: The Dalit Bandhu Scheme is a welfare program launched by the state government of Telangana in India in Aug'2021. The scheme is aimed at providing financial assistance to families belonging to the Scheduled Caste (SC) community in the state to help them become economically self-reliant.
benefits_summary: One-time capital assistance for SC families to start business ventures.
eligibility_criteria: All SC families in Telangana.
Resident of the selected pilot constituency/mandal.
Must have a valid Caste Certificate.
how_to_apply: Selection by District Administration/Local MLAs based on survey.

objective: The objective of the Haritha Haram program is to increase the green cover of the state from the existing 24% to 33% by planting and nurturing millions of saplings every year. The program also aims to create awareness among the public about the importance of preserving and protecting the environment.
benefits_summary: Massive plantation drive to increase green cover.
benefits_list: Plantation in avenues, schools, and offices.
eligibility_criteria: All citizens, institutions, and communities in Telangana.
how_to_apply: Collect saplings from nearest nursery or Gram Panchayat.
required_documents: No documents required for taking saplings.

issuing_authority: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department
objective: To encourage higher education among girl students who completed their schooling (Class 6 to 12) in government schools by providing monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000.
benefits_summary: Financial incentive to increase enrollment and retention of girls in higher education.
benefits_list: Monthly incentive of ₹1,000 deposited into bank account.
Paid till completion of UG degree/Diploma/ITI course.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
Studied Class 6 to 12 in Government Schools (Govt/Corp/Muni/Adi Dravidar/Tribal Welfare/Kallar Reclamation/Forest Dept schools).
Pursuing Higher Education (Arts/Science/Engineering/Medicine/Polytechnic/ITI).
This scheme is available for: Female.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Higher Education Assurance Scheme portal.

issuing_authority: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department
objective: To provide nutritious breakfast to primary school students (Classes 1 to 5) in government schools to combat malnutrition, improve attendance and learning outcomes.
benefits_summary: Ensures children start their day with a nutritious meal, improving health and concentration.
benefits_list: Hot cooked breakfast served on all working days.
Menu includes idli, pongal, kichadi, rava upma with sambar/chutney.
eligibility_criteria: Students studying in Classes 1 to 5.
Enrolled in Government Schools (Corporation/Municipality/Panchayat Union/Government Primary Schools).
how_to_apply: Automatically available to all enrolled students in eligible schools.

issuing_authority: Department of Health and Family Welfare
objective: A doorstep healthcare delivery scheme to provide essential healthcare services, including screening for non-communicable diseases, medicine delivery, and physiotherapy for the elderly and others in need.
benefits_summary: Brings healthcare services to the doorstep of citizens, focusing on NCD control.
benefits_list: Home-based screening for Hypertension and Diabetes.
Doorstep delivery of drugs for NCD patients.
Physiotherapy and Palliative Care at home.
Referral to PHC/GH for further treatment.
eligibility_criteria: Residents of Tamil Nadu.
Target Group: Elderly (45+ years), PwDs, Bedridden patients, NCD patients.
how_to_apply: Health workers (Women Health Volunteers/Mid-level Health Providers) visit households for screening.

objective: To provide monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000 to empower women heads of eligible families who meet the specified economic criteria, having an annual family income of less than ₹2.5 lakh.
benefits_summary: Recognizes women's unpaid labor and provides social security.
benefits_list: Direct Benefit Transfer to bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Female Head of Family (as per Ration Card).
Annual family income < ₹2.5 Lakhs.
Land holding < 5 acres (wet) or 10 acres (dry).
Annual electricity consumption < 3600 units.
how_to_apply: Apply at special camps organized in Ration Shops (PDS outlets).

scheme_name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS)
issuing_authority: Department of Health and Family Welfare
objective: Provides quality healthcare and financial protection to eligible low-income families by offering coverage up to ₹5 lakh per year for specified treatments and surgeries in empanelled hospitals.
benefits_summary: Cashless health insurance for BPL families covering high-end procedures.
benefits_list: Coverage up to ₹5 Lakhs per family per year.
Includes follow-up treatments and diagnostic tests.
Cashless hospitalization in Govt and Private hospitals.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Tamil Nadu.
Annual family income < ₹1,20,000.
Member of unorganized labor welfare board (automatically covered).
how_to_apply: Apply for Smart Card at Village Administrative Officer (VAO)/Collectorate with income certificate.

objective: A massive skill development scheme for students in colleges and universities to enhance their skills in emerging technologies, coding, robotics, and languages to improve their employability.
benefits_summary: Upskilling platform for students to bridge the industry-academia gap.
benefits_list: Free courses on Industry 4.0 technologies (AI, ML, Data Science).
eligibility_criteria: School students (Class 9-12).
College students (Arts, Science, Engineering, Polytechnic).
Youth seeking employment.
how_to_apply: Register on the Naan Mudhalvan portal through school/college or individually.

issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department / Labour Dept
objective: A social welfare scheme focused on identifying, rescuing, and rehabilitating individuals trapped in bonded labor, providing them with financial assistance and support for a fresh start.
benefits_summary: Comprehensive rehabilitation package for rescued bonded laborers.
benefits_list: Immediate financial relief of ₹20,000 upon rescue.
Rehabilitation assistance up to ₹1 Lakh (Men), ₹2 Lakh (Women/Children), ₹3 Lakh (Special Cases).
Land allotment and house site pattas.
eligibility_criteria: Identified and released bonded laborer.
Holding a Release Certificate issued by the RDO/Sub-Collector.
how_to_apply: Handled by District Administration upon rescue. Beneficiary must present Release Certificate.

scheme_name: Adi Dravidar/Tribal Welfare - Special Guides for Hostels
issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: Provides special educational guides and learning materials to students residing in hostels managed by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department to improve their academic performance.
benefits_summary: Free study materials (Question Banks/Guides) for hostel students appearing for board exams.
benefits_list: Free guides for 10th and 12th standard subjects.
Available to students in ADW/TW hostels.
eligibility_criteria: Students staying in ADW/TW hostels.
Studying in Class 10 or 12.
Belong to SC/ST community (generally).
how_to_apply: Provided automatically by the Hostel Warden/School Headmaster.

scheme_name: Adi Dravidar/Tribal Welfare - Academic Achievement Awards (Chief Minister's Merit Award)
issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: Provides financial incentives and prizes to Adi Dravidar and Tribal students who excel in public examinations (10th & 12th Std), including district-level and subject-wise awards, to encourage academic excellence.
benefits_summary: Cash awards for top-ranking SC/ST students.
benefits_list: State Level Toppers: Higher cash incentives.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST/SCC students.
Studying in Govt/Govt-aided schools.
Secured District/State ranks in 10th or 12th Public Exams.
how_to_apply: School Headmaster submits list of toppers to District ADW Officer.

scheme_name: Adi Dravidar/Tribal Welfare - Free Education and Exam Fee Reimbursement
issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: Ensures free education up to 12th Std by reimbursing tuition fees and covering the public examination fees for 10th and 12th Std students from SC/ST communities.
benefits_summary: Waives tuition fees and exam fees for SC/ST/SCC students.
benefits_list: No tuition fee for SC/ST/SCC students in Govt/Aided schools.
Exam fee payment directly to Directorate of Govt Exams.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST/SCC/Christian Convert students.
No income limit for tuition fee waiver.
Parent's income < ₹2.5 Lakhs (for some specific fee components).
how_to_apply: Through School Headmaster.

scheme_name: Adi Dravidar/Tribal Welfare - Free Textbooks and Stationery
objective: Provides essential learning materials, including textbooks and stationery, free of cost to students from Adi Dravidar and Tribal communities to support their education.
benefits_summary: Supply of free educational kits to students.
benefits_list: Free Uniforms (4 sets), Footwear, and School Bag.
eligibility_criteria: All students in Govt/Govt-aided schools (Classes 1-12).
No community restriction for general scheme; specific focus on welfare schools.
how_to_apply: Distributed in school at the start of the academic year.

scheme_name: Admission of Adi Dravidar/Tribal students in reputed schools
issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: A scheme to facilitate the admission of meritorious Adi Dravidar and Tribal students into reputed private schools for their higher secondary education (+1), with the government bearing the educational expenses.
benefits_summary: Provides quality education in reputed private schools for bright SC/ST students.
benefits_list: Admission to Class 11 in top private schools.
Govt pays entire fee + hostel charges (approx ₹28,000 p.a.).
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST students who secured high marks in 10th Std (top 10 boys/girls in district).
Parent's annual income < ₹1,00,000.
Studying in ADW/Tribal/Govt schools.
how_to_apply: Selection by District Collector/ADW Officer based on 10th marks.

objective: Afforestation schemes designed to increase green cover in tribal areas while providing sustainable employment opportunities to tribal community members through forest-related operations.
benefits_summary: Livelihood generation for tribals through forestry works.
benefits_list: Wage employment in nursery raising, planting, and maintenance.
eligibility_criteria: Tribal families living in/near forests.
Members of Village Forest Committees (VFCs).
how_to_apply: Contact District Forest Officer or Tribal Welfare Officer.

issuing_authority: Anna Institute of Management / ADW Dept
objective: Provides financial assistance and coaching support for aspiring candidates preparing for All India Service Examinations like I.A.S., I.P.S., I.R.S., etc.
benefits_summary: Free residential coaching for UPSC Civil Services Prelims and Mains.
benefits_list: Free coaching, boarding, and lodging (at All India Civil Services Coaching Centre, Chennai).
Additional financial incentive of ₹50,000 for clearing Prelims (for SC/ST).
eligibility_criteria: Graduate from Tamil Nadu.
Applying for UPSC Civil Services Exam.
Pass the entrance test conducted by the Institute.
Reservation for SC/ST/BC/MBC applied.
how_to_apply: Apply online for the entrance exam conducted by AICSCC.

issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: An award given to two Adi Dravidar students (one boy, one girl) in each district who secure the first rank in the 12th Std Public Examination and continue their higher education.
benefits_summary: Rewards academic excellence among SC students.
benefits_list: Cash award of ₹2,000 for first year.
₹1,500 per annum for subsequent years of study.
eligibility_criteria: SC Hindu student.
Secured 1st Rank in District in 12th Std.
Pursuing higher education.
No income limit.
how_to_apply: Apply through the College Principal to the District ADW Officer.

issuing_authority: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) Department
objective: Provides a subsidy to Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) for the purchase of generator sets to ensure uninterrupted power supply and maintain production.
benefits_summary: Subsidy to MSMEs to offset power shortage issues.
benefits_list: 25% subsidy on the cost of Generator Set (up to 320 KVA).
eligibility_criteria: Registered Micro, Small, Medium Enterprises in Tamil Nadu.
Manufacturing enterprises only.
Must have purchased and installed a new Genset.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the MSME Online Portal or District Industries Centre (DIC).

issuing_authority: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) Department
objective: Offers capital and investment subsidies to new and existing enterprises in the state to promote industrial growth, investment, and employment generation.
benefits_summary: Capital subsidy for setting up or expanding MSME units.
benefits_list: 25% Capital Subsidy on the value of eligible plant and machinery.
Maximum subsidy: ₹1.5 Crores (varies by scheme component like NEEDS, UYEGP).
eligibility_criteria: New MSMEs or existing MSMEs undertaking expansion/diversification.
Located in Tamil Nadu.
Must have Udyam Registration.
how_to_apply: Apply online via MSME Department portal.

issuing_authority: Tamil Nadu Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: A comprehensive welfare scheme providing financial assistance to registered women workers for events such as marriage, childbirth, miscarriage or termination of pregnancy.
benefits_summary: Maternity and marriage assistance for female construction/unorganized workers.
benefits_list: Maternity Assistance: ₹6,000 per month for 3 months (Total ₹18,000).
eligibility_criteria: Registered member of the Welfare Board.
This scheme is available for: Female (Worker or Dependent Daughter).
Renewal of membership must be current.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Labour Officer (Social Security Scheme).

issuing_authority: Tamil Nadu Unorganized Workers Welfare Board
objective: Provides immediate financial assistance to the family of a deceased registered worker to help cover the expenses related to funeral rites.
benefits_summary: Funeral expenses grant for registered workers.
benefits_list: Natural Death Assistance: ₹20,000 (in addition to funeral aid).
eligibility_criteria: Deceased must be a registered member of any Unorganized Workers Welfare Board.
Claimant must be the nominee/legal heir.
how_to_apply: Apply to the Labour Officer with Death Certificate.

issuing_authority: Tamil Nadu Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: Offers financial aid to registered workers for the purchase of prescription spectacles to ensure their visual health and ability to work.
benefits_summary: Reimbursement for spectacles for workers.
eligibility_criteria: Registered worker in the Welfare Board.
Must require spectacles as per Doctor's prescription.
No income limit (must be a manual worker).
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Labour Office.

scheme_name: Assistance to Lawyers for Starting Practice
issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: Provides a one-time grant to new law graduates from SC/ST communities to help them set up their own practice by purchasing books, furniture, and other essentials.
benefits_summary: Financial aid for young SC/ST lawyers to establish their practice.
benefits_list: Helps in paying Bar Council fees and buying law books/gowns.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST/SCC Law Graduate.
Enrolled in the Bar Council of Tamil Nadu.
Parent's annual income < ₹2.5 Lakhs.
Age: Below 30 years.
how_to_apply: Apply to the District ADW Officer.

objective: An award of Rs. 20,000 to recognize and honor writers for their significant contributions to Tamil literature and culture.
benefits_summary: Awards for best Tamil literary works.
benefits_list: Categories: Poetry, Novel, Drama, Short Story, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Tamil Writers/Authors.
Book published in the relevant year.
Resident of Tamil Nadu.
how_to_apply: Submit books and application to the Tamil Development Department.

scheme_name: Book Bank Scheme for Professional Courses
issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: A scheme where essential textbooks for professional courses like Medical, Engineering, Law, MBA, etc., are purchased and made available to SC/ST students through a library system.
benefits_summary: Provides expensive professional course books to SC/ST students via Book Banks.
benefits_list: Books for Medical, Engineering, Law, MBA, Polytechnic, Agriculture, Veterinary courses.
One set of books for every 2 students.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST/SCC students pursuing professional courses.
No income limit.
Studying in colleges with attached hostels.
how_to_apply: Accessed through the College/Hostel Library.

issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: Aims to provide designated burial grounds and proper pathways to these grounds for communities, ensuring dignified final rites for all.
benefits_summary: Acquisition of land for burial grounds and pathways for SC/ST habitations.
benefits_list: Provision of land for burial ground.
Construction of pathway to burial ground.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST habitations without adequate burial facilities.
Based on community demand.
how_to_apply: Community representation to the District Collector/TAHDCO.

issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: A merit-based award providing Rs. 3000 per annum for five years to SC/ST students who excel academically and pursue higher education in professional courses.
benefits_summary: Incentive for top-ranking SC/ST students to pursue professional degrees.
benefits_list: Given to top 1000 boys and 1000 girls based on 12th marks.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST/SCC students.
Secured high marks in 12th Std.
Admitted to Professional Courses (Medical/Engg/etc.).
No income limit.
how_to_apply: Selected automatically based on merit list from DGE.

issuing_authority: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
objective: A scheme for the construction of community halls in villages and towns to provide a common space for social, cultural, and community functions, primarily for Adi Dravidar and Tribal communities.
benefits_summary: Provides community infrastructure for social gatherings.
benefits_list: Construction of Community Hall with basic amenities.
Cost borne by the government (approx ₹50 Lakhs per hall).
Available for weddings and public functions at nominal rent.
eligibility_criteria: Habitations with significant SC/ST population.
Availability of suitable land.
Request from the local community/Panchayat.
how_to_apply: Submit representation to the District Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Officer through the Panchayat President.

objective: A housing scheme dedicated to constructing and providing houses free of cost to families belonging to tribal communities who do not own a permanent house.
benefits_summary: Provision of free pucca houses to homeless tribal families.
benefits_list: Construction of house with solar power facility.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
Belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST).
Must not own a pucca house.
BPL family.
how_to_apply: Apply to the District Tribal Welfare Officer or through the Block Development Officer (BDO).

objective: Various programs to provide certified, high-quality seeds for crops like maize, oilseeds, paddy, and pulses at subsidized rates to improve crop yield and quality.
benefits_summary: Supply of quality seeds at subsidized rates to farmers.
benefits_list: Subsidy on seed cost (e.g., 50% for pulses/oilseeds).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
Must own or lease agricultural land.
how_to_apply: Contact the Assistant Agricultural Officer (AAO) at the nearest AEC.

objective: Initiatives like distribution of Gypsum, Rhizobium packets, and soil health cards to help farmers understand and improve soil fertility for better agricultural output.
benefits_summary: Promotes soil health management for sustainable agriculture.
benefits_list: Subsidy on Gypsum, Zinc Sulphate, and Bio-fertilizers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
Applicant's employment status: Farmer.
Land samples must be provided for testing.
how_to_apply: Submit soil samples to the nearest Soil Testing Laboratory or via AAO.

objective: A wide range of loan schemes offered through District Central Cooperative Banks for purposes like housing, consumer durables, professional needs, women entrepreneurs, and small transport operators.
benefits_summary: Access to credit at affordable interest rates through cooperative banks.
benefits_list: Crop Loans (Interest-free if repaid on time).
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Tamil Nadu.
Member of the Primary Agricultural Cooperative Credit Society (PACCS).
Creditworthiness as per bank norms.
how_to_apply: Apply at the local Primary Agricultural Cooperative Credit Society (PACCS) or DCCB branch.

issuing_authority: Department of Agriculture Marketing and Agri Business
objective: Promotes the formation of Farmers Interest Groups and provides them with training on modern agricultural techniques, pest management, and production systems to enhance productivity.
benefits_summary: Capacity building and collective bargaining power for farmers.
benefits_list: Training on value addition and marketing.
eligibility_criteria: Group of 15-20 farmers.
Must be residents of the same village/block.
Willing to work collectively.
how_to_apply: Contact the Agricultural Officer (Agri Business) at the block level.

objective: A set of welfare schemes for journalists, including a family benefit fund, medical fund, pension for retired journalists, and family pension to support them and their families.
benefits_summary: Social security for accredited journalists.
benefits_list: Medical assistance from Journalists' Welfare Fund.
eligibility_criteria: Accredited Journalist with minimum 20 years of service.
Resident of Tamil Nadu.
Retired and in indigent circumstances (Income limits apply).
how_to_apply: Apply to the Director of Information and Public Relations, Chennai.

issuing_authority: Tamil Nadu Corporation for Development of Women (TNCDW)
objective: A state-sponsored scheme to provide financial support and opportunities for asset-less individuals to start micro-enterprises and secure a sustainable livelihood.
benefits_summary: Loan and subsidy for the ultra-poor to create livelihood assets.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for petty shops, livestock, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Asset-less poor identified through PIP (Participatory Identification of Poor).
Resident of Tamil Nadu.
Priority to destitute women/widows/differently-abled.
how_to_apply: Contact the Village Poverty Reduction Committee (VPRC) or Panchayat Level Federation (PLF).

issuing_authority: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) Department
objective: A subsidy on electricity tariffs for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to reduce their operational costs and enhance their competitiveness.
benefits_summary: Subsidy on power consumption charges for new MSMEs.
benefits_list: 20% subsidy on power consumption charges for the first 36 months.
eligibility_criteria: New Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises.
Commenced production on or after specified date.
Using LT power connection.
how_to_apply: Apply online via MSME Online Portal or DIC.

objective: Provides loans through cooperative banks for the purchase of milch animals like cows and buffaloes to promote dairy farming and provide a supplementary income for rural families.
benefits_summary: Credit support for purchasing dairy animals.
benefits_list: Loan for purchase of 1 or 2 milch animals.
Tie-up with Aavin for milk procurement.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Tamil Nadu.
Member of Milk Producers' Cooperative Society.
Ability to rear animals.
how_to_apply: Apply through the local Milk Cooperative Society or PACCS.

scheme_name: New Health Insurance Scheme (NHIS) for Employees and Pensioners
objective: A comprehensive health insurance scheme for government employees, pensioners, and their families, providing coverage for various medical treatments and hospitalizations.
benefits_summary: Cashless health insurance for Govt employees and pensioners.
benefits_list: Coverage up to ₹5 Lakhs (and up to ₹10 Lakhs for specified critical illnesses) over 4 years.
eligibility_criteria: State Government Employees, Teachers, and Pensioners.
Family members dependent on them.
how_to_apply: Automatically enrolled. ID cards issued by the Insurance Company (currently United India Insurance).

objective: A merit award for students who secure top ranks in the 12th Std public examinations, encouraging academic excellence.
benefits_summary: Cash award for top ranking BC/MBC students in Class 12.
benefits_list: Cash Prize of ₹5,000 per annum for 4-5 years (Professional courses).
Awarded to 2 boys and 2 girls in each district.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to BC/MBC/DNC community.
Secured 1st or 2nd rank in the district in 12th Std Public Exam.
Admitted to a professional college.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Backward Classes Welfare Officer.

objective: Government of India and State sponsored scholarships sanctioned from 11th standard up to research level for students from SC, ST, and OBC communities to support their education.
benefits_summary: Scholarships for BC/MBC/DNC students to pursue education.
benefits_list: Reimbursement of Tuition Fees and Special Fees.
eligibility_criteria: Belong to BC/MBC/DNC.
Annual family income < ₹2.5 Lakhs (varies by specific scholarship type).
Studying in recognized institutions.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Educational Scholarship Portal or Institution.

issuing_authority: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) Department
objective: A scheme to promote self-employment among educated unemployed youth by providing them with subsidized loans through banks to start their own business ventures.
benefits_summary: Loan with subsidy for youth to start micro-enterprises.
benefits_list: Subsidy of 25% of project cost (Max ₹2.5 Lakhs).
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Tamil Nadu.
Age: 18-45 years (General), 18-55 (Special categories).
Minimum 8th Std Pass.
Family income < ₹5 Lakhs per annum.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the MSME Online Portal (UYEGP section).

issuing_authority: Dr. Babu Jagjivan Ram Leather Industries Development Corporation Ltd (LIDKAR)
objective: The "Charmashilpa Scheme" aims to modernize the leather artisans' production methods by offering financial assistance to setup mechanized production units. This support helps leather artisan SHGs and cooperative societies enhance the quality of their products, making them more competitive.
benefits_summary: Financial support to leather artisans for mechanization and infrastructure.
benefits_list: Subsidized loans for setting up mechanized leather units.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: SC (Leather Artisan).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Office of LIDKAR or online.

issuing_authority: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Pension Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that aims to provide a monthly pension to registered construction workers who have reached 60 years of age and is a beneficiary of the Board for at least 3 years.
benefits_summary: Monthly pension for registered construction workers after retirement.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Registered Construction Worker for at least 3 years.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Retired (Age 60+).
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Labour Officer/Senior Labour Inspector.

objective: Udyogini is an innovative scheme to assist women in gaining self-reliance and economic independence through self-employment, mainly through trade and the service sector. It also provides for a subsidy on loans for undertaking business activities/ micro enterprises through banks, etc.
benefits_summary: Subsidized loans for women entrepreneurs to start businesses.
benefits_list: Subsidy: 30% for General (Max ₹90,000), 50% for SC/ST (Max ₹1.5 Lakhs).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 55 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹1,50,000.
This scheme is available for: Female.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Office of KSWDC or CDPO office.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme For Purchase Of Taxi / Goods Vehicle / Passenger Autorickshaw
objective: Those beneficiaries who are sanctioned / approved for loan from the Banks for the purchase of Passenger Autorickshaw / Goods Vehicle / Taxi will be provided a subsidy of 33% of the value of the vehicle or a maximum subsidy of Rs. 2.5 Lakhs. Women will be given priority.
benefits_summary: Subsidy for minority community members to purchase commercial vehicles.
benefits_list: Subsidy of 33% or ₹2.5 Lakhs (whichever is less).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Religious Minority Community.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹4,50,000.
Valid Driving License.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KMDC portal.

objective: Under the Shrama Shakthi Loan scheme, religious minority community entrepreneurs will be trained in artistic and technical skills and will be provided a loan of ₹50,000/- with a 4% rate of interest, to be paid in 36 installments.
benefits_summary: Low-interest loans for minority artisans and skilled workers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Minority Community.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 55 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000 (Rural) / ₹3.5 Lakh (Urban).
Skilled artisan/technician.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KMDC portal.

issuing_authority: Department of Health and Family Welfare
objective: A post-natal care scheme providing a kit with 19 essential items for the mother and newborn baby, given to women who deliver in government health facilities to improve maternal and infant health.
benefits_summary: Free kit containing essentials for mother and baby after delivery.
benefits_list: Kit includes baby clothes, soap, oil, mosquito net, etc.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Women delivering in Government Hospitals.
BPL families (Thayi Card holder).
how_to_apply: Provided by the hospital staff upon discharge.

objective: This scheme envisages partnership with corporate aggregators like OLA and UBER to facilitate self-employment of rural youth through Radio Taxi and other successful cab transport ventures. Rural youth from SC/ST communities will be provided training, mentoring and other enhanced financial assistance.
benefits_summary: Financial support for SC/ST youth to own taxis in partnership with aggregators.
benefits_list: Subsidy of ₹5 Lakhs for vehicle purchase.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Belong to SC/ST community.
Age: 21-45 years.
Annual family income < ₹5,00,000.
Minimum 10th Pass and Valid Driving License.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Social Welfare Department portal or District Offices.

objective: The Social Welfare Department of the Government of Karnataka has launched the Simple Marriage scheme, which offers incentives to couples belonging to the SC community. The scheme aims to promote the idea of simple marriages and provide financial assistance to eligible couples.
benefits_summary: Cash incentive for simple marriages involving SC individuals to discourage lavish spending.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
One or both spouses must belong to SC community.
Marriage must be simple (mass marriage/temple/registrar).
Age: 21-42 years.
how_to_apply: Apply to the District Social Welfare Officer.

objective: Under this scheme, Subsidies will be provided to the religious minority communities with the help of Nationalized / Scheduled banks to start or improve a small-scale handicraft industry, service sector, and agro-based activities.
benefits_summary: Subsidy on bank loans for minority entrepreneurs.
benefits_list: Subsidy: 50% or ₹1,00,000 (whichever is less) for Unit cost up to ₹2 Lakhs.
Subsidy: 33% or ₹2 Lakhs (whichever is less) for Unit cost ₹2-10 Lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Minority Community.
Age: 18-55 years.
Annual family income < ₹6,00,000.
Applicant's employment status: Self-employed/Unemployed.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KMDC portal.

objective: The "Incentive Scheme for Sericulture Reelers" is aimed at improving the financial condition of minority communities involved in silk reeling and spinning. The corporation will provide loans to support working capital and basic infrastructure for silk spinners.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance to minority silk reelers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Minority Community.
Engaged in Silk Reeling.
Age: 18-60 years.
how_to_apply: Apply via KMDC portal.

objective: The "Overseas Educational Loan Scheme" objective is to provide financial/loan assistance to students belonging to minority communities who are pursuing graduate or postgraduate degree courses in recognized universities abroad.
benefits_summary: Low-interest loan for minority students to study abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Minority Community.
Secured admission in foreign university.
Age: 18-35 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KMDC portal.

objective: The "Community Based Training Scheme" aims to provide training in heavy vehicle driving, shorthand, security services, office soft skills, retail sales, and beauty parlor courses to unemployed young men and women belonging to minority communities for self-employment or employment.
benefits_summary: Free skill training for minority youth.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Minority Community.
Unemployed youth aged 18-35.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KMDC portal or District Offices.

issuing_authority: Dr. Babu Jagjivan Ram Leather Industries Development Corporation Ltd (LIDKAR)
objective: The "Swavalambi/Sanchari Marata Malige" aims to assist SC leather artisans in establishing their own showrooms or mobile vans to sell their products directly to consumers. Financial assistance is provided in the form of a subsidy depending on the location of the business.
benefits_summary: Subsidy for setting up leather product shops or mobile sales vans.
benefits_list: Subsidy up to ₹2.00 Lakhs (or 50% of cost) for shop/van.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
SC Leather Artisan.
Age: 18-60 years.
how_to_apply: Apply through LIDKAR District Office.

issuing_authority: Dr. Babu Jagjivan Ram Leather Industries Development Corporation Ltd (LIDKAR)
objective: The Skill Development Training program aims to equip artisans with the skills necessary for the manufacture of footwear and leather goods. The project promotes the leather industry while enhancing the livelihoods of artisans.
benefits_summary: Training for SC leather artisans to improve skills.
benefits_list: Free training in modern leather manufacturing.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
SC Leather Artisan families.
Age: 18-45 years.
how_to_apply: Apply to LIDKAR District Coordinator.

scheme_name: Incentive For The Simple Marriage (Scheduled Tribe)
objective: The scheme "Incentive for the Simple Marriage" was launched by the Tribal Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka. The scheme provides an incentive of ₹50,000/- to ST individuals residing in Karnataka who marry another ST individual on or after 11th August 2015.
benefits_summary: Financial incentive for simple marriages within the ST community.
eligibility_criteria: Both spouses must be ST.
Resident of Karnataka.
Simple marriage ceremony.
how_to_apply: Apply to the District Tribal Welfare Officer.

objective: The PRABHUDDHA OVERSEAS SCHOLARSHIP scheme selects around 250 undergraduate and 150 postgraduate and PhD students from the SC/ST communities, providing them with financial assistance to pursue higher studies abroad.
benefits_summary: Full scholarship for SC/ST students to study abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
SC/ST Students.
Admission in foreign university.
Income < ₹8 Lakhs (100% funding), ₹8-15 Lakhs (50% or 33% funding).
how_to_apply: Apply online through the E-Kalyan/Social Welfare portal.

objective: Under this scheme, the Social Welfare Department, Govt of Karnataka will give a stipend to the Law Graduates belonging to SC/ST Community residing in Karnataka. This will help them to practice law in various courts and training courses will also be organized under the guidance of senior advocates.
benefits_summary: Monthly stipend for SC/ST law graduates during early practice.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
SC/ST Law Graduate.
Enrolled in Bar Council.
Age: 21-30 years.
Income limits apply.
how_to_apply: Apply to the District Social Welfare Office.

issuing_authority: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides a monthly pension to beneficiaries partially disabled due to worksite accidents or diseases, with an additional ex-gratia payment based on disability percentage.
benefits_summary: Pension and compensation for disabled construction workers.
benefits_list: Monthly Pension: ₹2,00,000 (Total Disability) or Pension based on %.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Construction Worker.
Disability due to work accident/disease.
Medical certification required.
how_to_apply: Submit claim to the Labour Officer.

objective: The scheme "Griha Lakshmi" aims to provide financial assistance to women heads of households in Karnataka. The scheme offers a monthly benefit of ₹2,000/- to eligible women, helping them manage household expenses and improve their economic stability.
benefits_summary: Monthly financial assistance to the woman head of the family.
benefits_list: Direct Benefit Transfer to bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Woman Head of the Family (as per Ration Card).
Resident of Karnataka.
Not an Income Tax payee.
Husband not an Income Tax payee.
Not claiming GST returns.
how_to_apply: Register at Grama One/Karnataka One/Bangalore One centers or via Seva Sindhu portal.

scheme_name: Incentive for the Inter Caste Marriage Couple (SC)
objective: The Social Welfare Department of the Government of Karnataka has launched the "Incentive for the Inter Caste Marriage Couple", which offers incentives to couples where one spouse is from the SC community and the other is non-SC. The scheme aims to promote inter-caste marriages.
benefits_summary: Financial incentive for inter-caste marriages involving SCs.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
One spouse must be SC.
Marriage registered.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Social Welfare portal.

objective: The "Vrutti Protsaha Loan Scheme" is an initiative by the Karnataka Minority Development Corporation (KMDC) to promote self-employment and economic activities among minority communities. The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible individuals from minority communities.
benefits_summary: Duplicate entry/Similar to Self Employment Scheme. Provides loans for business.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
Belong to Minority Community.
Income < ₹6 Lakhs.
how_to_apply: Apply via KMDC portal.

objective: The "Arivu Education Loan Scheme," launched by the Minorities Welfare Department, offers loans to students pursuing professional courses like MBBS, B.E., B.Tech., MBA, MCA, LLB, and others in various fields.
benefits_summary: Education loan for minority students to pursue professional courses (CET/NEET based).
benefits_list: Loan amount varies by course (e.g., up to ₹3 Lakhs for Medical, ₹1 Lakh for Engg per year).
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
Minority Student selected via CET/NEET.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KMDC Arivu portal.

objective: This scheme is a fully subsidized scheme where bore wells, pumps, and electrification are provided to small and marginal farmers from minority/SC/ST/OBC communities living in rural areas for irrigation purposes.
benefits_summary: Free borewell drilling and pump set installation for irrigation.
benefits_list: Supply of Pump Set and Accessories.
Cost up to ₹3.5 - 4.0 Lakhs covered by Govt.
eligibility_criteria: Small/Marginal Farmer.
Resident of Karnataka.
Belong to SC/ST/OBC/Minority.
Land holding 1.20 to 5 acres.
No existing borewell.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Seva Sindhu or respective Corporation portal.

objective: It was a relief scheme to provide financial support to destitute weavers of the state engaged in the handloom and textile industry whose livelihood was adversely affected by the pandemic. Now continues as annual support.
benefits_summary: Annual financial assistance to Handloom and Powerloom weavers.
eligibility_criteria: Registered Handloom/Powerloom Weaver.
Resident of Karnataka.
Seva Sindhu registration.
how_to_apply: Apply via Seva Sindhu portal.

scheme_name: Vidyasiri Food And Accommodation Scholarship Scheme
objective: Vidyasiri-Food and Accommodation Scholarship Scheme (VFAS) is a government initiative in Karnataka that provides financial assistance (Rs. 1,500 monthly) to students belonging to backward classes pursuing post-matric courses who have not secured hostel admission.
benefits_summary: Monthly allowance for food and accommodation for OBC students not in hostels.
benefits_list: Supports students staying in PG/private accommodation.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Karnataka.
OBC Student (Cat-1, 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B).
Income < ₹2.5 Lakhs.
Applied for but did not get Govt Hostel seat.
Rural student studying in city.
how_to_apply: Apply via SSP (State Scholarship Portal).

issuing_authority: Department of Women and Child Development
objective: The prime goal of this scheme aims to promote the birth of girl children in below poverty line (BPL) families. Financial assistance is provided to the girl child through her mother/father or natural guardian.
benefits_summary: Provides financial security to the girl child through health insurance, scholarships, and a maturity amount at age 18.
benefits_list: Health insurance cover up to ₹25,000 per year.
Annual scholarship ranging from ₹300 to ₹1,000 for classes 1st to 10th.
Maturity amount (approx. ₹34,751 to ₹1 Lakh) paid upon turning 18 years.
Insurance benefits of ₹1 Lakh in case of accident and ₹42,500 for natural death of the beneficiary.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Applicant's age must be between 0 and 18 years (Enrollment within 1 year of birth).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹1,20,000.
This scheme is available for: Female.
Limited to two girl children in a BPL family.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Anganwadi Centre or Child Development Project Officer (CDPO).

issuing_authority: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
objective: The "Marriage Assistance'' by the BOCW Welfare Board provides financial support to registered construction workers for the marriage expenses of their own or their two dependent children's marriages.
benefits_summary: Financial aid for the marriage expenses of construction workers or their children.
benefits_list: Applicable for the marriage of the worker or up to two dependent children.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Registered Construction Worker with active membership (min 1 year).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed/Worker.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the KBOCWWB portal or Seva Sindhu.

objective: Unnati, a first of its kind initiative of the Department of Social Welfare, intends to provide a comprehensive entrepreneurship platform for startups established by SC/ST entrepreneurs.
benefits_summary: Seed funding and support for tech-based startups led by SC/ST entrepreneurs.
benefits_list: Seed funding up to ₹50 Lakhs for innovative tech-based solutions.
Focus on rural problems like waste disposal, sanitation, and health.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 45 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: SC, ST.
Must have a registered startup with a viable tech idea.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Social Welfare Department's Unnati portal.

objective: Samruddhi scheme is an initiative from the Social Welfare Department. It serves as an enabler to the socially disadvantaged youth and women to take up entrepreneurial activities and promote self-employment.
benefits_summary: Subsidy and seed grant to take up franchises or dealerships of reputed companies.
benefits_list: Partnerships with retail, logistics, and service companies for franchises.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 35 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹2,00,000.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: SC, ST.
Required education level: Secondary (SSLC Pass).
how_to_apply: Apply online via the Samruddhi/Social Welfare portal.

scheme_name: Interest Subsidy Scheme for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Entrepreneurs
objective: The scheme aims to encourage SC/ST entrepreneurs to establish micro or small-scale service enterprises & to expand, diversify, or modernize their existing units by providing loans up to ₹10 crores at a 4% interest rate.
benefits_summary: Provides large-scale loans at a highly subsidized interest rate for SC/ST entrepreneurs.
benefits_list: Subsidy reimbursed by the Government to KSFC.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Karnataka.
Applicant's age must be between 21 and 50 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: SC, ST.
Must propose a viable industrial/service project.
how_to_apply: Apply offline at the nearest KSFC Branch Office.

issuing_authority: Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
objective: KSCSTE Post-Doctoral Fellowships will be provided to those who possess Ph.D. in science and engineering with high quality research publications in SCI journals. The program aims to encourage Ph.D. holders to pursue further research and to develop career as scientist.
benefits_summary: Monthly fellowship of ₹32,000/- (plus 10% HRA) along with an annual contingency grant of ₹50,000/-.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 27 and 40 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999 (No specific limit mentioned).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Doctorate (Ph.D. in Science/Engineering).
how_to_apply: Applications are invited online through the KSCSTE official website (typically in September-October).

issuing_authority: Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
objective: The “Back-to-Lab” programme of Women Scientists Division offers Research and Post-Doctoral Fellowship to women who have break in their career, to facilitate their re-entry in science research.
benefits_summary: Monthly fellowship of ₹22,000 - ₹30,000 (depending on qualification) plus HRA and annual contingent grant.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 30 and 50 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
This scheme is available for: Female.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
Required education level: Postgraduate or Ph.D.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the KSCSTE website when the call for proposals is announced.

scheme_name: Jeevanam Self-Employment Scheme To Dependents Of Crime Victims
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Jeevanam Self-Employment Scheme to Dependents of Crime Victims” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala to provide financial assistance for starting a self-employment venture.
benefits_summary: One-time financial assistance to start a self-employment venture.
benefits_list: Financial assistance for self-employment (amount varies based on project).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
Applicant must be a dependent of a crime victim.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Probation Office or Social Justice Department offices.

issuing_authority: Backward Classes Development Department, Government of Kerala
objective: The Scheme “OEC Pre-matric Educational Assistance” was started by the Backward Classes Development Department, to provide educational assistance to pre-matric students belonging to OEC who are studying in Government/Aided/Unaided schools from Classes 1st to 10th.
benefits_summary: Annual educational assistance ranging from ₹320 to ₹940 depending on the class.
benefits_list: Class I to IV: ₹320 per annum.
Class V to VII: ₹630 per annum.
Class VIII to X: ₹940 per annum.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 6 and 16 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000 (No limit for OEC, ₹6L for others).
Applicant must belong to one of the following communities: OBC/OEC.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Secondary.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Head of the School / Institution.

scheme_name: Educational Assistance To Children Of Prisoners (Pursuing Professional Course)
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Educational Assistance to Children of Prisoners (Pursuing Professional Course)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala to support children of prisoners in their higher education.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance of up to ₹100,000 per year for professional courses.
benefits_list: ₹100,000/- per student per year (covering annual fees & hostel fees).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 17 and 25 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate.
Parent must be a prisoner.
how_to_apply: Apply via SUNEETHI portal or District Probation Officer.
required_documents: Certificate from Jail Authority proving parent's imprisonment

scheme_name: Distress Relief Fund For The Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Distress Relief Fund for the Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala to provide financial aid for medical treatment to BPL persons with disabilities.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance of ₹5,000/- per year for medical treatment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹20,000 (Rural) / ₹22,375 (Urban) or BPL.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.

scheme_name: Vijayamritham Scheme - Cash Award For Meritorious Children With Disabilities
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Vijayamritham Scheme - Cash Award for Meritorious Children with Disabilities” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, to provide a cash award and memento to meritorious students with disabilities who excel in academics.
benefits_summary: Cash award of ₹3,000/- for Degree/PG/Professional course students with high marks.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 15 and 25 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
Must have secured high marks (usually >60-80%) in qualifying exams.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.

scheme_name: Marriage Assistance For Legally Married Transgender Couples
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Marriage Assistance for Legally Married Transgender Couples” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, intended to help deprived transgender individuals who are planning to get married legally to lead a steady normal family life.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance of ₹30,000/- for marriage expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
One or both partners must be Transgender.
Must be legally married.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal after marriage registration.

scheme_name: Marriage Assistance To Daughters Of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Marriage Assistance to Daughters of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)” provides one-time financial assistance for the marriage of daughters of differently-abled parents with low income.
benefits_summary: One-time assistance of ₹30,000/- for the marriage of the daughter.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
This scheme is available for: Female (Daughter).
Parent must be a person with disability.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (Parent).
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.

scheme_name: Matru Jyothi - Financial Assistance For PwD Mothers
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme “Matru Jyothi - Financial Assistance for PwD Mothers” launched by the Department of Social Justice, provides financial assistance to PwD mothers for the care of their newborn child.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance of ₹2,000/- towards expenses related to child birth and care.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 45 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
This scheme is available for: Female.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities.
Child must be a newborn.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.

scheme_name: Varnam Scheme For Transgender Students Pursuing Distance Education Courses
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
