objective: The scheme “Varnam Scheme for Transgender Students” provides financial assistance to Transgender students pursuing Degree/Diploma/ PG courses through distance education.
benefits_summary: Financial scholarship to cover course fees and other expenses.
benefits_list: Scholarship amount varies based on course (approx. ₹10,000 - ₹20,000 per course duration).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate, Postgraduate.
Must be a Transgender person.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.
required_documents: Admission Receipt/ID Card from Open University

objective: Kerala Government has launched a noble initiative “Snehapoorvam Scheme” to provide financial assistance to orphans who are living in the family, with their relatives, friends, or the support of the community.
benefits_summary: Monthly financial assistance ranging from ₹300 to ₹1000 based on education level.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 0 and 18 years (extended for students).
Annual family income must not exceed ₹20,000 (Rural) / ₹22,375 (Urban).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Must be an orphan or have lost one parent.
how_to_apply: Submit application to the Head of the Institution (School/College).

Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Apply through the Backward Classes Development Department regional offices.

issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: Under the scheme, financial assistance is to be provided to eligible widows and divorced women from BPL families for their remarriage.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance of ₹25,000/- for remarriage expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 50 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000 (BPL).
This scheme is available for: Female (Widow/Divorcee).
Must be a remarriage.
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.
required_documents: Proof of first marriage (Death Cert of husband/Divorce deed)

scheme_name: Marriage Assistance To Differently Abled Women (Parinayam-II)
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: The scheme provides one-time financial assistance for the marriage of unmarried differently-abled women from low-income families.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance of ₹30,000/- for marriage.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
This scheme is available for: Female.
This scheme is specifically for persons with disabilities (Applicant herself).
how_to_apply: Apply online via SUNEETHI portal.

issuing_authority: State Horticulture Mission / Department of Agriculture
objective: Aims to promote vertical gardening in urban and peri-urban areas to increase self-sufficiency and produce pesticide-free vegetables by providing subsidized vertical garden structures.
benefits_summary: Subsidy of 75% on the cost of the Arka Vertical Garden kit.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999 (Open to all, subject to availability).
Must reside in urban/peri-urban area.
how_to_apply: Apply via the official portal of SHM Kerala or local Krishi Bhavan.

objective: The scheme provides scholarships to students from minority religious communities studying Chartered Accounts/Cost & Management Accounts/Company Secretaryship courses.
benefits_summary: Annual scholarship of ₹15,000/-.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 17 and 30 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate (pursuing CA/CS/CMA).
Must belong to a Minority Community.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Directorate of Minority Welfare website.

objective: This scheme provides financial assistance per annum to students from minority religious communities domiciled in Kerala who are pursuing a diploma in nursing and paramedical courses.
benefits_summary: Annual scholarship of ₹15,000/-.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 17 and 25 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹800,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Required education level: Graduate/Diploma in Nursing/Paramedical.
Must belong to a Minority Community.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Directorate of Minority Welfare website.

scheme_name: Educational Assistance to Children of Women Headed Families
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice / Women and Child Development
objective: The scheme extends a helping hand to women-headed families (BPL/Priority) by providing financial assistance for the education of their children.
benefits_summary: Educational assistance ranging from ₹300 to ₹1000 per month.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 6 and 21 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000 (BPL).
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Student.
Household must be headed by a woman (Widow/Divorcee/Abandoned).
how_to_apply: Apply through the local Anganwadi or ICDS office.
required_documents: Proof of Woman Headed Household status

scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme For Dependents Of Prisoners
issuing_authority: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
objective: This scheme provides financial assistance to dependents of prisoners from BPL families to start a self-employment venture and secure a livelihood.
benefits_summary: One-time financial assistance for self-employment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Un-employed.
Applicant must be a dependent of a prisoner.
how_to_apply: Apply via the District Probation Office.

objective: Supports startups that have purchased or sourced Technology licenses from any Government research Institution in India by reimbursing part of the cost.
benefits_summary: Reimbursement of 90% of technology transfer cost up to ₹10 Lakhs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 55 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed (Startup Founder).
Must be a registered startup with KSUM.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Kerala Startup Mission portal.

objective: This scheme aims to encourage entrepreneurial activity in every household by setting up at least one MSME unit per family, with a focus on women's entrepreneurship and interest subvention on loans.
benefits_summary: Interest subvention on loans up to ₹10 Lakhs and handholding support.
benefits_list: Interest Subvention: Up to 4% (subject to guidelines).
Waiver of licensing fees for specific categories.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Industries Centre (DIC) or online via local body.

objective: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance as a back-ended subsidy to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises engaged in manufacturing activities in the State, proportional to the capital investment made.
benefits_summary: Investment subsidy up to ₹30 Lakhs (varies by category and region).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 65 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Must be a registered MSME.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the Directorate of Industries & Commerce website.

scheme_name: Kerala Stressed MSMEs Revival & Rehabilitation Scheme
objective: This scheme intends to provide financial and other handholding support to the MSMEs which show signs of stress and to convert their non-productive assets into productive assets.
benefits_summary: Financial assistance for restarting operations, settling liabilities, or modernization.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Unit must be classified as 'Stressed' or 'NPA'.
how_to_apply: Apply to the District Industries Centre (DIC).

scheme_name: Scheme for Interest Subvention to Nano Household Enterprises
objective: This scheme has been introduced to promote the Nano and household enterprises in the state by providing interest subvention on their term loans.
benefits_summary: Interest subvention on bank loans (reimbursement of interest portion).
benefits_list: Reimbursement of interest paid to the bank (up to a limit).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 65 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Investment in Plant & Machinery below ₹10 Lakhs.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Industries Centre (DIC).

scheme_name: Revival and Rehabilitation Scheme for Defunct MSMEs and Cashew Processing Units
objective: This scheme envisages supporting defunct MSMEs in the manufacturing sector including cashew processing units through assistance in the form of capital grants and working capital incentives.
benefits_summary: Capital grant and interest subvention for restarting the unit.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Unit must be closed/defunct for a specified period.
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Industries Centre (DIC).

issuing_authority: Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
objective: This Scheme facilitates Academia-Industry interaction by undertaking projects in collaboration with an industrial partner which should result in commercializing the product or process or service.
benefits_summary: Financial support for research project with industry collaboration.
benefits_list: Project Grant (Amount varies based on proposal).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 27 and 50 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed (Researcher/Faculty).
Required education level: Postgraduate.
Must have an Industry Partner.
how_to_apply: Apply online via KSCSTE website.

issuing_authority: Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
objective: Sastraposhini is a programme envisaged by KSCSTE for strengthening science education in schools of Kerala by providing grants for lab equipment and teacher training.
benefits_summary: Grant for establishing/upgrading science laboratories in schools.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Government-employed (School Authorities).
Must be a Government or Aided School.
how_to_apply: School Headmaster/Principal must apply to KSCSTE.

issuing_authority: Department of Industries and Commerce / Handicrafts Development Corporation
objective: This scheme provides one-time financial assistance (grant) to artisans in the handicrafts sector for setting up handicraft enterprises.
benefits_summary: Grant assistance for tools and equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹100,000.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Must be a registered artisan.
how_to_apply: Apply through District Industries Centre (DIC) or Handicrafts Development Corporation.

scheme_name: Scheme for Margin Money Grant to Nano Units
objective: A loan-linked scheme that aims to provide financial assistance as margin money to Nano units in the State engaged in manufacturing, job work & service activity.
benefits_summary: Margin money grant assistance for bank loans.
benefits_list: Grant: Up to 30-40% of the project cost (Max limit applies).
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 65 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Project cost must be below ₹10 Lakhs (Nano unit).
how_to_apply: Apply through the District Industries Centre (DIC).

scheme_name: Soft Loan Scheme for Women Entrepreneurs
objective: This scheme supports women-led startups with a soft loan of up to ₹15 Lakhs as working capital for implementing works and projects received from Government departments and PSUs in Kerala.
benefits_summary: Soft loan up to ₹15 Lakhs at 6% simple interest.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Kerala.
Applicant's age must be between 18 and 55 years.
Annual family income must not exceed ₹9,999,999.
This scheme is available for: Female.
Applicant's employment status must be one of the following: Self-employed.
Must be a KSUM registered startup with >51% women shareholding.
how_to_apply: Apply online via Kerala Startup Mission portal.
